-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- Hot reload: the loader can swap this plugin's code in place, so every ALWAYS-ON connection
-- (the ones made at module load rather than per window mount) is registered here and disconnected
-- by the teardown. Without it a reload would leave the old copy's loops running forever.
local function SVFXlive(c)
	local reg = _G.__SineVFX_LIVE
	if not reg then reg = {}; _G.__SineVFX_LIVE = reg end
	reg[#reg+1] = c
	return c
end
--  Sine VFX Plugin  –  Modular Entry Point
--  Structure:
--    Utilities/  Theme, Helpers, UIKit, Window
--    Modules/    EmitterAPI, ColorPalette
--    Windows/    EmitWindow, CodeWindow, ColorWindow
-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

-- Boot shim. Normally this runs as a real plugin Script and `script`/`plugin` are provided by
-- Studio. Under the auto-updating LOADER there is no plugin Script for this file: the loader
-- rebuilds the module tree, then runs this source as a chunk and hands it the pieces a chunk
-- cannot see (`plugin` is per-script, not a _G global). Everything else is untouched.
local __BOOT = _G.__SineVFX_BOOT
local plugin = plugin or (__BOOT and __BOOT.plugin)
local script = script or (__BOOT and __BOOT.script)
local root = (__BOOT and __BOOT.root) or script.Parent  -- ServerStorage.SineVFX

-- Edit-only: never initialise during play/run testing
if not game:GetService("RunService"):IsEdit() then return end


-- ── Tester access gate ────────────────────────────────────────
-- Only whitelisted UserIds (public GitHub list) may load the plugin.
local _TesterGate = require(root.Utilities.TesterGate)
_TesterGate.plugin = plugin  -- lets the gate cache a known-good tester so a later network blip / denied HTTP prompt doesn't lock them out
local authorized, reason, uid = _TesterGate.check()
if not authorized then
	warn(("[SineVFX] Access restricted to testers. UserId %d not authorized (%s).")
		:format(uid, reason))
	return
end

-- ── Settings proxy: cache reads + write-behind ────────────────────────────────
-- plugin:GetSetting/SetSetting are ~10-29ms each (Roblox re-serialises the whole
-- settings blob per call), so touching settings on window open / layout load stalled
-- the UI. This proxy caches every read and coalesces writes onto a spread background
-- flush, so callers see instant get/set. All modules use ctx.plugin, which is this.
local _realPlugin = plugin
local plugin = (function(rp)
	local cache, loaded, dirty = {}, {}, {}
	local scheduled, flushing = false, false
	local px = {}
	function px.GetSetting(_, key)
		if loaded[key] then return cache[key] end
		local v = rp:GetSetting(key); cache[key] = v; loaded[key] = true; return v
	end
	local function eq(a, b)
		if a == b then return true end
		if type(a) ~= "table" or type(b) ~= "table" then return false end
		for k, v in pairs(a) do if b[k] ~= v then return false end end
		for k, v in pairs(b) do if a[k] ~= v then return false end end
		return true
	end
	local function flush()
		if flushing then return end
		local keys = {}; for k in pairs(dirty) do keys[#keys+1] = k end; dirty = {}
		if #keys == 0 then return end
		flushing = true
		task.spawn(function()
			for _, k in ipairs(keys) do
				rp:SetSetting(k, cache[k])
				task.wait(0.03)  -- ~1 (29ms) write per ~2 frames: persist without tanking FPS
			end
			flushing = false
			if next(dirty) then flush() end  -- writes that arrived mid-flush
		end)
	end
	function px.SetSetting(_, key, value)
		if loaded[key] and eq(cache[key], value) then return end  -- unchanged: no disk write at all
		cache[key] = value; loaded[key] = true; dirty[key] = true
		if not scheduled then scheduled = true; task.defer(function() scheduled = false; flush() end) end
	end
	function px.flushNow(_)
		local keys = {}; for k in pairs(dirty) do keys[#keys+1] = k end; dirty = {}
		for _, k in ipairs(keys) do rp:SetSetting(k, cache[k]) end
	end
	return setmetatable(px, {
		__index = function(_, k)
			local v = rp[k]
			if type(v) == "function" then return function(_, ...) return v(rp, ...) end end
			return v
		end,
		__newindex = function(_, k, v) rp[k] = v end,
	})
end)(_realPlugin)

-- ── Require modules ────────────────────────────────────────────
local Theme      = require(root.Utilities.Theme)
local Helpers    = require(root.Utilities.Helpers)
local Preloader  = require(root.Utilities.Preloader)
local UIKitFactory  = require(root.Utilities.UIKit)
local WinFactoryFn  = require(root.Utilities.Window)

local EmitterAPI    = require(root.Modules.EmitterAPI)
local Updater       = require(root.Modules.Updater)
local EmitterRuntime= require(root.Modules.EmitterRuntime)
local TrailRuntime  = require(root.Modules.EmitterRuntime).Trail
local BeamRuntime   = require(root.Modules.EmitterRuntime).Beam

local EmitWindowFn    = require(root.Windows.EmitWindow)
local CodeWindowFn    = require(root.Windows.CodeWindow)
local ColorWindowFn   = require(root.Windows.ColorWindow)
local ShifterWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ShifterWindow)
local ResizerWindowFn  = require(root.Windows.ResizerWindow)
local ParticleCounterWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ParticleCounterWindow)
local RetimerWindowFn  = require(root.Windows.RetimerWindow)
local CopierWindowFn  = require(root.Windows.CopierWindow)
local TrailPreviewWindowFn = require(root.Windows.TrailPreviewWindow)
local VfxModuleWindowFn = require(root.Windows.VfxModuleWindow)
local SettingsWindowFn = require(root.Windows.SettingsWindow)
local CurveWindowFn   = require(root.Windows.CurveWindow)
local ColSeqWindowFn  = require(root.Windows.ColSeqWindow)
local ColorWheelFn    = require(root.Windows.ColorWheel)
local ToolsWindowFn   = require(root.Windows.ToolsWindow)
local HubWindowFn      = require(root.Windows.HubWindow)
local ThemeEditorWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ThemeEditorWindow)
local ImportThemeWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ImportThemeWindow)
local ExportThemeWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ExportThemeWindow)
local ConfirmWindowFn = require(root.Windows.ConfirmWindow)
local TransformWindowFn = require(root.Windows.TransformWindow)

-- ── Services ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
local toolbar          = plugin:CreateToolbar("Sine VFX")
local toggleBtn        = toolbar:CreateButton("Sine VFX", "Toggle Plugin Windows", "rbxassetid://117583440180772")
local coreGui          = game:GetService("CoreGui")
local selection        = game:GetService("Selection")
local userInputService = game:GetService("UserInputService")
local tweenService     = game:GetService("TweenService")
local CHS              = game:GetService("ChangeHistoryService")
local textService      = game:GetService("TextService")
local runService       = game:GetService("RunService")
local collectionService= game:GetService("CollectionService")

-- ── Persistent sound widget (always alive, never destroyed on UI toggle) ──
local soundWidget = plugin:CreateDockWidgetPluginGui(
	"SineVFX_SoundPlayer",
	DockWidgetPluginGuiInfo.new(Enum.InitialDockState.Float, false, false, 0, 0, 0, 0)
)
soundWidget.Title = ""; soundWidget.Enabled = false

-- ── Shared context table (passed into all window modules) ─────
local pcBursts = {}  -- transient :Emit() bursts for the Particle Counter: {emitter,n,expiry}
-- Depth-capped descendant walk shared by ctx.descendantsTo and ctx.getTrails3D. A real local
-- rather than a ctx field because `local ctx = {...}` does not bring `ctx` into scope inside its
-- own initialiser, so fields cannot call each other through ctx.
local function descendantsTo(rootInst, maxDepth)
	maxDepth = maxDepth or 4
	local out = {}
	local function rec(inst, d) if d<=0 then return end for _,ch in ipairs(inst:GetChildren()) do out[#out+1]=ch; rec(ch, d-1) end end
	rec(rootInst, maxDepth)
	return out
end

-- True if `inst` is a tagged clone sitting in the preview container -- output we spawned, not
-- something the user authored, so it must never show up as a selectable transformed trail/beam.
-- Nesting inside a RenderPart is deliberately NOT this case: that ribbon is authored content and
-- gets its full row set (including Count/Delay/Duration) like any standalone one.
local function previewCopy(inst)
	local a = inst and inst.Parent
	while a and a ~= game do
		if a.Name == "SineVFXParticles" then return true end
		a = a.Parent
	end
	return false
end

-- True if `inst` is strictly INSIDE a RenderPart (the emitter's visual template) -- content the user
-- nested there. The RenderPart itself is NOT "inside" it, so it stays non-transformable.
-- STRUCTURAL ONLY -- no selection test in here. getTransformables asks this about instances that
-- are by definition directly selected, so a "picked directly" escape would make it always false and
-- silently stop anything inside a RenderPart from being transformable. The tool-gating rule
-- (direct selection wins) lives in ctx.isInsideEmitter / Transformed.isInsideEmitter instead.
local function insideRenderPart(inst)
	local a = inst and inst.Parent
	while a and a ~= game do
		if a.Name == "RenderPart" and a.Parent and collectionService:HasTag(a.Parent, "SineVFX3D") then return true end
		a = a.Parent
	end
	return false
end


-- ── Version / update state ──────────────────────────────────────────
-- The startup update check is async, and Settings is usually already built by the time it answers,
-- so listeners re-render whatever is showing the version.
local _updateState = { checking = true }
local _updateListeners = {}
local function _setUpdateState(st)
	_updateState = st or { checking = false }
	for _, fn in ipairs(_updateListeners) do pcall(fn) end
end
local ctx = {
	plugin          = plugin,
	pcBursts        = pcBursts,
	pcRecordEmit    = function(em, n)
		n = tonumber(n) or 0
		if not em or n <= 0 then return end
		local lmin, lmax, ts = 2, 2, 1
		pcall(function()
			if em:IsA("ParticleEmitter") then
				local lt=em.Lifetime; lmin=lt.Min; lmax=lt.Max
				local t=em.TimeScale; if t and t>0 then ts=t end
			end
		end)
		if lmax <= 0 then lmax = 0.5 end
		pcBursts[#pcBursts+1] = {emitter=em, n=n, born=os.clock(), lmin=lmin, lmax=lmax, ts=ts}
		if #pcBursts > 400 then table.remove(pcBursts, 1) end
	end,
	coreGui         = coreGui,
	selection       = selection,
	-- The Transform feature's eligible instances. Two kinds, and they never mix:
	--   * childless BaseParts (a part WITH children is excluded)  -> a 3D particle emitter
	--   * childless Trails, but ONLY when the selection is nothing but those -> a 3D trail
	-- The Trail rule is what keeps Emit and Transform mutually exclusive. A childless part is
	-- never emittable, so offering Transform for one can't clash with the Emit window. A Trail
	-- IS emittable (it's a toggleable), so if it could be transformed alongside anything else,
	-- both the Emit window and the Transform button would show at once -- the one thing the
	-- two-GUI swap is built to avoid. Trails-only selection => EmitWindow hides (it checks that
	-- every toggleable it found is in this list) and Transform stands in for it.
	-- Shared by EmitWindow (self-hide) + TransformWindow (show).
	-- A transformed ribbon sitting inside a RenderPart: authored template content. Editable when
	-- you select it directly, but it never emits standalone copies (they would pin themselves to the
	-- hidden template and show up as stray ribbons at the emitter), so Emit leaves it alone.
	ribbonInRenderPart = insideRenderPart,
	getTransformables = function()
		local seen, list = {}, {}
		local sel = selection:Get()
		-- Is every selected object an untransformed Trail? Decided up front: the answer gates
		-- whether ANY trail is eligible, so it can't be a per-object test in the loop below.
		local ribbonsOnly = false   -- every selected obj is a childless untransformed Trail OR Beam
		do
			local n = 0
			for _, obj in pairs(sel) do
				if obj == game or obj == workspace then continue end
				n += 1
				-- A Trail with ANYTHING inside it is excluded, mirroring the childless-BasePart rule:
				-- nested content means the user is doing something with this trail already, so Emit
				-- stays and Transform keeps out of the way. Safe against transformed trails even
				-- though transforming GIVES the trail a child (its Properties folder) -- the tag test
				-- rejects those first, so the child count only ever judges untransformed trails.
				local eligible = (obj:IsA("Trail") or obj:IsA("Beam"))
					and #obj:GetChildren() == 0
					and not collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFXTransformedTrail")
					and not collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFXTransformedBeam")
				if not eligible then n = -1; break end
			end
			ribbonsOnly = (n > 0)
		end
		for _, obj in pairs(sel) do
			if obj == game or obj == workspace then continue end
			if seen[obj] then continue end
			-- A Trail nested inside a 3D emitter is template content (it rides on each particle),
			-- not a standalone effect -- same exclusion the parts branch makes below.
			if ribbonsOnly and (obj:IsA("Trail") or obj:IsA("Beam")) then
				local anc, tagged = obj.Parent, false
				while anc and anc ~= game do
					if collectionService:HasTag(anc, "SineVFX3D") then tagged = true; break end
					anc = anc.Parent
				end
				-- Allow a childless ribbon nested INSIDE a RenderPart to be transformed; only ribbons
				-- riding directly under a 3D emitter (not in its template) stay out.
				if (not tagged) or insideRenderPart(obj) then seen[obj] = true; list[#list+1] = obj end
				continue
			end
			if obj:IsA("BasePart") and not obj:IsA("Terrain") and #obj:GetChildren() == 0 and not seen[obj] then
				-- skip a part that IS, or is INSIDE, a transformed emitter (part stays as the
				-- emitter root now, so a transformed part has children + the tag; its RenderPart
				-- clone is a childless part whose ancestor carries the tag)
				if collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFX3D") then continue end
				local anc, tagged = obj.Parent, false
				while anc and anc ~= game do
					if collectionService:HasTag(anc, "SineVFX3D") then tagged = true; break end
					anc = anc.Parent
				end
				-- A childless part nested INSIDE a RenderPart is still transformable (turn it into its
				-- own nested 3D emitter). Only skip parts under a 3D emitter that are NOT in the template
				-- (and the RenderPart itself: childless, but the emitter body, not nested content).
				if tagged and not insideRenderPart(obj) then continue end
				seen[obj] = true; list[#list+1] = obj
			end
		end
		return list
	end,
	-- Selected SineVFX3D emitters (the transformed PART, resolved from the part itself or any
	-- selected descendant e.g. its RenderPart). Used by PropertiesWindow + runtime.
	getEmitters = function()
		local seen, list = {}, {}
		for _, obj in pairs(selection:Get()) do
			local e = nil
			if obj:IsA("BasePart") and collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFX3D") then
				e = obj
			else
				local anc = obj.Parent
				while anc and anc ~= game do
					if collectionService:HasTag(anc, "SineVFX3D") then e = anc; break end
					anc = anc.Parent
				end
				-- Content the user nested INSIDE the RenderPart (the emitter's visual template) is
				-- edited as itself, not as the whole 3D Particle class -- so selecting it must NOT
				-- resolve to the emitter. The RenderPart itself still resolves (it's the body).
				if e then
					local rp = e:FindFirstChild("RenderPart")
					if rp and obj ~= rp and obj:IsDescendantOf(rp) then e = nil end
				end
			end
			if e and not seen[e] then seen[e] = true; list[#list+1] = e end
		end
		return list
	end,
	-- Selected SineVFXTransformedTrail trails (the transformed Trail itself, or one resolved from a
	-- selected descendant e.g. its Properties folder). The Trail counterpart of getEmitters;
	-- used by PropertiesWindow to populate the "Transformed Trail" class.
	getTrails3D = function()
		local seen, list = {}, {}
		local function add(t)
			if t and not seen[t] and not previewCopy(t) then seen[t] = true; list[#list+1] = t end
		end
		for _, obj in pairs(selection:Get()) do
			if obj == game or obj == workspace then continue end
			-- the object itself
			if obj:IsA("Trail") and collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFXTransformedTrail") then add(obj) end
			-- ...anything transformed BELOW it: selecting the part (or a model, or a folder) that
			-- holds the trail should show the trail's rows, which is how every other class in
			-- Properties already behaves. Without this you'd have to select the Trail itself.
			for _, d in pairs(descendantsTo(obj)) do
				-- ...but NOT content nested in a RenderPart: that is the emitter's template, and
				-- selecting the emitter should show the emitter's rows, not its trail's. Select the
				-- trail itself when you want to edit it.
				if d:IsA("Trail") and collectionService:HasTag(d, "SineVFXTransformedTrail") and not insideRenderPart(d) then add(d) end
			end
			-- ...and anything transformed ABOVE it, so selecting a transformed trail's own
			-- Properties folder or Configuration still resolves back to the trail.
			local anc = obj.Parent
			while anc and anc ~= game do
				if anc:IsA("Trail") and collectionService:HasTag(anc, "SineVFXTransformedTrail") then add(anc); break end
				anc = anc.Parent
			end
		end
		return list
	end,
	-- Selected SineVFXTransformedBeam beams (the transformed Beam itself, or one resolved from a
	-- selected descendant e.g. its Properties folder). The Beam counterpart of getTrails3D; used by
	-- PropertiesWindow to populate the "Transformed Beam" class.
	getBeams3D = function()
		local seen, list = {}, {}
		local function add(t)
			if t and not seen[t] and not previewCopy(t) then seen[t] = true; list[#list+1] = t end
		end
		for _, obj in pairs(selection:Get()) do
			if obj == game or obj == workspace then continue end
			if obj:IsA("Beam") and collectionService:HasTag(obj, "SineVFXTransformedBeam") then add(obj) end
			for _, d in pairs(descendantsTo(obj)) do
				-- see getTrails3D: template content is not surfaced by selecting its emitter
				if d:IsA("Beam") and collectionService:HasTag(d, "SineVFXTransformedBeam") and not insideRenderPart(d) then add(d) end
			end
			local anc = obj.Parent
			while anc and anc ~= game do
				if anc:IsA("Beam") and collectionService:HasTag(anc, "SineVFXTransformedBeam") then add(anc); break end
				anc = anc.Parent
			end
		end
		return list
	end,
	-- True if `inst` lives INSIDE a SineVFX3D emitter (e.g. a ParticleEmitter/Beam/Sound the user
	-- nested in a RenderPart). The generic tools use this to ignore emitter-internal template content
	-- so a 3D emitter's own nested VFX isn't emitted/edited/counted/previewed as if it were standalone.
	version = function() return Updater.VERSION end,
	updateState = function() return _updateState end,
	onUpdateState = function(fn)
		if type(fn) == "function" then _updateListeners[#_updateListeners+1] = fn end
	end,
	isInsideEmitter = function(inst)
		-- Direct selection wins (same rule as Transformed.isInsideEmitter): clicking the nested
		-- ParticleEmitter/Sound/Trail itself means the user wants THAT instance, so every window
		-- treats it normally. Only reaching it via the emitter keeps it hidden.
		if inst then
			for _, o in ipairs(selection:Get()) do if o == inst then return false end end
		end
		local a = inst and inst.Parent
		while a and a ~= game do
			if collectionService:HasTag(a, "SineVFX3D") then return true end
			a = a.Parent
		end
		return false
	end,
	userInputService = userInputService,
	tweenService    = tweenService,
	CHS             = CHS,
	textService     = textService,
	runService      = runService,
	soundRef        = { widget = soundWidget },   -- persistent, always valid
	globalRevertColorPreview = nil,
	-- windows push imperatively-styled refreshers here (e.g. Properties axis buttons); called by applyInPlace on live theme change so an in-place (no-remount) theme switch still refreshes derived colors
	themeRefreshers = {},
	-- Depth-limited descendant collector (perf: don't walk an entire huge selection)
	descendantsTo = descendantsTo,
}

-- ── Instantiate UIKit and WinFactory (need ctx) ────────────────
local UIKit      = UIKitFactory(tweenService, Theme)

UIKit.initTooltips(coreGui, userInputService, plugin)  -- hover-description tooltips (reads SineVFX_tooltipsOn/Delay live)-- Migrate legacy interface settings whose meaning changed (corner px->multiplier, order 0->10000)
do
	local _do = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_displayOrder"))
	if _do ~= nil and _do < 100 then plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_displayOrder", nil) end
	local _cr = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_cornerRadius"))
	if _cr ~= nil and _cr > 2 then plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_cornerRadius", nil) end
end
local WinFactory = WinFactoryFn(ctx, Theme, UIKit)
local LayoutStore
local _suppressAutoSave = false
-- Auto-save layout on window move/resize (debounced, 1 heartbeat delay)
do
	local _asConn = nil
	WinFactory.onWindowSaved = function()
		if _suppressAutoSave then return end
		if _asConn then _asConn:Disconnect(); _asConn=nil end
		_asConn = SVFXlive(game:GetService("RunService").Heartbeat:Connect(function()
			_asConn:Disconnect(); _asConn=nil
			if not LayoutStore or _suppressAutoSave then return end
			local active = LayoutStore.getActive()
			-- "Default" is IMMUTABLE -- never auto-save over it (the user's own arrangement persists
			-- independently via each window's SineVFX_win_<key>). Default stays the built-in preset.
			if active == "Default" then return end
			local wins, open, corners, scale, edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn = LayoutStore.snapshot()
			-- update the saved entry
			local saved = LayoutStore.savedLayouts()
			local found = false
			for _,l in ipairs(saved) do
				if l.name == active then l.wins=wins; l.open=open; l.corners=corners; l.scale=scale; l.edgeMargin=edgeMargin; l.edgeMarginOn=edgeMarginOn; found=true; break end
			end
			if not found then saved[#saved+1] = {name=active, wins=wins, open=open, corners=corners, scale=scale, edgeMargin=edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn=edgeMarginOn} end
			LayoutStore.saveList(saved)
		end))
	end
end
local rethemeRemount, applyThemeProps, _applyLayout  -- assigned later
local ThemeStore = require(root.Utilities.ThemeStore)(Theme, plugin, function() if rethemeRemount then rethemeRemount() end end)
if Theme.C.windowBg == nil then Theme.C.windowBg = Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end  -- safety: ensure independent default survives require cache
LayoutStore = require(root.Utilities.LayoutStore)(plugin)
do  -- one-time: reset all theme opacities to visible (undo accidental full transparency)
	if plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_opacityResetV1")~=true then
		local themes=ThemeStore.list()
		for _,t in ipairs(themes) do t.props=t.props or {}; for _,k in ipairs({"bgOpacity","baseOpacity","surfaceOpacity","accentOpacity","borderOpacity","textOpacity","dangerOpacity","imageOpacity"}) do t.props[k]=1 end end
		ThemeStore.saveList(themes)
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_opacityResetV1", true)
	end
end
if ThemeStore.seedPresets then ThemeStore.seedPresets() end  -- one-time: add built-in starter themes

if ThemeStore.fixToggleOff then ThemeStore.fixToggleOff() end 
if ThemeStore.fixTreeLine then ThemeStore.fixTreeLine() end  -- one-time: give each preset its own tree-line color -- one-time: lift toggleOffColor off base so OFF toggles are visible
ThemeStore.applySilent()  -- mutate Theme.C from the persisted active theme before any window builds
if Theme.C.windowBg == nil then Theme.C.windowBg = Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
	if Theme.C.content == nil then Theme.C.content = Color3.fromRGB(35,45,70) end
	if Theme.C.header == nil then Theme.C.header = Color3.fromRGB(42,52,78) end
	if Theme.C.hub == nil then Theme.C.hub = Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
-- corner radius is now theme-driven (Theme "All Corners")

-- Kick off image preloading immediately (non-blocking, fire-and-forget)
Preloader.preload()

-- ── Init EmitterAPI ────────────────────────────────────────────
EmitterAPI.init(ctx.soundRef, Helpers)
	EmitterRuntime.init()
	TrailRuntime.init()
	BeamRuntime.init()
if EmitterAPI.setEmitHook then EmitterAPI.setEmitHook(ctx.pcRecordEmit) end
shared.sv = EmitterAPI.sv

-- ── Window instances ───────────────────────────────────────────
local Emit_WINDOW_ID    = "EmitterController"
local CODE_WINDOW_ID    = "VFXCode"
local COLOR_WINDOW_ID   = "ColorControl"
local SHIFTER_WINDOW_ID = "Shifter"
local RESIZER_WINDOW_ID  = "Resizer"
local PARTICLECOUNTER_WINDOW_ID = "ParticleCounter"
local RETIMER_WINDOW_ID  = "Retimer"
local COPIER_WINDOW_ID   = "Copier"
local CURVE_WINDOW_ID    = "CurveEditor"
local COLSEQ_WINDOW_ID   = "ColSeqEditor"
local COLORWHEEL_WINDOW_ID = "ColorWheel"
local TOOLS_WINDOW_ID    = "Tools"
	local TRAILPREVIEW_WINDOW_ID = "TrailPreview"
	local VFXMODULE_WINDOW_ID = "VfxModule"
	local SETTINGS_WINDOW_ID = "Settings"
local THEME_EDITOR_WINDOW_ID = "ThemeEditor"
local IMPORT_THEME_WINDOW_ID = "ImportTheme"
local EXPORT_THEME_WINDOW_ID = "ExportTheme"

-- ── App window registry ────────────────────────────────────────
local App = { windows = {}, _listeners = {} }
function App:register(id, handlers) self.windows[id] = handlers end
function App:onChange(fn) table.insert(self._listeners, fn) end
function App:_notify(id) for _, fn in ipairs(self._listeners) do fn(id) end end
-- Editor windows are transient (opened via Props 3-dots) and never persist open state
local TRANSIENT = { [CURVE_WINDOW_ID]=true, [COLSEQ_WINDOW_ID]=true, [COLORWHEEL_WINDOW_ID]=true, [THEME_EDITOR_WINDOW_ID]=true, [IMPORT_THEME_WINDOW_ID]=true, [EXPORT_THEME_WINDOW_ID]=true, [CONFIRM_WINDOW_ID]=true }

function App:toggle(id)
	local win = self.windows[id]
	if not win then return end
	if win.isOpen() then
		win.unmount()
	else
		UIKit.setInstant(true)
		local okM,errM = pcall(function() win.mount(App, id) end)
		if okM then WinFactory.focusLast() else warn("[SineVFX] mount error: "..tostring(errM)) end
		task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false); if applyThemeProps then applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps, true) end; _suppressAutoSave = false end)
	end
	if not TRANSIENT[id] then
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id, self.windows[id].isOpen())
	end
	self:_notify(id)
end
function App:closeAll()
	for _, win in pairs(self.windows) do win.unmount() end
end

-- ── Plugin button ──────────────────────────────────────────────
local pluginEnabled = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_open") == true  -- restore last open state

-- ── Auto-hide tool windows when nothing SineVFX-editable is selected ──────────
-- Off by default (SineVFX_hideWhenNoEmittable). Hide/show flips ScreenGui.Enabled
-- (NOT unmount/mount, which rebuilt every window's UI = lag). Settings/Hub/Transform
-- stay enabled so the toggle is always reachable (no lock-out).
local CollSvc = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local coreGuiSvc = game:GetService("CoreGui")
local EDITABLE_CLASSES = {"ParticleEmitter","Beam","Trail","Light","Sound"}
local KEEP_GUI = { SineVFX_Hub=true, SineVFX_HubFAB=true, SineVFX_Transform=true, SineVFX_Settings=true }
local hideWhenEmpty = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_hideWhenNoEmittable") == true
local _hiddenGuis = {}   -- ScreenGuis we disabled (only these get re-enabled)

local function isEditable(inst)
	if CollSvc:HasTag(inst, "SineVFX3D") then return true end
	for _,cls in ipairs(EDITABLE_CLASSES) do if inst:IsA(cls) then return true end end
	return false
end
-- Allocation-free early-exit walk (capped depth); avoids building a full descendant table.
local function anyEditableDeep(inst, depth)
	if isEditable(inst) then return true end
	if depth<=0 then return false end
	for _,ch in ipairs(inst:GetChildren()) do
		if anyEditableDeep(ch, depth-1) then return true end
	end
	return false
end
local function selectionEditable()
	for _,obj in ipairs(selection:Get()) do
		if obj==game or obj==workspace then continue end
		if not obj:IsDescendantOf(game) then continue end
		if anyEditableDeep(obj, 5) then return true end
	end
	return false
end
local function restoreAutoHidden()
	for _,g in ipairs(_hiddenGuis) do
		if g and g.Parent then g.Enabled = true end
	end
	_hiddenGuis = {}
end
local function hideToolWindows()
	for _,g in ipairs(coreGuiSvc:GetChildren()) do
		if g:IsA("ScreenGui") and g.Name:sub(1,7)=="SineVFX" and not KEEP_GUI[g.Name] and g.Enabled then
			g.Enabled = false
			_hiddenGuis[#_hiddenGuis+1] = g
		end
	end
end
local function applyAutoHide()
	if not pluginEnabled then return end
	if not hideWhenEmpty then restoreAutoHidden(); return end
	if selectionEditable() then restoreAutoHidden() else hideToolWindows() end
end
local _ahQueued = false
local function queueAutoHide()
	if _ahQueued then return end
	_ahQueued = true
	task.defer(function() _ahQueued = false; applyAutoHide() end)
end
selection.SelectionChanged:Connect(queueAutoHide)
WinFactory.setHideWhenNoEmittable = function(on)
	hideWhenEmpty = (on and true or false)
	applyAutoHide()
end-- Re-theme: re-mount every open window so fresh builds read the mutated Theme.C
-- Apply non-color theme props (radius/bg image/opacities/font/text) to the live UI
applyThemeProps = function(p, onlyNew)
	p = p or ThemeStore.DEFAULT_PROPS
	local rr = tonumber(p.borderRadius) or 1
	pcall(function() UIKit.setCornerScale(rr) end)
	if UIKit.setPartScale then pcall(function() UIKit.setPartScale("window", tonumber(p.windowCorners) or 1); UIKit.setPartScale("card", tonumber(p.cardCorners) or 1); UIKit.setPartScale("button", tonumber(p.buttonCorners) or 1); UIKit.setPartScale("toggle", tonumber(p.toggleCorners) or 1) end) end
	if WinFactory.setThemeBg then WinFactory.setThemeBg(p.bgImage, p.imageFit, 1-(tonumber(p.imageTransparency) or 0), 1-(tonumber(p.bgTransparency) or 0), p.tileSize, p.imageColor, p.imageStatic) end
	local fam=nil
	if p.font and p.font~="Default" then fam=ThemeStore.fontFamily(p.font) end
	local tscale=tonumber(p.textScale) or 1
	ctx.themeTextScale=tscale
	local tSurf=tonumber(p.surfaceTransparency) or 0; local tBord=tonumber(p.borderTransparency) or 0; local tBase=tonumber(p.baseTransparency) or 0
	local tText=tonumber(p.textTransparency) or 0; local tAcc=tonumber(p.accentTransparency) or 0; local tDang=tonumber(p.dangerTransparency) or 0
	local tSurf2=tonumber(p.surface2Transparency) or 0; local tCurve=tonumber(p.curveBgTransparency) or 0; local tTopbar=tonumber(p.topbarTransparency) or 0; local tContent=tonumber(p.contentTransparency) or 0; local tHub=tonumber(p.hubTransparency) or 0; local tContentBord=tonumber(p.contentBorderTransparency) or 0; local tPropField=tonumber(p.propFieldTransparency) or 0; local tPropFieldBord=tonumber(p.propFieldBorderTransparency) or 0
	local Cl=Theme.C
	Theme.treeLineColor = (p.treeLineColor and p.treeLineColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.treeLineColor) or Color3.fromRGB(185,199,245)
	Theme.treeLineTransparency = tonumber(p.treeLineTransparency) or 0.7
	Theme.treeLineGroupTransparency = tonumber(p.treeLineGroupTransparency) or 0.92
	Theme.propFieldColor = (p.propFieldColor and p.propFieldColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.propFieldColor) or Color3.fromRGB(21,30,48)
	Theme.toggleOffColor = (p.toggleOffColor and p.toggleOffColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.toggleOffColor, Cl.base) or Cl.base
	Theme.toggleKnobColor = (p.toggleKnobColor and p.toggleKnobColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.toggleKnobColor, Cl.white) or Cl.white
		Theme.toggleKnobOnColor = (p.toggleKnobOnColor and p.toggleKnobOnColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.toggleKnobOnColor, Cl.white) or (Theme.toggleKnobColor or Cl.white)
		Theme.toggleKnobOffColor = (p.toggleKnobOffColor and p.toggleKnobOffColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.toggleKnobOffColor, Cl.textDim) or Cl.textDim
	Theme.dropdownBorderColor = (p.dropdownBorderColor and p.dropdownBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.dropdownBorderColor, Cl.surface) or Cl.surface
	Theme.buttonBorderColor = (p.buttonBorderColor and p.buttonBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.buttonBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.fieldBorderColor = (p.fieldBorderColor and p.fieldBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.fieldBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.toggleBorderColor = (p.toggleBorderColor and p.toggleBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.toggleBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.windowBorderColor = (p.windowBorderColor and p.windowBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.windowBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.propNameBorderColor = (p.propNameBorderColor and p.propNameBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.propNameBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.propValueBorderColor = (p.propValueBorderColor and p.propValueBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.propValueBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	Theme.contentBorderColor = (p.contentBorderColor and p.contentBorderColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(p.contentBorderColor, Cl.border) or Cl.border
	local function baseA(d,nm,cur) local v=d:GetAttribute(nm); if v==nil then v=cur; d:SetAttribute(nm,v) end return v end
	for _,g in ipairs(coreGui:GetChildren()) do
		if g:IsA("ScreenGui") and g.Name:sub(1,7)=="SineVFX" then
			if not (onlyNew and g:GetAttribute("SVFXpropsApplied")) then
			for _,d in ipairs(g:GetDescendants()) do
				if d:IsA("TextLabel") or d:IsA("TextButton") or d:IsA("TextBox") then
					if fam and fam.family then local bf=d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFont"); if not bf then bf=d.Font.Name; d:SetAttribute("SVFXbaseFont",bf) end; local wt=Enum.FontWeight.Regular; local wov=d:GetAttribute("SVFXfontWeight"); if wov then for _,w in ipairs(Enum.FontWeight:GetEnumItems()) do if w.Value==wov then wt=w; break end end elseif bf:find("Black") then wt=Enum.FontWeight.Heavy elseif bf:find("Bold") then wt=Enum.FontWeight.Bold elseif bf:find("Semibold") then wt=Enum.FontWeight.SemiBold elseif bf:find("Medium") then wt=Enum.FontWeight.Medium end; pcall(function() d.FontFace=Font.new(fam.family, wt) end)
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFamily") then local wov=d:GetAttribute("SVFXfontWeight"); local wt=Enum.FontWeight.Regular; if wov then for _,w in ipairs(Enum.FontWeight:GetEnumItems()) do if w.Value==wov then wt=w; break end end end; pcall(function() d.FontFace=Font.new(d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFamily"), wt) end) elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFont") then pcall(function() d.Font = Enum.Font[d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFont")] end) end
					local bts=d:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseTS"); if not bts then bts=d.TextSize; d:SetAttribute("SVFXbaseTS",bts) end
					d.TextSize=math.max(1, math.floor(bts*tscale+0.5))
					local btt=baseA(d,"SVFXbaseTT",d.TextTransparency); if btt<1 then d.TextTransparency=btt+(1-btt)*tText end
				end
				if d:IsA("UIStroke") and d:GetAttribute("SVFXborder") then
					local bst=baseA(d,"SVFXbaseST",d.Transparency); local tb = d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropfieldborder") and tPropFieldBord or (d:GetAttribute("SVFXcontentborder") and tContentBord or tBord); d.Transparency=bst+(1-bst)*tb
				end
				if d:IsA("UIStroke") then
					-- per-element outline COLOR (precedence: most-specific tag first)
					if d:GetAttribute("SVFXddoutline") then d.Color = Cl.border:Lerp(Cl.accent,0.5)
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXddborder") then d.Color = Theme.dropdownBorderColor or Cl.surface
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXcontentborder") then d.Color = Theme.contentBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXheaderborder") then d.Color = Cl.header  -- (grid redesign) properties grid outline tracks the header color
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropnameborder") then d.Color = Theme.propNameBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropvalborder") then d.Color = Theme.propValueBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropfieldborder") then d.Color = Theme.fieldBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXtoggleborder") then d.Color = Theme.toggleBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbordercat")=="window" then d.Color = Theme.windowBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbordercat")=="button" then d.Color = Theme.buttonBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbordercat")=="field" then d.Color = Theme.fieldBorderColor or Cl.border
					elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXbordercat")=="card" then d.Color = Cl.border
					end
				end
				if d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropfield") then d.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.propFieldColor or Cl.surface; local pfb=baseA(d,"SVFXbaseBT",d.BackgroundTransparency); d.BackgroundTransparency = pfb+(1-pfb)*tPropField end
				if d:GetAttribute("SVFXtoggleoff") and d:GetAttribute("ToggleIsOff") then d.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleOffColor or Cl.base end
				if d:GetAttribute("SVFXtoggleknob") then local _off = d.Parent and d.Parent:GetAttribute("ToggleIsOff"); d.BackgroundColor3 = _off and (Theme.toggleKnobOffColor or Cl.textDim) or (Theme.toggleKnobOnColor or Cl.white) end
				if d:IsA("GuiObject") and not d:IsA("TextBox") and not d:GetAttribute("SVFXwindowBg") and not d:GetAttribute("SVFXnoFade") and not d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropfield") then
					local bbt=baseA(d,"SVFXbaseBT",d.BackgroundTransparency)
					if bbt<1 then
						local tt=0
						if d:GetAttribute("SVFXdanger") then tt=tDang
						elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXtopbar") then tt=tTopbar
						elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXcontentbg") then tt=1  -- content list bg is ALWAYS transparent (shows window bg through it)
						elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXhub") then tt=tHub
						elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXhubTrans") then tt=tHub  -- hub transparency, but keep own (base) color
						elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.surface then tt=tSurf elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.base then tt=tBase elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.accent then tt=tAcc elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.danger then tt=tDang elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.surface2 then tt=tSurf2 elseif d.BackgroundColor3==Cl.CurveBG then tt=tCurve end
						d.BackgroundTransparency=bbt+(1-bbt)*tt
					end
				end
			end
			g:SetAttribute("SVFXpropsApplied", true) end
		end
	end
end
local function recolorGui(g)
	if not g then return end
	local prev=ThemeStore.prevColors; if not prev then return end
	local Cl=Theme.C; local KEYS=ThemeStore.KEYS
	-- NOTE: only recolor a key that actually CHANGED this apply (Cl[k]~=prev[k]). base & surface2 share the
	-- default RGB and base is first in KEYS, so without this guard an edited Button Color (surface2) is masked by base.
	local FGKEYS={"text","textDim","white","border","accent","handle","Pinned","loop","rep","repet","repet2","danger","base","surface","surface2","CurveBG"}
	-- Matching an element's CURRENT color against each key's OLD value is ambiguous when two
	-- keys shared that old value (e.g. a theme with Border == Base) -- skip the guess rather
	-- than risk recoloring an unrelated element to the key that was actually edited.
	local dup = ThemeStore.prevDup or {}
	local function uniq(v) return (dup[ThemeStore.c2s(v)] or 0) <= 1 end
	for _,d in ipairs(g:GetDescendants()) do
		if d:IsA("GuiObject") then
			if d:GetAttribute("SVFXslTrack") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.base
			elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXslFill") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.accent
			elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXslKnob") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.white
			elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXwindowBg") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.windowBg elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXcontentbg") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.content elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXhub") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.hub elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXdangerbtn") or d:GetAttribute("SVFXdanger") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.danger elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXtopbar") then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl.base elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXpropfield") or d:GetAttribute("SVFXhubTrans") then elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXtreeLineCls") then d.BackgroundColor3=Theme.treeLineColor or Cl.text; d.BackgroundTransparency=Theme.treeLineTransparency or 0.7 elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXtreeLineGrp") then d.BackgroundColor3=Theme.treeLineColor or Cl.text; d.BackgroundTransparency=Theme.treeLineGroupTransparency or 0.92 else local rk=d:GetAttribute("SVFXbgKey"); if rk then if Cl[rk] then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl[rk] end else for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do if k~="windowBg" and k~="content" and k~="hub" and k~="axisX" and k~="axisY" and k~="axisZ" and prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and d.BackgroundColor3==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d.BackgroundColor3=Cl[k]; d:SetAttribute("SVFXbgKey",k); break end end end end
		end
		if d:IsA("TextLabel") or d:IsA("TextButton") or d:IsA("TextBox") then
			if d:GetAttribute("SVFXdangertext") then d.TextColor3=Cl.danger:Lerp(Cl.white,0.4) else local rk=d:GetAttribute("SVFXfgKey"); if rk then if Cl[rk] then d.TextColor3=Cl[rk] end else for _,k in ipairs(FGKEYS) do if prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and d.TextColor3==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d.TextColor3=Cl[k]; d:SetAttribute("SVFXfgKey",k); break end end end end
		end
		if d:IsA("TextBox") then
			if d:GetAttribute("SVFXphaccent") then d.PlaceholderColor3=Cl.accent:Lerp(Cl.textDim,0.4)
			elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXphdim") then d.PlaceholderColor3=Cl.textDim:Lerp(Cl.base,0.4)
			else for _,k in ipairs(FGKEYS) do if prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and d.PlaceholderColor3==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d.PlaceholderColor3=Cl[k]; break end end end
		end
		if d:IsA("ImageLabel") or d:IsA("ImageButton") then
			local rk=d:GetAttribute("SVFXimgKey"); if rk then if Cl[rk] then d.ImageColor3=Cl[rk] end else for _,k in ipairs(FGKEYS) do if prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and d.ImageColor3==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d.ImageColor3=Cl[k]; d:SetAttribute("SVFXimgKey",k); break end end end
		end
		if d:IsA("UIStroke") then
			if d:GetAttribute("SVFXslStroke") then
				d.Color = Cl["accent"]
			elseif d:GetAttribute("SVFXborder") then
				-- SVFXborder is tagged ONLY on default-colored strokes (makeBorder col==nil), so
				-- unconditionally repaint to the live border color. The old Cl~=prev guard skipped
				-- the repaint whenever prevColors went stale under the live-preview throttle, which
				-- is why re-editing the border color "didn't take" the second time.
				d.Color = Cl["border"]
			else
				for _,k in ipairs(FGKEYS) do if prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and d.Color==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d.Color=Cl[k]; break end end
			end
		end
		local _bc=d:GetAttribute("BaseColor")
		if typeof(_bc)=="Color3" then
			for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do if k~="windowBg" and k~="content" and k~="hub" and k~="axisX" and k~="axisY" and k~="axisZ" and prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and _bc==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d:SetAttribute("BaseColor", Cl[k]); break end end
		end
		local _hb=d:GetAttribute("HoverBase")
		if typeof(_hb)=="Color3" then
			for _,k in ipairs(FGKEYS) do if prev[k] and Cl[k]~=prev[k] and _hb==prev[k] and uniq(prev[k]) then d:SetAttribute("HoverBase", Cl[k]); break end end
		end
	end
end
-- LIVE apply (no remount): recolor every SineVFX gui in place + apply props. Fast = no lag.
local function applyInPlace()
	-- Safety: fill colors that are genuinely missing (nil ONLY - equal colors
	-- can be a legitimate user theme choice and must not be clobbered)
	local Cl=Theme.C; if Cl.windowBg==nil then Cl.windowBg=Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
	if Cl.content==nil then Cl.content=Color3.fromRGB(35,45,70) end
	if Cl.header==nil then Cl.header=Color3.fromRGB(42,52,78) end
	if Cl.hub==nil then Cl.hub=Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
	-- Sync the tree-line scalars from the active theme's PROPS *before* recolorGui runs: recolorGui
	-- paints the tree lines from Theme.treeLine*, but applyThemeProps (which sets them) runs AFTER
	-- recolorGui below -- so without this pre-sync the lines lag one theme-switch behind (stale color).
	local _tp = ThemeStore.activeProps or {}
	Theme.treeLineColor = (_tp.treeLineColor and _tp.treeLineColor~="") and ThemeStore.s2c(_tp.treeLineColor) or Color3.fromRGB(185,199,245)
	Theme.treeLineTransparency = tonumber(_tp.treeLineTransparency) or 0.7
	Theme.treeLineGroupTransparency = tonumber(_tp.treeLineGroupTransparency) or 0.92
	for _,g in ipairs(coreGui:GetChildren()) do if g:IsA("ScreenGui") and g.Name:sub(1,7)=="SineVFX" then recolorGui(g) end end
	applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps)
	if WinFactory.refreshFocusColors then WinFactory.refreshFocusColors() end
	if ctx.themeRefreshers and #ctx.themeRefreshers>0 then
		local keep={}
		for _,fn in ipairs(ctx.themeRefreshers) do local ok,alive=pcall(fn); if ok and alive~=false then keep[#keep+1]=fn end end
		ctx.themeRefreshers=keep
	end
end
-- Throttle live theme preview so dragging a color slider doesn't re-walk every window (~2-3k
-- elements) every frame. Trailing edge guarantees the final value is applied.
local _liveThr=false; local _livePend=false
local function _liveThrottled()
	if _liveThr then _livePend=true; return end
	_liveThr=true; applyInPlace()
	task.delay(0.01, function() _liveThr=false; if _livePend then _livePend=false; _liveThrottled() end end)
end
ThemeStore.onLive = _liveThrottled
rethemeRemount = function()
	local openIds = {}
	for id, win in pairs(App.windows) do if win.isOpen() and id~=THEME_EDITOR_WINDOW_ID and id~=COLORWHEEL_WINDOW_ID then openIds[#openIds+1]=id end end
	UIKit.setInstant(true)
	-- Preserve window stacking across the remount: without this, rebuilding every window in
	-- arbitrary pairs() order sends the last-remounted one to the front, so toggling a theme
	-- reshuffled which window was on top. preserveZ makes each window restore its saved z.
	if WinFactory.setPreserveZ then WinFactory.setPreserveZ(true) end
	for _, id in ipairs(openIds) do pcall(function() App.windows[id].unmount() end) end
	for _, id in ipairs(openIds) do
		local okM, errM = pcall(function() App.windows[id].mount(App, id) end)
		if not okM then warn("[SineVFX] retheme mount error ("..tostring(id).."): "..tostring(errM)) end
	end
	if pluginEnabled then pcall(function() HubWin.unmount(); HubWin.mount(); TransformWin.unmount(); TransformWin.mount() end) end
	if WinFactory.setPreserveZ then WinFactory.setPreserveZ(false) end
	task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false); applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps) end)
end

_applyLayout = function(layout)
	if not layout then return end
	_suppressAutoSave = true
	-- Only re-apply scale/margin when they ACTUALLY differ from current (setUiScale defers a
	-- content-fit recompute for every window -- wasteful when the scale didn't change).
	if WinFactory.setUiScale and layout.scale and (not WinFactory.getUiScale or math.abs(WinFactory.getUiScale()-(tonumber(layout.scale) or 1))>1e-3) then
		pcall(function() WinFactory.setUiScale(layout.scale) end)
	end
	if WinFactory.setEdgeMargin and layout.edgeMargin ~= nil and (not WinFactory.getEdgeMargin or WinFactory.getEdgeMargin()~=(tonumber(layout.edgeMargin) or 5)) then
		pcall(function() WinFactory.setEdgeMargin(layout.edgeMargin) end)
	end
	if WinFactory.setEdgeMarginEnabled and layout.edgeMarginOn ~= nil and (not WinFactory.getEdgeMarginEnabled or WinFactory.getEdgeMarginEnabled()~=(layout.edgeMarginOn and true or false)) then
		pcall(function() WinFactory.setEdgeMarginEnabled(layout.edgeMarginOn) end)
	end
	UIKit.setInstant(true)
	local open = layout.open or {}
	-- Only TOGGLE windows whose open-state changes; windows that stay open are repositioned in place.
	for id, win in pairs(App.windows) do
		if id ~= SETTINGS_WINDOW_ID and not TRANSIENT[id] then
			local shouldOpen = open[id]==true
			if win.isOpen() and not shouldOpen then
				pcall(function() win.unmount() end)
			elseif shouldOpen and not win.isOpen() then
				pcall(function() win.mount(App, id) end)
			end
		end
	end
	-- Reposition every still-open window straight from the IN-MEMORY layout (not from settings), so
	-- we never block on the ~1s of plugin:SetSetting writes. Settings are persisted in the background
	-- below (chunked, cancellable) purely so a reload restores this layout.
	if WinFactory.reapplyAll then pcall(function() WinFactory.reapplyAll(nil, layout.wins) end) end
	if LayoutStore.writeToSettingsAsync then LayoutStore.writeToSettingsAsync(layout)
	else pcall(function() LayoutStore.writeToSettings(layout) end) end
	task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false); if applyThemeProps then applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps, true) end; _suppressAutoSave = false end)
end
local function openAll()
	UIKit.setInstant(true)
	for id, _ in pairs(App.windows) do
		if not App.windows[id].isOpen() then App.windows[id].mount(App, id) end
	end
	task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false) end)
end
local function closeAll()
	for id, win in pairs(App.windows) do
		if not TRANSIENT[id] then
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id, win.isOpen())
		end
		win.unmount()
	end
end

-- First-install layout seed + UI scale restore. Runs at LOAD (not only when
-- the plugin starts enabled) so a fresh install gets its default arrangement
-- the first time the user clicks the toolbar button.
if LayoutStore then
	if not plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_layoutSeeded") then
		-- A returning user whose flag was never set by the old code still has persisted per-window
		-- state -- detect that and DON'T clobber it; only a fresh install (no state) gets the seed.
		local _hasState = false
		for _, _k in ipairs(LayoutStore.STATE_KEYS or {}) do
			if plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_win_".._k) ~= nil then _hasState = true; break end
		end
		if not _hasState then
			local _act = LayoutStore.getActive()
			for _, _l in ipairs(LayoutStore.list()) do
				if _l.name == _act then pcall(LayoutStore.writeToSettings, _l); break end
			end
		end
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_layoutSeeded", true)
	end
	if WinFactory.setUiScale then pcall(function() WinFactory.setUiScale(tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_uiScale")) or 1) end) end
end

if pluginEnabled then
	-- Restore individual window open states + geometry.
	-- Seed the active (Default) layout into the per-window keys ONLY on a truly FIRST use, so a fresh
	-- install opens in a sensible arrangement. On EVERY later launch we do NOT re-seed -- each window
	-- restores from its OWN persisted geometry/open-state (SineVFX_win_<key> / SineVFX_winopen_<id>),
	-- i.e. wherever the user left it. (Re-seeding every launch was resetting window positions/sizes
	-- back to the layout on every reload -- the bug the user reported.)
	UIKit.setInstant(true)
	for id, win in pairs(App.windows) do
		if TRANSIENT[id] then continue end
		local wasOpen = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id)
		if wasOpen == nil then wasOpen = false end  -- default closed for new windows
		if wasOpen then local _ok,_e=pcall(win.mount, App, id); if not _ok then warn("[SineVFX] mount error ("..tostring(id).."): "..tostring(_e)) end end
	end
	task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false) end)
	HubWin.mount()
	TransformWin.mount()
	EmitterRuntime.setActive(true)
	TrailRuntime.setActive(true)
	BeamRuntime.setActive(true)
	task.defer(function() applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps) end)
	task.defer(applyAutoHide)
end

toggleBtn.Click:Connect(function()
	pluginEnabled = not pluginEnabled
	plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_open", pluginEnabled)
	if pluginEnabled then
		UIKit.setInstant(true)
		for id, win in pairs(App.windows) do
			if TRANSIENT[id] then continue end
			local wasOpen = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id)
			if wasOpen == nil then wasOpen = false end
			if wasOpen then local _ok,_e=pcall(win.mount, App, id); if not _ok then warn("[SineVFX] mount error ("..tostring(id).."): "..tostring(_e)) end end
		end
		task.defer(function() UIKit.setInstant(false) end)
		HubWin.mount()
		TransformWin.mount()
		EmitterRuntime.setActive(true)
		TrailRuntime.setActive(true)
		BeamRuntime.setActive(true)
		task.defer(function() applyThemeProps(ThemeStore.activeProps) end)
		task.defer(applyAutoHide)
	else
		closeAll()
		_hiddenGuis = {}
		HubWin.unmount()
		TransformWin.unmount()
		EmitterRuntime.setActive(false)
		TrailRuntime.setActive(false)
		BeamRuntime.setActive(false)
	end
end)

-- Update check: one HTTP read of the repo's version.json, deferred so it never delays startup.
-- Failure is silent by design (offline Studio, GitHub down) -- it only ever ADDS information.
task.defer(function()
	local ok, st = pcall(Updater.check)
	_setUpdateState(ok and st or { checking = false, err = "check failed" })
	if ok and st and st.behind then
		warn(("[SineVFX] Update available: v%s (you have v%s). Download: %s")
			:format(tostring(st.latest), tostring(Updater.VERSION), tostring(st.download)))
	end
end)
-- Full teardown. Used by Studio unload AND by the loader's in-place update: everything this
-- copy of the plugin owns has to be gone before a new copy is started, or the two fight over the
-- same GUIs and both sets of loops keep running.
local function teardown()
	plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_open", pluginEnabled)
	App:closeAll()
	HubWin.unmount()
	TransformWin.unmount()
	EmitterRuntime.stop()
	TrailRuntime.stop()
	BeamRuntime.stop()
	-- always-on loops registered via SVFXlive (per-window ones die with their unmount)
	local live = _G.__SineVFX_LIVE
	if live then
		for _, c in ipairs(live) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
		_G.__SineVFX_LIVE = nil
	end
	if plugin.flushNow then plugin:flushNow() end  -- persist all pending write-behind settings synchronously
end
_G.__SineVFX_TEARDOWN = teardown   -- the loader calls this before swapping in a new version
plugin.Unloading:Connect(teardown)

-- EmitWindow: Emitter Controller UI
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory, Helpers)
	local C       = Theme.C
	local TI      = Theme.TI
	local DRAG_TI = Theme.DRAG_TI
	local tw      = UIKit.tw

	local plugin          = ctx.plugin
	local selection       = ctx.selection
	local userInputService = ctx.userInputService
	local tweenService    = ctx.tweenService
	local runService      = ctx.runService
	local CHS             = ctx.CHS
	local coreGui         = ctx.coreGui

	local WIN_W    = 260
	local WIN_H    = 147
	local MIN_W    = 20
	local MAX_W    = 520
	local TOPBAR_H = 25
	local ROW_H    = 24
	local BTN_H    = 25
	local KSIZE    = 8
	local LABEL_W  = 72
	local TRACK_W  = 42
	local TRACK_H  = 22
	local KNOB_D   = 16
	local FLOAT_W   = 180
	local FLOAT_H   = 26
	local FLOAT_GAP = 8
	local BOX_SIDE  = 30
	local BOX_GAP   = 0
	local FLOAT_TI = Theme.FLOAT_TI

	-- Module-level state
	local isPinned            = false
	local runningRepeat       = false
	local stopAfterCycle      = false
	local suppressLoopCount   = 0  -- overlapping bursts each add 1; visual unblocks when ALL finish
	local cachedRoots         = {}
	local loopedSoundClones   = {}
	local soundCloneConns     = {}
	local loopConns           = {}
	local isTyping            = { Emit=false, Delay=false, Duration=false }
	local mainGui = nil
	local selectionConn, uisConn = nil, nil

	local function clearLoopConns()
		for _, c in pairs(loopConns) do c:Disconnect() end
		loopConns = {}
	end

	local function cleanup()
		runningRepeat = false; isPinned = false; cachedRoots = {}
		for _, cl in pairs(loopedSoundClones) do pcall(function() cl:Stop(); cl:Destroy() end) end
		for _, co in pairs(soundCloneConns)   do pcall(function() co:Disconnect() end) end
		loopedSoundClones = {}; soundCloneConns = {}
		clearLoopConns()
		if uisConn        then uisConn:Disconnect() end
		if selectionConn  then selectionConn:Disconnect() end
		if mainGui        then mainGui:Destroy() end
		mainGui = nil; uisConn = nil; selectionConn = nil
	end

	local function isOpen() return mainGui and mainGui.Parent ~= nil end

	local function mount(App, MAIN_WINDOW_ID)
		cleanup()
		isPinned = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Emit") == true

		mainGui = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		mainGui.Name = "SineVFX_EmitterController_v6"
		mainGui.ResetOnSpawn = false
		mainGui.ZIndexBehavior = Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		mainGui.DisplayOrder = 10000
		mainGui.Parent = coreGui

		local currentTargetHeight = WIN_H
		local contentMode         = "full"
		local BASE_H              = WIN_H
		local openRow             = nil
		local activeBox           = nil
		local activeAttr          = nil
		local sliderMin           = 0
		local sliderMax           = 1
		-- True while the panel's own Min/Max boxes are being typed into. Clicking one of them pulls
		-- focus OFF the field's textbox, which fires its FocusLost and would close the whole panel
		-- out from under the user mid-edit. Every hover/focus-driven close checks this; the
		-- deliberate closes (selection change, content mode) pass force=true and ignore it.
		local editingRange        = false
		local slidingActive       = false
		local mouseOnPanel        = false  -- hoisted: disarmRange() below reads it before its old declaration point
		local delayToken          = 0
		local historyInProgress   = false
		local historyLabel        = "SineVFX Change"
		local runningRepeatFake   = false

		-- Build window frame via WinFactory
		local win = WinFactory.new({
			stateKey="EmitterController",
			noSaveH=true,
			gui = mainGui, name = "WindowWrapper",
			w = WIN_W, h = WIN_H,
			pos = UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0.5, 0),
			minW = MIN_W, maxW = MAX_W,
			noYResize = true,  -- height is managed programmatically; handles show but don't resize
			topbarH = TOPBAR_H,
			getPinned = function() return isPinned end,
		})
		local main       = win.main
		local bgFrame    = win.bgFrame
		local topbar     = win.topbar
		local dragHandle = win.dragHandle
		local dragStroke = win.dragStroke
		local cSize      = win.currentSize

		-- Title
		local titleLbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
		titleLbl.Size = UDim2.new(1, -60, 1, 0); titleLbl.Position = UDim2.new(0, 12, 0, 0)
		titleLbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1; titleLbl.Text = "Emit"
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(titleLbl, "Emit particles from the selected VFX.") end
		titleLbl.TextColor3 = C.text; titleLbl.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold
		titleLbl.TextSize = 13; titleLbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		titleLbl.ZIndex = 3; titleLbl.Parent = topbar

		-- Pin & Close buttons
		local pinBtn, pinIcon = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, "120413064155653", -48, 15)
		local closeBtn, _     = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, "101421401454601", -26, 10)

		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned = not isPinned
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Emit", isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", isPinned)
			tw(pinIcon, { ImageColor3 = isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim })
		end)

		if isPinned then
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", true)
			pinIcon.ImageColor3 = C.Pinned
		end		closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() App:toggle(MAIN_WINDOW_ID) end)

		-- Dim title + topbar icons when unfocused
		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col       = focused and C.text    or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			local iconCol   = focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			local pinnedCol = focused and C.Pinned  or C.Pinned
			task.defer(function()
				for _, ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch, {TextColor3 = col}) end
					if ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then
						if ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") then
							tw(ch, {ImageColor3 = pinnedCol})
						else
							tw(ch, {ImageColor3 = iconCol})
						end
					end
				end
			end)
		end)	-- Content frame
		local content = Instance.new("Frame")
		content.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,1,-TOPBAR_H); content.Position = UDim2.new(0,0,0,TOPBAR_H)
		content.BackgroundTransparency = 1; content.Parent = bgFrame
		local layout = Instance.new("UIListLayout")
		layout.Padding = UDim.new(0,5); layout.HorizontalAlignment = Enum.HorizontalAlignment.Center
		layout.SortOrder = Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder; layout.Parent = content
		local pad = Instance.new("UIPadding")
		pad.PaddingTop = UDim.new(0,5); pad.PaddingBottom = UDim.new(0,5)
		pad.PaddingLeft = UDim.new(0,5); pad.PaddingRight = UDim.new(0,5); pad.Parent = content

		-- Row builder (no inline slider)
		local function makeRow(labelText, order)
			local row = Instance.new("Frame")
			row.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,0,ROW_H); row.BackgroundTransparency = 1
			row.LayoutOrder = order; row.Parent = content
			-- Label pill styled like the value box
			local lblFrame = Instance.new("Frame")
			lblFrame.Size = UDim2.new(0,LABEL_W-10,0,ROW_H-2); lblFrame.Position = UDim2.new(0,0,0,1)
			lblFrame.BackgroundColor3 = C.base; lblFrame.BorderSizePixel = 0
			lblFrame.ZIndex = 2; lblFrame.Parent = row
			UIKit.corner(lblFrame, 6)
			local lbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
			lbl.Size = UDim2.new(1,-8,1,0); lbl.Position = UDim2.new(0,6,0,0)
			lbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1
			lbl.Text = labelText; lbl.TextColor3 = C.textDim
			lbl.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold; lbl.TextSize = 12
			lbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; lbl.ZIndex = 3; lbl.Parent = lblFrame
			local box = Instance.new("TextBox")
			box.Size = UDim2.new(1,-LABEL_W+2,0,ROW_H-2); box.Position = UDim2.new(0,LABEL_W-3,0,1)
			box.BackgroundColor3 = C.base; box.TextColor3 = C.white
			box.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold; box.TextSize = 12
			box.ClearTextOnFocus = false; box.Text = (labelText == "Delay") and "0" or "1"
			box.BorderSizePixel = 0; box.Parent = row
			UIKit.corner(box, 6)
			UIKit.numericOnly(box)
			box.Focused:Connect(function() end)
			box.FocusLost:Connect(function() tw(box, { BackgroundColor3 = C.base    }) end)
			return box, row
		end

		local emitBox,     emitRow     = makeRow("Count",    1)
		local delayBox,    delayRow    = makeRow("Delay",    2)
		local durationBox, durationRow = makeRow("Duration", 3)

		-- Floating slider panel
		local floatPanel = Instance.new("Frame")
		floatPanel.Size = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0, FLOAT_H)  -- starts at 0 width, tweens open
		floatPanel.BackgroundColor3 = C.surface
		floatPanel.BorderSizePixel = 0
		floatPanel.Visible = false
		floatPanel.ClipsDescendants = true
		floatPanel.ZIndex = 20
		floatPanel.Parent = mainGui
		UIKit.corner(floatPanel, 7)
		

		-- Min box (left)
		local floatMinBox = Instance.new("TextBox")
		floatMinBox.Size = UDim2.new(0, BOX_SIDE, 1, -6)
		floatMinBox.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0, 0.5)
		floatMinBox.Position = UDim2.new(0, 3, 0.5, 0)
		floatMinBox.BackgroundColor3 = C.base

		UIKit.numericOnly(floatMinBox)		floatMinBox.TextColor3 = C.textDim
		floatMinBox.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold; floatMinBox.TextSize = 10
		floatMinBox.Text = "0"; floatMinBox.ClearTextOnFocus = false
		floatMinBox.BorderSizePixel = 0; floatMinBox.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Center
		floatMinBox.ZIndex = 21; floatMinBox.Parent = floatPanel
		UIKit.corner(floatMinBox, 5)

		-- Max box (right)
		local floatMaxBox = Instance.new("TextBox")
		floatMaxBox.Size = UDim2.new(0, BOX_SIDE, 1, -6)
		floatMaxBox.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(1, 0.5)
		floatMaxBox.Position = UDim2.new(1, -3, 0.5, 0)
		floatMaxBox.BackgroundColor3 = C.base

		UIKit.numericOnly(floatMaxBox)		floatMaxBox.TextColor3 = C.textDim
		floatMaxBox.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold; floatMaxBox.TextSize = 10
		floatMaxBox.Text = "1"; floatMaxBox.ClearTextOnFocus = false
		floatMaxBox.BorderSizePixel = 0; floatMaxBox.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Center
		floatMaxBox.ZIndex = 21; floatMaxBox.Parent = floatPanel
		UIKit.corner(floatMaxBox, 5)

		-- Slider in the middle
		local SL_PAD = BOX_SIDE + BOX_GAP + 3
		local sliderFrame = Instance.new("Frame")
		sliderFrame.Position = UDim2.new(0, SL_PAD, 0, 0)
		sliderFrame.Size = UDim2.new(1, -SL_PAD*2, 1, 0)
		sliderFrame.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		sliderFrame.ClipsDescendants = false
		sliderFrame.ZIndex = 21; sliderFrame.Parent = floatPanel
		local floatSl = UIKit.makeHSlider(sliderFrame, KSIZE, FLOAT_H/2 - 11)
		floatSl.fill.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
		floatSl.knob.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
		floatSl.kStroke:Destroy()
		floatSl.minLbl.Visible = false; floatSl.maxLbl.Visible = false

		-- Restore saved min/max
		floatMinBox.Text = tostring(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_EmitSlMin") or 0)
		floatMaxBox.Text = tostring(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_EmitSlMax") or 1)

		local function getSliderMin() return tonumber(floatMinBox.Text) or sliderMin end
		local function getSliderMax() return tonumber(floatMaxBox.Text) or sliderMax end

		local function positionFloatPanel(rowFrame)
			task.defer(function()
				if not (floatPanel and floatPanel.Parent) then return end
				local mainAbs = main.AbsolutePosition
				local mainW   = main.AbsoluteSize.X
				local rowAbs  = rowFrame.AbsolutePosition
				local rowH    = rowFrame.AbsoluteSize.Y
				local screenW = mainGui.AbsoluteSize.X
				local y = rowAbs.Y + (rowH - FLOAT_H) / 2
				local rightX = mainAbs.X + mainW + FLOAT_GAP
				if rightX + FLOAT_W <= screenW then
					floatPanel.Position = UDim2.new(0, rightX, 0, y)
				else
					floatPanel.Position = UDim2.new(0, mainAbs.X - FLOAT_W - FLOAT_GAP, 0, y)
				end
			end)
		end

			if floatPanel.Visible and openRow then positionFloatPanel(openRow) end

		main:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(function()
