					end
					chk(obj)
					for _,d in pairs(ctx.descendantsTo(obj)) do chk(d) end
				end
			end
			CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Copier After")
		end)

		rebuildList()
	end

	return { mount=mount, unmount=cleanup, isOpen=isOpen }
end

-- ToolsWindow: standalone tools list window
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory, App, WIN_IDS)
	local C      = Theme.C
	local ICONS  = Theme.ICONS
	local TXT    = Theme.TXT
	local tw     = UIKit.tw
	local plugin = ctx.plugin
	local userInputService = ctx.userInputService
	local coreGui = ctx.coreGui

	local TOPBAR_H  = 25
	local ITEM_H    = 28
	local ITEM_GAP  = 4
	local TPAD      = 4
	local SB_W      = 7
	local SB_GAP    = 3
	local DOT_SIZE  = 6
	local DOT_GAP   = 5

	local TOOL_ITEMS = {
		{ label = "Shifter",  id = WIN_IDS.shifter,  tip = "Add/subtract a value across selected VFX." },
		{ label = "Resizer",  id = WIN_IDS.resizer,  tip = "Scale sizes by a multiplier." },
		{ label = "Retimer",  id = WIN_IDS.retimer,  tip = "Speed up / slow down VFX." },
		{ label = "Copier",   id = WIN_IDS.copier,   tip = "Copy properties between VFX." },
		{ label = "VFX Code",     id = WIN_IDS.code,     tip = "Generate Luau for the VFX." },
		{ label = "Color Picker",    id = WIN_IDS.color,    tip = "Pick and apply colors." },
		{ label = "Properties",    id = WIN_IDS.props,    tip = "Edit all VFX properties." },
		{ label = "Trail Preview", id = WIN_IDS.trailpreview, tip = "Live Trail preview." },
		{ label = "Particle Counter", id = WIN_IDS.particlecounter, tip = "Live particle count." },
	}

	local N_ITEMS   = #TOOL_ITEMS
	local TOOLS_MIN_H = 20
	local TOOLS_MAX_H = TOPBAR_H + TPAD + N_ITEMS*(ITEM_H+ITEM_GAP)

	local toolsGui  = nil
	local isPinned  = false

	local function cleanup()
		if toolsGui then toolsGui:Destroy() end
		toolsGui = nil; isPinned = false
	end

	local function isOpen() return toolsGui ~= nil and toolsGui.Parent ~= nil end

	local function mount(AppRef, TOOLS_ID)
		cleanup()
		isPinned = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Tools") == true
		isPinned = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Tools") == true

		toolsGui = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		toolsGui.Name = "SineVFX_Tools"
		toolsGui.ResetOnSpawn = false
		toolsGui.ZIndexBehavior = Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		toolsGui.DisplayOrder = 10008
		toolsGui.Parent = coreGui

		local winW = 150
		local win = WinFactory.new({
			stateKey = "ToolsWindow",
			gui = toolsGui, name = "ToolsWrapper",
			w = winW, h = TOOLS_MAX_H,
			pos = UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0.5, 0),
			minW = 20, maxW = 260,
			minH = TOOLS_MIN_H, maxH = TOOLS_MAX_H,
			noYResize = false, noSaveH = false,
			topbarH = TOPBAR_H,
			getPinned = function() return isPinned end,
		})
		local main    = win.main
		local bgFrame = win.bgFrame
		local topbar  = win.topbar

		-- Title
		local titleLbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
		titleLbl.Size = UDim2.new(1,-60,1,0); titleLbl.Position = UDim2.new(0,12,0,0)
		titleLbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1; titleLbl.Text = "Tools"
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(titleLbl, "Open any SineVFX tool.") end
		titleLbl.TextColor3 = C.text; titleLbl.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold
		titleLbl.TextSize = 13; titleLbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		titleLbl.ZIndex = 6; titleLbl.Parent = topbar

		local pinBtn, pinIcon = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, ICONS.pin,   -48, 15)
		local closeBtn, _     = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, ICONS.close, -26, 10)

		if isPinned then
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", true)
			pinIcon.ImageColor3 = C.Pinned
		end
		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned = not isPinned
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Tools", isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", isPinned)
			tw(pinIcon, {ImageColor3 = isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim})
		end)
		if isPinned then
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", true)
			pinIcon.ImageColor3 = C.Pinned
		end
		closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..TOOLS_ID, false)
			AppRef:toggle(TOOLS_ID)
		end)

		-- Focus callback
		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col       = focused and C.text    or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			local iconCol   = focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			local pinnedCol = focused and C.Pinned  or C.Pinned
			task.defer(function()
				for _, ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch, {TextColor3 = col}) end
					if ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then
						if ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") then
							tw(ch, {ImageColor3 = pinnedCol})
						else
							tw(ch, {ImageColor3 = iconCol})
						end
					end
				end
			end)
		end)

		-- Content
		local content = Instance.new("Frame")
		content.Position = UDim2.new(0,0,0,TOPBAR_H); content.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,1,-TOPBAR_H)
		content.BackgroundTransparency = 1; content.ZIndex = 3; content.Parent = bgFrame

		local listContainer = Instance.new("Frame")
		listContainer.Position = UDim2.new(0,3,0,TPAD)
		listContainer.Size = UDim2.new(1,-6,1,-TPAD*2)
		listContainer.BackgroundTransparency = 1; listContainer.Parent = content

		local sc = UIKit.makeScrollableList(listContainer, {
			plugin=plugin, uis=userInputService, sbW=SB_W, sbGap=SB_GAP, OGap=2,
		})
		sc.layout.Padding = UDim.new(0, ITEM_GAP)

		-- Buttons
		for i, item in ipairs(TOOL_ITEMS) do
			local btn = Instance.new("TextButton")
			btn.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,0,ITEM_H)
			btn.BackgroundColor3 = C.base; btn.BackgroundTransparency = 0
			btn.Text = ""; btn.AutoButtonColor = false; btn.BorderSizePixel = 0
			btn.LayoutOrder = i; btn.ZIndex = 4; btn.Parent = sc.list
			UIKit.corner(btn, 6)

			-- Accent bar (left edge when active)
			local bar = Instance.new("Frame")
			bar.Size = UDim2.new(0,2,0.6,0)
			bar.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0,0.5)
			bar.Position = UDim2.new(0,3,0.5,0)
			bar.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
			bar.BackgroundTransparency = 1
			bar.BorderSizePixel = 0; bar.ZIndex = 6
			bar.Parent = btn
			UIKit.corner(bar, 99)

			-- Label
			local lbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
			lbl.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,1,0)
			lbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1
			lbl.Text = item.label
			lbl.TextColor3 = C.textDim
			lbl.Font = TXT.btn.font; lbl.TextSize = TXT.btn.size
			lbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Center
			lbl.ZIndex = 5; lbl.Parent = btn

			-- Init bar from current state
			local lastOn = AppRef.windows[item.id] and AppRef.windows[item.id].isOpen() or false
			bar.BackgroundTransparency = lastOn and 0 or 1

			-- Poll open-state, throttled ~10fps (user-driven; one loop per item so keep it cheap)
			local _barAcc = 0
			local barConn = ctx.runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
				_barAcc += dt
				if _barAcc < 0.1 then return end
				_barAcc = 0
				local on = AppRef.windows[item.id] and AppRef.windows[item.id].isOpen() or false
				if on ~= lastOn then
					lastOn = on
					tw(bar, {BackgroundTransparency = on and 0 or 1})
				end
			end)
			sc.list.AncestryChanged:Connect(function()
				pcall(function() barConn:Disconnect() end)
			end)

			btn.MouseEnter:Connect(function()
				tw(lbl, {TextColor3 = C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(1,1,1), Theme.HOVER_STRENGTH)})
			end)
			btn.MouseLeave:Connect(function()
				tw(lbl, {TextColor3 = C.textDim})
			end)
			btn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() AppRef:toggle(item.id) end)

		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(btn, item.tip) end		end

		-- Lock maxH
		win.setMaxH(TOOLS_MAX_H)
	end

	return { mount=mount, unmount=cleanup, isOpen=isOpen }
end

-- PropertiesWindow v2: structured pill-style property editor
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory)
	local C      = Theme.C
	local TXT    = Theme.TXT or {
		title = {font=Enum.Font.GothamBold,  size=13},
		body  = {font=Enum.Font.GothamMedium,size=12},
		btn   = {font=Enum.Font.GothamBold,  size=12},
		small = {font=Enum.Font.GothamMedium,size=11},
		code  = {font=Enum.Font.RobotoMono,  size=12},
	}
	local tw     = UIKit.tw
	local twNow  = UIKit.twNow
	local plugin = ctx.plugin
	local sel    = ctx.selection
	local CHS    = ctx.CHS
	local coreGui = ctx.coreGui
	local uis    = ctx.userInputService

	local TOPBAR_H = 25
	local ROW_H    = 28
	local HDR_H    = 22
	local WIN_W    = 270
	local MIN_H    = TOPBAR_H + 8 + 24 + 6  -- room for the search bar
	local SPLIT    = 0.34
	local NAME_CAP = 0.37  -- max NAME-column width as a fraction of the row (long names truncate sooner; scales with window). SPLIT is only the initial split.
	local GAP      = 4
	local AXIS_BTN, AXIS_BTN_GAP = 20, 4  -- shared X/Y/Z axis-button sizing (Size row + makePropRow reserve)
	local PAD      = 6
	local SB_W     = 6   -- matches SettingsWindow's list bar (sbW=6)
	local SB_GAP   = 6   -- gap between grid and scrollbar
	local SEARCH_H = 24

	local PILL  = C.header
	local INPUT = Theme.propFieldColor or C.surface
	local HDR_BG = C.header
	-- Tree indentation: group headers indent under the class; rows indent under the group.
	local CLS_INDENT = 0    -- (grid redesign) no class indent; groups are full-width
	local GRP_INDENT = 0    -- (grid redesign) no group indent; rows are full-width
	local CLS_LINE_T = 0.7  -- class line transparency (more solid, outermost)
	local GRP_LINE_T = 0.92  -- group line transparency (lighter, alongside props)
	local TREE_LINE_INSET_TOP = 7  -- inset between line top and the header above it (bigger gap)
	local TREE_LINE_INSET_BOT = 4  -- inset between line bottom and the last row

	-- Helper: select-all in a TextBox on focus, overriding click cursor placement
	local runService = game:GetService("RunService")
	local uis2 = game:GetService("UserInputService")
	local function trimBoxSelection(box)
		local s,e = box.SelectionStart, box.CursorPosition
		if s==-1 or e==-1 or s==e then return end
		local lo,hi = math.min(s,e), math.max(s,e)-1
		local txt = box.Text
		while lo<=hi and txt:sub(lo,lo):match("[^%w%-]") do lo=lo+1 end
		while hi>=lo and txt:sub(hi,hi):match("[^%w%-]") do hi=hi-1 end
		if lo<=hi then box.SelectionStart=lo; box.CursorPosition=hi+1 end
	end
	local function selectAllBox(box)
		box:CaptureFocus()
		-- On focus: wait for MB1 release then select all
		local conn; conn = runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
			if not uis2:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
				conn:Disconnect(); conn = nil
				if box:IsFocused() then
					box.SelectionStart=1; box.CursorPosition=#box.Text+1
				end
			end
		end)
		-- While focused: each click waits for release then trims selection (handles double-click word-select)
		box.InputBegan:Connect(function(inp)
			if inp.UserInputType==Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 and box:IsFocused() then
				local c2; c2=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
					if not uis2:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
						c2:Disconnect(); c2=nil
						trimBoxSelection(box)
					end
				end)
			end
		end)
	end
	local SCHEMA = {
		{ cls="ParticleEmitter", groups={
			{ name="Appearance", props={
				{p="Enabled",t="bool"},{p="Texture",t="asset"},{p="Size",t="numseq",range={min=0}},{p="Transparency",t="numseq",range={min=0,max=1}},
				{p="Squash",t="numseq"},{p="Color",t="colseq"},
				{p="LightEmission",t="num"},{p="LightInfluence",t="num"},{p="Brightness",t="num"},
				{p="Orientation",t="enum",opts={"FacingCamera","FacingCameraWorldUp","VelocityParallel","VelocityPerpendicular"}},
				{p="ZOffset",t="num"},
			}},
			{ name="Emission", props={
				{p="EmissionDirection",t="enum",opts={"Top","Bottom","Left","Right","Front","Back"}},
				{p="Rate",t="num"},{p="Speed",t="range"},{p="SpreadAngle",t="range"},
				{p="Lifetime",t="range"},{p="Rotation",t="range"},{p="RotSpeed",t="range"},
			}},
			{ name="Particles", props={
				{p="Drag",t="num"},{p="WindAffectsDrag",t="bool"},
				{p="VelocityInheritance",t="num"},{p="TimeScale",t="num"},
				{p="LockedToPart",t="bool"},
			}},
			{ name="Motion", props={
				{p="Acceleration",t="vec3"},
			}},
			{ name="EmitterShape", props={
				{p="Shape",t="enum",opts={"Box","Sphere","Cylinder","Disc"}},
				{p="ShapeStyle",t="enum",opts={"Volume","Surface"}},
				{p="ShapeInOut",t="enum",opts={"Outward","Inward","InAndOut"}},
				{p="ShapePartial",t="num"},
			}},
			{ name="Flipbook", props={
				{p="FlipbookLayout",t="enum",opts={"None","Grid2x2","Grid4x4","Grid8x8","Custom"}},
				{p="FlipbookSizeX",t="num"},{p="FlipbookSizeY",t="num"},
				{p="FlipbookFramerate",t="range"},{p="FlipbookMode",t="enum",opts={"Loop","OneShot","PingPong","Random"}},
				{p="FlipbookStartRandom",t="bool"},
			}},
		}},
		{ cls="Beam", groups={
			{ name="Appearance", props={
				{p="Enabled",t="bool"},{p="Texture",t="asset"},{p="Transparency",t="numseq",range={min=0,max=1}},{p="Color",t="colseq"},
				{p="TextureLength",t="num"},{p="TextureSpeed",t="num"},
				{p="TextureMode",t="enum",opts={"Stretch","Wrap","Static"}},
				{p="LightEmission",t="num"},{p="LightInfluence",t="num"},{p="Brightness",t="num"},
			}},
			{ name="Shape", props={
				{p="Width0",t="num"},{p="Width1",t="num"},
				{p="CurveSize0",t="num"},{p="CurveSize1",t="num"},
				{p="ZOffset",t="num"},{p="Segments",t="num"},{p="FaceCamera",t="bool"},
			}},
		}},
		{ cls="Trail", groups={
			{ name="Appearance", props={
				{p="Texture",t="asset"},{p="Transparency",t="numseq",range={min=0,max=1}},
				{p="Color",t="colseq"},{p="LightEmission",t="num"},{p="LightInfluence",t="num"},{p="Brightness",t="num"},
				{p="TextureMode",t="enum",opts={"Stretch","Wrap","Static"}},{p="TextureLength",t="num"},{p="FaceCamera",t="bool"},
			}},
			{ name="Emission", props={
				{p="Enabled",t="bool"},{p="Lifetime",t="num"},{p="MinLength",t="num"},{p="MaxLength",t="num"},
				{p="WidthScale",t="numseq",range={min=0,max=1}},
			}},
		}},
		{ cls="Light", groups={
			{ name="Appearance", props={
				{p="Enabled",t="bool"},{p="Brightness",t="num"},{p="Range",t="num"},
				{p="Color",t="color3"},{p="Shadows",t="bool"},
				{p="Angle",t="num"},
			}},
		}},
		{ cls="Sound", groups={
			{ name="Behavior", props={
				{p="SoundId",t="asset"},{p="Volume",t="num"},{p="PlaybackSpeed",t="num"},
				{p="RollOffMinDistance",t="num"},{p="RollOffMaxDistance",t="num"},{p="Looped",t="bool"},
				{p="RollOffMode",t="enum",opts={"Inverse","InverseTapered","Linear","LinearSquare"}},
			}},
		}},
	}
	-- ===== SineVFX 3D kinds (attribute-backed; blueprints = Utilities.EmitterSpec / TrailSpec) =====
	-- Rows here read/write ATTRIBUTES, not real props: the settings live on the Configurations under
	-- the instance's Properties folder. getGroupedVal/applyVal detect a tagged target and route
	-- through GetAttribute/SetAttribute on the holder.
	--
	-- There are TWO kinds (3D Particle = a tagged BasePart, Transformed Trail = a tagged Trail) and their
	-- attribute names DELIBERATELY collide -- both specs define Enabled, Color, Transparency,
	-- Lifetime, ZOffset. So every lookup here is keyed by KIND FIRST. One flat attrName->holder
	-- table would make a trail resolve the emitter's Configuration and vice versa: the row would
	-- read and write a Configuration the runtime never looks at, which surfaces only as a type
	-- error deep in the runtime, nowhere near here.
	local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
	local EmitterSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).EmitterSpec
	local TrailSpec   = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).TrailSpec
	local BeamSpec    = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).BeamSpec
	local Emitter3D = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).Emitter3D
	local Trail3D   = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).Trail3D
	local Beam3D    = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).Beam3D
	local EMITTER_TAG = EmitterSpec.TAG
	local TRAIL_TAG   = TrailSpec.TAG
	local BEAM_TAG    = BeamSpec.TAG
	local EMITTER_CLS = "3D Particle"
	local TRAIL_CLS   = "Transformed Trail"
	local BEAM_CLS    = "Transformed Beam"
	local KIND_TAGS   = { EMITTER_TAG, TRAIL_TAG, BEAM_TAG }
	local DEF_BY_KIND = { [EMITTER_TAG] = {}, [TRAIL_TAG] = {}, [BEAM_TAG] = {} }  -- tag -> attrName -> { attr, holder }
	-- Which kind `inst` is, or nil. An instance can only ever be one (a BasePart is not a Trail).
	local function kindOf(inst)
		if typeof(inst) ~= "Instance" then return nil end
		for _, tag in ipairs(KIND_TAGS) do
			if CollectionService:HasTag(inst, tag) then return tag end
		end
		return nil
	end
	-- True if `inst` is any 3D kind -- the tag test the plain-class walkers use to skip instances
	-- that must only ever appear under their own 3D class (a transformed Trail shows its wrapped
	-- rows, never the raw native Trail rows).
	local function isAnyKind(inst) return kindOf(inst) ~= nil end
	do
		local PASS = {bool=true,vec3=true,color3=true,colseq=true,numseq=true,num=true,enum=true,range=true,object=true,asset=true}
		local function tmap(t) return PASS[t] and t or "num" end
		local function rangeOf(d) if d.min or d.max then return {min=d.min or 0, max=d.max} end return nil end
		-- One builder per spec: both kinds render identically from their own blueprint, they just
		-- land in different SCHEMA classes and different DEF_BY_KIND tables.
		local function buildClass(spec, cls)
			local DEF = DEF_BY_KIND[spec.TAG]
			local groups = {}
			for _,g in ipairs(spec.groups) do
				local gname = g.name
				local function cfgHolder(m) local pf=m:FindFirstChild(spec.PROPS_FOLDER); return pf and pf:FindFirstChild(gname) or nil end
				local gprops = {}
				local axisRows = {}  -- axis-group label -> combined axisseq row (X/Y[/Z] toggle buttons)
				for _,d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
					DEF[d.a] = {attr=d.a, holder=cfgHolder}
					if d.axis then
						local row = axisRows[d.axis]
						if not row then
							-- An axis row is ONE row standing in for its X/Y/Z attrs, so it inherits the
							-- showIf of whichever axis attr built it -- they share a gate by construction.
							row = {p=d.axis, label=d.axis, t="axisseq", axes={}, holder=cfgHolder, range=rangeOf(d), showIf=d.showIf, desc=d.desc}
							axisRows[d.axis] = row
							gprops[#gprops+1] = row
						end
						row.axes[#row.axes+1] = d.a
					else
						gprops[#gprops+1] = {p=d.a, label=d.label or d.a, t=tmap(d.t), attr=d.a, holder=cfgHolder, range=rangeOf(d), opts=d.opts, intgr=d.intgr, strenum=(typeof(d.d)=="string"), showIf=d.showIf, desc=d.desc}
					end
				end
				groups[#groups+1] = {name=gname, props=gprops}
			end
			table.insert(SCHEMA, {cls=cls, groups=groups, emitter=true})
		end
		buildClass(EmitterSpec, EMITTER_CLS)
		buildClass(TrailSpec,   TRAIL_CLS)
		buildClass(BeamSpec,    BEAM_CLS)
	end
	-- Declarative row gating (spec `showIf`). The gated-on attr may live in a DIFFERENT group,
	-- so resolve its holder through the target's own kind table rather than assuming the row's
	-- own Configuration.
	-- Mixed selections follow the existing convention: shown if ANY selected target passes.
	local function _showIfHolder(inst, cond)
		local k = kindOf(inst)
		local d = k and DEF_BY_KIND[k][cond.a]
		return (d and d.holder and d.holder(inst)) or inst
	end
	-- A showIf is either ONE condition or an ARRAY of them (ALL must pass) -- "Lightning is on AND
	-- Route is Custom" needs the second form. `_showIfConds` normalises both to a list.
	local function _showIfConds(showIf)
		return showIf[1] and showIf or {showIf}
	end
	local function _showIfOne(inst, cond)
		local v = _showIfHolder(inst, cond):GetAttribute(cond.a)
		if cond.oneOf then
			for _, x in ipairs(cond.oneOf) do if v == x then return true end end
			return false
		end
		if cond.isnot ~= nil then return v ~= cond.isnot end
		return v == cond.eq
	end
	local function _showIfOK(inst, showIf)
		for _, cond in ipairs(_showIfConds(showIf)) do
			if not _showIfOne(inst, cond) then return false end
		end
		return true
	end

	local function _emitterHolder(t, prop)
		local k = kindOf(t)
		if not k then return nil end
		local d = DEF_BY_KIND[k][prop]
		if d then return (d.holder and d.holder(t)) or t, d.attr end
		return nil
	end

	local propGui=nil; local isPinned=false; local selConn=nil; local propConns={}; local structConns={}
	local rowBuilders={}

	local function clearPropConns()
		for _,c in pairs(propConns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
		propConns={}
	end

	local function cleanup()
		isPinned=false; clearPropConns()
		for _,c in ipairs(structConns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
		structConns={}
		if selConn then selConn:Disconnect() end
		if propGui then propGui:Destroy() end
		propGui=nil; selConn=nil
	end

	local function isOpen() return propGui~=nil and propGui.Parent~=nil end

	local function getTargets(cls)
		if cls==EMITTER_CLS then return (ctx.getEmitters and ctx.getEmitters()) or {} end
		if cls==TRAIL_CLS   then return (ctx.getTrails3D and ctx.getTrails3D()) or {} end
		if cls==BEAM_CLS    then return (ctx.getBeams3D and ctx.getBeams3D()) or {} end
		local seen,list={},{}
		for _,obj in pairs(sel:Get()) do
			if obj==game or obj==workspace or obj==game:GetService("Players") then continue end
			if not obj:IsDescendantOf(game) then continue end
			local function chk(inst)
				if seen[inst] then return end; seen[inst]=true
				if isAnyKind(inst) then return end  -- 3D kinds show only under their own class
				if inst:IsA(cls) then table.insert(list,inst) end
			end
			chk(obj)
		end
		return list
	end
	-- ===== cross-class property merge =====
	-- Two selected instances of different classes share a property only when its GROUP + NAME +
	-- TYPE all match (type-matched so a value can never be written to an incompatible property).
	local PROP_CLASSES = {}  -- mergeKey ("group|name|type") -> array of class names that define it
	for _,cg in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
		for _,g in ipairs(cg.groups) do
			for _,d in ipairs(g.props) do
				local mk = g.name.."|"..tostring(d.p).."|"..tostring(d.t)
				PROP_CLASSES[mk] = PROP_CLASSES[mk] or {}
				table.insert(PROP_CLASSES[mk], cg.cls)
			end
		end
	end
	-- Global display order so same-named GROUPS (and same group+name+type PROPS) from different
	-- classes line up into one merged section instead of appearing per-class block.
	local GROUP_ORDER = {}   -- group name -> global order
	local PROP_SLOT = {}     -- mergeKey  -> slot within its group (unique props each get their own)
	do
		local gn=0; local slotOf={}
		for _,cg in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
			for _,g in ipairs(cg.groups) do
				if not GROUP_ORDER[g.name] then gn=gn+1; GROUP_ORDER[g.name]=gn; slotOf[g.name]=0 end
				for _,d in ipairs(g.props) do
					local mk = g.name.."|"..tostring(d.p).."|"..tostring(d.t)
					if not PROP_SLOT[mk] then slotOf[g.name]=slotOf[g.name]+1; PROP_SLOT[mk]=slotOf[g.name] end
				end
			end
		end
	end
	-- every selected instance whose class defines this exact property (union across classes)
	local function getPropTargets(groupName, p, t)
		local mk = groupName.."|"..tostring(p).."|"..tostring(t)
		local classes = PROP_CLASSES[mk]
		if not classes then return {} end
		if #classes==1 then return getTargets(classes[1]) end
		local seen,list = {},{}
		for _,c in ipairs(classes) do
			for _,inst in ipairs(getTargets(c)) do if not seen[inst] then seen[inst]=true; list[#list+1]=inst end end
		end
		return list
	end

	-- Same result as calling getTargets(cls) once per SCHEMA class, but walks the
	-- selection + its descendants ONE time instead of once per class -- refresh()
	-- was doing a full descendant walk of the same selection per class on every
	-- SelectionChanged, which compounds badly with big selections.
	local function getAllTargets()
		local byClass={}
		for _,clsGroup in ipairs(SCHEMA) do byClass[clsGroup.cls]={} end
		local seen={}
		local function chk(inst)
			if seen[inst] then return end; seen[inst]=true
			if isAnyKind(inst) then return end  -- 3D kinds show only under their own class (never as a raw BasePart/Trail)
			for cls,list in pairs(byClass) do
				if inst:IsA(cls) then table.insert(list,inst) end
			end
		end
		for _,obj in pairs(sel:Get()) do
			if obj==game or obj==workspace or obj==game:GetService("Players") then continue end
			if not obj:IsDescendantOf(game) then continue end
			chk(obj)
		end
		local _em = (ctx.getEmitters and ctx.getEmitters()) or {}
		-- Emitters authored before a spec group existed are missing its Configuration, and the holder
		-- lookup would silently fall back to the PART -- rows would then read/write an attribute the
		-- runtime never looks at. Backfill on selection so old emitters gain new groups (e.g. Lightning).
		for _, e in ipairs(_em) do Emitter3D.ensure(e) end
		byClass[EMITTER_CLS]=_em
		-- Same backfill contract for trails (Trail3D.ensure), and for the same reason: a trail
		-- authored before a spec attr existed is missing its Configuration entry, and the holder
		-- lookup would silently fall back to the Trail itself.
		local _tr = (ctx.getTrails3D and ctx.getTrails3D()) or {}
		for _, t in ipairs(_tr) do Trail3D.ensure(t) end
		byClass[TRAIL_CLS]=_tr
		-- Same backfill contract for beams (Beam3D.ensure): a beam authored before a spec attr
		-- existed is missing its Configuration entry, and the holder lookup would silently fall
		-- back to the Beam itself.
		local _bm = (ctx.getBeams3D and ctx.getBeams3D()) or {}
		for _, b in ipairs(_bm) do Beam3D.ensure(b) end
		byClass[BEAM_CLS]=_bm
		return byClass
	end

	local function fmt(n)
		if type(n)~="number" then return tostring(n) end
		return tostring(math.floor(n*1000+0.5)/1000)
	end

	local function getGroupedVal(targets,prop)
		local val,mixed,init=nil,false,false
		for _,t in pairs(targets) do
			local eh,eattr=_emitterHolder(t,prop)
			local ok,v
			if eh then ok,v=pcall(function() return eh:GetAttribute(eattr) end)
			else ok,v=pcall(function() return t[prop] end) end
			if not ok then continue end
			if not init then val=v;init=true
			elseif tostring(val)~=tostring(v) then mixed=true;break end
		end
		return val,mixed
	end

	local _wpToken=0
	-- >0 while the graph editor owns the edits. The graph has its OWN undo/redo, so its live
	-- writes must not each become a Studio undo step -- the whole session collapses into a single
	-- waypoint set when the editor closes (see graphQuiet). Deliberately NOT CHS:SetEnabled(false):
	-- that is global, so it would kill undo for every other edit made meanwhile, and leaves undo
	-- broken for good if it ever sticks off (see the note in TrailPreviewWindow).
	local _wpQuiet=0
	local sliderDragging=false  -- while true, skip textbox relayout so it doesn't shake mid-drag
	local function applyVal(targets,prop,val)
		for _,t in pairs(targets) do
			local eh,eattr=_emitterHolder(t,prop)
			if eh then pcall(function() eh:SetAttribute(eattr,val) end)
			else pcall(function() t[prop]=val end) end
		end
		if _wpQuiet>0 then return end
		-- coalesce rapid live edits (slider/wheel/handle drags) into ONE undo waypoint
		-- instead of one per frame, so Ctrl+Z steps through whole edits, not frames.
		_wpToken+=1; local my=_wpToken
		task.delay(0.2, function() if my==_wpToken then CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Props") end end)
	end
	-- Wrap a graph editor's live-apply callback so its writes raise no waypoints, and hand back
	-- an onCommit that closes the session with exactly one. Cancel does NOT use this: revertEach
	-- sets its own Before/After pair around the restore.
	local function graphQuiet(fn)
		local quiet=function(...)
			_wpQuiet+=1
			local ok,err=pcall(fn, ...)
			_wpQuiet=math.max(0, _wpQuiet-1)   -- never strand the counter on an error
			if not ok then warn("[SineVFX] graph apply: "..tostring(err)) end
		end
		local onCommit=function() pcall(function() CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Graph") end) end
		return quiet, onCommit
	end
	-- Same attribute-aware indirection as getGroupedVal/applyVal, for the few spots (modal
	-- editors: curve/colseq/colorwheel open + their Cancel-revert) that read/write a SINGLE
	-- target's raw property directly instead of going through the grouped helpers above.
	-- No waypoint side-effects here -- callers (revertEach etc.) manage their own CHS waypoints.
	local function rawGet(tg, prop)
		local eh,eattr=_emitterHolder(tg,prop)
		if eh then local ok,v=pcall(function() return eh:GetAttribute(eattr) end); return ok and v or nil end
		local ok,v=pcall(function() return tg[prop] end); return ok and v or nil
	end
	local function rawSet(tg, prop, val)
		local eh,eattr=_emitterHolder(tg,prop)
		if eh then pcall(function() eh:SetAttribute(eattr,val) end); return end
		pcall(function() tg[prop]=val end)
	end

	-- ===== Inline value sliders (numeric props) =====
	-- Per-property slider domains. The textbox stays authoritative and may hold values
	-- beyond these; the slider thumb just clamps to the ends for out-of-range values.
	local SLR={
		LightEmission={0,1},LightInfluence={0,1},ShapePartial={0,1},
		Transparency={0,1},Reflectance={0,1},ShadowSoftness={0,1},
		Brightness={0,10},Rate={0,100},ZOffset={-10,10},Drag={0,10},
		Speed={0,50},Lifetime={0,5},Rotation={-360,360},RotSpeed={-360,360},
		FlipbookFramerate={0,60},SpreadAngle={0,180},
		FlipbookSizeX={1,16},FlipbookSizeY={1,16},VelocityInheritance={0,1},TimeScale={0,10},
		TextureLength={0,10},TextureSpeed={-10,10},
		Width0={0,10},Width1={0,10},CurveSize0={-10,10},CurveSize1={-10,10},
		Segments={1,100},MinLength={0,10},Range={0,60},Angle={0,180},
		Volume={0,3},PlaybackSpeed={0,4},RollOffMaxDistance={0,1000},
		Bounce={0,1},Friction={0,1},
	}
	local function sliderDomain(p) local r=SLR[p]; if r then return r[1],r[2] end return 0,10 end
	-- Build an inline slider into `area`. dual=true -> two thumbs (range lo/hi with a
	-- connecting fill). onChange(v1[,v2]) fires while dragging. Returns position(v1[,v2]).
	local function buildSlider(area,dual,slrMin,slrMax,onChange,onEnd)
		local dom={min=slrMin,max=slrMax}
		local track=Instance.new("Frame")
		track.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0.5); track.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,0.5,0)
		track.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,6); track.BackgroundColor3=C.base; track:SetAttribute("SVFXnoFade",true)
		track.BorderSizePixel=0; track.ZIndex=4; track.Parent=area
		UIKit.corner(track,3)
		local fill=Instance.new("Frame")
		fill.BackgroundColor3=C.accent; fill.BorderSizePixel=0; fill.ZIndex=5; fill.Parent=track
		UIKit.corner(fill,3)
		local function mkThumb()
			local th=Instance.new("Frame")
			th.Size=UDim2.new(0,8,0,13); th.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
			th.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,0.5,0); th.BackgroundColor3=C.accent
			th.BorderSizePixel=0; th.ZIndex=7; th.Parent=track
			UIKit.corner(th,4)
			
			local hb=Instance.new("TextButton")
			hb.BackgroundTransparency=1; hb.Text=""; hb.Size=UDim2.new(0,22,1,10)
			hb.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5); hb.Position=UDim2.new(0.5,0,0.5,0)
			hb.ZIndex=8; hb.Parent=th
			return th,hb
		end
		local th1,hb1=mkThumb()
		local th2,hb2; if dual then th2,hb2=mkThumb() end
		local cur1,cur2=slrMin,(dual and slrMax or slrMin)
		local dragConn
		local function recompute()
			local lo=math.min(cur1,(dual and cur2 or cur1)); local hi=math.max(cur1,(dual and cur2 or cur1))
			dom.min=math.min(slrMin,lo); dom.max=math.max(slrMax,hi)
		end
		local function span() local sp=dom.max-dom.min; return (sp>0) and sp or 1 end
		local function frac(v) return math.clamp((v-dom.min)/span(),0,1) end
		-- thumbs are 8px wide; inset their centre by half-width at each end so they never
		-- hang off the track edges ("a little clamp" left/right).
		local THW=8
		local function thumbPos(f) return UDim2.new(f,(0.5-f)*THW,0.5,0) end
		local function position(v1,v2)
			if v1~=nil then cur1=v1 end; if dual and v2~=nil then cur2=v2 end
			-- keep the domain frozen while dragging (no jitter); recompute expands the range
			-- to fit out-of-range values, or collapses back to base, only once settled.
			if dragConn==nil then recompute() end
			-- snap instantly while actively dragging; tween otherwise (same tw helper as
			-- the window resize) so value/external updates glide into place.
			local anim=(dragConn==nil)
			local f1=frac(cur1); local p1=thumbPos(f1)
			if anim then tw(th1,{Position=p1}) else th1.Position=p1 end
			if dual then
				local f2=frac(cur2); local p2=thumbPos(f2)
				if anim then tw(th2,{Position=p2}) else th2.Position=p2 end
				local lo=math.min(f1,f2); local hi=math.max(f1,f2)
				local fp,fs=UDim2.new(lo,0,0,0),UDim2.new(hi-lo,0,1,0)
				if anim then tw(fill,{Position=fp,Size=fs}) else fill.Position=fp; fill.Size=fs end
			else
				local fp,fs=UDim2.new(0,0,0,0),UDim2.new(f1,0,1,0)
				if anim then tw(fill,{Position=fp,Size=fs}) else fill.Position=fp; fill.Size=fs end
			end
		end
		local function valAt(px)
			local ap=track.AbsolutePosition.X; local aw=track.AbsoluteSize.X
			return dom.min+math.clamp((px-ap)/math.max(1,aw),0,1)*span()
		end
		local function startDrag(which)
			if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect() end
			sliderDragging=true
			-- relative drag: remember the grab point & values so the thumb moves WITH the
			-- mouse by the same delta instead of teleporting under the cursor.
			local startMX=uis:GetMouseLocation().X
			local startV1,startV2=cur1,cur2; local startAW=track.AbsoluteSize.X
			dragConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
				if not uis:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
					if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect(); dragConn=nil end
					sliderDragging=false
					-- settle: collapse domain to base if value is back in range (or keep it
					-- expanded if still out of range), tweening the thumb to its real spot.
					position(cur1, dual and cur2 or nil)
					if onEnd then onEnd() end
					return
				end
				local aw=track.AbsoluteSize.X
				local dv=(uis:GetMouseLocation().X-startMX)/math.max(1,startAW)*span()
				if dual then
					if which==2 then cur2=math.clamp(startV2+dv, cur1, dom.max)
					else cur1=math.clamp(startV1+dv, dom.min, cur2) end
					position(cur1,cur2); onChange(cur1,cur2)
				else
					cur1=math.clamp(startV1+dv, dom.min, dom.max)
					position(cur1); onChange(cur1)
				end
			end)
		end
		hb1.MouseButton1Down:Connect(function() startDrag(1) end)
		if dual then hb2.MouseButton1Down:Connect(function() startDrag(2) end) end
		local trackHit=Instance.new("TextButton")
		trackHit.BackgroundTransparency=1; trackHit.Text=""; trackHit.AutoButtonColor=false
		trackHit.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0.5); trackHit.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,0.5,0)
		trackHit.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,1,12); trackHit.ZIndex=6; trackHit.Parent=track
		trackHit.MouseButton1Down:Connect(function()
			-- no teleport: pick the nearest thumb (dual) and start a relative drag
			if dual then
				local v=valAt(uis:GetMouseLocation().X)
				if math.abs(v-cur1)<=math.abs(v-cur2) then startDrag(1) else startDrag(2) end
			else
				startDrag(1)
			end
		end)
		return position
	end

	local function shortenAsset(full)
		if not full or full=="" then return "" end
		if #full>18 then return "..."..full:sub(-14) else return full end
	end

	local function mount(App, PROPS_ID)
		cleanup()
		PILL = C.header; INPUT = Theme.propFieldColor or C.surface; HDR_BG = C.header
		isPinned=plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Props")==true

		propGui=Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		propGui.Name="SineVFX_Props"; propGui.ResetOnSpawn=false
		propGui.ZIndexBehavior=Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		propGui.DisplayOrder=10007; propGui.Parent=coreGui

		local win=WinFactory.new({
			stateKey="PropsWindow", noSaveH=true,
			gui=propGui, name="PropsWrapper",
			w=WIN_W, h=MIN_H,
			pos=UDim2.new(0.5,0,0.3,0),
			minW=20, maxW=600, minH=MIN_H, maxH=2000,
			topbarH=TOPBAR_H,
			getPinned=function() return isPinned end,
		})
		local main=win.main; local bgFrame=win.bgFrame; local topbar=win.topbar
		local searchQuery=""
		local refresh  -- forward decl; filters rows by the search box

		-- Topbar
		local titleLbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
		titleLbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-60,1,0); titleLbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,12,0,0)
		titleLbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; titleLbl.Text="Properties"
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(titleLbl, "Edit all VFX properties.") end
		titleLbl.TextColor3=C.text; titleLbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold
		titleLbl.TextSize=13; titleLbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		titleLbl.ZIndex=6; titleLbl.Parent=topbar

		local pinBtn,pinIcon=UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"120413064155653",-48,15)
		local closeBtn,_=UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"101421401454601",-26,10)
		if isPinned then pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",true); pinIcon.ImageColor3=C.Pinned end
		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned=not isPinned; plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Props",isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",isPinned)
			tw(pinIcon,{ImageColor3=isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim})
		end)
		closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() App:toggle(PROPS_ID) end)
		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col=focused and C.text or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			local ic=focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			local pc=focused and C.Pinned or C.Pinned
			task.defer(function()
				for _,ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch,{TextColor3=col}) end
					if ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then
						tw(ch,{ImageColor3=ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") and pc or ic})
					end
				end
			end)
		end)

		-- ── Search bar ──
		local searchBar=Instance.new("Frame")
		searchBar.Position=UDim2.new(0,PAD,0,TOPBAR_H+PAD)
		searchBar.Size=UDim2.new(1,-PAD*2,0,SEARCH_H)
		searchBar.BackgroundColor3=C.base; searchBar.BorderSizePixel=0; searchBar.Parent=bgFrame
		UIKit.corner(searchBar,7)
		local searchBox=Instance.new("TextBox")
		searchBox.Size=UDim2.new(1,-34,1,0); searchBox.Position=UDim2.new(0,10,0,0)
		searchBox.BackgroundTransparency=1; searchBox.Text=""
		searchBox.PlaceholderText="Search..."; searchBox.PlaceholderColor3=C.textDim
		searchBox.ClearTextOnFocus=false; searchBox.TextColor3=C.white
		searchBox.Font=TXT.body.font; searchBox.TextSize=TXT.body.size
		searchBox.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; searchBox.ZIndex=3; searchBox.Parent=searchBar
		local clearBtn=Instance.new("TextButton")
		clearBtn.Size=UDim2.new(0,18,0,18); clearBtn.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,0.5)
		clearBtn.Position=UDim2.new(1,-5,0.5,0); clearBtn.BackgroundTransparency=1
		clearBtn.Text=""; clearBtn.AutoButtonColor=false; clearBtn.Visible=false
		clearBtn.ZIndex=4; clearBtn.Parent=searchBar
		local clearIcon=Instance.new("ImageLabel")
		clearIcon.Size=UDim2.new(0,9,0,9); clearIcon.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
		clearIcon.Position=UDim2.new(0.5,0,0.5,0); clearIcon.BackgroundTransparency=1
		clearIcon.ImageColor3=C.textDim; clearIcon.ZIndex=5; clearIcon.Parent=clearBtn
		UIKit.setIconImage(clearIcon,"101421401454601")
		UIKit.hoverLift(clearIcon, clearBtn)
		clearBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() searchBox.Text="" end)
		searchBox:GetPropertyChangedSignal("Text"):Connect(function()
			searchQuery=(searchBox.Text or ""):gsub("^%s+",""):gsub("%s+$",""):lower()
			clearBtn.Visible=#searchQuery>0
			if refresh then refresh() end
		end)

		-- listContainer with PAD from edges
		local listContainer=Instance.new("Frame")
		listContainer.Position=UDim2.new(0,PAD+1,0,TOPBAR_H+PAD+SEARCH_H+PAD)
		listContainer.Size=UDim2.new(1,-(PAD*2+2),1,-(TOPBAR_H+PAD*2+SEARCH_H+PAD+2))
		listContainer.BackgroundTransparency=1; listContainer.Parent=bgFrame

		local propListWrapper=Instance.new("CanvasGroup")
		propListWrapper.Size=UDim2.new(1,-(SB_W+SB_GAP+2),1,0)  -- reserve the scrollbar gutter (Shifter-style)
		propListWrapper.BorderSizePixel=0; propListWrapper.BackgroundColor3=(C.content or C.base); propListWrapper:SetAttribute("SVFXcontentbg", true); propListWrapper.Parent=listContainer; propListWrapper.ClipsDescendants=true; UIKit.makeBorder(propListWrapper, C.header):SetAttribute("SVFXheaderborder",true)
		UIKit.corner(propListWrapper,7)

		local scroll=Instance.new("ScrollingFrame")
		scroll.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,1,0); scroll.BackgroundTransparency=1; scroll.BorderSizePixel=0
		scroll.ScrollBarThickness=0; scroll.ScrollBarImageTransparency=1
		pcall(function() scroll.TopImage=""; scroll.MidImage=""; scroll.BottomImage="" end)
		scroll.CanvasSize=UDim2.new(0,0,0,0); scroll.ClipsDescendants=true
		scroll.Parent=propListWrapper
		-- (grid redesign) round the scroll's clip to match the wrapper so the grid's
		-- very top and very bottom rows get smoothly rounded corners.
		UIKit.corner(scroll,7)

		-- Scrollbar
		local sbTrack=Instance.new("Frame")
		sbTrack.Size=UDim2.new(0,SB_W,1,0); sbTrack.Position=UDim2.new(1,-SB_W,0,0)
		sbTrack.BackgroundColor3=C.base; sbTrack.BorderSizePixel=0; sbTrack.Parent=listContainer
		UIKit.corner(sbTrack,3)
		local sbThumb=Instance.new("Frame")
		sbThumb.BackgroundColor3=C.slider; sbThumb.BackgroundTransparency=0.4
		sbThumb.BorderSizePixel=0; sbThumb.ZIndex=26; sbThumb.Parent=sbTrack; UIKit.corner(sbThumb,3)

		local pad  -- the scroll content UIPadding (assigned below); updateSB adjusts its right pad
		local function updateSB()
			scroll.ScrollBarThickness=0; scroll.ScrollBarImageTransparency=1
			local ch=scroll.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y; local vh=scroll.AbsoluteSize.Y
			local _S=(WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1
			if scroll.CanvasSize.Y.Offset-8<=vh/_S+3 or vh<=0 then
				if sbTrack.Visible then sbTrack.Visible=false end
				local _fw=UDim2.new(1,0,1,0); if propListWrapper.Size~=_fw then propListWrapper.Size=_fw end  -- no bar -> content fills the gutter
				return
			end
			if not sbTrack.Visible then sbTrack.Visible=true end
			local _nw=UDim2.new(1,-(SB_W+SB_GAP+2),1,0); if propListWrapper.Size~=_nw then propListWrapper.Size=_nw end  -- bar shown -> reserve its gutter
			local ratio=math.clamp(vh/ch,0.1,1)
			local posR=scroll.CanvasPosition.Y/math.max(1,ch-vh)
			sbThumb.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,ratio,0); sbThumb.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,posR*(1-ratio),0)
		end
		scroll:GetPropertyChangedSignal("CanvasPosition"):Connect(updateSB)
		scroll:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(updateSB)

		-- Keep the scroll position across selection changes. Deselecting hides every row, the
		-- canvas collapses and Roblox clamps CanvasPosition to 0 with it -- so only record while
		-- the canvas is actually taller than the view, otherwise the collapse eats the saved value.
		local savedScrollY=0
		local restoringScroll=false
		scroll:GetPropertyChangedSignal("CanvasPosition"):Connect(function()
			if restoringScroll then return end
			if scroll.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y>scroll.AbsoluteSize.Y+1 then savedScrollY=scroll.CanvasPosition.Y end
		end)
		local _restoreConn
		local function restoreScroll()
			if _restoreConn then _restoreConn:Disconnect() end
			restoringScroll=true
			local rc=0
			_restoreConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
				-- the canvas height fits over the next few frames; keep re-applying until it settles
				if not scroll.Parent then _restoreConn:Disconnect(); _restoreConn=nil; restoringScroll=false; return end
				rc+=1
				local maxY=math.max(0,scroll.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y-scroll.AbsoluteSize.Y)
				if maxY>0 then scroll.CanvasPosition=Vector2.new(0,math.min(savedScrollY,maxY)) end
				if rc>=8 then _restoreConn:Disconnect(); _restoreConn=nil; restoringScroll=false end
			end)
		end

		-- Scrollbar drag
		local sbDragging=false
		sbThumb.InputBegan:Connect(function(inp)
			if inp.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then return end
			sbDragging=true; plugin:Activate(false)
			local startY=uis:GetMouseLocation().Y; local startPos=scroll.CanvasPosition.Y
			local mc=uis.InputChanged:Connect(function(i)
				if i.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.MouseMovement or not sbDragging then return end
				local dy=uis:GetMouseLocation().Y-startY
				local ch2=scroll.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y; local vh2=scroll.AbsoluteSize.Y
				scroll.CanvasPosition=Vector2.new(0,math.clamp(startPos+dy*(ch2/math.max(1,sbTrack.AbsoluteSize.Y)),0,ch2-vh2))
			end)
			local ec; ec=uis.InputEnded:Connect(function(i)
				if i.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then return end
				sbDragging=false; mc:Disconnect(); ec:Disconnect(); plugin:Deactivate()
			end)
		end)
		scroll.InputChanged:Connect(function(inp)
			if inp.UserInputType==Enum.UserInputType.MouseWheel then
				local ch2=scroll.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y; local vh2=scroll.AbsoluteSize.Y
				scroll.CanvasPosition=Vector2.new(0,math.clamp(scroll.CanvasPosition.Y-inp.Position.Z*ROW_H*1.5,0,math.max(0,ch2-vh2)))
			end
		end)

		local contentReady=false  -- gate height-fit until the first refresh hides irrelevant rows
		local savedH=plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_PropsH") or 300

		local layout=Instance.new("UIListLayout")
		-- (grid redesign) no gap between rows so they form a contiguous grid; the
		-- rounded, clipping propListWrapper gives the whole grid its top/bottom corners.
		layout.SortOrder=Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder; layout.Padding=UDim.new(0,0); layout.Parent=scroll
		pad=Instance.new("UIPadding")
		pad.PaddingTop=UDim.new(0,0); pad.PaddingBottom=UDim.new(0,0)
		pad.PaddingLeft=UDim.new(0,0); pad.PaddingRight=UDim.new(0,0)
		pad.Parent=scroll

		-- Snap the window to fit the currently-VISIBLE rows, capped at the user's saved
		-- height. Instant, not tweened: a tween mid-rebuild stuttered on instance switch,
		-- and at mount every row is briefly visible (inflated) which flashed full-height.
		local fitConn=nil
		local function fitHeight()
			if not main or not main.Parent then if fitConn then fitConn:Disconnect(); fitConn=nil end return end
			if not contentReady then return end
			if win.isResizing() then return end  -- never fight a manual drag-resize (was snapping down)
			local S=(WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1
			local totalH=layout.AbsoluteContentSize.Y/S
			if totalH<=0 then
				if fitConn then fitConn:Disconnect(); fitConn=nil end
				main.Size=UDim2.new(0,main.Size.X.Offset,0,MIN_H)
				win.currentSize.h=MIN_H; win.setMaxH(MIN_H); return  -- nothing to show -> clamp small
			end
			local contentMaxH=math.min(TOPBAR_H+PAD*2+SEARCH_H+PAD+totalH+2, 2000)
			win.setMaxH(contentMaxH)  -- clamp the resize at content height: can't drag past what's shown
			local targetH=math.min(savedH or 300, contentMaxH)
			local curH=main.AbsoluteSize.Y/S
			if fitConn then fitConn:Disconnect(); fitConn=nil end
			if math.abs(curH-targetH)>1 then
				main.Size=UDim2.new(0,main.Size.X.Offset,0,targetH)
				win.currentSize.h=targetH
			end
		end

		-- Coalesce content-size driven refit so updateSB/fitHeight (which resize main
		-- and reflow the list) can't re-enter this signal 80 levels deep.
		local _fitQueued=false
		local function queueFit()
			if _fitQueued then return end
			_fitQueued=true
			task.defer(function()
				_fitQueued=false
				if not main.Parent then return end
				local totalH=layout.AbsoluteContentSize.Y/((WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1)
				scroll.CanvasSize=UDim2.new(0,0,0,totalH)
				updateSB()
				fitHeight()
			end)
		end
		layout:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteContentSize"):Connect(queueFit)
		if WinFactory.onScaleChanged then
			local unsub = WinFactory.onScaleChanged(function() if main.Parent then queueFit() end end)
			propConns[#propConns+1] = { Disconnect = function() if unsub then pcall(unsub); unsub = nil end end }
		end

		local listSizeConn=nil
		local MIN_LIST_VIS=5
		listContainer.Visible=listContainer.AbsoluteSize.Y>=MIN_LIST_VIS
		listSizeConn=listContainer:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(function()
			listContainer.Visible=listContainer.AbsoluteSize.Y>=MIN_LIST_VIS
			updateSB()
		end)

		-- Persist the user's chosen height. ONLY a manual handle drag counts:
		-- win.isResizing() is true only mid-drag, so content-driven tweens and
		-- viewport re-clamps on instance switch never overwrite savedH. Debounced
		-- so only the settled height is written, not every heartbeat of the drag.
		local saveToken=0
		main:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(function()
			if not win.isResizing() then return end  -- only persist a manual drag-resize
			saveToken+=1
			local myToken=saveToken
			task.delay(0.2,function()
				if myToken~=saveToken then return end  -- superseded by a newer change
				local S=(WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1
				local h=main.AbsoluteSize.Y/S
				if h>MIN_H+10 then savedH=h; plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_PropsH",h) end
			end)
		end)

		-- ── Builders ──
		local order=0
		local collapsed={}
		local userCollapsed={}
		local clsCollapsedMap={}
		rowBuilders={}

		local relayoutFns={}
		-- Hover descriptions shown as tooltips on each property row (keyed by property/attr name).
	-- PROP_DESC describes a row; OPT_DESC[prop][option] describes each choice INSIDE its dropdown,
	-- so a dropdown explains itself per button instead of cramming every option into one row tooltip.
	-- Only listed props get them; anything absent (e.g. Material's ~30 self-describing names) is skipped.
	local OPT_DESC = {
		Route = {
			Default = "Particles follow their normal path from Speed and Acceleration.",

		RoutePick = {

			Nearest    = "Each particle picks whichever target is closest to where it spawned.",
			Even       = "Targets are handed out in turn, so each one gets an even share. A particle still flies to just one of them.",
			InOrder    = "Each particle travels through every target in turn, from Target1 onward, and stops at the last one.",
		},
		Orientation = {
			None                  = "Particles keep the emitter's own orientation.",
			FacingCamera          = "Particles always turn flat toward the camera.",
			FacingCameraWorldUp   = "Particles face the camera but stay upright in the world.",
			VelocityParallel      = "Particles line up along the direction they are moving.",
			VelocityPerpendicular = "Particles turn side on to the direction they are moving.",
		},
		EmissionDirection = {

			Left = "Particles fly out of the left face.", Right = "Particles fly out of the right face.",
			Front = "Particles fly out of the front face.", Back = "Particles fly out of the back face.",
		},
		Shape = {

			Cylinder = "Spawn inside a cylinder region.", Disc = "Spawn inside a flat disc region.",
		},
		ShapeStyle = {
			Volume  = "Spawn anywhere inside the shape.",
			Surface = "Spawn only on the shape's outer shell.",

	local PROP_DESC = {
		Enabled="Whether the emitter is producing particles.",
		Texture="The image used for each particle.",

		FlipbookSizeY="Number of rows in the animated texture sheet.",
		ShapeOn="Emit from a 3D region instead of a single point.",
		ShapeRadius="Size of the spawn region, in studs.",
		ShapeSize="Half-extent of the spawn region per local axis (studs), as an X/Y/Z graph over the emitter cycle (loops over Lifetime). Non-uniform axes make ellipsoids, flat discs, or stretched boxes.",

		Aim="How much each weave point aims at the target (centre) vs wanders. 0 random, 1 straight at it.",

		Target3="Target 3. Click to select its ObjectValue, then set Value in Studio's Properties. Right click to clear.",

		Target7="Target 7. Click to select its ObjectValue, then set Value in Studio's Properties. Right click to clear.",
		Target8="Target 8. Click to select its ObjectValue, then set Value in Studio's Properties. Right click to clear.",
		RoutePick="How targets are handed out. Random, Nearest, and Even send a particle to one target. InOrder sends it through all of them.",

		ScaleAttachments="Move nested attachments in/out as the particle's size animates, so beams/trails between them scale with it.",

		StepRate="Updates per second (0 = paused). Lower is cheaper but choppier.",

	local function makePropRow(propName, layoutOrder, extraLeftSpace)
			extraLeftSpace = extraLeftSpace or 0
			local indent=CLS_INDENT+GRP_INDENT
			-- Outer container: full width, picked up by UIListLayout
			local outer=Instance.new("Frame")
			outer.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,ROW_H); outer.BackgroundTransparency=1
			outer.BorderSizePixel=0; outer.LayoutOrder=layoutOrder; outer.Parent=scroll
			-- Inner row: indented inside the outer container
			local row=Instance.new("Frame")
			row.Size=UDim2.new(1,-indent,0,ROW_H); row.Position=UDim2.new(0,indent,0,0)
			-- (grid redesign) the WHOLE row is the value-field color, so the name column,
			-- value column and the X/Y/Z reserve strip all share one background.
			row.BackgroundColor3=INPUT; row.BorderSizePixel=0; row.Parent=outer; row:SetAttribute("SVFXpropfield",true)

			-- (grid redesign) name cell is flat (no field bg / corner / border); it's
			-- just a transparent left column holding the label. Grid lines are drawn below.
			local lp=Instance.new("Frame")
			lp.Size=UDim2.new(SPLIT,-GAP/2,1,0); lp.BackgroundTransparency=1
			lp.BorderSizePixel=0; lp.ZIndex=2; lp.Parent=row
			local lbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
			lbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-15,1,0); lbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,6,0,0)
			lbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; lbl.Text=propName
			lbl.TextTruncate = Enum.TextTruncate.AtEnd
			lbl.TextColor3=C.text; lbl.Font=TXT.body.font; lbl.TextSize=TXT.body.size+1
			lbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left;
			lbl.ZIndex=3; lbl.Parent=lp

			-- Reserved area (e.g. the Size row's X/Y/Z toggle buttons): a third slot between
			-- the label and value box, sized/positioned by relayoutRow in the SAME pass as
			-- lp/vp so there's only ever one source of truth for the row's label width.
			local reserved = nil
			if extraLeftSpace > 0 then
				reserved = Instance.new("Frame")
				reserved.BackgroundTransparency = 1
				reserved.ZIndex = 2
				reserved.Parent = row
			end

			local vp=Instance.new("Frame")
			vp.Position=UDim2.new(SPLIT,GAP/2,0,0)
			vp.Size=UDim2.new(1-SPLIT,-GAP/2,1,0)
			vp.BackgroundColor3=INPUT; vp:SetAttribute("SVFXpropfield",true); vp.BorderSizePixel=0; vp.ZIndex=2; vp.Parent=row
			-- (grid redesign) square inset value cell; grid lines drawn separately.
			vp.ClipsDescendants=true
			-- vertical divider between the name and value columns (x set in relayoutRow)
			local vdiv=Instance.new("Frame")
			vdiv.BorderSizePixel=0; vdiv.BackgroundColor3=C.header; vdiv.ZIndex=3
			vdiv.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,1,0); vdiv.Parent=row
			-- horizontal row separator along the bottom of the row (full width)
			local hsep=Instance.new("Frame")
			hsep.BorderSizePixel=0; hsep.BackgroundColor3=C.header; hsep.ZIndex=1
			hsep.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,1); hsep.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,1,0); hsep.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,1); hsep.Parent=outer
			-- whole-row hover highlight: lift the row + value cell slightly. Reads the live
			-- resting field colour (Theme.propFieldColor) so it survives theme changes.
			local function _restCol() return Theme.propFieldColor or INPUT end
			outer.MouseEnter:Connect(function() local r=_restCol(); local hl=r:Lerp(C.accent,0.04); UIKit.tw(row,{BackgroundColor3=hl}); UIKit.tw(vp,{BackgroundColor3=hl}) end)
			outer.MouseLeave:Connect(function() local r=_restCol(); UIKit.tw(row,{BackgroundColor3=r}); UIKit.tw(vp,{BackgroundColor3=r}) end)
			local _tsr=game:GetService("TextService")
			local function relayoutRow()
				local rw=row.AbsoluteSize.X; if rw<=0 then return end
				-- (grid redesign) FIXED column split so every row's name/value boundary
				-- aligns into one straight vertical grid divider across the whole list.
				local lpW=math.floor(SPLIT*rw+0.5)
				local vpStart=lpW+((extraLeftSpace>0) and (extraLeftSpace+GAP*2) or 0)
				if lp.Size.X.Offset==lpW and vp.Position.X.Offset==vpStart then return end  -- no-op: break re-entrant AbsoluteSize cascade
				lp.Size=UDim2.new(0,lpW,1,0)
				vdiv.Position=UDim2.new(0,lpW,0,0)
				if reserved then reserved.Position=UDim2.new(0,lpW+GAP,0,0); reserved.Size=UDim2.new(0,extraLeftSpace,1,0) end
				vp.Position=UDim2.new(0,vpStart,0,0); vp.Size=UDim2.new(1,-vpStart,1,0)
			end
			do local _rb=false row:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(function() if _rb then return end _rb=true relayoutRow() _rb=false end) end; table.insert(relayoutFns,relayoutRow)
			relayoutRow()
			return outer,vp,reserved,lp
		end

		local allDdFrames={}  -- track all enum dropdowns to close on collapse
		local ddReg={}  -- {close fns} so opening one dropdown closes the others
		local allClsSections={}
		local clsElements={}  -- cls -> { list of {el=Instance, base=relativeLayoutOrder} }
		local clsOrderState={} -- cls -> current applied base (to detect change)
		local CLASS_SPAN=1000  -- layout order room per class block
		for schemaIdx,clsGroup in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
			local cls=clsGroup.cls
			clsElements[cls]={}
			local clsBase=schemaIdx*CLASS_SPAN
			local relOrder=0
			local clsChildren={}
			local clsCollapsed=false
			local grpCollapseFns={}
			-- Class header (collapsible dropdown)
			local clsHdr=Instance.new("TextButton")
			clsHdr.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,HDR_H+8); clsHdr.BackgroundColor3=HDR_BG
			clsHdr.BorderSizePixel=0; clsHdr.Text=""; clsHdr.AutoButtonColor=false
			clsHdr.LayoutOrder=clsBase+relOrder; relOrder+=1
			clsHdr:SetAttribute("ClsHeader",cls); clsHdr.Parent=scroll
			table.insert(clsElements[cls],clsHdr)
			UIKit.corner(clsHdr,6)
			local clsArrow=Instance.new("ImageLabel")
			clsArrow.Size=UDim2.new(0,10,0,10); clsArrow.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0.5)
			clsArrow.Position=UDim2.new(0,8,0.5,0); clsArrow.BackgroundTransparency=1
			UIKit.setIconImage(clsArrow,"116140507142780"); clsArrow.ImageColor3=C.textDim
			clsArrow.Rotation=0; clsArrow.ScaleType=Enum.ScaleType.Fit; clsArrow.ZIndex=3; clsArrow.Parent=clsHdr
			local clsLbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
			clsLbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-26,1,0); clsLbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,22,0,0)
			clsLbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; clsLbl.Text=cls
			clsLbl.TextColor3=C.textDim; clsLbl.Font=TXT.title.font; clsLbl.TextSize=TXT.title.size
			clsLbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; clsLbl.ZIndex=3; clsLbl.Parent=clsHdr
			clsHdr.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
				if not clsCollapsed then
					-- Collapse
					clsCollapsed=true; clsArrow.Rotation=-90; clsCollapsedMap[cls]=true
					plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_propsOpen_"..cls, false)
					for gi,fn2 in ipairs(grpCollapseFns) do
						clsChildren[gi].Visible=false
						fn2(true)
					end
					for _,df in ipairs(allDdFrames) do df.Visible=false end
				else
					-- Expand — restore user's group collapse state
					clsCollapsed=false; clsArrow.Rotation=0; clsCollapsedMap[cls]=false
					plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_propsOpen_"..cls, true)
					for gi,fn2 in ipairs(grpCollapseFns) do
						clsChildren[gi].Visible=true
						fn2(userCollapsed[cls..clsGroup.groups[gi].name]==true)
					end
				end
			end)
			clsHdr.Visible=false
			-- Restore saved open state (default = open)
			-- (grid redesign) class headers are hidden, so a class can never be
			-- collapsed by the user; always treat it as expanded so its groups show.
			clsCollapsed=false; clsCollapsedMap[cls]=false
			do
				local _cls=cls; local _clsChildren=clsChildren; local _grpFns=grpCollapseFns; local _dds=allDdFrames; local _arrow=clsArrow
				table.insert(allClsSections,{
					hdr=clsHdr,
					collapse=function()
						if clsCollapsed then return end
						clsCollapsed=true; _arrow.Rotation=-90
						for _,fn2 in ipairs(_grpFns) do fn2(true) end
						for _,ch in pairs(_clsChildren) do ch.Visible=false end
						for _,df in ipairs(_dds) do df.Visible=false end
					end
				})
			end
			for _,grp in ipairs(clsGroup.groups) do
				-- Reserve order slot for group header BEFORE prop rows
				local grpOrder=GROUP_ORDER[grp.name]*100000; relOrder+=1
				local grpRows={}

				for _,def in ipairs(grp.props) do
					local p=def.p; local t=def.t
					-- (merge) value editors below target every selected instance with this exact
					-- property, not just this class's -- so same-named props edit together.
					local function getTargets(cls) return getPropTargets(grp.name, p, t) end
					local axisReserve = (t=="axisseq" and def.axes) and (#def.axes*AXIS_BTN + (#def.axes-1)*AXIS_BTN_GAP) or 0
					local row,vp,reservedArea,namePill=makePropRow(def.label or p, GROUP_ORDER[grp.name]*100000 + (PROP_SLOT[grp.name.."|"..tostring(p).."|"..tostring(t)] or 0)*100 + schemaIdx, axisReserve); relOrder+=1
					table.insert(grpRows, row)

					-- on the NAME pill only: hovering a value field (textbox, slider, dropdown) should
					-- not pop the row's description over what you are editing.
					if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(namePill, def.desc or PROP_DESC[def.p]) end
					table.insert(clsElements[cls],row)

					local updateFn

					if t=="num" then
						local slArea=Instance.new("Frame")

							ob.Text=opt; ob.TextColor3=C.textDim; ob.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold

						local ddH=#sortedOpts*DDR_H+math.max(0,#sortedOpts-1)*3+6

							ddOpen=false; ddOpenedUp=false; tw(arrIco,{Rotation=0})

						local vpBtn=Instance.new("TextButton")

						local typeBuffer=""; local lastChar=""; local lastMatchIdx=0  -- declared BEFORE the click handler so its reset writes THESE, not globals
						vpBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()

								local vpAbs=vp.AbsolutePosition
								local wAbs=bgFrame.AbsolutePosition
								local _sc=(WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1; local w=vp.AbsoluteSize.X/_sc+6
								local ddH2=math.min(ddH,165)  -- ~6 rows visible (was ~4)
								local yDown=(vpAbs.Y-wAbs.Y)/_sc+ROW_H

								else

									local yTop=(vpAbs.Y-wAbs.Y)/_sc-ddH2
									ddFrame.Size=UDim2.new(0,w,0,ddH2)
									ddFrame.Position=UDim2.new(0,xOff,0,yTop)

								restyleAll(true)
								updateDdSB()

									if inp.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 and inp.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.Touch then return end
									local mp=uis:GetMouseLocation()
									local function inside(g) local ap=g.AbsolutePosition; local as=g.AbsoluteSize; return mp.X>=ap.X and mp.X<=ap.X+as.X and mp.Y>=ap.Y and mp.Y<=ap.Y+as.Y end
									if inside(ddFrame) or inside(vpBtn) or inside(sbTrack) then return end

							task.defer(function()

								for i,ob in ipairs(optBtns) do

									end

						-- Track scroll to move dropdown with the list
						-- (typeBuffer/lastChar/lastMatchIdx hoisted above the open handler)
						-- true item-center scroll target: 3px top padding + (idx-1) rows of
						-- DDR_H+3 (row height + the UIListLayout gap) to the row's top, +DDR_H/2
						-- to its center, minus half the REAL visible height (not a guessed const)

						local function centerCanvasFor(idx)

							else

								end
							end
						end
						local typeTimer=nil
						sinkBox:GetPropertyChangedSignal("Text"):Connect(function()

							local ch=tx:sub(-1):lower()

							if not ddOpen then return end
							local vpAbs2=vp.AbsolutePosition
							local wAbs2=bgFrame.AbsolutePosition
							local _sc=(WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1; local xOff2=(vpAbs2.X-wAbs2.X)/_sc-3
							if ddOpenedUp then ddFrame.Position=UDim2.new(0,xOff2,0,(vpAbs2.Y-wAbs2.Y)/_sc-ddFrame.AbsoluteSize.Y/_sc)
							else ddFrame.Position=UDim2.new(0,xOff2,0,(vpAbs2.Y-wAbs2.Y)/_sc+ROW_H) end
							updateDdSB()
						end)
						updateFn=function(targets)
							local v,mx=getGroupedVal(targets,p)
							if mx then valLbl.Text="..."
							elseif v~=nil then
								local s2=tostring(v); local last=s2; for seg in s2:gmatch("[^.]+") do last=seg end; valLbl.Text=last
							else valLbl.Text="" end

					end

					rowBuilders[cls..grp.name..p]={update=updateFn,row=row}
				end

				-- Create group header with pre-reserved order slot (indented under the class)

				grpOuter.BorderSizePixel=0; grpOuter.LayoutOrder=grpOrder; grpOuter.Parent=scroll
				grpHdr.Size=UDim2.new(1,-CLS_INDENT,0,HDR_H); grpHdr.Position=UDim2.new(0,CLS_INDENT,0,0)
				grpHdr.BackgroundColor3=HDR_BG
				grpHdr.BorderSizePixel=0; grpHdr.Text=""; grpHdr.AutoButtonColor=false

		-- Determine the order classes first appear in the selection (top-level selection order,
		-- then descendants). The class of whatever you selected first shows first.
		local SCHEMA_INDEX={}
		for i,cg in ipairs(SCHEMA) do SCHEMA_INDEX[cg.cls]=i end
		local function getClassOrder()
			local seenCls={}; local order={}
			local function consider(inst)
				for _,cg in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
					if not seenCls[cg.cls] and inst:IsA(cg.cls) then
						seenCls[cg.cls]=true; table.insert(order,cg.cls)
					end
				end
			end
			for _,obj in ipairs(sel:Get()) do
				if obj==game or obj==workspace or obj==game:GetService("Players") then continue end
				if not obj:IsDescendantOf(game) then continue end
				consider(obj)
				for _,d in ipairs(ctx.descendantsTo(obj)) do consider(d) end
			end
			-- append any schema classes not seen, in schema order, so they keep stable positions
			for _,cg in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
				if not seenCls[cg.cls] then table.insert(order,cg.cls) end
			end
			return order
		end

		-- Reassign each class block's LayoutOrder base to match selection order,
		-- preserving the internal relative order of each element within its block.
		local lastOrderKey=nil
		local function reorderClasses() do return end end local function _reorderClasses_unused()
			local order=getClassOrder()
			local key=table.concat(order,",")
			if key==lastOrderKey then return end
			lastOrderKey=key
			for newIdx,cls in ipairs(order) do
				local els=clsElements[cls]; if not els then continue end
				local oldBase=SCHEMA_INDEX[cls]*CLASS_SPAN
				local newBase=newIdx*CLASS_SPAN
				for _,el in ipairs(els) do
					local rel=el.LayoutOrder-oldBase
					el.LayoutOrder=newBase+rel
				end
				-- keep SCHEMA_INDEX in sync so subsequent reorders use the new base
				SCHEMA_INDEX[cls]=newIdx
			end
		end

		-- Tree lines: one vertical line per class (down its groups) and per group (down its rows).
		-- Parented to scroll, positioned in drawTreeLines() after layout settles using AbsolutePositions.
		local updateMisc
		local clsLineByCls={}    -- cls -> Frame
		local grpLineByKey={}    -- cls..grpName -> Frame
		local function ensureLine(store,key)
			if store[key] then return store[key] end
			local ln=Instance.new("Frame")
			ln.BackgroundColor3=C.text; ln.BackgroundTransparency=0.5
			ln.BorderSizePixel=0; ln.ZIndex=20; ln.Visible=false; ln:SetAttribute("SVFXnoFade", true)
			-- Parent to propListWrapper (outside UIListLayout) so layout doesn't consume these frames
			ln.Parent=propListWrapper
			ln.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,0)
			ln.ClipsDescendants = true
			store[key]=ln
			return ln
		end
		local function drawTreeLines()
			-- (grid redesign) tree lines removed. Hide any that already exist and bail
			-- before the layout math so no vertical connector lines are drawn.
			for _,ln in pairs(clsLineByCls) do ln.Visible=false end
			for _,ln in pairs(grpLineByKey) do ln.Visible=false end
			do return end
			-- Y is canvas-relative so lines track scrolling. X uses scroll-space coords:
			-- scroll has UIPadding.PaddingLeft=4, so scroll content starts at wAbs.X+4.
			-- Class line sits at the left edge of group headers = PAD + CLS_INDENT
			-- Group line sits at the left edge of prop rows    = PAD + CLS_INDENT + GRP_INDENT
			local tlCol = Theme.treeLineColor or C.text
			local clsT = Theme.treeLineTransparency or CLS_LINE_T
			local grpT = Theme.treeLineGroupTransparency or math.min(1, clsT + (GRP_LINE_T - CLS_LINE_T))
			local PAD_L = 4  -- matches UIPadding.PaddingLeft on scroll
			local CLS_LINE_X  = PAD_L + CLS_INDENT - 8
			local GRP_LINE_X  = PAD_L + CLS_INDENT + GRP_INDENT - 8
			local wAbs=propListWrapper.AbsolutePosition
			local sOff=scroll.CanvasPosition
			local wrapperH=propListWrapper.AbsoluteSize.Y
			if wrapperH<=0 then wrapperH=math.huge end
			local function topY(el) return el.AbsolutePosition.Y - wAbs.Y end
			local searching=(searchQuery~="")
			for _,clsGroup in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
				local cls=clsGroup.cls
				local clsHdr=clsHeaders[cls]
				local clsLine=ensureLine(clsLineByCls,cls)
				-- class line: sits at the left edge of group headers (children of clsHdr)
				if clsHdr and clsHdr.Visible and (searching or not clsCollapsedMap[cls]) then
					local top=topY(clsHdr)+clsHdr.AbsoluteSize.Y
					local bottom=top
					for _,el in ipairs(clsElements[cls]) do
						if el~=clsHdr and el.Visible then
							local b=topY(el)+el.AbsoluteSize.Y
							if b>bottom then bottom=b end
						end
					end
					if bottom>top+1 then
						local lt=math.max(0,top+TREE_LINE_INSET_TOP)
						local lb=math.min(wrapperH,bottom-TREE_LINE_INSET_BOT)
						if lb>lt then
							clsLine.Position=UDim2.new(0,CLS_LINE_X,0,lt)
							clsLine.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,lb-lt)
							clsLine.BackgroundColor3=tlCol; clsLine.BackgroundTransparency=clsT; clsLine:SetAttribute("SVFXtreeLineCls", true); clsLine.Visible=true
						else clsLine.Visible=false end
					else clsLine.Visible=false end
				else clsLine.Visible=false end
				-- group lines: sits at the left edge of prop rows (children of grpHdr)
				for _,grp in ipairs(clsGroup.groups) do
					local key=cls..grp.name
					local grpHdr=grpHeaders[key]
					local grpLine=ensureLine(grpLineByKey,key)
					if grpHdr and grpHdr.Visible and (searching or (not collapsed[key] and not clsCollapsedMap[cls])) then
						local top=topY(grpHdr)+grpHdr.AbsoluteSize.Y
						local bottom=top
						for _,def in ipairs(grp.props) do
							local rb=rowBuilders[key..def.p]
							if rb and rb.row.Visible then
								local b=topY(rb.row)+rb.row.AbsoluteSize.Y
								if b>bottom then bottom=b end
							end
						end
						if bottom>top+1 then
							local lt=math.max(0,top+TREE_LINE_INSET_TOP)
							local lb=math.min(wrapperH,bottom-TREE_LINE_INSET_BOT)
							if lb>lt then
								grpLine.Position=UDim2.new(0,GRP_LINE_X,0,lt)
								grpLine.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,lb-lt)
								grpLine.BackgroundColor3=tlCol; grpLine.BackgroundTransparency=grpT; grpLine:SetAttribute("SVFXtreeLineGrp", true); grpLine.Visible=true
							else grpLine.Visible=false end
						else grpLine.Visible=false end
					else grpLine.Visible=false end
				end
			end
		end

		-- ===== Misc section =====
		-- Bottom-of-list home for switches that are plugin VIEW state, not emitter properties, so
		-- they don't belong in any spec group. Currently: the Shape wireframe outline. Shown only
		-- when the selection is a 3D Particle with Shape on (that outline is all there is to hide).
		local miscHdr, miscBtn
		local function miscTargets()
			local out = {}
			for _, inst in ipairs(getAllTargets()["3D Particle"] or {}) do
				local pf = inst:FindFirstChild("Properties")
				local shp = pf and pf:FindFirstChild("Shape")
				if shp and shp:GetAttribute("ShapeOn") == true then out[#out+1] = inst end
			end
			return out
		end
		do
			miscHdr = Instance.new("Frame")
			miscHdr.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,0,HDR_H); miscHdr.BackgroundColor3 = HDR_BG
			miscHdr.BorderSizePixel = 0; miscHdr.LayoutOrder = 10000000   -- last block in the list, always
			miscHdr.Visible = false; miscHdr.Parent = scroll
			local msep = Instance.new("Frame")
			msep.BorderSizePixel = 0; msep.BackgroundColor3 = C.header; msep.ZIndex = 1
			msep.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0,1); msep.Position = UDim2.new(0,0,1,0)
			msep.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,0,1); msep.Parent = miscHdr
			local mlbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
			mlbl.Size = UDim2.new(1,-20,1,0); mlbl.Position = UDim2.new(0,11,0,0)
			mlbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1; mlbl.Text = "Misc"; mlbl.TextColor3 = C.text
			mlbl.Font = TXT.body.font; mlbl.TextSize = TXT.body.size+1
			mlbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; mlbl.ZIndex = 3; mlbl.Parent = miscHdr

			-- Accent bar. Not a real toggle control on purpose: the label carries the state
			-- ("Hide"/"Show" = what the click DOES), so there is nothing to read wrong.
			-- 3px breathing room each side so the bar reads as a control sitting IN the list, not
			-- another full-bleed grid row. Wrapper takes the layout slot; the button insets inside it.
			local miscWrap = Instance.new("Frame")
			miscWrap.Size = UDim2.new(1,0,0,25); miscWrap.BackgroundTransparency = 1
			miscWrap.BorderSizePixel = 0; miscWrap.LayoutOrder = 10000001  -- ...and the bar sits under its own header
			miscWrap.Visible = false; miscWrap.Parent = scroll
			miscBtn = Instance.new("TextButton")
			-- even 3px inset on all four sides (25 = 3 + 19 + 3).
			miscBtn.Size = UDim2.new(1,-6,0,19); miscBtn.Position = UDim2.new(0,3,0,3)
			miscBtn.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent; miscBtn.BorderSizePixel = 0
			miscBtn.AutoButtonColor = false; miscBtn.Text = "Hide Shape Frame"
			-- accent is a light tint in most themes, so near-white label text washes out; pick the
			-- readable side of the fill instead of hardcoding one.
			local _al = 0.299*C.accent.R + 0.587*C.accent.G + 0.114*C.accent.B
			miscBtn.TextColor3 = (_al > 0.55) and C.base or C.text
			miscBtn.Font = TXT.btn.font; miscBtn.TextSize = TXT.btn.size
			miscBtn.Parent = miscWrap
			do local mc = Instance.new("UICorner"); mc.CornerRadius = UDim.new(0,4); mc.Parent = miscBtn end

			local function shown()
				-- absent == drawn, so "any target still showing it" decides what the click means
				for _, inst in ipairs(miscTargets()) do
					if inst:GetAttribute("SineVFX_ShapeGizmo") ~= false then return true end
				end
				return false
			end
			local function syncMisc()
				miscBtn.Text = shown() and "Hide Shape Frame" or "Show Shape Frame"
			end
			-- UIKit.hoverLift keeps this identical to every other button (HoverBase attribute, one
			-- connection pair, standard tween); the propOverride lifts the FILL, and 0.18 keeps it a
			-- hint rather than a flash -- the default 0.4 was far too hard on a solid accent bar.
			UIKit.hoverLift(miscBtn, nil, 0.18, nil, "BackgroundColor3")
			miscBtn.Active = true; miscBtn.Selectable = true; miscBtn.ZIndex = 4
			miscBtn.Activated:Connect(function()
				local hide = shown()
				for _, inst in ipairs(miscTargets()) do
					-- NOT `hide and false or nil`: `and false` is falsy, so `or nil` always wins and
					-- the attribute could only ever be cleared. Needs a real branch.
					if hide then
						inst:SetAttribute("SineVFX_ShapeGizmo", false)   -- hidden
					else
						inst:SetAttribute("SineVFX_ShapeGizmo", nil)     -- back to the default (drawn)
					end
				end
				syncMisc()
			end)
			updateMisc = function()
				local n = #miscTargets()
				miscHdr.Visible = n > 0; miscWrap.Visible = n > 0
				if n > 0 then
					miscBtn.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
					miscBtn:SetAttribute("HoverBase", C.accent)   -- hoverLift reads its resting colour from here
					syncMisc()
				end
			end
		end

		refresh=function()
			clearPropConns()
			reorderClasses()
			local _shownGroups={}; local _shownKeys={}
			local q=searchQuery; local searching=(q~="")
			local targetsByClass=getAllTargets()
			for _,clsGroup in ipairs(SCHEMA) do
				local cls=clsGroup.cls
				local targets=targetsByClass[cls]; local has=#targets>0
				-- a class/group name match shows that whole branch
				local clsMatch=(not searching) or (cls:lower():find(q,1,true)~=nil)
				local anyGroupShown=false
				for _,grp in ipairs(clsGroup.groups) do
					local key=cls..grp.name
					local grpOuter=grpHeaders[key] and grpHeaders[key].Parent
					local grpMatch=clsMatch or (grp.name:lower():find(q,1,true)~=nil)
					local anyPropShown=false
					for _,def in ipairs(grp.props) do
						local rb=rowBuilders[key..def.p]; if not rb then continue end
						-- attribute-backed rows (Emit) are always available on the class
						if def.t=="attrnum" or def.attr or def.axes then
							-- Route gates the whole Custom sub-tree. Shown if ANY selected emitter is
							-- Custom (matches how mixed selections behave elsewhere). TargetN slots also
							-- need Target(N-1) filled. Watched in the rb.update watcher below, NOT further
							-- down the function -- this branch continues before reaching that code.
							local gateOK=true
							local _slotN=tonumber(def.p:match("^Target(%d+)$"))
							if def.showIf then
								gateOK=false
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									if _showIfOK(inst, def.showIf) then gateOK=true; break end
								end
							elseif def.p=="RouteSmooth" then
								-- only InOrder has corners to round
								gateOK=false
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									local h=def.holder and def.holder(inst)
									if h and h:GetAttribute("Route")=="Custom" and h:GetAttribute("RoutePick")=="InOrder" then gateOK=true; break end
								end
							elseif def.p=="RouteRandom" then
								-- scatters AROUND a destination, so it applies to Center as well as Custom
								gateOK=false
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									local h=def.holder and def.holder(inst)
									local r=h and h:GetAttribute("Route")
									if r=="Center" or r=="Custom" then gateOK=true; break end
								end
							elseif _slotN or def.p=="RoutePick" then
								gateOK=false
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									local h=def.holder and def.holder(inst)
									if h and h:GetAttribute("Route")=="Custom" then
										-- RoutePick just needs Custom. A TargetN slot appears once
										-- Target(N-1) actually holds something, so the list grows as it
										-- is filled -- no count attribute to drift out of range.
										if (not _slotN) or _slotN==1 then gateOK=true; break end
										local _f=h:FindFirstChild(EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER)
										local _prev=_f and _f:FindFirstChild("Target"..(_slotN-1))
										if _prev and _prev:IsA("ObjectValue") and _prev.Value then gateOK=true; break end
									end
								end
							end
							local visA
							if searching then
								local pm=grpMatch or ((def.label or def.p):lower():find(q,1,true)~=nil)
								visA=has and gateOK and pm
							else
								visA=has and gateOK and not collapsed[key] and not clsCollapsedMap[cls]
							end
							do local _mk=grp.name.."|"..tostring(def.p).."|"..tostring(def.t); if visA and _shownKeys[_mk] then rb.row.Visible=false else rb.row.Visible=visA; if visA then _shownKeys[_mk]=true end end end
							if visA then anyPropShown=true end
							if has and rb.update then
								rb.update(getPropTargets(grp.name, def.p, def.t))
								local watchAttrs = def.axes or {def.attr or def.p}
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									local watch=(def.holder and def.holder(inst)) or inst
									for _,attrName in ipairs(watchAttrs) do
										local okA,cA=pcall(function()
											return watch and watch:GetAttributeChangedSignal(attrName):Connect(function()
												if not inst.Parent then return end
												if rb.update then rb.update(getPropTargets(grp.name, def.p, def.t)) end
												-- Route/RoutePick decide which rows below them are VISIBLE, so a
												-- value update is not enough -- recompute the whole tree. Must
												-- live here: this branch `continue`s, so a watcher added further
												-- down never runs for attribute-backed rows.
												if attrName=="Route" or attrName=="RoutePick" then task.defer(refresh) end
										end)
										end)
										if okA then table.insert(propConns,cA) end
									end
								end
							end
							if def.showIf then
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									for _,cond in ipairs(_showIfConds(def.showIf)) do
										local h = _showIfHolder(inst, cond)
										local okG,cG = pcall(function()
											return h and h:GetAttributeChangedSignal(cond.a):Connect(function()
												if inst.Parent then task.defer(refresh) end
											end)
										end)
										if okG then table.insert(propConns,cG) end
									end
								end
							end
							if def.p=="Target1" then
								for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
									local h=def.holder and def.holder(inst)
									local f=h and h:FindFirstChild(EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER)
									if f then
										for _,sig in ipairs({f.ChildAdded, f.ChildRemoved}) do
											local okS,cS=pcall(function() return sig:Connect(function() task.defer(refresh) end) end)
											if okS then table.insert(propConns,cS) end
										end
										for _,ov in ipairs(f:GetChildren()) do
											if ov:IsA("ObjectValue") then
												local okV,cV=pcall(function()
