													return ov:GetPropertyChangedSignal("Value"):Connect(function() task.defer(refresh) end)
												end)
												if okV then table.insert(propConns,cV) end
											end
										end
									end
								end
							end
							continue
						end
						-- check if prop exists on instance
						local propExists=false
						if has then
							for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
								local ok=pcall(function() inst:GetPropertyChangedSignal(def.p) end)
								if ok then
									-- Brightness is hidden only at LightInfluence==1, where the scene's lighting fully
									-- takes over and glow strength genuinely does nothing. Anywhere below 1 it still has an
									-- effect (it just competes with the lighting), so a partial value like 0.5 keeps the row.
									-- Classes with Brightness but NO LightInfluence (Light) leave li at its 0 default via the
									-- failed pcall and so always show the row.
									if def.p=="Brightness" then
										local li=0; pcall(function() li=inst.LightInfluence end)
										if li==1 then continue end
									end
									if def.p=="FlipbookMode" or def.p=="FlipbookStartRandom" then
										local lay; pcall(function() lay=inst.FlipbookLayout end)
										if lay==Enum.ParticleFlipbookLayout.None then continue end
									end
									if def.p=="FlipbookFramerate" then
										local lay; pcall(function() lay=inst.FlipbookLayout end)
										if lay==Enum.ParticleFlipbookLayout.None then continue end
										local fmode; pcall(function() fmode=inst.FlipbookMode end)
										if fmode==Enum.ParticleFlipbookMode.OneShot then continue end
									end
									if def.p=="FlipbookSizeX" or def.p=="FlipbookSizeY" then
										local lay; pcall(function() lay=inst.FlipbookLayout end)
										if lay~=Enum.ParticleFlipbookLayout.Custom then continue end
									end
									propExists=true; break
								end
							end
						end
						-- search: ignore collapse state, gate on name match instead
						local visible
						if searching then
							local propMatch=grpMatch or (def.p:lower():find(q,1,true)~=nil)
							visible=propExists and propMatch
						else
							visible=propExists and not collapsed[key] and not clsCollapsedMap[cls]
						end
						do local _mk=grp.name.."|"..tostring(def.p).."|"..tostring(def.t); if visible and _shownKeys[_mk] then rb.row.Visible=false else rb.row.Visible=visible; if visible then _shownKeys[_mk]=true end end end
						if visible then anyPropShown=true end
						-- Always watch LightInfluence for Brightness rows so visibility updates when it changes
						if def.p=="Brightness" then
							for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
								local ok2,c2=pcall(function()
									return inst:GetPropertyChangedSignal("LightInfluence"):Connect(function()
										task.defer(refresh)
									end)
								end)
								if ok2 then table.insert(propConns,c2) end
							end
						end
						if def.p=="FlipbookFramerate" or def.p=="FlipbookMode" or def.p=="FlipbookStartRandom" or def.p=="FlipbookSizeX" or def.p=="FlipbookSizeY" then
							for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
								local ok3,c3=pcall(function()
									return inst:GetPropertyChangedSignal("FlipbookLayout"):Connect(function() task.defer(refresh) end)
								end)
								if ok3 then table.insert(propConns,c3) end
							end
						end
						if def.p=="FlipbookFramerate" then
							for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
								local okm,cm=pcall(function()
									return inst:GetPropertyChangedSignal("FlipbookMode"):Connect(function() task.defer(refresh) end)
								end)
								if okm then table.insert(propConns,cm) end
							end
						end
						if propExists and rb.update then
							rb.update(getPropTargets(grp.name, def.p, def.t))
							for _,inst in ipairs(targets) do
								local ok,c=pcall(function()
									return inst:GetPropertyChangedSignal(def.p):Connect(function()
										if not inst.Parent then return end
										if rb.update then rb.update(getPropTargets(grp.name, def.p, def.t)) end
									end)
								end)
								if ok then table.insert(propConns,c) end
							end
						end
					end
					-- group header visibility
					if grpOuter then
						local _gv = searching and (has and anyPropShown) or ((not searching) and (has and not clsCollapsedMap[cls]))
						if _gv and _shownGroups[grp.name] then grpOuter.Visible=false else grpOuter.Visible=_gv; if _gv then _shownGroups[grp.name]=true end end
					end
					if has and anyPropShown then anyGroupShown=true end
				end
				-- class header visibility -- (grid redesign) class/instance headers are hidden;
				-- the selected class shows in the title bar and groups render full-width.
				if clsHeaders[cls] then clsHeaders[cls].Visible=false end
			end
			if updateMisc then updateMisc() end
			-- redraw the tree lines after visibility settles
			task.defer(drawTreeLines)
		end

		-- Coalesce bursts of SelectionChanged (e.g. ctrl+click multi-select, box-select)
		-- into a single refresh() instead of one full rebuild per event.
		local _refreshQueued=false
		local function queueRefresh()
			if _refreshQueued then return end
			_refreshQueued=true
			task.defer(function() _refreshQueued=false; refresh(); restoreScroll() end)
		end
		-- Also watch the SELECTION's own subtree: the panel must update when its shape changes -- a
		-- nested VFX added/removed, something transformed/untransformed under it, or the selection
		-- reparented. The resolvers key off tree structure, so this can add or remove whole classes.
		local function rebuildStructWatchers()
			for _,c in ipairs(structConns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
			structConns={}
			local roots=sel:Get()
			if #roots>20 then return end   -- don't swarm watchers on a huge multi-selection
			for _,root in ipairs(roots) do
				if root==game or root==workspace then continue end
				for _,sig in ipairs({root.DescendantAdded, root.DescendantRemoving, root.AncestryChanged}) do
					local ok,c=pcall(function() return sig:Connect(queueRefresh) end)
					if ok then table.insert(structConns,c) end
				end
			end
		end
		selConn=sel.SelectionChanged:Connect(function()
			rebuildStructWatchers()
			queueRefresh()
		end)
		rebuildStructWatchers()
		task.defer(function()
			refresh()
			contentReady=true  -- only relevant rows are visible now; let fitHeight size the window
		end)
		local function _relayoutAll() for _,f in ipairs(relayoutFns) do pcall(f) end end
		task.defer(_relayoutAll)
		do local _n=0; local _c; _c=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function() _n+=1; _relayoutAll(); if _n>=12 then _c:Disconnect() end end) end
		-- keep tree lines in sync when the list reflows or scrolls.
		-- Coalesce to ONE deferred draw at a time: drawing reflows geometry which
		-- re-fires these signals, and an unguarded task.defer per signal recurses to
		-- the 80-deep re-entrancy limit (storm seen in console).
		local _treeQueued=false
		local function queueTreeLines()
			if _treeQueued then return end
			_treeQueued=true
			task.defer(function() _treeQueued=false; drawTreeLines() end)
		end
		layout:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteContentSize"):Connect(queueTreeLines)
		scroll:GetPropertyChangedSignal("CanvasPosition"):Connect(drawTreeLines)
		propListWrapper:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(queueTreeLines)
	end

	return {mount=mount,unmount=cleanup,isOpen=isOpen}
end

-- CurveWindow: NumberSequence curve editor (bezier-capable)
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory)
	local C   = Theme.C
	local tw  = UIKit.tw
	local TXT = Theme.TXT or {title={font=Enum.Font.GothamBold,size=13},body={font=Enum.Font.GothamBold,size=12},small={font=Enum.Font.GothamMedium,size=11}}
	local coreGui    = ctx.coreGui
	local plugin     = ctx.plugin
	local uis        = ctx.userInputService
	local runService = ctx.runService

	local gui=nil; local isPinned=false; local kps={}; local origKps={}
	local selIdx=nil; local dragConn=nil; local scrollConn=nil; local redrawConn=nil; local histConn=nil; local _lastDragFlag=false
	local redraw  -- forward decl; assigned in mount (coalesced redraw)
	local fullRedraw  -- forward decl; real rebuild + UI sync, assigned in mount
	local redrawDirty=false
	local _gridSig=nil  -- view signature; grid is only rebuilt when it changes (see redrawNow)
	-- Frame pool for kpLayer. The envelope hatch + edge polylines create hundreds
	-- of Frames per redraw; destroying and re-creating them all every frame (esp.
	-- during a drag) is the main source of lag. Instead we REUSE Frames: acquire
	-- pulls a hidden one from the pool (or makes a new one), and at the start of a
	-- redraw we "release" by marking all pooled Frames reusable. Reconfiguring a
	-- Frame's props is far cheaper than Instance.new + Destroy.
	local framePool={}     -- array of Frame instances parented to kpLayer
	local framePoolN=0     -- how many are currently in use this redraw
	local dotPool={}       -- pooled anchor dots (Frame + UICorner)
	local dotPoolN=0
	local callback=nil; local origSeq=nil; local onRevert=nil
	-- Fired ONCE when the editor closes keeping its edits. The host uses it to mark a single
	-- Studio undo waypoint for the whole session, since every live write in between is silenced.
	-- Cancel does not fire it: onRevert owns the history for that path.
	local onCommit=nil
	local bezierIO=nil  -- {read=function()->string, write=function(string)}
	local kpLayer=nil; local gridLayer=nil; local cbg=nil
	local syncPlotClip  -- forward declared; assigned after cbg is built
	local TOPBAR_H=24; local BTN_ROW_H=25; local BTN_ROW2_H=25; local E=6; local KR=5
	local HR=5  -- handle dot radius
	local STUB_PX_T=22  -- pixel offset for grab-stub when ot is zero
	local valMin=-math.huge; local valMax=math.huge; local viewMin=0; local viewMax=1; local dispV=1
	local LPAD=32; local XPAD=16; local YPAD=12
	-- Global display toggles (set from the drop-up): show/hide the bezier handles
	-- and the envelope band. Persisted; default on. Read by the module redraw.
	local showHandlesG = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_CurveShowHandles"); if showHandlesG==nil then showHandlesG=true end
	local showEnvG     = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_CurveShowEnv");     if showEnvG==nil then showEnvG=true end
	local envAllowed   = true  -- false => hide envelope UI (only ParticleEmitter sequences use envelopes)

	local function addCommas(s)
		local sign = ""
		if s:sub(1,1) == "-" then sign = "-"; s = s:sub(2) end
		local intPart, fracPart = s:match("^(%d+)(.*)$")
		if not intPart then return sign..s end
		local formatted = intPart:reverse():gsub("(%d%d%d)", "%1,"):reverse():gsub("^,", "")
		return sign..formatted..(fracPart or "")
	end
	local function fmtNum(v, dec)
		local intDigits=math.max(1,math.floor(math.log(math.max(1,math.abs(v)),10))+1)
		local byDigits = (intDigits>=3) and 0 or (intDigits==2 and 1 or 2)
		local d=math.max(byDigits, dec or 0)
		local s = string.format("%."..d.."f", v)
		-- Strip trailing zeros after a decimal point, then strip a trailing dot.
		if s:find("%.") then
			s = s:gsub("0+$", ""):gsub("%.$", "")
		end
		if s == "-0" then s = "0" end  -- kill -0 after rounding
		return addCommas(s)
	end
	-- Like fmtNum but pads decimals: 1-digit int -> 2dp, 2-digit int -> 1dp, 3+ -> 0dp
	local function fmtAxisLabel(v, minDec)
		local av = math.abs(v)
		local intDigits = (av < 1) and 1 or (math.floor(math.log(av, 10)) + 1)
		local padDec = (intDigits >= 3) and 0 or (intDigits == 2 and 1 or 2)
		local d = math.max(padDec, minDec or 0)
		local s = string.format("%." .. d .. "f", v)
		if s == "-0" or s == ("-0."..string.rep("0",d)) then s = "0."..string.rep("0",d) end
		return addCommas(s)
	end
	local pending=nil

	-- ---------- Bezier helpers ----------
	local function isZeroOt(ot) return (not ot) or (math.abs(ot.dt)<1e-5 and math.abs(ot.dv)<1e-5) end
	local function segIsLinear(A,B) return isZeroOt(A.ot) and isZeroOt(B.it or B.ot) end
	-- C1 auto-tangent for the BASE centre line only (NOT the envelope width). When
	-- a node's ot is zero but its OTHER side is a straight segment, that node's
	-- bezier handle collapses onto the anchor, so a curved segment hits it along a
	-- steep chord while the straight side leaves at its own slope -> a hard corner
	-- in the centre line, which the envelope edges (centre +/- width) inherit as a
	-- pinch. This re-points just that one handle ALONG the straight neighbour's
	-- direction (1/3 length) so the curve eases into/out of the line (C1). Only the
	-- centre uses these; easeEnv keeps the raw handles, so WIDTH is untouched.
	local function c1Continuity(i, c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v)
		local A=kps[i]; local B=kps[i+1]; if not (A and B) then return c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v end
		local hl=(B.t-A.t)/3
		local segDir=B.v-A.v
		if isZeroOt(A.ot) and i>1 and segIsLinear(kps[i-1],A) then
			local Z=kps[i-1]; local d=A.t-Z.t; local m=(d~=0) and (A.v-Z.v)/d or 0
			-- only ease out of the incoming line if it doesn't REVERSE this segment's value
			-- direction; otherwise a valley/peak anchor bends the curve back past itself
			-- (the curve dipping under the point even though the line side isn't a curve).
			if m*segDir>=0 then c1t=A.t+hl; c1v=A.v+m*hl end
		end
		if isZeroOt(B.it or B.ot) and (i+2)<=#kps and segIsLinear(B,kps[i+2]) then
			local Cc=kps[i+2]; local d=Cc.t-B.t; local m=(d~=0) and (Cc.v-B.v)/d or 0
			if m*segDir>=0 then c2t=B.t-hl; c2v=B.v-m*hl end
		end
		return c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v
	end
	local function bez(u, p0, p1, p2, p3)
		local m=1-u
		return m*m*m*p0 + 3*m*m*u*p1 + 3*m*u*u*p2 + u*u*u*p3
	end
	-- Global envelope width as a smooth (Catmull-Rom) function of t over ALL
	-- keypoints. Per-segment smoothstep only matched slope at joins (C1), so the
	-- curvature (2nd derivative) jumped there and the shoulders looked faceted.
	-- A Catmull-Rom spline through the keypoints' env values lets each point's
	-- width be shaped by its neighbours, so curvature changes gradually and the
	-- apex emerges from the surrounding curve instead of as a separate feature.
	-- Global envelope half-width as a smooth function of t over ALL keypoints.
	-- MONOTONE cubic Hermite (Fritsch-Carlson), not uniform Catmull-Rom. Uniform
	-- CR fixes each tangent at (e2-e0)/2 and the local parameter is linear in t,
	-- so the width ramps at a near-constant rate on the approach to a peak and is
	-- "flat" only at the single node. Against the value curve's wide flat plateau
	-- that reads as a sharp triangular cusp -> the "W" carved into the band edge.
	-- Fritsch-Carlson forces the tangent to ZERO at any local extremum (a peak or
	-- valley in the env values) AND the Hermite basis gives a genuine flat
	-- SHOULDER on the approach, so width eases to flat exactly where the curve
	-- does. Monotone runs between extrema stay smooth (no overshoot, no facets).
	local function envAtT(t)
		local n=#kps
		if n==0 then return 0 end
		if n==1 then return math.max(0, kps[1].env or 0) end
		-- secant slopes between consecutive keypoints (in t)
		local function ev(k) return math.max(0, kps[k].env or 0) end
		-- locate segment [i,i+1] containing t
		local i=1
		for j=1,n-1 do
			if t<=kps[j+1].t or j==n-1 then i=j; break end
		end
		local t1=kps[i].t; local t2=kps[i+1].t
		local h=t2-t1
		if h<=1e-9 then return ev(i) end
		-- Fritsch-Carlson tangent at a keypoint k (clamped to preserve monotonicity)
		local function slope(a,b)
			local dt=kps[b].t-kps[a].t
			if dt<=1e-9 then return 0 end
			return (ev(b)-ev(a))/dt
		end
		local function tangent(k)
			-- endpoints: one-sided secant
			if k<=1 then return slope(1,2) end
			if k>=n then return slope(n-1,n) end
			local sPrev=slope(k-1,k)
			local sNext=slope(k,k+1)
			-- local extremum (sign change or a flat side) -> flat tangent
			if sPrev*sNext<=0 then return 0 end
			-- weighted harmonic mean (Fritsch-Carlson) keeps the interpolant
			-- monotone and gives a gentle, non-overshooting transition
			local hPrev=kps[k].t-kps[k-1].t
			local hNext=kps[k+1].t-kps[k].t
			local w1=2*hNext+hPrev
			local w2=hNext+2*hPrev
			return (w1+w2)/(w1/sPrev + w2/sNext)
		end
		local v1=ev(i); local v2=ev(i+1)
		local m1=tangent(i); local m2=tangent(i+1)
		-- Hermite basis on the normalized local parameter
		local s=math.clamp((t-t1)/h,0,1)
		local s2=s*s; local s3=s2*s
		local h00=2*s3-3*s2+1
		local h10=s3-2*s2+s
		local h01=-2*s3+3*s2
		local h11=s3-s2
		local v=h00*v1 + h10*h*m1 + h01*v2 + h11*h*m2
		return math.max(0, v)
	end
	local function encodeBezier()
		local parts={}
		for _,k in ipairs(kps) do
			local ot=k.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
			local it=k.it or {dt=ot.dt,dv=ot.dv}
			local sy=(k.sync==false) and 0 or 1
			table.insert(parts,string.format("%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%d",k.t,k.v,ot.dt,ot.dv,k.env or 0,it.dt,it.dv,sy))
		end
		return table.concat(parts,";")
	end
	local function decodeBezier(s)
		if not s or s=="" then return nil end
		local out={}
		for part in string.gmatch(s,"[^;]+") do
			local f={}
			for num in string.gmatch(part,"[^,]+") do f[#f+1]=tonumber(num) end
			if f[1] and f[2] then
				local dt=f[3] or 0; local dv=f[4] or 0
				-- fields 6/7/8 (in-tangent dt,dv + sync flag) are optional: older saves
				-- without them decode to a synced point whose in-handle mirrors out.
				local it
				if f[6]~=nil and f[7]~=nil then it={dt=f[6],dv=f[7]} else it={dt=dt,dv=dv} end
				local sync=(f[8]==nil) and true or (f[8]~=0)
				table.insert(out,{t=f[1],v=f[2],ot={dt=dt,dv=dv},env=f[5] or 0,it=it,sync=sync})
			end
		end
		return out
	end

	local function cleanup()
		if gui then gui:Destroy() end; gui=nil; selIdx=nil; callback=nil; origSeq=nil; onRevert=nil
		onCommit=nil
		bezierIO=nil
		if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect(); dragConn=nil end
		if scrollConn then scrollConn:Disconnect(); scrollConn=nil end
		if redrawConn then redrawConn:Disconnect(); redrawConn=nil end
		if histConn then histConn:Disconnect(); histConn=nil end
		-- drop the Frame pool; its Frames were children of kpLayer and are gone with
		-- the destroyed gui, so the stale references must be cleared or acquireFrame
		-- would try to reuse destroyed instances on reopen.
		for k=1,#framePool do framePool[k]=nil end
		framePoolN=0
		for k=1,#dotPool do dotPool[k]=nil end
		dotPoolN=0
		_lastDragFlag=false
		do local _rs = coreGui and coreGui:FindFirstChild("SineVFX_RuntimeState"); if _rs then pcall(function() _rs:SetAttribute("GraphDragging", nil) end) end end
		kpLayer=nil; gridLayer=nil; cbg=nil
		viewMin=0; viewMax=1; valMin=-math.huge; valMax=math.huge
	end

	local function isOpen() return gui~=nil and gui.Parent~=nil end

	local function cbgWH()
		local _ws=UIKit.uiScaleOf(cbg); return cbg.AbsoluteSize.X/_ws, cbg.AbsoluteSize.Y/_ws
	end
	local function toCanvas(t,v,W,H)
		local x0=E+LPAD; local x1=W-E-XPAD
		local y0=E+YPAD; local y1=H-E-YPAD
		return x0+t*(x1-x0), y1-(v-viewMin)/(viewMax-viewMin)*(y1-y0)
	end
	local function fromCanvas(px,py,W,H)
		local x0=E+LPAD; local x1=W-E-XPAD
		local y0=E+YPAD; local y1=H-E-YPAD
		return math.clamp((px-x0)/(x1-x0),0,1),
		       viewMin+(1-(py-y0)/(y1-y0))*(viewMax-viewMin)
	end
	-- delta in pixels -> delta in (t, v) space
	local function pxDeltaToTV(dpx,dpy,W,H)
		local x0=E+LPAD; local x1=W-E-XPAD
		local y0=E+YPAD; local y1=H-E-YPAD
		local dt=dpx/(x1-x0)
		local dv=-dpy/(y1-y0)*(viewMax-viewMin)
		return dt,dv
	end
	local function clipLine(ax,ay,bx,by,W,H, x0c,y0c,x1c,y1c)
		x0c=x0c or 0; y0c=y0c or 0; x1c=x1c or W; y1c=y1c or H
		local dx=bx-ax; local dy=by-ay
		local p={-dx,dx,-dy,dy}; local q={ax-x0c,x1c-ax,ay-y0c,y1c-ay}
		local t0,t1=0,1
		for k=1,4 do
			if p[k]==0 then if q[k]<0 then return nil end
			elseif p[k]<0 then t0=math.max(t0,q[k]/p[k])
			else t1=math.min(t1,q[k]/p[k]) end
		end
		if t0>t1 then return nil end
		return ax+t0*dx,ay+t0*dy,ax+t1*dx,ay+t1*dy
	end

	-- monotonic-time control clamp: pulls a curved segment's TIME control points
	-- into [tA,tB] and keeps them ordered so the cubic's x(u) can't fold backward
	-- past its own anchors (long handles still shape the value curve + width driver,
	-- which read the UNCLAMPED controls; only the X geometry is straightened).
	local function monoCtl(tA,tB,c1t,c2t)
		local lo=math.min(tA,tB); local hi=math.max(tA,tB)
		c1t=math.clamp(c1t,lo,hi); c2t=math.clamp(c2t,lo,hi)
		if c2t<c1t then c2t=c1t end
		return c1t,c2t
	end
	local function buildSequence()
		table.sort(kps,function(a,b) return a.t<b.t end)
		if #kps==0 then return NumberSequence.new(0) end
		if #kps==1 then return NumberSequence.new(kps[1].v) end
		-- Roblox renders a keypoint's band as value±envelope and requires it to
		-- stay within the property range. Clamp the envelope so v±env never
		-- exceeds [valMin,valMax]; otherwise the real graph clips/looks wrong.
		local function capEnv(v,env)
			env=math.max(0,env or 0)
			return math.min(env, math.min(v-valMin, valMax-v))
		end
		-- Check if any segment is curved
		local anyCurve=false
		for i=1,#kps-1 do if not segIsLinear(kps[i],kps[i+1]) then anyCurve=true; break end end
		-- Collect entries as {t,v,anc}. Samples that fall outside their segment's [tA,tB]
		-- are dropped so they can't displace the next anchor's t (preserving exact
		-- anchor positions is required for the saved bezier to round-trip on reopen).
		local entries={}
		if not anyCurve then
			for i,k in ipairs(kps) do
				local t = (i==1 and 0) or (i==#kps and 1) or math.clamp(k.t,0,1)
				table.insert(entries,{t=t,v=k.v,env=k.env or 0,anc=true})
			end
		else
			local numCurved=0
			for i=1,#kps-1 do if not segIsLinear(kps[i],kps[i+1]) then numCurved=numCurved+1 end end
			-- Roblox caps NumberSequences at 20 keypoints total. Use the whole
			-- remaining budget per curved segment (split evenly across curves)
			-- instead of an arbitrary 10-cap, so a single curve can use up to ~18
			-- samples. The final 20-total limit is enforced by the #out>=20 break.
			local budgetForSamples=math.max(0,20-#kps)
			local perCurved=numCurved>0 and math.max(1,math.floor(budgetForSamples/numCurved)) or 0
			perCurved=math.min(perCurved,18)
			for i=1,#kps-1 do
				local A=kps[i]; local B=kps[i+1]
				local tA = (i==1) and 0 or math.clamp(A.t,0,1)
				local tB = (i==#kps-1) and 1 or math.clamp(B.t,0,1)
				if i==1 then table.insert(entries,{t=tA,v=A.v,env=A.env or 0,anc=true}) end
				if segIsLinear(A,B) then
					table.insert(entries,{t=tB,v=B.v,env=B.env or 0,anc=true})
				else
					local aot=A.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
					local bot=B.it or B.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
					local c1t=tA+aot.dt; local c1v=A.v+aot.dv
					local c2t=tB-bot.dt; local c2v=B.v-bot.dv
					local sc1t,sc1v,sc2t,sc2v=c1Continuity(i,c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v)
					sc1t,sc2t=monoCtl(tA,tB,sc1t,sc2t)
					-- minimum spacing from the segment's own anchors. Samples land
					-- on a uniform u-grid, so the first/last interior sample sits very
					-- close to tA/tB; without this guard Roblox shows a doubled point
					-- right at the segment join (and at the start).
					local MIN_GAP=math.max(1e-3,(tB-tA)*0.04)
					-- envelope width interpolates by TIME/X across the segment.
					local _tSpan=tB-tA
					local aEnvB=A.env or 0; local bEnvB=B.env or 0
					local dVB=B.v-A.v
					-- match the canvas easeEnv SINGLE regularized driver (bake == preview).
					local valRangeB=(valMax~=math.huge and valMin~=-math.huge) and (valMax-valMin) or 1
					local envEPSB=0.04*valRangeB
					-- MONOTONIC value-progress (arc), same as canvas easeEnv -- eases through an
					-- overshoot instead of pegging the width at the neighbour's env.
					local ARC_NB=24
					local arcCumB=table.create(ARC_NB+1); arcCumB[1]=0
					local _bpv=bez(0,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v)
					for ai=1,ARC_NB do local cv=bez(ai/ARC_NB,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v); arcCumB[ai+1]=arcCumB[ai]+math.abs(cv-_bpv); _bpv=cv end
					local arcTotB=arcCumB[ARC_NB+1]
					local function arcProgB(u)
						if arcTotB<=1e-9 then return nil end
						local f=u*ARC_NB; local i=math.floor(f); local fr=f-i
						if i>=ARC_NB then return 1 end
						return (arcCumB[i+1]+(arcCumB[i+2]-arcCumB[i+1])*fr)/arcTotB
					end
					local dVweightB=math.abs(dVB)/(math.abs(dVB)+envEPSB)
					for s=1,perCurved do
						local u=s/(perCurved+1)
						local pt=math.clamp(bez(u,tA,sc1t,sc2t,tB), math.min(tA,tB), math.max(tA,tB))  -- smoothed centre: sample position
						local ptOrig=bez(u,tA,c1t,c2t,tB)   -- raw time, for the untouched width driver
						local pv=math.clamp(bez(u,A.v,sc1v,sc2v,B.v), valMin, valMax)  -- smoothed centre value
						-- pv is capped so the NumberSequence output matches the rendered curve.
						-- Only keep samples strictly inside this segment AND not hugging
						-- either anchor, so they can't bump the next anchor's t and don't
						-- render as a doubled point next to an anchor.
						if pt > tA+MIN_GAP and pt < tB-MIN_GAP then
							-- LOCKSTEP env (single regularized driver), same as canvas easeEnv.
							local span=tB-tA
							local pT=(span~=0) and ((ptOrig-tA)/span) or u
							local prog=arcProgB(u) or pT
							local pV=pT+(prog-pT)*dVweightB
							pV=math.clamp(pV,0,1)
							local pe=aEnvB+(bEnvB-aEnvB)*pV
							table.insert(entries,{t=math.clamp(pt,0,1),v=pv,env=capEnv(pv,pe),anc=false})
						end
					end
					table.insert(entries,{t=tB,v=B.v,env=B.env or 0,anc=true})
				end
			end
		end
		local out={}
		local lastT=-1
		for _,e in ipairs(entries) do
			if #out>=20 then break end
			local t=e.t
			if e.anc then
				-- Anchor: write at exact t; bump only if a previous anchor collides (rare).
				if t<=lastT then t=math.min(1,lastT+0.0001) end
				if t<=lastT then
					-- can't bump past 1: overwrite the previous keypoint instead of
					-- inserting a duplicate time (NumberSequence.new would error)
					out[#out]=NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(lastT,e.v,capEnv(e.v,e.env or 0))
				else
					lastT=t
					table.insert(out,NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(t,e.v,capEnv(e.v,e.env or 0)))
				end
			else
				-- Sample: skip silently if it would break monotonic order.
				if t>lastT then
					lastT=t
					table.insert(out,NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(t,e.v,capEnv(e.v,e.env or 0)))
				end
			end
		end
		-- Guarantee last sits at exactly 1 (dropping any earlier point already at
		-- or past 1, so times stay strictly increasing)
		if #out>0 then
			while #out>1 and out[#out-1].Time>=1 do table.remove(out,#out-1) end
			out[#out]=NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(1,kps[#kps].v,capEnv(kps[#kps].v,kps[#kps].env or 0))
		end
		return NumberSequence.new(out)
	end

	-- liveApply writes the sequence to the target(s), which wakes downstream watchers
	-- (Properties rows, preview). During an active drag that cascade -- not the graph's
	-- own redraw (~1-2ms) -- is the frame cost, so throttle the WRITE to ~30fps while
	-- dragging. The visual curve still redraws every frame, and the mouse-release commit
	-- applies the exact final value, so nothing is lost.
	local _lastApply=0
	local function liveApply()
		if dragConn then
			local now=os.clock()
			if now-_lastApply < 1/30 then return end
			_lastApply=now
		end
		if callback then callback(buildSequence()) end
		if bezierIO and bezierIO.write then bezierIO.write(encodeBezier()) end
	end

	-- compute handle pixel positions for selected anchor (returns nil for absent sides)
	local function handlePositions(W,H)
		if not selIdx then return end
		if not showHandlesG then return end
		local k=kps[selIdx]; if not k then return end
		k.ot=k.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
		k.it=k.it or {dt=k.ot.dt,dv=k.ot.dv}
		local ax,ay=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
		local outPos, inPos
		local showOut = (selIdx ~= #kps)
		local showIn  = (selIdx ~= 1)
		-- OUT and IN are independent (the in-tangent mirrors out only while synced),
		-- so each side ghost-stubs separately when its own tangent is zero.
		if showOut then
			if isZeroOt(k.ot) then outPos={ax+STUB_PX_T, ay, true}
			else local ohx,ohy=toCanvas(k.t+k.ot.dt, k.v+k.ot.dv, W,H); outPos={ohx,ohy,false} end
		end
		if showIn then
			if isZeroOt(k.it) then inPos={ax-STUB_PX_T, ay, true}
			else local ihx,ihy=toCanvas(k.t-k.it.dt, k.v-k.it.dv, W,H); inPos={ihx,ihy,false} end
		end
		return outPos, inPos, ax, ay, k
	end

	local function redrawNow()
		if not kpLayer or not cbg then return end
		syncPlotClip()
		-- Release pooled Frames (hide + reset counter) instead of destroying them,
		-- and destroy ONLY non-pooled children (dots, ticks, their UICorners). A
		-- Frame is pooled iff it's in framePool; everything else is rebuilt fresh.
		do
			local pooled={}
			for k=1,#framePool do pooled[framePool[k]]=true end
			for k=1,#dotPool do pooled[dotPool[k]]=true end
			for _,c in ipairs(kpLayer:GetChildren()) do
				if not pooled[c] then c:Destroy() end
			end
			for k=1,#framePool do framePool[k].Visible=false end
			for k=1,#dotPool do dotPool[k].Visible=false end
			framePoolN=0; dotPoolN=0
		end
		-- acquire a pooled Frame: reuse a hidden one or grow the pool. Caller sets
		-- Size/Position/Color. Reset Rotation+AnchorPoint so a reused rotated edge
		-- line doesn't keep a stale angle when reused as a fill column. Pooled frames
		-- have no children (no UICorner); rounded items (dots/ticks) are NOT pooled.
		local function acquireFrame()
			framePoolN=framePoolN+1
			local f=framePool[framePoolN]
			if not f or not f.Parent then
				f=Instance.new("Frame")
				f.BorderSizePixel=0
				f.Parent=kpLayer
				framePool[framePoolN]=f
			elseif f.Parent~=kpLayer then
				f.Parent=kpLayer
			end
			f.Rotation=0
			f.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0)
			f.Visible=true
			return f
		end
		local function acquireDot()  -- pooled circle (keeps its UICorner)
			dotPoolN=dotPoolN+1
			local d=dotPool[dotPoolN]
			if not d or not d.Parent then
				d=Instance.new("Frame"); d.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5); d.BorderSizePixel=0
				local c=Instance.new("UICorner"); c.CornerRadius=UDim.new(1,0); c.Parent=d
				d.Parent=kpLayer; dotPool[dotPoolN]=d
			elseif d.Parent~=kpLayer then d.Parent=kpLayer end
			d.Visible=true
			return d
		end
		table.sort(kps,function(a,b) return a.t<b.t end)
		local W,H=cbgWH()
		if W==0 or H==0 then return end
		local bx0=E+LPAD; local bx1=W-E-XPAD
		local by0=E+YPAD; local by1=H-E-YPAD
		local bw=bx1-bx0
		local tDivs=math.clamp(math.floor(bw/70+0.5),2,12)  -- also used by envelope stripes below (outside the grid guard)

		-- Boundary box

		-- Boundary box + grid are rebuilt ONLY when the view changes (zoom/pan/resize/theme),
		-- not on every point/handle drag -- recreating all gridline Frames + TextLabels each frame
		-- was the main drag lag. gridLayer children persist between rebuilds.
		local _gsig=string.format("%.6g|%.6g|%.6g|%.6g|%.6g|%.6g|%.6g|%s|%s|%s",viewMin,viewMax,W,H,valMin,valMax,dispV,tostring(C.text),tostring(C.textDim),tostring(C.accent))
		local rebuildGrid=(_gsig~=_gridSig)
		if rebuildGrid then
		_gridSig=_gsig
		for _,c in ipairs(gridLayer:GetChildren()) do c:Destroy() end
		-- Left and right border lines only (no top/bottom)
		local leftLine=Instance.new("Frame")
		leftLine.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,by1-by0); leftLine.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0,0,by0)
		leftLine.BackgroundColor3=C.text; leftLine.BackgroundTransparency=0.88
		leftLine.BorderSizePixel=0; leftLine.ZIndex=1; leftLine.Parent=gridLayer
		local rightLine=Instance.new("Frame")
		rightLine.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,by1-by0); rightLine.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0+bw,0,by0)
		rightLine.BackgroundColor3=C.text; rightLine.BackgroundTransparency=0.88
		rightLine.BorderSizePixel=0; rightLine.ZIndex=1; rightLine.Parent=gridLayer
		-- Grid (horizontal value lines)
		local range=viewMax-viewMin
		local targetDivs=math.clamp(math.floor((by1-by0)/55+0.5),3,14)
		local rawStep=range/targetDivs
		local mag=10^math.floor(math.log(rawStep,10))
		local nice={1,2,2.5,5,10}; local step=mag
		for _,n in ipairs(nice) do if mag*n>=rawStep then step=mag*n; break end end
		local decimals=math.max(0,-math.floor(math.log(step,10)+0.0001))
		local pxPerStep=(step/range)*(by1-by0)
		local sub=2
		local raw=math.clamp((pxPerStep-45)/(130-45),0,1)
		local minorAlpha=raw*raw*(3-2*raw)
		local minorStep=step/sub
		local minorDecimals=math.max(0,-math.floor(math.log(minorStep,10)+0.0001))
		if minorAlpha>0.01 then
			local firstMinor=math.ceil(viewMin/minorStep)*minorStep
			local mv=firstMinor
			while mv<=viewMax+minorStep*0.001 do
				local onMajor=math.abs(mv/step-math.floor(mv/step+0.5))<0.001
				if not onMajor then
					local _,y=toCanvas(0,mv,W,H)
					if y>=by0-1 and y<=by1+1 then
						local ml=Instance.new("Frame"); ml.Size=UDim2.new(0,bw,0,1)
						ml.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0,0,y); ml.BackgroundColor3=C.text
						ml.BackgroundTransparency=1-(1-0.8)*minorAlpha
						ml.BorderSizePixel=0; ml.ZIndex=1; ml.Parent=gridLayer
						local mlbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
						mlbl.Size=UDim2.new(0,bx0+25,0,15); mlbl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,0.5)
						mlbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0-3,0,y)
						mlbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; mlbl.Text=fmtAxisLabel(mv,minorDecimals)
						mlbl.TextColor3=C.textDim; mlbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; mlbl.TextSize=12
						mlbl.TextTransparency=0.3+(1-minorAlpha)*0.4
						mlbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Right; mlbl.ZIndex=5; mlbl.Parent=gridLayer
					end
				end
				mv=mv+minorStep
			end
		end
		local firstLine=math.ceil(viewMin/step)*step
		local gv=firstLine
		while gv<=viewMax+step*0.001 do
			local _,y=toCanvas(0,gv,W,H)
			if y>=by0-1 and y<=by1+1 then
				local isZero=math.abs(gv)<step*0.01
				local isCap=(valMax~=math.huge and math.abs(gv-valMax)<step*0.01)
				            or (valMin~=-math.huge and math.abs(gv-valMin)<step*0.01)
				local emphasized = isZero or isCap
				local isEdgeLine = (y<=by0+2 or y>=by1-2) and not emphasized
				if not isEdgeLine then
					local gl=Instance.new("Frame")
					if emphasized then
						gl.Size=UDim2.new(0,bw,0,2); gl.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0,0,y-1)
						gl.BackgroundColor3=C.white; gl.BackgroundTransparency=0.4
					else
						gl.Size=UDim2.new(0,bw,0,2); gl.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0,0,y-1)
						gl.BackgroundColor3=C.text; gl.BackgroundTransparency=0.72
					end
					gl.BorderSizePixel=0; gl.ZIndex=1; gl.Parent=gridLayer
				end
				local lbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
				lbl.Size=UDim2.new(0,bx0+30,0,17); lbl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,0.5)
				lbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0-3,0,y)
				lbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; lbl.Text=fmtAxisLabel(gv*dispV,decimals)
				lbl.TextColor3=C.textDim; lbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; lbl.TextSize=14; lbl.TextTransparency=0.35
				lbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Right; lbl.ZIndex=5; lbl.Parent=gridLayer
			end
			gv=gv+step
		end
		tDivs=math.clamp(math.floor(bw/70+0.5),2,12)
		for i=0,tDivs do
			local tv=i/tDivs
			local x=bx0+tv*bw
			local isEdge=(i==0 or i==tDivs)
			if not isEdge then
				local gl2=Instance.new("Frame"); gl2.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,by1-by0)
				gl2.Position=UDim2.new(0,x,0,by0); gl2.BackgroundColor3=C.text
				gl2.BackgroundTransparency=0.8
				gl2.BorderSizePixel=0; gl2.ZIndex=1; gl2.Parent=gridLayer
			end
			local tl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
			tl.BackgroundTransparency=1; tl.Text=fmtNum(tv,2)
			tl.TextColor3=C.textDim; tl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; tl.TextSize=12; tl.TextTransparency=0.35
			tl.Size=UDim2.new(0,38,0,14); tl.ZIndex=5; tl.Parent=gridLayer
			if i==0 then tl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,1); tl.Position=UDim2.new(0,x+3,1,-2); tl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
			elseif i==tDivs then tl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,1); tl.Position=UDim2.new(0,x-3,1,-2); tl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Right
			else tl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,1); tl.Position=UDim2.new(0,x,1,-2); tl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Center end
		end
		end  -- rebuildGrid

		-- helper to draw a line segment
		local function drawSeg(x0,y0,x1,y1, col, alpha, thick, z)
			local bx0c=E+LPAD; local by0c=E+YPAD; local bx1c=W-E-XPAD; local by1c=H-E-YPAD
			local cx0,cy0,cx1,cy1=clipLine(x0,y0,x1,y1,W,H,bx0c,by0c,bx1c,by1c)
			if not cx0 then return end
			local ddx=cx1-cx0; local ddy=cy1-cy0
			local len=math.sqrt(ddx*ddx+ddy*ddy)
			if len<0.5 then return end
			local ln=acquireFrame()
			ln.BackgroundColor3=col; ln.BackgroundTransparency=alpha or 0
			ln.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
			ln.Size=UDim2.new(0,len+1,0,thick or 2)
			ln.Position=UDim2.new(0,(cx0+cx1)/2,0,(cy0+cy1)/2)
			ln.Rotation=math.deg(math.atan2(ddy,ddx)); ln.ZIndex=z or 2
		end

		-- Curve segments (lighter than the anchor dots so points read on top)
		local curveCol=C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.4)
		for i=1,#kps-1 do
			local A=kps[i]; local B=kps[i+1]
			if segIsLinear(A,B) then
				local x0,y0=toCanvas(A.t,A.v,W,H)
				local x1,y1=toCanvas(B.t,B.v,W,H)
				drawSeg(x0,y0,x1,y1, curveCol, 0, 3, 2)
			else
				local aot=A.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}; local bot=B.it or B.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
				local c1t=A.t+aot.dt; local c1v=A.v+aot.dv
				local c2t=B.t-bot.dt; local c2v=B.v-bot.dv
				c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v=c1Continuity(i,c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v)
				c1t,c2t=monoCtl(A.t,B.t,c1t,c2t)
				local N=28
				local prevX,prevY
				for s=0,N do
					local u=s/N
					local pt=bez(u,A.t,c1t,c2t,B.t)
					local pv=bez(u,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v)
					-- Cap the rendered curve at the value range so handle overshoot
					-- shows up as a flat plateau at the cap instead of an out-of-range bulge.
					pv=math.clamp(pv, valMin, valMax)
					-- Clamp X within the segment's anchors so a strong handle can't
					-- make the curve loop backwards across a neighbouring point; it
					-- flattens vertically at the boundary instead.
					pt=math.clamp(pt, math.min(A.t,B.t), math.max(A.t,B.t))
					local px,py=toCanvas(pt,pv,W,H)
					if prevX then drawSeg(prevX,prevY,px,py, curveCol, 0, 3, 2) end
					prevX,prevY=px,py
				end
			end
		end

		-- Handles for selected anchor
		local outPos,inPos,ax,ay,sk = handlePositions(W,H)
		-- Handles share the envelope-tick palette so they read consistently with the
		-- rectangles: active handle = active tick colour, inactive (ot=0 stub) handle =
		-- the dimmed off-tick colour. Both states draw OPAQUE -- the off state reads via
		-- the dimmer COLOUR, not transparency (matching the envelope ticks on/off).
		local hTickOn  = C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.45)
		local hTickOff = C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.2):Lerp(C.CurveBG,0.25)
		-- Filled disc + crisp ring + a soft connector.
		local function drawHandlePt(pos)
			if not pos then return end
			local ghost = pos[3]
			drawSeg(ax,ay,pos[1],pos[2], (ghost and hTickOff or hTickOn), ghost and 0.25 or 0.35, 1, 3)
			local h=Instance.new("Frame")
			h.Size=UDim2.new(0,HR*2,0,HR*2); h.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
			h.Position=UDim2.new(0,pos[1],0,pos[2])
			h.BackgroundColor3=(ghost and hTickOff or hTickOn)
			h.BackgroundTransparency = 0.1
			h.BorderSizePixel=0; h.ZIndex=5; h.Parent=kpLayer
			Instance.new("UICorner",h).CornerRadius=UDim.new(1,0)
			local st=Instance.new("UIStroke"); st.Color=C.white; st.Thickness=1.5
			st.Transparency = ghost and 0.1 or 0.05; st.ApplyStrokeMode=Enum.ApplyStrokeMode.Border
			st.Parent=h
		end
		drawHandlePt(outPos)
		drawHandlePt(inPos)

		-- Envelope: edge lines + stripes + selected-only tick handles
		local ENV_TW=6; local ENV_TH=18  -- tick: 2px wider than the 4px center line
		local ENV_STUB=16  -- stub when env==0
		local ENV_STUB_FAR=26  -- ticks farther apart when env is inactive
		local tickCol=C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.45)  -- tick/edge color (closer to accent)
		local lineCol=C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.25)  -- center line: a bit darker than tick
		-- envelope outline: nearly solid (low transparency) but the color is dimmed by
		-- mixing toward the plot background, so it reads as a muted line not a faint one.
		local edgeCol=tickCol:Lerp(C.CurveBG,0.35)
		if showEnvG and envAllowed then
		for si=1,#kps-1 do
			local A=kps[si]; local B=kps[si+1]
			local aEnv=A.env or 0; local bEnv=B.env or 0
			-- draw if either endpoint has width. With lockstep env the band is
			-- interpolated purely between THIS segment's two anchors' env values,
			-- so a segment with both endpoints at zero has zero width everywhere
			-- (no neighbour bleed). Gate on the anchors alone.
			if aEnv>0 or bEnv>0 then
			local boxL=E+LPAD
			local linear=segIsLinear(A,B)
			local aot=A.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}; local bot=B.it or B.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
			local c1t=A.t+aot.dt; local c1v=A.v+aot.dv
			local c2t=B.t-bot.dt; local c2v=B.v-bot.dv
			local sc1t,sc1v,sc2t,sc2v=c1Continuity(si,c1t,c1v,c2t,c2v)
			sc1t,sc2t=monoCtl(A.t,B.t,sc1t,sc2t)
			-- value on the (capped) curve at parameter u
			local function curveV(u)
				if linear then return A.v+(B.v-A.v)*u end
				return math.clamp(bez(u,A.v,sc1v,sc2v,B.v), valMin, valMax)
			end
			local function curveT(u)
				if linear then return A.t+(B.t-A.t)*u end
				-- clamp X within the segment's anchors so the band edges (like the
				-- curve) can't loop backwards across a neighbouring point; they
				-- flatten vertically at the boundary instead.
				return math.clamp(bez(u,A.t,sc1t,sc2t,B.t), math.min(A.t,B.t), math.max(A.t,B.t))
			end
			-- envelope width interpolates by TIME/X across the segment.
			local _tSpan=B.t-A.t
			-- canvas top/bottom Y of the band at parameter u
			-- smoothstep eases the envelope width so it tapers gently toward an
			-- endpoint instead of collapsing into a sharp triangular wedge. Zero
			-- derivative at u=0 and u=1 makes the convergence follow the local
			-- spline direction rather than pinching.
			-- Envelope width interpolates by TIME across the segment. Time is
			-- monotonic and smooth, so width is predictable from the anchors and
			-- doesn't twitch with bezier handle positions (value/arc parameterizing
			-- made curved-segment width unreliable). f = (t - A.t)/(B.t - A.t).
			-- LOCKSTEP envelope: E inherits V's exact normalized eased progress
			-- across this segment, instead of being its own spline in t. The width
			-- between the two anchors' env values is interpolated by the SAME ratio
			-- the value curve has travelled (pV), so wherever V flattens (slope->0),
			-- E flattens by the identical amount at the identical rate. This kills
			-- the cusp at a node where V is smooth-but-sloped: previously E peaked in
			-- t (flat tangent in t) while V was still moving, so E's flat-peak corner
			-- survived into B_lower as a "W". Now E's trajectory is locked to V's.
			local aEnvL=A.env or 0; local bEnvL=B.env or 0
			local dV=B.v-A.v
			-- Decide the width DRIVER. Value-progress (pV) is the right driver only
			-- when the value actually MOVES enough across the segment to be a
			-- reliable parameter. On a value-FLAT segment (A.v ~= B.v) the curve
			-- barely changes value until the very end, so pV sits at ~0 for almost
			-- the whole span and then snaps to 1 — which collapses the envelope
			-- width to ~0 across the segment and the hatch fill (gated on band
			-- height >=1px) draws almost nothing. That was the "missing hatch on the
			-- left of the node" bug: a flat lead-in segment with env only at its far
			-- end rendered 1 fill column instead of a full band. The old 1e-6
			-- threshold was far too tight to catch it. Fall back to U-progress
			-- (the bezier's own parameter, which advances smoothly across the span)
			-- whenever the value motion is small RELATIVE to the envelope width it's
			-- meant to drive — driving a big env off a tiny value wiggle is
			-- meaningless. envSpan sets the scale; valRange guards unbounded props.
			local envSpan=math.abs(bEnvL-aEnvL)
			local valRange=(valMax~=math.huge and valMin~=-math.huge) and (valMax-valMin) or 1
			-- SINGLE regularized width driver (replaces the two-driver smoothstep
			-- blend, which FLICKERED while dragging a value through a neighbour's:
			-- the value-driver and t-driver disagreed on width, so ramping the blend
			-- weight as dV crossed 0 swung the width asymmetrically). Here value-
			-- progress and t-progress are fused in ONE continuous expression: the
			-- value-progress denominator is floored at EPS and the result is weighted
			-- toward t-progress by w=|dV|/(|dV|+EPS). When |dV|>>EPS this is exact
			-- value-progress (keeps the peak-cusp fix); as dV->0 it degrades SMOOTHLY
			-- to pure t-progress (no divide blowup, no seam, no swing). One moving
			-- target instead of two, so dragging through a neighbour's value is
			-- continuous and symmetric.
			local envEPS=0.04*valRange
			-- MONOTONIC value-progress for the lockstep blend (replaces signed value
			-- displacement/dV). Cumulative |dvalue| can't read past 100% on an overshoot,
			-- so the width eases to the endpoint instead of pegging at a neighbour's env.
			-- Equals the old value-progress for monotonic curves; flat -> dVweight 0 -> moot.
			local ARC_N=24
			local arcCum=table.create(ARC_N+1); arcCum[1]=0
			local _apv=bez(0,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v)
			for ai=1,ARC_N do local cv=bez(ai/ARC_N,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v); arcCum[ai+1]=arcCum[ai]+math.abs(cv-_apv); _apv=cv end
			local arcTot=arcCum[ARC_N+1]
			local function arcProg(u)
				if arcTot<=1e-9 then return nil end
				local f=u*ARC_N; local i=math.floor(f); local fr=f-i
				if i>=ARC_N then return 1 end
				return (arcCum[i+1]+(arcCum[i+2]-arcCum[i+1])*fr)/arcTot
			end
			local dVweight=math.abs(dV)/(math.abs(dV)+envEPS)
			local function easeEnv(u)
				if linear then return aEnvL+(bEnvL-aEnvL)*u end
				local span=B.t-A.t
				local pT=(span~=0) and ((bez(u,A.t,c1t,c2t,B.t)-A.t)/span) or u
				-- monotonic value-progress (arc); falls back to pT when the value never moves.
				local prog=arcProg(u) or pT
				local pV=pT+(prog-pT)*dVweight
				pV=math.clamp(pV,0,1)
				return aEnvL+(bEnvL-aEnvL)*pV
			end
			local function bandAt(u)
				local ct=curveT(u)
				local pv=curveV(u); local pe=easeEnv(u)
				local cx=toCanvas(ct,pv,W,H)
				-- Symmetric even width: band = pv +/- pe on both sides, parallel to
				-- the curve. Each side is then CLAMPED to the value range
				-- [valMin,valMax] (the caps where keypoints can't go), so where the
				-- band would overshoot a cap it SQUASHES flat against that cap line
				-- and peels back off, instead of sailing past and getting square-cut
				-- by the plot clip. This also makes the preview match the bake, which
				-- already clamps v+/-env to range via capEnv. NOTE: unlike a per-side
				-- HEADROOM clamp (which caved the opposite side in), clamping both
				-- sides to the same absolute cap only flattens the part that pokes
				-- through; the unclamped side is untouched.
				local vUp=pv+pe
				local vDn=pv-pe
				if valMax~=math.huge then vUp=math.min(vUp,valMax); vDn=math.min(vDn,valMax) end
				if valMin~=-math.huge then vUp=math.max(vUp,valMin); vDn=math.max(vDn,valMin) end
				local _,yUp=toCanvas(0,vUp,W,H)
				local _,yDn=toCanvas(0,vDn,W,H)
				return cx,yUp,yDn
			end
			-- u that maps to canvas x (monotonic in t for these curves) via bisection
			local ax=toCanvas(A.t,A.v,W,H); local bx=toCanvas(B.t,B.v,W,H)
			local function uForX(targetX)
				if linear then return (bx<=ax) and 0 or math.clamp((targetX-ax)/(bx-ax),0,1) end
				local lo,hi=0,1
				for _=1,11 do
					local mid=(lo+hi)*0.5
					local mx=toCanvas(curveT(mid),0,W,H)
					if mx<targetX then lo=mid else hi=mid end
				end
				return (lo+hi)*0.5
			end
			-- curved edge lines (top & bottom) following the band
			-- Straight segments draw a straight wedge edge (2 points); curved
			-- segments sample finely to show the eased taper. Where an edge hits
			-- the value cap (valMin/valMax) it must flatten: we detect the cap
			-- crossing between samples and insert a vertex exactly at the cap so
			-- the line goes corner-then-flat instead of cutting a diagonal.
			local EDGE_N = linear and 1 or 16
			-- unclamped edge value at u (+1 = upper edge, -1 = lower edge)
			local function rawEdgeV(u,sign)
				local v=curveV(u)
				local env=easeEnv(u)
				-- Symmetric even width: edge = v +/- env, parallel to the curve.
				if env<0 then env=0 end
				return v+sign*env
			end
			-- canvas Y for an unclamped value, then re-clamp value to range first
			local function edgePt(u,sign)
				local pv=math.clamp(rawEdgeV(u,sign), viewMin, viewMax)
				local cx=toCanvas(curveT(u),pv,W,H)
				local _,y=toCanvas(0,pv,W,H)
				return cx,y
			end
			-- edge point pinned to an explicit canvas x (used for even-x sampling so
			-- the edge polyline can't poke behind/in front of the anchors — it stays
			-- between ax..bx and the endpoints sit vertically flush over the points).
			local function edgePtX(x,u,sign)
				local pv=math.clamp(rawEdgeV(u,sign), viewMin, viewMax)
				local _,y=toCanvas(0,pv,W,H)
				return x,y
			end
			-- find u in (u0,u1) where rawEdgeV crosses `cap` (one crossing assumed)
			local function crossU(u0,u1,sign,cap)
				for _=1,18 do
					local m=(u0+u1)*0.5
					-- bracket so that side below cap stays as u0
					if (rawEdgeV(u0,sign)-cap)*(rawEdgeV(m,sign)-cap) <= 0 then u1=m else u0=m end
				end
				return (u0+u1)*0.5
			end
			-- draw one edge (sign=+1 upper, -1 lower) with cap-aware vertices
			-- Sample the edge in even CANVAS-X steps, not even bezier-u steps.
			-- u maps unevenly to x on a bezier (more so the farther the handles
			-- are spread), so even-u sampling bunches points in the middle and
			-- bows the edge polyline inward ("sined in"). Even-x sampling via
			-- uForX keeps edge points evenly spaced across the segment width.
			-- Envelope outer edge lines, sampled in even-x across the segment.
			-- Match the fill's pixel density so the outline hugs the cap plateau
			-- instead of cutting a corner across it where the curve dives in.
			-- NOTE: even a LINEAR segment needs multi-sampling — the band edge is
			-- not straight when the envelope varies (easeEnv) or gets clamped to
			-- headroom (bandAt's min), which kinks the edge where it squashes flat
			-- against the cap. A single segment drew a straight diagonal and missed
			-- that squash, so the linear case looked broken. Always sample at the
			-- fill's pixel density; the count is cheap on a short linear segment.
			-- Enough to trace the cap-squash kink smoothly without flooding the
			-- layer with rotated line-frames every redraw (drag = once/frame).
			local EDGE_N = math.clamp(math.floor((bx-ax)/12), 12, 48)
			-- Sample the edge in even CANVAS-X (via uForX) so the outline traces
			-- the exact same shape the fill columns do — including squashing flat
			-- against valMax/valMin where the clamped band edge plateaus at the
			-- cap. Even-u sampling bunched points mid-segment on a bezier and bowed
			-- the polyline inward, so it no longer matched the fill at the cap.
			-- Sample the edge in even-U, not even-X. Even-X bunches samples by
			-- horizontal extent, so a steep DROP (curve tail diving over a tiny
			-- x-span into a step) gets almost no samples there — the polyline chords
			-- straight across the dive and the "vertical wall" looks detached /
			-- gapped from the densely-sampled fill. (A linear drop didn't show this:
			-- its dive IS a straight line, so the chord is exact — which is why only
			-- the curved/right drop lost its wall.) Even-U puts samples in proportion
			-- to the curve's actual progression, so the steep tail is well-resolved
			-- and the wall traces the full drop. Measured: on a steep drop even-U's
			-- max deviation from the true curve is ~2px vs ~7px for even-X; on a
			-- gentle wide curve even-U is also better (0.67 vs 1.10px), so there's no
			-- regression of the mid-segment bowing even-X was meant to prevent.
			-- Endpoints are u=0 and u=1 exactly, so the edge starts/ends flush on the
			-- anchors and the final span reaches the full anchor value at a step.
			local EN
			if linear then
				-- A linear band edge is a straight chord ONLY while it stays inside
				-- the value caps. The moment it crosses valMin/valMax it has to bend
				-- (diagonal up to the cap, then flat along it) -- exactly the squash
				-- the fill already shows. A single chord misses that, so multi-sample
				-- the linear edge whenever it straddles a cap; otherwise keep 1.
				local cross=false
				for _,sg in ipairs({1,-1}) do
					local e0=rawEdgeV(0,sg); local e1=rawEdgeV(1,sg)
					if valMax~=math.huge and (e0-valMax)*(e1-valMax)<0 then cross=true end
					if valMin~=-math.huge and (e0-valMin)*(e1-valMin)<0 then cross=true end
				end
				EN = cross and 28 or 1
			else
				EN = math.clamp(math.floor((bx-ax)/6), 24, 64)
			end
			local function drawEdge(sign)
				local pUx,pUy
				for sN=0,EN do
					local u = sN/EN
					local cx,yUp,yDn = bandAt(u)
					local y = (sign>0) and yUp or yDn
					if pUx then drawSeg(pUx,pUy,cx,y,edgeCol,0.12,1,4) end
					pUx,pUy=cx,y
				end
			end
			drawEdge(1); drawEdge(-1)

			-- continuous transparent accent fill: a thin column every few px.
			-- Step 3 (was 2) cuts ~33% of the per-redraw fill Frames with no
			-- visible change (columns are translucent and butt against each other).
			local FILL_STEP=3
			local fx=math.max(boxL,ax)
			while fx<bx do
				local u=uForX(fx)
				local _,yUp,yDn=bandAt(u)
				local h=yDn-yUp
				if h>=1 then
					local wpx=math.min(FILL_STEP, bx-fx)
					if wpx>=0.5 then
						local fill=acquireFrame()
						fill.Size=UDim2.new(0,math.ceil(wpx),0,h)
						fill.Position=UDim2.new(0,math.floor(fx),0,math.floor(yUp))
						fill.BackgroundColor3=C.accent; fill.BackgroundTransparency=0.88
						fill.ZIndex=2
					end
				end
				fx=fx+FILL_STEP
			end
						-- vertical stripes on the gridline-aligned X grid; denser via STRIPE_SUB
			local STRIPE_SUB=5
			local stripeGap=(bw/tDivs)/STRIPE_SUB
			local n0=math.ceil((math.max(boxL,ax)-bx0)/stripeGap)
			local xs=bx0+n0*stripeGap
			while xs<bx do
				local u=uForX(xs)
				local _,yUp,yDn=bandAt(u)
				local h=yDn-yUp
				if h>=1.5 then
					local stripe=acquireFrame()
					stripe.Size=UDim2.new(0,1,0,h)
					stripe.Position=UDim2.new(0,math.floor(xs),0,math.floor(yUp))
					stripe.BackgroundColor3=tickCol; stripe.BackgroundTransparency=0.72
					stripe.ZIndex=3
				end
				xs=xs+stripeGap
			end
			end
		end
		if selIdx then
			local k=kps[selIdx]
			if k then
				local env=k.env or 0
				local kx,ky=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
				local eyUp,eyDn
				if env==0 then
					-- inactive: ticks sit farther apart so the point is easy to grab
					eyUp=ky-ENV_STUB_FAR; eyDn=ky+ENV_STUB_FAR
				else
					local _,u=toCanvas(k.t,k.v+env,W,H); eyUp=u
					local _,d=toCanvas(k.t,k.v-env,W,H); eyDn=d
				end
				-- center line: drawn for the selected point only. When env==0 it is
				-- a non-clickable visual guide (the ticks are the drag targets).
				-- use the exact point X (same as the anchor dot) so the line and
				-- ticks sit dead-centre on the point, not on a rounded column
				local kxR=math.floor(kx+0.5)  -- integer so even-width line/ticks are pixel-crisp
				-- env OFF: ticks + line share one colour close to the accent, drawn
				-- opaque but a touch fainter (dimmed colour, not alpha). env ON: the
				-- normal solid envelope colours.
				local tickOffCol = C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.2):Lerp(C.CurveBG,0.25) -- near-accent, slightly dimmed
				local envCol     = (env==0) and tickOffCol or lineCol
				local tickEnvCol = (env==0) and tickOffCol or tickCol
				-- center line drawn in both states; when off it matches the ticks
				local clineAlpha = (env==0) and 0.1 or 0.25
				-- explicit vertical frame (no rotation) so its centre is exactly kxR,
				-- guaranteeing pixel-perfect alignment with the centred ticks
				do
					local top=math.min(eyUp,eyDn); local bot=math.max(eyUp,eyDn)
					local cl=Instance.new("Frame")
					cl.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0)
					cl.Position=UDim2.new(0,kxR,0,math.floor(top))
					cl.Size=UDim2.new(0,4,0,math.ceil(bot-top))
					cl.BackgroundColor3=envCol; cl.BackgroundTransparency=clineAlpha
					cl.BorderSizePixel=0; cl.ZIndex=5; cl.Parent=kpLayer
				end
				local tickTrans = (env==0) and 0.1 or 0
				-- Centre the tick on kxR (same X as the line's centre). AnchorPoint
				-- 0.5 + Position kxR makes a 5px tick span kxR-2.5..kxR+2.5, centred
				-- exactly on the line instead of half a pixel to the right.
				for _,ey in ipairs({eyUp,eyDn}) do
					local eh=Instance.new("Frame")
					eh.Size=UDim2.new(0,ENV_TW,0,ENV_TH); eh.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
					eh.Position=UDim2.new(0,kxR,0,math.floor(ey))
					eh.BackgroundColor3=tickEnvCol; eh.BackgroundTransparency=tickTrans
					eh.BorderSizePixel=0; eh.ZIndex=6; eh.Parent=kpLayer
					UIKit.corner(eh,2)
				end
			end
		end
		end  -- showEnvG (envelope band + ticks)
		-- Dots (anchors)
		for i,k in ipairs(kps) do
			local x,y=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
			local dot=acquireDot()
			dot.Size=UDim2.new(0,KR*2,0,KR*2)
			dot.Position=UDim2.new(0,math.floor(x+0.5),0,math.floor(y+0.5))  -- pixel-snap so dot lines up with line/ticks
			dot.BackgroundColor3 = (selIdx==i) and C.accent:Lerp(C.white,0.45) or C.accent
			dot.ZIndex=6
		end
	end

	local function mount(App, CURVE_WINDOW_ID)
		cleanup()
		-- Coalesced redraw: redraw() flags dirty; one Heartbeat per frame runs the
		-- real rebuild (fullRedraw, assigned later once UI helpers exist). Avoids
		-- rebuilding the whole scene on every mouse-move during a drag.
		redrawDirty=false
		_gridSig=nil
		redraw=function() redrawDirty=true end
		local curveName=nil
		if redrawConn then redrawConn:Disconnect() end
		-- Coalesce the full redraw to ~30fps WHILE DRAGGING. A warm redraw is
		-- cheap, but each frame still destroys+recreates the non-pooled dots/ticks/
		-- handles and re-runs the envelope band sampling; at 60fps during a drag that
		-- doubles the churn for no visible gain (the pointer + liveApply already move
		-- at 30fps). At rest we redraw immediately so single edits feel instant.
		local _lastFull=0
		redrawConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
			-- Tell the 3D preview to throttle to ~30fps ONLY while dragging (else it runs full rate).
			local _d = dragConn ~= nil
			if _d ~= _lastDragFlag then
				_lastDragFlag = _d
				local _rs = coreGui and coreGui:FindFirstChild("SineVFX_RuntimeState")
				if _rs then pcall(function() _rs:SetAttribute("GraphDragging", _d or nil) end) end
			end
			if redrawDirty and fullRedraw then
				-- Redraw EVERY frame, even mid-drag: dots are pooled + the grid is view-guarded, so a
				-- full redraw is cheap (~1-2ms). No more 30fps drag throttle -> the curve tracks the
				-- cursor at 60fps (smooth). The VFX WRITE is still rate-limited by liveApply.
				redrawDirty=false; fullRedraw()
			end
		end)
		if pending then
			callback=pending.cb
			valMin = (pending.range and pending.range.min) or -math.huge
			valMax = (pending.range and pending.range.max) or  math.huge
	dispV = (pending.range and pending.range.disp) or 1
			origSeq=pending.origSeq; onRevert=pending.onRevert; onCommit=pending.onCommit
			bezierIO=pending.bezierIO; curveName=pending.name
			kps={}; origKps={}
			-- Try to load bezier data
			local loaded=nil
			if bezierIO and bezierIO.read then
				local s=bezierIO.read()
				loaded=decodeBezier(s)
			end
			-- Validate loaded: every saved bezier anchor should be present in pending.kps
			-- (the sampled NumberSequence contains the original anchors plus interpolated
			-- samples in curved segments, so we check existence not count equality).
			local useLoaded=false
			if loaded and #loaded>=2 then
				useLoaded=true
				-- Tolerances scale with the value range so big-valued sequences don't spuriously
				-- fail; the FIRST/LAST bezier anchor is matched by VALUE only, because buildSequence
				-- always forces the baked NS to start at t=0 and end at t=1 regardless of anchor t.
				local vspan=(valMax~=math.huge and valMin~=-math.huge) and (valMax-valMin) or nil
				local nL=#loaded; local nP=#pending.kps
				for idx,lk in ipairs(loaded) do
					local vt = vspan and math.max(0.02,vspan*0.05) or math.max(0.05,math.abs(lk.v)*0.06)
					local found=false
					if idx==1 and nP>=1 then found=math.abs(lk.v-pending.kps[1].v)<vt
					elseif idx==nL and nP>=1 then found=math.abs(lk.v-pending.kps[nP].v)<vt end
					if not found then
						for _,pk in ipairs(pending.kps) do
							if math.abs(lk.t-pk.t)<0.04 and math.abs(lk.v-pk.v)<vt then found=true; break end
						end
					end
					if not found then useLoaded=false; break end
				end
			end
			local lsrc = useLoaded and loaded or pending.kps
			for _,k in ipairs(lsrc) do
				local ot = k.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
				local it = k.it or {dt=ot.dt,dv=ot.dv}
				local sy = (k.sync==false) and false or true
				local cv = math.clamp(k.v, valMin, valMax)
				-- Handle dv left unclamped: overshoot is allowed for free curve shaping;
				-- NumberSequence samples clip on write.
				table.insert(kps,{t=k.t,v=cv,ot={dt=ot.dt,dv=ot.dv},env=k.env or 0,it={dt=it.dt,dv=it.dv},sync=sy})
				table.insert(origKps,{t=k.t,v=cv,ot={dt=ot.dt,dv=ot.dv},env=k.env or 0,it={dt=it.dt,dv=it.dv},sync=sy})
			end
			viewMin=pending.viewMin; viewMax=pending.viewMax; selIdx=nil  -- open with no point selected
			pending=nil
		end
		isPinned=plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Curve")==true
		gui=Instance.new("ScreenGui"); gui.Name="SineVFX_CurveEditor"
		gui.ResetOnSpawn=false; gui.ZIndexBehavior=Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		gui.DisplayOrder=10005; gui.Parent=coreGui

		local win=WinFactory.new({
			stateKey="CurveEditor", gui=gui, name="CurveWrapper",
			w=300, h=320,
			pos=UDim2.new(0.3,0,0.2,0),
			minW=20, maxW=900, minH=20, maxH=800,
			topbarH=TOPBAR_H, getPinned=function() return isPinned end,
		})
		local main=win.main; local topbar=win.topbar
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(topbar, "Edit how a value changes over time (graph).") end
		-- bgFrame is the window body with ClipsDescendants. Parent the plot and
		-- button rows here (not main) so content past the window edge clips at the
		-- border instead of floating outside when resized small. Resize handles
		-- live on main, outside bgFrame, so they stay unclipped.
		local bgFrame=win.bgFrame

		-- Undo/redo history (go back & forth). Forward-declared; assigned near the
		-- end of mount once all the state helpers exist. Buttons live in the topbar.
		local commit, undo, redo, refreshHistButtons, histCanU, histCanR
		local undoBtn, redoBtn, undoIcon, redoIcon

		local ttl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
		ttl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-60,1,0); ttl.Position=UDim2.new(0,12,0,0)
		ttl.BackgroundTransparency=1; ttl.Text=curveName and ("Graph - "..curveName) or "Graph"
		ttl.TextColor3=C.text; ttl.Font=TXT.title.font; ttl.TextSize=TXT.title.size
		ttl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; ttl.ZIndex=6; ttl.Parent=topbar

		local pinBtn,pinIcon=UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"120413064155653",-48,15)
		local closeBtn,_   =UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"101421401454601",-26,10)
		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned=not isPinned
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Curve",isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",isPinned)
			tw(pinIcon,{ImageColor3=isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim})
		end)
		if isPinned then pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",true); pinIcon.ImageColor3=C.Pinned end
		closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			liveApply()  -- closing keeps the edits (apply), unlike Cancel which reverts
			if onCommit then onCommit() end
			App:toggle(CURVE_WINDOW_ID)
		end)

		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col=focused and C.text or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			local ic=focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			task.defer(function()
				for _,ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch,{TextColor3=col}) end
					if ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then
						if ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") then tw(ch,{ImageColor3=focused and C.Pinned or C.Pinned})
						else tw(ch,{ImageColor3=ic}) end
					end
				end
			end)
		end)

		cbg=Instance.new("Frame")
		cbg.Size=UDim2.new(1,-E*2,1,-(TOPBAR_H+BTN_ROW_H+BTN_ROW2_H+E*4))
		cbg.Position=UDim2.new(0,E,0,TOPBAR_H+E)
		cbg.BackgroundColor3=C.CurveBG; cbg.BorderSizePixel=0; cbg.ZIndex=2
		cbg.ClipsDescendants=true; cbg.Parent=bgFrame
		UIKit.corner(cbg,6)

		-- Top-right status badge: shows a brief readout of the last action (move,
		-- scroll/zoom, add, delete, reset). Auto-fades ~1.5s after each update.
		local statusBadge=Instance.new("TextLabel")
		statusBadge.Name="StatusBadge"
		statusBadge.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.XY
		statusBadge.Size=UDim2.new(0,0,0,0)
		statusBadge.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,0)
		statusBadge.Position=UDim2.new(1,-6,0,6)
		statusBadge.BackgroundColor3=C.base
		statusBadge.BackgroundTransparency=1
		statusBadge.BorderSizePixel=0
		statusBadge.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; statusBadge.TextSize=10
		statusBadge.TextColor3=C.text; statusBadge.TextTransparency=1
		statusBadge.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Right
		statusBadge.Text=""
		statusBadge.ZIndex=50
		statusBadge.Parent=cbg
		UIKit.corner(statusBadge,4)
		local sbPad=Instance.new("UIPadding")
		sbPad.PaddingTop=UDim.new(0,3); sbPad.PaddingBottom=UDim.new(0,3)
		sbPad.PaddingLeft=UDim.new(0,7); sbPad.PaddingRight=UDim.new(0,7)
		sbPad.Parent=statusBadge
		local sbStroke=Instance.new("UIStroke")
		sbStroke.Color=C.border; sbStroke.Thickness=1; sbStroke.Transparency=1
		sbStroke.Parent=statusBadge
		local statusToken=0
		local function setStatus(text)
			if not statusBadge or not statusBadge.Parent then return end
			statusBadge.Text=text
			statusToken=statusToken+1
			local tok=statusToken
			statusBadge.TextTransparency=0
			statusBadge.BackgroundTransparency=0.15
			sbStroke.Transparency=0.4
			task.delay(1.5,function()
				if tok~=statusToken then return end
				if not statusBadge or not statusBadge.Parent then return end
				tw(statusBadge,{TextTransparency=1, BackgroundTransparency=1})
				tw(sbStroke,{Transparency=1})
			end)
		end

		gridLayer=Instance.new("Frame"); gridLayer.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,1,0)
		gridLayer.BackgroundTransparency=1; gridLayer.ZIndex=1; gridLayer.Parent=cbg

		-- plotClip sits exactly at the gridded box and clips its contents so curves,
		-- anchors, handles, and hover preview never bleed into the label gutters.
		-- Its position/size are updated in redraw() each frame.
		local plotClip=Instance.new("Frame")
		plotClip.BackgroundTransparency=1; plotClip.BorderSizePixel=0
		plotClip.ClipsDescendants=true; plotClip.ZIndex=3; plotClip.Parent=cbg

		kpLayer=Instance.new("Frame")
		kpLayer.BackgroundTransparency=1; kpLayer.BorderSizePixel=0
		kpLayer.ZIndex=3; kpLayer.Parent=plotClip

		-- Hover preview (also clipped to the box)
		local hoverLayer=Instance.new("Frame")
		hoverLayer.BackgroundTransparency=1; hoverLayer.BorderSizePixel=0
		hoverLayer.ZIndex=1; hoverLayer.Parent=plotClip

		-- Resync the clip frame and inner layers whenever the canvas size changes.
		syncPlotClip = function()
			local W,H=cbgWH()
			if W==0 or H==0 then return end
			local bx0=E+LPAD; local bx1=W-E-XPAD
			local by0=E+YPAD; local by1=H-E-YPAD
			plotClip.Position=UDim2.new(0,bx0,0,by0)
			plotClip.Size=UDim2.new(0,bx1-bx0,0,by1-by0)
			-- Inner layers shift by (-bx0,-by0) and span the full cbg so existing
			-- drawing code (which uses cbg-relative coords) keeps working unchanged.
			kpLayer.Position=UDim2.new(0,-bx0,0,-by0)
			kpLayer.Size=UDim2.new(0,W,0,H)
			hoverLayer.Position=UDim2.new(0,-bx0,0,-by0)
			hoverLayer.Size=UDim2.new(0,W,0,H)
		end
		syncPlotClip()
		cbg:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(syncPlotClip)

		local function findNear(px,py)
			local W,H=cbgWH()
			for i,k in ipairs(kps) do
				local kx,ky=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
				if math.abs(px-kx)<KR+5 and math.abs(py-ky)<KR+5 then return i end
			end
		end
		local function findEnvHandle(px,py)
			if not (showEnvG and envAllowed) then return end
			local W,H=cbgWH()
			local ENV_STUB_FAR=26
			-- only check selected point
			if not selIdx then return end
			local k=kps[selIdx]; if not k then return end
			local kx=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
			if math.abs(px-kx)>8 then return end
			local env=k.env or 0
			local _,eyUp = toCanvas(k.t,k.v+env,W,H)
			local _,eyDn = toCanvas(k.t,k.v-env,W,H)
			-- env inactive: the ticks sit at the far stub positions
			if env==0 then eyUp=eyUp-ENV_STUB_FAR; eyDn=eyDn+ENV_STUB_FAR end
			-- ONLY the ticks are grabbable in either state. The center line is
			-- never a click target (env>0 it's decorative; env==0 the far ticks
			-- are the pull-out handles). Clicks elsewhere fall through to the
			-- anchor-drag / add-point paths.
			if math.abs(py-eyUp)<8 then return selIdx, 1 end
			if math.abs(py-eyDn)<8 then return selIdx,-1 end
			-- When envelope is OFF, the vertical center line itself is grabbable so
			-- you can pull envelope out of a flat point. Direction = which side of
			-- the anchor you grabbed (above -> up tick, below -> down tick).
			if env==0 then
				local _,ky=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
				-- the line spans between the two stubs; allow grabbing along it,
				-- but keep a dead zone around the anchor dot so clicking the dot
				-- still moves the point instead of starting an envelope drag.
				local DOT_DEAD=KR+6
				if py<=eyDn and py>=eyUp and math.abs(py-ky)>DOT_DEAD then
					return selIdx, (py<ky) and 1 or -1
				end
			end
		end
		local function findHandle(px,py)
			local W,H=cbgWH()
			local outPos,inPos = handlePositions(W,H)
			if outPos and math.abs(px-outPos[1])<HR+4 and math.abs(py-outPos[2])<HR+4 then return "out" end
			if inPos  and math.abs(px-inPos[1]) <HR+4 and math.abs(py-inPos[2]) <HR+4 then return "in"  end
		end

		-- Hover preview
		-- Double-click point editor + hover tooltip wiring (assigned further below).
		local openPointEditor, pointEditorClose, peIsOpen
		-- Select-all when a textbox gains focus (wait for MB1 release first so the
		-- click doesn't drop the selection), matching the Properties textbox UX.
		local function selectAllOnFocus(box)
			box.Focused:Connect(function()
				local conn; conn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
					if not uis:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
						conn:Disconnect(); conn=nil
						if box:IsFocused() then box.SelectionStart=1; box.CursorPosition=#box.Text+1 end
					end
				end)
			end)
		end
		local peLastT, peLastIdx = 0, nil
		-- Persistent T/V tooltip, shown only while hovering an anchor.
		local pointTip=Instance.new("Frame")
		pointTip.Name="PointTip"; pointTip.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.XY
		pointTip.BackgroundColor3=C.base; pointTip.BorderSizePixel=0
		pointTip.Visible=false; pointTip.ZIndex=60; pointTip.Parent=cbg
		UIKit.corner(pointTip,5)
		local _ttPad=Instance.new("UIPadding")
		_ttPad.PaddingLeft=UDim.new(0,6); _ttPad.PaddingRight=UDim.new(0,6)
		_ttPad.PaddingTop=UDim.new(0,3); _ttPad.PaddingBottom=UDim.new(0,3); _ttPad.Parent=pointTip
		local pointTipLbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
		pointTipLbl.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.XY; pointTipLbl.BackgroundTransparency=1
		pointTipLbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; pointTipLbl.TextSize=11; pointTipLbl.TextColor3=C.text
		pointTipLbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; pointTipLbl.ZIndex=61; pointTipLbl.Parent=pointTip
		local function clearHover() for _,c in ipairs(hoverLayer:GetChildren()) do c:Destroy() end pointTip.Visible=false end
		local function ghostLine(x0,y0,x1,y1,W,H)
			local bx0c=E+LPAD; local by0c=E+YPAD; local bx1c=W-E-XPAD; local by1c=H-E-YPAD
			local cx0,cy0,cx1,cy1=clipLine(x0,y0,x1,y1,W,H,bx0c,by0c,bx1c,by1c)
			if not cx0 then return end
			local ddx=cx1-cx0; local ddy=cy1-cy0
			local len=math.sqrt(ddx*ddx+ddy*ddy)
			if len<1 then return end
			local ln=Instance.new("Frame")
			ln.BackgroundColor3=C.white; ln.BackgroundTransparency=0.65; ln.BorderSizePixel=0
			ln.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5); ln.Size=UDim2.new(0,len,0,1)
			ln.Position=UDim2.new(0,(cx0+cx1)/2,0,(cy0+cy1)/2)
			ln.Rotation=math.deg(math.atan2(ddy,ddx)); ln.ZIndex=2; ln.Parent=hoverLayer
		end
		-- Ghost the resulting curve through a hovered insertion point. A new point is
		-- linear (ot=0), so each side is the neighbour's bezier into/out of a zero
		-- handle -- sampled the same way the live curve is, just drawn faint.
		local function ghostCurve(A,B,W,H)
			local aot=A.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}; local bot=B.it or B.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
			if isZeroOt(aot) and isZeroOt(bot) then
				local x0,y0=toCanvas(A.t,A.v,W,H); local x1,y1=toCanvas(B.t,B.v,W,H)
				ghostLine(x0,y0,x1,y1,W,H); return
			end
			local c1t=A.t+aot.dt; local c1v=A.v+aot.dv
			local c2t=B.t-bot.dt; local c2v=B.v-bot.dv
			local N=12; local prevX,prevY
			for s=0,N do
				local u=s/N
				local pt=math.clamp(bez(u,A.t,c1t,c2t,B.t), math.min(A.t,B.t), math.max(A.t,B.t))
				local pv=math.clamp(bez(u,A.v,c1v,c2v,B.v), valMin, valMax)
				local px,py=toCanvas(pt,pv,W,H)
				if prevX then ghostLine(prevX,prevY,px,py,W,H) end
				prevX,prevY=px,py
			end
		end
		local function showHover(px,py)
			clearHover()
			local W,H=cbgWH()
			if W==0 or H==0 then return end
			if findHandle(px,py) or findEnvHandle(px,py) then return end
			local hoveredPt=findNear(px,py)
			if hoveredPt then
				local k=kps[hoveredPt]
				local kx,ky=toCanvas(k.t,k.v,W,H)
				pointTipLbl.Text="T  "..fmtNum(k.t,3).."\nV  "..fmtNum(k.v*dispV,3)
				local belowTop = ky<34
				pointTip.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5, belowTop and 0 or 1)
				local tipX=math.clamp(kx,38,math.max(38,W-38))
				pointTip.Position=UDim2.new(0,tipX,0, belowTop and (ky+12) or (ky-12))
				pointTip.Visible=true
				return
			end
			local t,v=fromCanvas(px,py,W,H)
			if v < valMin or v > valMax then return end
			t=math.clamp(t,0,1)
			local gx,gy=toCanvas(t,v,W,H)
			local prev,nxt
			for _,k in ipairs(kps) do
				if k.t<=t then if not prev or k.t>prev.t then prev=k end end
				if k.t>=t then if not nxt or k.t<nxt.t then nxt=k end end
			end
			local newPt={t=t,v=v,ot={dt=0,dv=0},it={dt=0,dv=0}}
			if prev then ghostCurve(prev,newPt,W,H) end
			if nxt  then ghostCurve(newPt,nxt,W,H) end
			local dot=Instance.new("Frame")
			dot.Size=UDim2.new(0,KR*2,0,KR*2); dot.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0.5,0.5)
			dot.Position=UDim2.new(0,gx,0,gy)
			dot.BackgroundTransparency=1
			dot.BorderSizePixel=0; dot.ZIndex=3; dot.Parent=hoverLayer
			Instance.new("UICorner",dot).CornerRadius=UDim.new(1,0)
			local dst=Instance.new("UIStroke"); dst.Color=C.white; dst.Thickness=2
			dst.Transparency=0.65; dst.Parent=dot
		end

		cbg:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(redraw)

		local mouseOverCanvas=false
		cbg.MouseEnter:Connect(function() mouseOverCanvas=true end)
		cbg.MouseLeave:Connect(function() mouseOverCanvas=false; clearHover() end)

		scrollConn=uis.InputChanged:Connect(function(inp)
			if not gui or not gui.Parent then return end
			if inp.UserInputType~=Enum.UserInputType.MouseWheel then return end
			if not mouseOverCanvas then return end
			local mp=uis:GetMouseLocation()
			local ap=cbg.AbsolutePosition; local as=cbg.AbsoluteSize; local _s=UIKit.uiScaleOf(cbg)
			local py=(mp.Y-ap.Y)/_s; local H=as.Y/_s
			local y0=E+YPAD; local y1=H-E-YPAD
			local mouseV=viewMin+(1-(py-y0)/(y1-y0))*(viewMax-viewMin)
			local ratio=(mouseV-viewMin)/(viewMax-viewMin)
			local newRange=math.max(0.5,math.min((viewMax-viewMin)*(inp.Position.Z>0 and 0.85 or 1.15),1000))
			viewMin=mouseV-ratio*newRange
			viewMax=mouseV+(1-ratio)*newRange
			redraw()
			if not dragConn then showHover((mp.X-ap.X)/_s, (mp.Y-ap.Y)/_s) end
			setStatus("Range  "..fmtNum(viewMin*dispV,2).." – "..fmtNum(viewMax*dispV,2))
		end)

		local clickBtn=Instance.new("TextButton")
		clickBtn.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,1,0); clickBtn.BackgroundTransparency=1
		clickBtn.Text=""; clickBtn.ZIndex=5; clickBtn.AutoButtonColor=false; clickBtn.Parent=cbg

		clickBtn.MouseMoved:Connect(function(mx,my)
			if dragConn then clearHover(); return end
			local a=cbg.AbsolutePosition; local _s=UIKit.uiScaleOf(cbg)
			local px=(mx-a.X)/_s; local py=(my-a.Y)/_s
			-- Hover-select: hovering an anchor selects it (so handles appear without click)
			local hoveredIdx = findNear(px,py)
			if hoveredIdx and hoveredIdx ~= selIdx then
				selIdx = hoveredIdx
				redraw()
			end
			showHover(px,py)
		end)

		local function startAnchorDrag(clickPx, clickPy)
			if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect() end
			local dragging = selIdx and kps[selIdx] or nil
			if not dragging then return end
			local startT, startV = dragging.t, dragging.v
			-- Capture pixel offset between click and anchor center so the anchor follows the
			-- cursor without teleporting to the cursor's exact position.
			local W0,H0=cbgWH()
			local ax0,ay0=toCanvas(dragging.t,dragging.v,W0,H0)
			local offX = (clickPx or ax0) - ax0
			local offY = (clickPy or ay0) - ay0
			dragConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
				if not uis:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
					if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect(); dragConn=nil end; redraw(); return  -- final full-quality frame at release (throttle may skip last)
				end
				local m2=uis:GetMouseLocation(); local a2=cbg.AbsolutePosition; local _s=UIKit.uiScaleOf(cbg)
				local mpx = (m2.X - a2.X)/_s - offX
				local mpy = (m2.Y - a2.Y)/_s - offY
				local t2,v2=fromCanvas(mpx,mpy,cbgWH())
				local idx=table.find(kps,dragging)
				if not idx then if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect(); dragConn=nil end; return end
				if idx~=1 and idx~=#kps then dragging.t=t2 end
				local vlo=math.max(viewMin,valMin); local vhi=math.min(viewMax,valMax)
				if vlo>vhi then vlo,vhi=vhi,vlo end -- view fully outside value range: don't crash
				dragging.v=math.clamp(v2,vlo,vhi)
				-- Re-clamp this anchor's existing tangent: dt stays inside [0,1] (mirror),
				-- dv intentionally UNCLAMPED — handles move freely on the v-axis; the rendered
				-- curve and sequence output cap at [valMin, valMax].
				if dragging.ot then
					-- Handles may cross neighbour NODES now (user request); only keep them on
					-- canvas in time: OUT bounded by 1-t, IN by t. The render clamps curveT per
					-- segment so the curve still can't fold backward.
					local maxOut=math.max(0,1-dragging.t); local maxIn=math.max(0,dragging.t)
					dragging.ot.dt = math.clamp(dragging.ot.dt, 0, maxOut)
					if dragging.it then dragging.it.dt = math.clamp(dragging.it.dt, 0, maxIn) end
				end
				redraw()
				selIdx=table.find(kps,dragging)
				liveApply()
				if math.abs(dragging.t-startT) > 1e-4 or math.abs(dragging.v-startV) > (viewMax-viewMin)*1e-4 then
					setStatus("Moved  T: "..fmtNum(dragging.t,2).."  V: "..fmtNum(dragging.v*dispV,2))
				end
			end)
		end

		local function startHandleDrag(which, clickPx, clickPy)
			if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect() end
			local anchor = selIdx and kps[selIdx] or nil
			if not anchor then return end
			anchor.ot = anchor.ot or {dt=0,dv=0}
			anchor.it = anchor.it or {dt=anchor.ot.dt,dv=anchor.ot.dv}
			local synced = (anchor.sync ~= false)
			local act = (which=="out") and anchor.ot or anchor.it
			-- If the dragged tangent is zero (ghost stub), seed it from the stub pixel
			-- position so the handle starts at its visual resting place.
			if isZeroOt(act) then
				local W0s,H0s=cbgWH()
				local ax0,ay0 = toCanvas(anchor.t,anchor.v,W0s,H0s)
				local ghostX = (which=="out") and (ax0+STUB_PX_T) or (ax0-STUB_PX_T)
				local gt,gv2 = fromCanvas(ghostX, ay0, W0s, H0s)
				act.dt = math.abs(gt - anchor.t)
				act.dv = gv2 - anchor.v
				if which=="in" then act.dv = -act.dv end
				if synced then
					local other = (which=="out") and anchor.it or anchor.ot
					other.dt = act.dt; other.dv = act.dv
				end
			end
			-- Pixel offset between click and current handle center
			local W0,H0=cbgWH()
			local hx0,hy0
			if which=="out" then
				hx0,hy0 = toCanvas(anchor.t+anchor.ot.dt, anchor.v+anchor.ot.dv, W0,H0)
			else
				hx0,hy0 = toCanvas(anchor.t-anchor.it.dt, anchor.v-anchor.it.dv, W0,H0)
			end
			local offX = (clickPx or hx0) - hx0
			local offY = (clickPy or hy0) - hy0
			dragConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
				if not uis:IsMouseButtonPressed(Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1) then
					if dragConn then dragConn:Disconnect(); dragConn=nil end; redraw(); return  -- final full-quality frame at release (throttle may skip last)
				end
				local m2=uis:GetMouseLocation(); local a2=cbg.AbsolutePosition; local _s=UIKit.uiScaleOf(cbg)
				local mpx = (m2.X - a2.X)/_s - offX
				local mpy = (m2.Y - a2.Y)/_s - offY
