			local def=PROPDEF[f.prop]; if not def then return end
			local row
			if def.kind=="num" then row=addNumField(parent, def)
			elseif def.kind=="enum" then row=addDropField(parent, def, def.options, false)
			elseif def.kind=="font" then row=addDropField(parent, def, FONT_OPTS, true)
			elseif def.kind=="image" then row=addImageField(parent, def)
			elseif def.kind=="size" then row=addSizeField(parent, def)
			elseif def.kind=="bool" then row=addBoolField(parent, def)
			elseif def.kind=="color" then row=addPropColorField(parent, def) end
			if def.key=="tileSize" and row then condFns[#condFns+1]=function() if row.Parent then row.Visible=(cur.props.imageFit=="Tile") end end end
			return row
		end

		local sectionPanels={}
		local fitHeight
		local _prevFit
		local function addSection(title, fields)
			local sec=Instance.new("Frame"); sec.BackgroundColor3=C.base; sec.BorderSizePixel=0; sec.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,0); sec.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.Y; sec.Parent=sc.list
			UIKit.corner(sec,8)
			local sl=Instance.new("UIListLayout"); sl.SortOrder=Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder; sl.Parent=sec
			local header=Instance.new("TextButton"); header.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,BH); header.BackgroundTransparency=1; header.Text=""; header.AutoButtonColor=false; header.LayoutOrder=0; header.Parent=sec
			local hl=Instance.new("TextLabel"); hl.BackgroundTransparency=1; hl.Position=UDim2.new(0,12,0,0); hl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-36,1,0)
			hl.Text=title; hl.TextColor3=C.textDim; hl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; hl.TextSize=14; hl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; hl.Parent=header
			local arrow=Instance.new("ImageLabel"); arrow.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,0.5); arrow.Position=UDim2.new(1,-12,0.5,0); arrow.Size=UDim2.new(0,12,0,12)
			arrow.BackgroundTransparency=1; arrow.ImageColor3=C.textDim; arrow.Rotation=180; arrow.Parent=header
			if UIKit.setIconImage then UIKit.setIconImage(arrow,"116140507142780") else arrow.Image="rbxassetid://116140507142780" end
			local panel=Instance.new("Frame"); panel.BackgroundTransparency=1; panel.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,0); panel.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.Y; panel.Visible=true; panel.LayoutOrder=1; panel.Parent=sec; sectionPanels[#sectionPanels+1]=panel
			local ppad=Instance.new("UIPadding"); ppad.PaddingLeft=UDim.new(0,6); ppad.PaddingRight=UDim.new(0,6); ppad.PaddingBottom=UDim.new(0,6); ppad.Parent=panel
			local pbg=Instance.new("Frame"); pbg.BackgroundColor3=PANELBG; pbg.BorderSizePixel=0; pbg.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,0,0); pbg.AutomaticSize=Enum.AutomaticSize.Y; pbg.Parent=panel
			UIKit.corner(pbg,6)
			local pbpad=Instance.new("UIPadding"); pbpad.PaddingTop=UDim.new(0,6); pbpad.PaddingBottom=UDim.new(0,6); pbpad.PaddingLeft=UDim.new(0,6); pbpad.PaddingRight=UDim.new(0,6); pbpad.Parent=pbg
			local pll=Instance.new("UIListLayout"); pll.SortOrder=Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder; pll.Padding=UDim.new(0,GAP); pll.Parent=pbg
			for _,f in ipairs(fields) do addField(pbg, f) end
			header.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() panel.Visible=not panel.Visible; arrow.Rotation=panel.Visible and 180 or 0; if fitHeight then fitHeight() end end)
		end
		for _,s in ipairs(SECTIONS) do addSection(s.title, s.fields) end
		refreshCond()

		fitHeight = function()
			if not main or not main.Parent then return end
			local _vph=(workspace.CurrentCamera and workspace.CurrentCamera.ViewportSize.Y) or 800
			if win.setMaxH then win.setMaxH(math.max(360, _vph-40)) end
		end
		sc.layout:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteContentSize"):Connect(function() task.defer(fitHeight) end)
		task.defer(fitHeight)

		-- bottom bar (Apply just closes; edits are already live. Cancel reverts.)
		local bar=Instance.new("Frame"); bar.BackgroundTransparency=1; bar.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,1)
		bar.Position=UDim2.new(0,GAP,1,-GAP); bar.Size=UDim2.new(1,-GAP*2,0,BBAR_H); bar.Parent=bgFrame
		local barl=Instance.new("UIListLayout"); barl.FillDirection=Enum.FillDirection.Horizontal; barl.Padding=UDim.new(0,GAP); barl.SortOrder=Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder; barl.Parent=bar
		local function colBtn(text,lo) local b=Instance.new("TextButton"); b.Size=UDim2.new(0.25,-4.5,1,0); b.LayoutOrder=lo; b.Text=text; b.Parent=bar; return b end
		local importBtn=colBtn("Import",1); styleReset(importBtn)
		local exportBtn=colBtn("Export",2); styleReset(exportBtn)
		local cancelBtn=colBtn("Cancel",3); styleReset(cancelBtn)
		local applyBtn=colBtn("Apply",4); styleAccent(applyBtn)
		applyBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() persist(); ThemeStore.applyLive(cur.name); if ThemeStore.refresh then ThemeStore.refresh() end; App:toggle(WIN_ID) end)
		cancelBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			local themes=ThemeStore.list()
			if wasNew then
				local out={}; for _,t in ipairs(themes) do if t.name~=cur.name then out[#out+1]=t end end; ThemeStore.saveList(out)
			else
				local found=false
				for i,t in ipairs(themes) do if t.name==cur.name then themes[i]={name=snapName,colors=snapColors,props=snapProps}; found=true; break end end
				if not found then themes[#themes+1]={name=snapName,colors=snapColors,props=snapProps} end
				ThemeStore.saveList(themes)
			end
			App:toggle(WIN_ID); liveTok+=1; ThemeStore.applyLive(wasActive); if ThemeStore.refresh then ThemeStore.refresh() end
		end)
		exportBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			persist(); ThemeStore.pendingExport={ name=cur.name, colors=cur.colors, props=cur.props }
			ctx.pendingExportRaw = nil  -- never let a stale raw payload shadow this theme
			if ThemeStore.refresh then ThemeStore.refresh() end
			if ids.export then App:toggle(ids.export) end
		end)
		importBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			ctx.pendingImportFn = function(code)
				local t,err = ThemeStore.import(code); if not t then return false, err end
				cur.colors=t.colors; cur.props=t.props; persist()
				ThemeStore.pendingNew=false; ThemeStore.pendingEdit=cur.name
				ThemeStore.applyLive(cur.name)
				task.defer(function() if App.windows[WIN_ID] and App.windows[WIN_ID].isOpen() then App:toggle(WIN_ID); task.defer(function() App:toggle(WIN_ID) end) end end)
				return true
			end
			ctx.pendingImportTitle="Import Into Theme"
			if ids.import then App:toggle(ids.import) end
		end)
		-- Editing a theme turns it on; if a DIFFERENT theme is turned on elsewhere while
		-- editing, close the editor (prevents desync + pending live-apply clobbering it).
		-- Only (re)activate on open if it's a NEW theme or a DIFFERENT theme than what's already active.
		-- Re-applying the already-active theme on open caused visible "stuff changes when I open the editor".
		-- Theme was activated up-front (before the UI built) so the editor renders in the edited
		-- theme; applyLive stays for the incremental single-color edits during editing.
		if ThemeStore.refresh then ThemeStore.refresh() end
		local _startApply = ThemeStore.applyCounter or 0
		local _closeWatch
		local _cwAcc = 0
		_closeWatch = runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
			if not isOpen() then if _closeWatch then _closeWatch:Disconnect(); _closeWatch=nil end return end
			-- Throttle ~10fps: this only watches for a rare EXTERNAL theme switch (applyCounter bump
			-- from the Settings window) to auto-close the editor. Comparing applyCounter every frame at
			-- 60fps was pure idle CPU while the editor sat open; 0.1s latency on the auto-close is
			-- imperceptible. isOpen() stays per-frame so the self-disconnect on close is still prompt.
			_cwAcc += dt
			if _cwAcc < 0.1 then return end
			_cwAcc = 0
			if (ThemeStore.applyCounter or 0) ~= _startApply then
				if _closeWatch then _closeWatch:Disconnect(); _closeWatch=nil end
				liveTok += 1
				App:toggle(WIN_ID)
			end
		end)
	end

	return { mount=mount, unmount=cleanup, isOpen=isOpen }
end

-- ConfirmWindow: reusable confirm dialog as a REAL (X-resizable, Y-locked) window.
-- A danger button sets ctx.pendingConfirm = {title, message, confirmText, onConfirm} then App:toggle()s this window.
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory)
	local C=Theme.C; local tw=UIKit.tw
	local plugin=ctx.plugin; local coreGui=ctx.coreGui
	local WIN_W=300; local TOPBAR_H=25; local WIN_H=120; local GAP=6; local BTN_H=26
	local gui=nil; local isPinned=false
	local function cleanup() if gui then gui:Destroy(); gui=nil end end
	local function isOpen() return gui~=nil and gui.Parent~=nil end

	local function mount(App, WIN_ID)
		cleanup()
		local cfg = ctx.pendingConfirm or {}; ctx.pendingConfirm=nil
		isPinned = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Confirm")==true
		gui=Instance.new("ScreenGui"); gui.Name="SineVFX_Confirm"
		gui.ResetOnSpawn=false; gui.ZIndexBehavior=Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		gui.DisplayOrder=10020; gui.Parent=coreGui
		-- Auto-size the dialog HEIGHT to its message so a short message gets a short window.
		local textService = game:GetService("TextService")
		local CHROME = TOPBAR_H + GAP*3 + BTN_H  -- everything except the wrapped message text
		local function msgHeightFor(logicalW)
			local mw = math.max(20, logicalW - GAP*2)
			-- measure at the THEME's text scale: applyThemeProps scales the real
			-- label after mount, so a fixed-14 measurement under-sizes the dialog
			local tscale = tonumber(ctx.themeTextScale) or 1
			local ok, sz = pcall(function() return textService:GetTextSize(cfg.message or "", math.ceil(14*tscale), Enum.Font.GothamMedium, Vector2.new(mw, 100000)) end)
			return (ok and sz and math.ceil(sz.Y) + 4) or 40
		end
		local savedW = (plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_win_Confirm") or {}).w or WIN_W
		local initH = math.clamp(msgHeightFor(savedW) + CHROME, 66, 500)
		local win=WinFactory.new({ stateKey="Confirm", gui=gui, name="ConfirmWrapper",
			w=WIN_W,h=initH,pos=UDim2.new(0.5,0,0.42,0), minW=240,maxW=480,minH=66,maxH=500, noYResize=true, noSaveH=true,
			topbarH=TOPBAR_H, getPinned=function() return isPinned end })
		local main=win.main; local bgFrame=win.bgFrame; local topbar=win.topbar
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(topbar, "Confirm or cancel this action.") end

		local titleLbl=Instance.new("TextLabel"); titleLbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-60,1,0); titleLbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,12,0,0)
		titleLbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; titleLbl.Text=cfg.title or "Confirm"; titleLbl.TextColor3=C.text
		titleLbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; titleLbl.TextSize=13; titleLbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		titleLbl.ZIndex=6; titleLbl.Parent=topbar
		local pinBtn,pinIcon=UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"120413064155653",-48,15)
		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned=not isPinned; plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_Confirm",isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",isPinned); tw(pinIcon,{ImageColor3=isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim})
		end)
		if isPinned then pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive",true); pinIcon.ImageColor3=C.Pinned end
		local closeBtn,_=UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar,"101421401454601",-26,10)
		closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() App:toggle(WIN_ID) end)
		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col=focused and C.text or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			local ic=focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0),0.15)
			task.defer(function()
				for _,ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch,{TextColor3=col}) end
					if ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then tw(ch,{ImageColor3=ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") and C.Pinned or ic}) end
				end
			end)
		end)

		local msg=Instance.new("TextLabel"); msg.BackgroundTransparency=1
		msg.Position=UDim2.new(0,GAP,0,TOPBAR_H+GAP); msg.Size=UDim2.new(1,-GAP*2,1,-(TOPBAR_H+GAP+GAP+BTN_H+GAP))
		msg.Text=cfg.message or ""; msg.TextColor3=C.textDim; msg.Font=Enum.Font.GothamMedium; msg.TextSize=14
		msg.TextWrapped=true; msg.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; msg.TextYAlignment=Enum.TextYAlignment.Top
		msg.Parent=bgFrame

		-- Cancel (LEFT) neutral
		local cancelB=Instance.new("TextButton"); cancelB.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,1)
		cancelB.Position=UDim2.new(0,GAP,1,-GAP); cancelB.Size=UDim2.new(0.5,-9,0,BTN_H)
		cancelB.BackgroundColor3=C.base; cancelB.AutoButtonColor=false; cancelB.BorderSizePixel=0
		cancelB.Text="Cancel"; cancelB.TextColor3=C.text; cancelB.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; cancelB.TextSize=12
		cancelB.Parent=bgFrame; UIKit.corner(cancelB,6); cancelB:SetAttribute("BaseColor", C.base)
		UIKit.hoverLift(cancelB)  -- same fade-on-hover as every other Cancel button (Curve/ColSeq/ColorWheel)

		-- Confirm (RIGHT) danger
		local confB=Instance.new("TextButton"); confB.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(1,1)
		confB.Position=UDim2.new(1,-GAP,1,-GAP); confB.Size=UDim2.new(0.5,-9,0,BTN_H)
		confB.BackgroundColor3=C.danger; confB.BackgroundTransparency=0.5; confB.AutoButtonColor=false; confB.BorderSizePixel=0; confB:SetAttribute("SVFXdanger",true)
		confB.Text=cfg.confirmText or "Confirm"; confB.TextColor3=C.danger:Lerp(Color3.new(1,1,1),0.4); confB.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold; confB.TextSize=12
		confB.Parent=bgFrame; UIKit.corner(confB,6)
		confB.MouseEnter:Connect(function() tw(confB,{BackgroundTransparency=0.32}) end)
		confB.MouseLeave:Connect(function() tw(confB,{BackgroundTransparency=0.5}) end)

		-- Keep the height fitted to the message; re-fit when the user resizes WIDTH (text re-wraps).
		local function fitH(w)
			w = w or main.Size.X.Offset
			local newH = math.clamp(msgHeightFor(w) + CHROME, 66, 500)
			if win.setMaxH then win.setMaxH(newH) end
			main.Size = UDim2.new(0, w, 0, newH); win.currentSize.h = newH
		end
		local lastW = main.Size.X.Offset
		main:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(function()
			local w = main.Size.X.Offset
			if math.abs(w - lastW) < 1 then return end
			lastW = w; fitH(w)
		end)
		task.defer(function() if main.Parent then fitH() end end)

		cancelB.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function() App:toggle(WIN_ID) end)
		confB.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			local cb=cfg.onConfirm
			App:toggle(WIN_ID)
			if cb then cb() end
		end)
	end
	return { mount=mount, unmount=cleanup, isOpen=isOpen }
end

-- ParticleCounterWindow: fixed Hub-styled badge (pos = Settings SineVFX_pcCorner), kept above
-- all windows, accent border, faded text. SIMULATED live particle count for the SELECTION ONLY
-- (0 when nothing is selected, plus any :Emit() bursts). LOOP: the expensive workspace scan is throttled (~0.3s), the particle
-- sim ticks every frame for a smooth ramp/fluctuation, and the displayed number is EASED so it
-- glides instead of snapping.
--   * ParticleEmitters simulated per-particle (births at Rate, deaths after random Lifetime in
--     [Min,Max], aged by TimeScale); huge ones fall back to analytic Rate*avgLife/TimeScale.
--   * :Emit() bursts (ctx.pcRecordEmit) decay smoothly; always counted.
--   * Beam / Trail / PointLight / SpotLight / SurfaceLight -> +1 each (enabled).
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory)
	local C       = Theme.C
	local plugin  = ctx.plugin
	local coreGui = ctx.coreGui
	local selection = ctx.selection
	local runService = ctx.runService or game:GetService("RunService")
	local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
	local EmitterRuntime = require(script.Parent.Parent.Modules.EmitterRuntime)
	local TrailRuntime   = require(script.Parent.Parent.Modules.EmitterRuntime).Trail
	local BeamRuntime    = require(script.Parent.Parent.Modules.EmitterRuntime).Beam
	local spec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).EmitterSpec
	local trailSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).TrailSpec
	local beamSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).BeamSpec

	local CORNER  = 9
	local WIN_W   = 108
	local WIN_H   = 30
	local M       = 10
	local K_CORNER= "SineVFX_pcCorner"
	local SIM_CAP = 4000
	local ANALYTIC_SCOPE_LIMIT = 60

	local POS = {
		TL={0,0, M, M},   TC={0.5,0, 0, M},   TR={1,0, -M, M},
		CL={0,0.5, M, 0},                     CR={1,0.5, -M, 0},
		BL={0,1, M, -M},  BC={0.5,1, 0, -M},  BR={1,1, -M, -M},
	}

	local gui      = nil
	local loopConn = nil
	local selConn  = nil
	local conns    = {}
	local sims     = {}    -- [emitter] = {deaths={...}, frac, last}
	local scopeEmitters = {}  -- cached list of in-scope ParticleEmitters (refreshed by gatherScope)
	local scope3D  = {}   -- [part]=true : in-scope SineVFX3D emitter parts (live clones counted via EmitterRuntime)
	local scopeTrails = {}  -- [trail]=true : in-scope transformed trails (emitted copies counted via TrailRuntime)
	local scopeBeams  = {}  -- [beam]=true : in-scope transformed beams (emitted copies counted via BeamRuntime)
	local baseCount = 0       -- enabled beams/trails/lights in scope (refreshed by gatherScope)

	local function cleanup()
		if loopConn then loopConn:Disconnect(); loopConn=nil end
		if selConn  then selConn:Disconnect();  selConn=nil  end
		for _,c in ipairs(conns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
		conns={}
		for k in pairs(sims) do sims[k]=nil end
		scopeEmitters={}; baseCount=0; scope3D={}; scopeTrails={}; scopeBeams={}
		if gui then gui:Destroy() end
		gui=nil
	end

	local function isOpen() return gui and gui.Parent~=nil end

	local function commas(n)
		local out = tostring(n):reverse():gsub("(%d%d%d)","%1,"):reverse()
		return (out:gsub("^,",""))
	end

	local function simEmitter(em, now, emitting, forceAnalytic)
		local lt = em.Lifetime; local lmin,lmax = lt.Min, lt.Max
		local ts = em.TimeScale; if not ts or ts<=0 then ts=1 end
		local rate = emitting and em.Rate or 0
		local analytic = rate*((lmin+lmax)*0.5)/ts
		if forceAnalytic or analytic > SIM_CAP then sims[em]=nil; return analytic end
		local sdat = sims[em]
		if not sdat then
			if not emitting then return 0 end
			sdat={deaths={}, frac=0, last=now}; sims[em]=sdat
		end
		local dt = now - sdat.last; sdat.last = now
		if dt<0 then dt=0 elseif dt>1 then dt=1 end
		if emitting and rate>0 then
			sdat.frac = sdat.frac + rate*dt
			local toEmit = math.floor(sdat.frac); sdat.frac = sdat.frac - toEmit
			if toEmit > SIM_CAP then toEmit = SIM_CAP end
			local deaths = sdat.deaths
			for k=1,toEmit do deaths[#deaths+1] = now + (lmin+(lmax-lmin)*math.random())/ts end
		end
		local kept = {}
		for _,dtime in ipairs(sdat.deaths) do if dtime>now then kept[#kept+1]=dtime end end
		sdat.deaths = kept
		if #kept==0 and not emitting then sims[em]=nil end
		return #kept
	end

	local function burstAlive(b, now)
		local age = now - (b.born or now); if age<0 then age=0 end
		local ts = b.ts or 1; if ts<=0 then ts=1 end
		local ageSim = age * ts
		local lmin = b.lmin or 0; local lmax = b.lmax or 1
		if ageSim <= lmin then return b.n end
		if ageSim >= lmax then return 0 end
		return b.n * (1 - (ageSim-lmin)/math.max(1e-6,(lmax-lmin)))
	end

	-- EXPENSIVE: walk the selection/workspace once and cache the emitter list + static base count.
	-- Throttled (called ~3x/sec + on SelectionChanged), NOT every frame.
	local function gatherScope()
		local emitters={}
		local baseN=0
		local scopeSet={}
		local s3d={}
		local sTrails={}
		local sBeams={}
		local function consider(inst)
			if inst:IsA("BasePart") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, spec.TAG) then s3d[inst]=true; return end
			-- A transformed trail draws its own ribbon exactly like a normal Trail (+1 when enabled),
			-- and ALSO has emitted copies. Those live in the Terrain container, out of scope for this
			-- walk, so they are counted via TrailRuntime in computeTarget instead.
			if inst:IsA("Trail") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, trailSpec.TAG) then
				sTrails[inst]=true
				local ok,en = pcall(function() return inst.Enabled end)
				if ok and en then baseN=baseN+1 end
				return
			end
			-- A transformed beam draws its own ribbon exactly like a normal Beam (+1 when enabled),
			-- and ALSO has emitted copies counted via BeamRuntime in computeTarget.
			if inst:IsA("Beam") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, beamSpec.TAG) then
				sBeams[inst]=true
				local ok,en = pcall(function() return inst.Enabled end)
				if ok and en then baseN=baseN+1 end
				return
			end
			if inst:IsA("ParticleEmitter") then
				emitters[#emitters+1]=inst; scopeSet[inst]=true
			elseif inst:IsA("Beam") or inst:IsA("Trail")
				or inst:IsA("PointLight") or inst:IsA("SpotLight") or inst:IsA("SurfaceLight") then
				local ok,en=pcall(function() return inst.Enabled end)
				if ok and en then baseN=baseN+1 end
			end
		end
		pcall(function()
			local sel=selection:Get()
			if #sel>0 then
				local seen={}
				for _,obj in ipairs(sel) do
					if obj==game or obj==workspace then continue end
					if seen[obj] then continue end; seen[obj]=true
					consider(obj)
					-- depth-limited like every other window (a selected map root with
					-- huge descendant counts used to freeze Studio per selection change)
					for _,d in ipairs(ctx.descendantsTo(obj, 8)) do if not seen[d] then seen[d]=true; consider(d) end end
				end
			end
			-- No selection: scope stays empty on purpose. We no longer scan the whole
			-- workspace -- the counter only reflects the CURRENT SELECTION's enabled VFX,
			-- plus any :Emit() bursts (added in computeTarget regardless of scope).
		end)
		for em in pairs(sims) do if not scopeSet[em] or not em.Parent then sims[em]=nil end end
		scopeEmitters=emitters; baseCount=baseN; scope3D=s3d; scopeTrails=sTrails; scopeBeams=sBeams
	end

	-- CHEAP: tick the sim on the cached emitters + bursts. Runs every frame.
	local function computeTarget(now)
		local total = baseCount
		local forceAnalytic = #scopeEmitters > ANALYTIC_SCOPE_LIMIT
		for _,em in ipairs(scopeEmitters) do
			if em.Parent then
				local ok,en = pcall(function() return em.Enabled end)
				local emitting = ok and en and em.Rate>0
				if emitting or sims[em] then
					local okc, alive = pcall(simEmitter, em, now, emitting, forceAnalytic)
					if okc and alive then total = total + alive end
				end
			end
		end
		local okc3, c3 = pcall(EmitterRuntime.countForParts, scope3D)
		if okc3 and c3 then total = total + c3 end
		local okt, ct = pcall(TrailRuntime.countForTrails, scopeTrails)
		if okt and ct then total = total + ct end
		local okb, cb = pcall(BeamRuntime.countForBeams, scopeBeams)
		if okb and cb then total = total + cb end
		local bursts = ctx.pcBursts
		if bursts then
			local i=1
			while i<=#bursts do
				local b=bursts[i]
				local dead = (not b) or (not b.emitter) or (not b.emitter.Parent)
				local alive = 0
				if not dead then alive = burstAlive(b, now); if alive<=0.5 then dead=true end end
				if dead then table.remove(bursts, i) else total = total + alive; i=i+1 end
			end
		end
		return total
	end

	local function mount(App, WIN_ID)
		cleanup()

		gui=Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		gui.Name="SineVFX_ParticleCounter"
		gui.ResetOnSpawn=false
		gui.ZIndexBehavior=Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		gui.DisplayOrder=20000
		gui.Parent=coreGui
		if WinFactory.keepGuiOnTop then pcall(function() WinFactory.keepGuiOnTop(gui) end) end

		local badge=Instance.new("Frame")
		badge.Name="Badge"
		badge.Size=UDim2.new(0,WIN_W,0,WIN_H)
		badge.BackgroundColor3=C.hub; badge:SetAttribute("SVFXhub",true)
		badge.BorderSizePixel=0; badge.Parent=gui
		UIKit.corner(badge,CORNER)
		-- crisp solid accent border (manual stroke so the theme system doesn't fade it to 0.3)
		local bstroke=Instance.new("UIStroke")
		bstroke.Color=C.accent; bstroke.Thickness=1; bstroke.Transparency=0
		bstroke.ApplyStrokeMode=Enum.ApplyStrokeMode.Border; bstroke.Parent=badge
		-- follow the global UI Scale (Settings slider) like real windows
		if WinFactory.registerScaled then pcall(function() WinFactory.registerScaled(badge) end) end

		local function applyCorner(id)
			local p = POS[id] or POS.BC
			badge.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(p[1], p[2])
			badge.Position    = UDim2.new(p[1], p[3], p[2], p[4])
		end
		local lastCorner = plugin:GetSetting(K_CORNER) or "BC"
		applyCorner(lastCorner)

		local lbl=Instance.new("TextLabel")
		if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(lbl, "Live count of particles alive in the scene.") end
		lbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-14,1,0); lbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,7,0,0)
		lbl.BackgroundTransparency=1
		lbl.Text="0 Particles"
		lbl.TextColor3=C.textDim; lbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold
		lbl.TextSize=12; lbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Center
		lbl.TextYAlignment=Enum.TextYAlignment.Center
		lbl.ZIndex=2; lbl.Parent=badge

		-- prime the scope + displayed value so it doesn't ease up from 0 on open
		gatherScope()
		local shown = computeTarget(os.clock())
		local last = -1
		local function setLabel(disp)
			if disp~=last then last=disp; lbl.Text=commas(disp).." "..(disp==1 and "Particle" or "Particles") end
		end
		setLabel(math.floor(shown+0.5))

		-- selection change -> rescan immediately (no waiting for the throttle)
		selConn=selection.SelectionChanged:Connect(gatherScope)

		local scanAcc=0; local cornerAcc=0; local simAcc=0
		loopConn=runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
			-- Don't run the scope scan / particle sim when the counter isn't actually shown
			-- (gui disabled or badge hidden) -- no point simulating a count nobody can see.
			if not (gui and gui.Parent and gui.Enabled and badge.Visible) then return end
			-- poll the Settings corner (cheap, every ~0.3s) -- kept outside the sim throttle
			cornerAcc=cornerAcc+dt
			if cornerAcc>=0.3 then cornerAcc=0
				local cur = plugin:GetSetting(K_CORNER) or "BC"
				if cur~=lastCorner then lastCorner=cur; applyCorner(cur) end
			end
			-- Throttle the scope scan + particle sim + label to ~30fps: a live count doesn't need 60fps,
			-- and computeTarget is the plugin's most expensive per-frame call (per-particle sim over the
			-- whole selection). Accumulate real dt so the ease stays time-correct.
			simAcc=simAcc+dt
			-- ~15Hz: a live particle count doesn't need faster updates, and the shown value is eased
			-- with real dt so it still glides. Halves the plugin's most expensive per-frame call.
			if simAcc < 0.066 then return end
			local sdt=simAcc; simAcc=0
			scanAcc=scanAcc+sdt
			if scanAcc>=1.0 then scanAcc=0; gatherScope() end
			local target = computeTarget(os.clock())
			shown = shown + (target-shown)*math.clamp(sdt*10,0,1)
			if math.abs(target-shown) < 0.5 then shown = target end
			setLabel(math.floor(shown+0.5))
		end)
	end

	return { mount=mount, unmount=cleanup, isOpen=isOpen }
end

-- TransformWindow: independent floating "Transform" button (its OWN ScreenGui, not part
-- of the Emit window). Shows when the selection holds transformable instances; the Emit window
-- hides itself for that same selection so the two never overlap.
--
-- Two kinds are transformable, each with its own blueprint and builder:
--   childless BasePart -> 3D particle emitter (Utilities.EmitterSpec, transform/untransform)
--   Trail              -> 3D trail            (Utilities.TrailSpec,  transformTrail/untransformTrail)
-- transformAny() routes by class. Eligibility itself lives in Main's ctx.getTransformables --
-- notably a Trail only qualifies when the selection is Trails-only, which is what keeps the
-- Emit/Transform swap exclusive (a Trail, unlike a childless part, is emittable).
--
-- Position is a 4-corner setting (SineVFX_transformCorner) just like the Hub FAB, changed
-- live from Settings.
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit, WinFactory)
	local C          = Theme.C
	local plugin     = ctx.plugin
	local coreGui    = ctx.coreGui
	local selection  = ctx.selection
	local runService = ctx.runService
	local CHS        = ctx.CHS
	local spec       = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).EmitterSpec
	local trailSpec  = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).TrailSpec
	local beamSpec   = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).BeamSpec
	local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")

	local MARGIN = 10
	local BTN_W, BTN_H = 120, 34
	local K_CORNER = "SineVFX_transformCorner"

	local gui = nil
	local selConn, pollConn = nil, nil

	local function isOpen() return gui ~= nil and gui.Parent ~= nil end
	local function cleanup()
		if selConn  then selConn:Disconnect();  selConn  = nil end
		if pollConn then pollConn:Disconnect(); pollConn = nil end
		if gui then gui:Destroy(); gui = nil end
	end

	-- Turn a plain BasePart into a SineVFX 3D emitter (blueprint = Utilities.EmitterSpec):
	-- wrap in a tagged Model, ghost the part into a reusable RenderPart, and lay down the
	-- Properties folder of grouped Configurations. Originals are stashed for a clean revert.
	local function transform(part)
		-- The original part STAYS as the emitter root (invisible marker); a CLONE of it becomes
		-- the RenderPart template (what each particle looks like, unlocked so it's easy to tweak).
		-- Clone WITH children: attachments/trails/emitters already on the part are what make the
		-- particle look like the part, and Clone() remaps internal Attachment0/1 refs to the copies
		-- for free. (This used to ClearAllChildren(), which silently left every nested effect behind
		-- on the now-invisible root part -- it read as "the 3D particle doesn't clone what's inside
		-- it".) The originals are then removed from the root so nothing renders twice; untransform()
		-- moves them back out of RenderPart.
		local render = part:Clone()
		-- SineVFX bookkeeping the clone must never carry (a part transformed once before).
		do
			local sp = render:FindFirstChild(spec.PROPS_FOLDER); if sp then sp:Destroy() end
			local sr = render:FindFirstChild(spec.RENDER_NAME);  if sr then sr:Destroy() end
		end
		-- Nested tags (a transformed Trail inside) are deliberately LEFT on the copies: those rows
		-- stay editable in Properties and drive the template the particles clone from.
		for _, ch in ipairs(part:GetChildren()) do ch:Destroy() end
		render.Name = spec.RENDER_NAME
		render.Locked = false
		render.Anchored = true
		render.CanCollide = false
		render.CastShadow = false
		render.Transparency = 1  -- hidden template (same as the source part); only spawned particles render
		for _, tg in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTags(render)) do CollectionService:RemoveTag(render, tg) end
		local origTrans = part.Transparency
		part:SetAttribute("_origTransparency", origTrans)
		part.Transparency = 1  -- the emitter root itself is just a marker now; only clones render
		-- EmitCount has no useful nil-default (0 particles = the Emit button silently does
		-- nothing) unlike EmitDelay/EmitDuration where nil->0 IS the sensible default, so seed
		-- it explicitly -- otherwise a fresh 3D particle's Emit button no-ops until the user
		-- happens to type into the Count box for this specific instance.
		part:SetAttribute("EmitCount", 1)
		local props = Instance.new("Folder"); props.Name = spec.PROPS_FOLDER
		for _, g in ipairs(spec.groups) do
			local cfg = Instance.new("Configuration"); cfg.Name = g.name
			for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do if d.t ~= "object" then cfg:SetAttribute(d.a, d.d) end end  -- object rows are child instances, not attributes
			cfg.Parent = props
		end
		-- Size is an ABSOLUTE dimension in studs, not a scale multiplier -- seed it from the
		-- RenderPart's own authored size so a freshly-transformed part looks unchanged.
		local appCfg = props:FindFirstChild("Appearance")
		if appCfg then
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeX", NumberSequence.new(render.Size.X))
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeY", NumberSequence.new(render.Size.Y))
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeZ", NumberSequence.new(render.Size.Z))
			appCfg:SetAttribute("Material", render.Material)  -- keep the part's own material, not the spec default
			appCfg:SetAttribute("Transparency", NumberSequence.new(origTrans))  -- keep the part's own transparency
			appCfg:SetAttribute("Color", ColorSequence.new(render.Color))  -- keep the part's own color, not the spec default white
		end
		props.Parent = part
		render.Parent = part
		CollectionService:AddTag(part, spec.TAG)
		return part
	end

	-- Reverse transform(): restore the part's original props/name and unwrap it; anything the
	-- user nested inside RenderPart (a Trail, etc.) rides along and is kept.
	local function untransform(part)
		CollectionService:RemoveTag(part, spec.TAG)
		local r = part:FindFirstChild(spec.RENDER_NAME)
		if r then
			-- Hand the nested content back to the part instead of destroying it with the template:
			-- transform() moved these copies IN, so revert has to move them back out or the user
			-- loses whatever they authored inside RenderPart.
			for _, ch in ipairs(r:GetChildren()) do ch.Parent = part end
			r:Destroy()
		end
		local pf = part:FindFirstChild(spec.PROPS_FOLDER); if pf then pf:Destroy() end
		local ot = part:GetAttribute("_origTransparency")
		if ot ~= nil then part.Transparency = ot end
		part:SetAttribute("_origTransparency", nil)
		return part
	end

	-- Turn a plain Trail into a SineVFX TRANSFORMED TRAIL (blueprint = Utilities.TrailSpec).
	-- The trail KEEPS RENDERING and keeps its own Enabled: TrailRuntime drives it live from these
	-- attributes, so editing Colour/Size/Transparency changes what you see straight away. (An
	-- earlier version disabled the source and treated it as a hidden template, which meant every
	-- property edit appeared to do nothing.) It only gains the tag and a Properties folder.
	--
	-- Seeding: every attr whose name matches a real Trail property starts from the LIVE value, so a
	-- freshly transformed trail looks exactly like the trail you authored. Two don't match by name
	-- and are seeded explicitly:
	--   Gradient <- trail.Color  (the along-length ColorSequence; our Color is the over-life one)
	--   Enabled  <- NOT seeded   (see below)
	local function transformTrail(trail)
		local props = Instance.new("Folder"); props.Name = trailSpec.PROPS_FOLDER
		for _, g in ipairs(trailSpec.groups) do
			local cfg = Instance.new("Configuration"); cfg.Name = g.name
			for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
				if d.t == "object" then continue end  -- object rows are child instances, not attributes
				local v = d.d
				-- Enabled means DRAW (the emit switch is Emission > Emitting, a separate attr), so it
				-- MUST be seeded from the live ribbon. Leaving it on the spec default (true) silently
				-- switched a disabled Trail/Beam back on the moment it was transformed, and the
				-- runtime then drove it on forever.
				local ok, live = pcall(function() return (trail :: any)[d.a] end)
				if ok and live ~= nil then v = live end
				cfg:SetAttribute(d.a, v)
			end
			cfg.Parent = props
		end
		local app = props:FindFirstChild("Appearance")
		if app then
			app:SetAttribute("Gradient", trail.Color)                     -- along-length colour (the trail's own)
			app:SetAttribute("TransparencyGradient", trail.Transparency)  -- along-length transparency (the trail's own)
			app:SetAttribute("Transparency", NumberSequence.new(0))       -- over-life extra: none to start
			app:SetAttribute("TextureLength", NumberSequence.new((typeof(trail.TextureLength) == "number") and trail.TextureLength or 1))
		end
		local emg = props:FindFirstChild("Emission")
		if emg then
			emg:SetAttribute("MinLength", NumberSequence.new((typeof(trail.MinLength) == "number") and trail.MinLength or 0.1))
			emg:SetAttribute("MaxLength", NumberSequence.new((typeof(trail.MaxLength) == "number") and trail.MaxLength or 0))
		end
		-- EmitCount has no useful nil-default (0 copies = the Emit button silently does nothing),
		-- unlike EmitDelay/EmitDuration where nil->0 IS the sensible default. Same reason the part
		-- path seeds it: otherwise Emit no-ops until the user happens to type into the Count box.
		trail:SetAttribute("EmitCount", 1)

		props.Parent = trail
		CollectionService:AddTag(trail, trailSpec.TAG)
		return trail
	end

	-- Reverse transformTrail(): drop the tag + folder, and that is the whole revert. The source
	-- trail was never hidden and its attachments were never moved (Size scales WidthScale, not the
	-- attachment spacing), so there is nothing to restore. TrailRuntime cleans up the copies it
	-- emitted on its own: they are pure runtime output living in the Terrain container.
	local function untransformTrail(trail)
		CollectionService:RemoveTag(trail, trailSpec.TAG)
		local pf = trail:FindFirstChild(trailSpec.PROPS_FOLDER); if pf then pf:Destroy() end
		trail:SetAttribute("EmitCount", nil)
		trail:SetAttribute("_origEnabled", nil)  -- legacy: an older transform stashed this
		return trail
	end

	-- Turn a plain Beam into a SineVFX TRANSFORMED BEAM (blueprint = Utilities.BeamSpec). Mirrors
	-- transformTrail exactly: the beam KEEPS RENDERING and keeps its own Enabled, BeamRuntime drives
	-- it live from these attributes, and it only gains the tag and a Properties folder. Every attr
	-- whose name matches a real Beam property seeds from the LIVE value, so a freshly transformed
	-- beam looks exactly like the beam you authored. Two are handled specially:
	--   Gradient <- beam.Color  (the along-length ColorSequence; our Color is the over-life one)
	--   Enabled  <- NOT seeded  (ours means "emit copies", the Beam's means "draw")
	local function transformBeam(beam)
		local props = Instance.new("Folder"); props.Name = beamSpec.PROPS_FOLDER
		for _, g in ipairs(beamSpec.groups) do
			local cfg = Instance.new("Configuration"); cfg.Name = g.name
			for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
				if d.t == "object" then continue end  -- object rows are child instances, not attributes
				local v = d.d
				-- see transformTrail: Enabled means DRAW and must come from the live beam, or
				-- transforming a disabled beam silently switches it back on.
				local ok, live = pcall(function() return (beam :: any)[d.a] end)
				if ok and live ~= nil then v = live end
				cfg:SetAttribute(d.a, v)
			end
			cfg.Parent = props
		end
		local app = props:FindFirstChild("Appearance")
		if app then
			app:SetAttribute("Gradient", beam.Color)                    -- along-length colour (the beam's own)
			app:SetAttribute("TransparencyGradient", beam.Transparency) -- along-length transparency (the beam's own)
			app:SetAttribute("Transparency", NumberSequence.new(0))     -- over-life extra: none to start
			for _, a in ipairs({"TextureLength","TextureSpeed"}) do
				local ok, live = pcall(function() return (beam :: any)[a] end)
				app:SetAttribute(a, NumberSequence.new((ok and typeof(live) == "number") and live or 1))
			end
		end
		-- Width0/Width1/CurveSize0/CurveSize1 are NumberSequences over lifetime, but the live Beam
		-- exposes them as plain numbers -- the generic loop above seeded raw numbers, so re-seed
		-- them wrapped so the type matches the spec (and the graph editor has something to open).
		local shape = props:FindFirstChild("Shape")
		if shape then
			for _, a in ipairs({"Width0","Width1","CurveSize0","CurveSize1"}) do
				local ok, live = pcall(function() return (beam :: any)[a] end)
				local n = (ok and typeof(live) == "number") and live or (a:sub(1,5) == "Width" and 1 or 0)
				shape:SetAttribute(a, NumberSequence.new(n))
			end
		end
		-- EmitCount has no useful nil-default (0 copies = the Emit button silently does nothing).
		beam:SetAttribute("EmitCount", 1)

		props.Parent = beam
		CollectionService:AddTag(beam, beamSpec.TAG)
		return beam
	end

	-- Reverse transformBeam(): drop the tag + folder. The source beam was never hidden and its
	-- attachments were never moved, so there is nothing else to restore. BeamRuntime cleans up the
	-- copies it emitted on its own (pure runtime output in the Terrain container).
	local function untransformBeam(beam)
		CollectionService:RemoveTag(beam, beamSpec.TAG)
		local pf = beam:FindFirstChild(beamSpec.PROPS_FOLDER); if pf then pf:Destroy() end
		beam:SetAttribute("EmitCount", nil)
		return beam
	end

	-- getTransformables() hands back both kinds (childless BaseParts and Trails); route each to
	-- its own builder rather than making either one guess.
	local function transformAny(inst)
		if inst:IsA("Trail") then return transformTrail(inst) end
		if inst:IsA("Beam")  then return transformBeam(inst) end
		return transform(inst)
	end

	local function mount()
		cleanup()
		gui = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		gui.Name           = "SineVFX_Transform"
		gui.ResetOnSpawn   = false
		gui.ZIndexBehavior = Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Global
		gui.DisplayOrder   = 10002
		gui.Parent         = coreGui
		WinFactory.keepGuiOnTop(gui)

		local btn = Instance.new("TextButton")
		btn.Size             = UDim2.new(0, BTN_W, 0, BTN_H)
		btn.BackgroundColor3 = C.base; btn.BackgroundTransparency = 0; btn:SetAttribute("SVFXhubTrans", true)  -- base color, hub transparency
		btn.AutoButtonColor  = false; btn.BorderSizePixel = 0
		btn.Text             = "Transform"
		btn.TextColor3       = C.textDim; btn.Font = Enum.Font.GothamBold; btn.TextSize = 15
		btn.Visible          = false
		btn.ZIndex           = 5
		btn.Parent           = gui
		UIKit.corner(btn, 8)
		local btnStroke = UIKit.makeBorder(btn, C.accent, 1.5)
		UIKit.hoverLift(btn, btn)  -- dim text brightens toward white on hover
		WinFactory.registerScaled(btn)
		-- Live theme switch (applyLive) skips this button on the fast path: its background is tagged
		-- SVFXhubTrans, which recolorGui treats as a no-op, so a changed C.base never reaches it (and the
		-- dim text / accent stroke only recolor via fragile value-matching). Re-read Theme.C here so the
		-- button fully recolors live. Runs after recolorGui/applyThemeProps; self-prunes when the gui is gone.
		if ctx.themeRefreshers then
			table.insert(ctx.themeRefreshers, function()
				if not btn or not btn.Parent then return false end
				btn.BackgroundColor3 = C.base
				btn.TextColor3       = C.textDim
				btn:SetAttribute("HoverBase", C.textDim)
				if btnStroke and btnStroke.Parent then btnStroke.Color = C.accent end
				return true
			end)
		end

		-- corner placement (Settings > "Transform Pos"). Default top-left.
		local corner = plugin:GetSetting(K_CORNER) or "TL"
		local function applyCorner(c)
			corner = c
			local right  = (c == "BR" or c == "TR")
			local bottom = (c == "BR" or c == "BL")
			local ax = right and 1 or 0
			local ay = bottom and 1 or 0
			btn.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(ax, ay)
			btn.Position    = UDim2.new(ax, right and -MARGIN or MARGIN, ay, bottom and -MARGIN or MARGIN)
		end
		applyCorner(corner)

		local function refresh()
			btn.Visible = #ctx.getTransformables() > 0
		end

if UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(btn, "Hand the selection over to SineVFX. A part becomes a particle emitter that spawns copies of itself; a Trail or Beam gains graph-driven properties and can emit copies.") end
				btn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			local made = {}
			for _, p in ipairs(ctx.getTransformables()) do
				local ok2, m = pcall(transformAny, p)
				if ok2 and m then made[#made+1] = m end
			end
			if #made > 0 then
				selection:Set(made)
				pcall(function() CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Transform") end)
			end
			refresh()
		end)

		selConn = selection.SelectionChanged:Connect(refresh)
		local lastScale = (WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1
		local _pollAcc = 0
		pollConn = runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
			-- Throttle ~4fps: the corner SETTING + ui scale are user-driven and change rarely, so
			-- polling them (a plugin:GetSetting on this always-present button) is pure idle waste. A
			-- quarter-second latency on a corner/scale change is imperceptible.
			_pollAcc += dt
			if _pollAcc < 0.25 then return end
			_pollAcc = 0
			local c = plugin:GetSetting(K_CORNER) or "TL"
			if c ~= corner then applyCorner(c) end
			local s = (WinFactory.getUiScale and WinFactory.getUiScale()) or 1
			if s ~= lastScale then lastScale = s; applyCorner(corner) end
		end)
		gui.AncestryChanged:Connect(function()
			if not gui or not gui.Parent then if pollConn then pollConn:Disconnect(); pollConn = nil end end
		end)
		refresh()
	end

	return { mount = mount, unmount = cleanup, isOpen = isOpen }
end

-- ColorPalette: color preview/apply/revert logic
-- Usage: local CP = require(this)(ctx, resetSlidersFn)
--   ctx needs: selection, CHS
--   getTargetsFn: function() -> array of {inst, prop, seq}
--   resetSlidersFn: called after commit/reset
return function(ctx, getTargetsFn, resetSlidersFn)
	local CHS       = ctx.CHS
	local selection = ctx.selection

	local savedColors    = {}
	local originalColors = {}
	local isPreviewing   = false
	local currentH, currentS, currentV = 0, 1, 1
	local currentColor  = Color3.fromHSV(0, 1, 1)
	local activeMode    = "Replace" -- "Replace" or "Multiply"

	local CP = {}

	function CP.getCurrentColor() return currentColor end
	function CP.getActiveMode()   return activeMode end
	function CP.isPreviewing()    return isPreviewing end

	function CP.setColor(h, s, v)
		currentH = h; currentS = s; currentV = v
		currentColor = Color3.fromHSV(h, s, v)
	end

	function CP.setMode(mode)
		activeMode = mode
	end

	function CP.cacheOriginals()
		originalColors = {}
		savedColors    = {}
		for _, t in ipairs(getTargetsFn()) do
			pcall(function()
				local val = t.inst[t.prop]
				originalColors[t.inst] = { prop=t.prop, val=val, seq=t.seq }
				savedColors[t.inst]    = { prop=t.prop, val=val, seq=t.seq }
			end)
		end
	end

	local function applyColorToData(data)
		if data.seq then
			local newKps = {}
			for _, kp in ipairs(data.val.Keypoints) do
				local nc = currentColor
				if activeMode == "Multiply" then
					local c = kp.Value
					nc = Color3.new(math.clamp(c.R*nc.R,0,1), math.clamp(c.G*nc.G,0,1), math.clamp(c.B*nc.B,0,1))
				end
				table.insert(newKps, ColorSequenceKeypoint.new(kp.Time, nc))
			end
			return ColorSequence.new(newKps)
		else
			local nc = currentColor
			if activeMode == "Multiply" then
				local c = data.val
				nc = Color3.new(math.clamp(c.R*nc.R,0,1), math.clamp(c.G*nc.G,0,1), math.clamp(c.B*nc.B,0,1))
			end
			return nc
		end
	end

	function CP.revertPreview()
		if not isPreviewing then return end
		for inst, data in pairs(savedColors) do
			pcall(function() inst[data.prop] = data.val end)
		end
		isPreviewing = false
	end

	function CP.applyPreview()
		if not isPreviewing then return end
		for inst, data in pairs(savedColors) do
			pcall(function() inst[data.prop] = applyColorToData(data) end)
		end
	end

	function CP.startPreview()
		isPreviewing = true
		CP.applyPreview()
	end

	function CP.commitPreview()
		if not isPreviewing then return end
		CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Color Change Before")
		for inst, data in pairs(savedColors) do
			pcall(function() inst[data.prop] = applyColorToData(data) end)
		end
		isPreviewing = false
		CP.cacheOriginals()
		CHS:SetWaypoint("SineVFX Color Change After")
		if resetSlidersFn then resetSlidersFn() end
	end

	function CP.resetToOriginal()
		CP.revertPreview()
		for inst, data in pairs(originalColors) do
			pcall(function() inst[data.prop] = data.val end)
		end
		CP.cacheOriginals()
		if resetSlidersFn then resetSlidersFn() end
	end

	return CP
end

-- EmitterRuntime: edit-mode PREVIEW for SineVFX 3D emitters (blueprint = Utilities.EmitterSpec).
-- While active, every tagged part whose Appearance.Enabled==true clones its RenderPart into a
-- flushable container under workspace.Terrain ("SineVFXParticles", Archivable=false) and animates
-- the clones: spawn at the part CENTRE, fly along the part's up axis at Speed, animate SizeX/Y/Z
-- and Transparency over Lifetime (NumberSequence + envelope, like real ParticleEmitter props --
-- envelope is a fixed per-particle random offset picked once at spawn), and follow the Color
-- gradient. Preview only.
--
-- Nested effects inside RenderPart (a ParticleEmitter/Trail/Sound/etc. the user drops in): this
-- module does NOTHING special with them. It just :Clone()s RenderPart as-is -- so a spawned
-- particle carries the nested content in EXACTLY the state the user authored on the template
-- (a plain mirror; no forcing on/off). When the 3D particle emits, it just emits. On death the
-- clone is Destroyed, which naturally stops whatever it was playing.
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService        = game:GetService("RunService")
local EmitterSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).EmitterSpec
local TAG, PROPS, RENDER = EmitterSpec.TAG, EmitterSpec.PROPS_FOLDER, EmitterSpec.RENDER_NAME
local Theme = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Theme)
local GEN_ATTR = "SineVFX_RuntimeGen"
-- Particle container name. A HOST BINDING (like GEN_ATTR/genHolder) so the SIM CORE never
-- hardcodes it. Plugin and module deliberately SHARE this folder. Trade-off: the preview's flush()
-- empties the whole folder, so stopping the preview also clears any module-spawned particles in
-- edit mode. Harmless in Play (no plugin there).
local CONTAINER = "SineVFXParticles"
-- Host gate for continuous Rate emission (see the SIM CORE marker). The plugin preview always owns
-- it in edit mode; the shipped module overrides this to stand down while the preview is running.
local function RATE_EMIT() return true end
-- Preview sim rate. Was true (~30fps) to keep editing cheap, but that made the preview visibly
-- choppier than the shipped module, which runs full rate -- so the thing you author looked worse
-- than the thing you ship. Now full rate in both. If a heavy scene drags while editing, the
-- per-emitter StepRate option (Optimization group) throttles individual emitters instead.
local PREVIEW_THROTTLE = false
-- generation token holder: CoreGui is per-Studio (no Team Create replication,
-- never saved into the place), while still shared with any stale plugin copy
-- in THIS Studio session, which is the case the token exists for
local _genFolder  -- cached; step() checks this every frame, so avoid a per-frame CoreGui:FindFirstChild
local function genHolder()
	if _genFolder and _genFolder.Parent then return _genFolder end
	local cg = game:GetService("CoreGui")
	local f = cg:FindFirstChild("SineVFX_RuntimeState")
	if not f then
		f = Instance.new("Folder")
		f.Name = "SineVFX_RuntimeState"
		f.Parent = cg
	end
	_genFolder = f
	return f
end
-- ===== SIM CORE (BEGIN) =====================================================================
-- Everything between these markers is SLICED VERBATIM into the shipped runtime module by
-- VfxModuleWindow (see buildModuleSrc). It used to be hand-copied there, and drifted every single
-- time this file changed -- the planted copy was missing the whole bezier weave, all routing, and
-- nested spawn. One copy now; do not reintroduce a second.
--
-- The core may ONLY use names the host supplies above this marker:
--   CollectionService, TAG, PROPS, RENDER, EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER, GEN_ATTR, genHolder(),
--   CONTAINER, RATE_EMIT(), PREVIEW_THROTTLE
-- It must NOT touch Theme, RunService, Selection, or anything plugin-only -- the runtime module has
-- no access to those. genHolder in particular is host-specific: the plugin uses CoreGui (which a
-- shipped game cannot read), the module uses Terrain.
local M = {}
local conn
local active = false
local myGeneration = 0
local container
local particles = {}   -- {inst, part, vel, age, life}
local accs = {}        -- [part] = emission accumulator
local shapeClk = {}    -- [part] = emitter cycle clock (s) for animated spawn Shape
local optAcc = {}      -- [part] = StepRate throttle accumulator (Optimization group)
local _idleAcc = 0     -- idle fast-path accumulator (see step)
local _previewAcc = 0  -- 30fps preview throttle accumulator
local MAX = 1500
-- Spacing (min-gap) scope. A MANUAL Emit burst (M.burst) tags its particles with a unique batch id
-- and only spaces against particles in the SAME batch, so pressing Emit again doesn't treat the
-- previous burst's still-alive particles as obstacles. 0 = continuous Rate emission (spaces against
-- all live particles of the emitter, as before).
local _curBatch = 0
local _burstSeq = 0
local function ensureContainer()
	if container and container.Parent then return container end
	local terrain = workspace.Terrain
	container = terrain:FindFirstChild(CONTAINER)
	if not container then
		container = Instance.new("Folder")
		container.Name = CONTAINER
		container.Archivable = false
		container.Parent = terrain
	end
	return container
end
-- NO PART POOL. Every spawn clones the RenderPart fresh and every death destroys it. Recycling was
-- an allocation optimisation, but a banked part carries its whole authored state -- nested contents,
-- attachment positions, ribbon properties, transformed attributes -- and every one of those had to be
-- re-synced by hand on reuse. Whatever got missed showed up as a particle rendering something the
-- template no longer said. A fresh clone is right by construction.
-- Nested > EnableOnSpawn / EmitOnSpawn, done the way the PLUGIN would do it to that instance,
-- not with a bare `.Enabled = true`:
--   * a transformed Trail/Beam is driven from its ATTRIBUTES, so writing .Enabled is undone by
--     driveSource on the very next frame -- it has to go through Appearance.Enabled, and "emit"
--     means Emission.Emitting + a burst of its own EmitCount, exactly like the Emit button.
--   * a native ParticleEmitter emits its EmitCount, not a bare single :Emit().
-- EmitDelay/EmitDuration are honoured the same way the Emit button honours them.
local function _nestedFire(ch, doEnable, doEmit)
	if not (doEnable or doEmit) then return end
	local isTrail = CollectionService:HasTag(ch, "SineVFXTransformedTrail")
	local isBeam  = CollectionService:HasTag(ch, "SineVFXTransformedBeam")
	local delay = tonumber(ch:GetAttribute("EmitDelay")) or 0
	local dur   = tonumber(ch:GetAttribute("EmitDuration")) or 0
	local count = tonumber(ch:GetAttribute("EmitCount")) or 0

	-- An EMIT with nothing to emit does nothing at all: Count 0 means no burst, Duration 0 means no
	-- on-window, so both 0 is a no-op for every kind (matching EmitWindow's toggleable rule). ENABLE
	-- is a separate instruction and is not subject to it. Sounds have no Count, so Duration 0 just
	-- means "play once, natural length".
	local nothingToEmit = (count <= 0 and dur <= 0)
	local function fire()
		if not ch.Parent then return end
		if isTrail or isBeam then
			local pf  = ch:FindFirstChild("Properties")
			local emg = pf and pf:FindFirstChild("Emission")
			-- ENABLE on a transformed ribbon means Emission > Emitting, NOT Appearance > Enabled.
			-- Enabling a ParticleEmitter starts it emitting continuously; the ribbon equivalent of
			-- that is Emitting. Appearance.Enabled only says "draw the source ribbon" and is left
			-- exactly as authored -- nothing here force-enables what the user turned off.
			if doEnable and emg then emg:SetAttribute("Emitting", true) end
			if doEmit and nothingToEmit then return end
			if doEmit then
				if count > 0 then
					local kit = isBeam and M.Beam or M.Trail
					if kit and kit.burst then pcall(function() kit.burst(ch, count) end) end
				end
				if dur > 0 and emg then
					emg:SetAttribute("Emitting", true)
					task.delay(dur, function() if ch.Parent and emg.Parent then emg:SetAttribute("Emitting", false) end end)
				end
			end
			return
		end
		if ch:IsA("ParticleEmitter") then
			if doEnable then ch.Enabled = true end
			if doEmit and count > 0 then pcall(function() ch:Emit(count) end) end
		elseif ch:IsA("Sound") then
			pcall(function() ch:Play() end)
		elseif ch:IsA("Beam") or ch:IsA("Trail") or ch:IsA("Light") then
			if doEnable or dur > 0 then
				ch.Enabled = true
				if dur > 0 then task.delay(dur, function() if ch.Parent then ch.Enabled = false end end) end
			end
		end
	end

	if delay > 0 then task.delay(delay, fire) else fire() end
end
local function acquire(template)
	return template:Clone()
end
local function release(inst, template)
	-- No banking: a dead particle is destroyed outright, which also stops whatever it was playing
	-- and takes its nested contents with it.
	if inst then pcall(function() inst:Destroy() end) end
end
local function flush()
	for _, p in ipairs(particles) do if p.inst then pcall(function() p.inst:Destroy() end) end end
	table.clear(particles); table.clear(accs); table.clear(optAcc)
	if container then pcall(function() container:Destroy() end); container = nil end
end
local function gget(holder, name, default)
	if not holder then return default end
	local v = holder:GetAttribute(name)
	if v == nil then return default end
	return v
end
local function cfgOf(part, name)
	local pf = part:FindFirstChild(PROPS)
	return pf and pf:FindFirstChild(name) or nil
end
-- ===== FINAL DESTINATION =====
-- Attributes cannot hold Instance references, so Custom targets are ObjectValue children under
-- Properties/Particles/Destinations. Resolved per-particle and re-read every frame, so a particle
-- tracks a target that moves. NOTE: this is deliberately NOT p.anchor -- anchor is the orbit centre
-- for AnchorRotation/AnchorRotSpeed, and repointing it would silently move every vortex effect.
local DESTF = EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER
local _seqIdx = {}   -- [part] = round-robin cursor for RoutePick="Even"
-- uniform-ish random point inside a ball of radius r (cube-root keeps it from clumping at the
-- centre, which a naive radius*random() would do)
local function randInBall(r)
	local u = math.random()^(1/3)
	local z = math.random()*2 - 1
	local th = math.random()*2*math.pi
	local s = math.sqrt(math.max(0, 1 - z*z))
	return Vector3.new(s*math.cos(th), s*math.sin(th), z) * (u*r)
end
local function destWorld(inst)
	if not inst or not inst.Parent then return nil end
	if inst:IsA("BasePart") then return inst.Position end
	if inst:IsA("Attachment") then return inst.WorldPosition end
	if inst:IsA("Model") then local ok, cf = pcall(function() return inst:GetPivot() end); if ok then return cf.Position end end
	return nil
end
-- world -> the particle's TRACKING space (part-local when locked, world otherwise)
local function destTracked(inst, part, locked)
	local w = destWorld(inst); if not w then return nil end
	return locked and part.CFrame:PointToObjectSpace(w) or w
end
local function destList(part)
	local pc = cfgOf(part, "Particles"); if not pc then return nil end
	local f = pc:FindFirstChild(DESTF); if not f then return nil end
	-- Stop at the first empty slot. The UI reveals TargetN only once Target(N-1) is filled, so the
	-- filled slots are contiguous; reading past a gap would route through a target whose row is
	-- hidden. There is no count attribute -- the slots themselves are the source of truth.
	local out
	for i = 1, 8 do
		local ov = f:FindFirstChild("Target" .. i)
		if not (ov and ov:IsA("ObjectValue") and ov.Value and destWorld(ov.Value)) then break end
		out = out or {}; out[#out+1] = ov.Value
	end
	return out
end
-- Catmull-Rom through the route points. CR passes exactly THROUGH every control point, which is the
-- whole reason it is used here: an InOrder route must still visit each target, so a corner-rounding
-- fillet (which cuts inside the corner and misses the target) is the wrong shape.
local function crPoint(p0,p1,p2,p3,u)
	local u2 = u*u; local u3 = u2*u
	return 0.5*((2*p1) + (-p0+p2)*u + (2*p0-5*p1+4*p2-p3)*u2 + (-p0+3*p1-3*p2+p3)*u3)
end
-- Point on route segment i (pts[i] -> pts[i+1]) at u, blended from the straight chord (sm=0, hard
-- corners) to the full spline (sm=1).
local function routeSegPt(pts, i, u, sm)
	local P1 = pts[math.min(#pts, i)]
	local P2 = pts[math.min(#pts, i+1)]
	local lin = P1:Lerp(P2, u)
	if sm <= 0 then return lin end
	local P0 = pts[math.max(1, i-1)]
	local P3 = pts[math.min(#pts, i+2)]
	return lin:Lerp(crPoint(P0,P1,P2,P3,u), sm)
end
-- pick ONE target for a spawning particle. basePosW is its world spawn point (for "Nearest").
local function pickDest(part, basePosW)
	local list = destList(part); if not list then return nil end
	local n = #list; if n == 1 then return list[1] end
	local mode = gget(cfgOf(part,"Particles"), "RoutePick", "Random")
	if mode == "Nearest" then
		local best, bd = list[1], math.huge
		for _, t in ipairs(list) do
			local w = destWorld(t)
			if w then local d = (w - basePosW).Magnitude; if d < bd then best, bd = t, d end end
		end
		return best
	elseif mode == "Even" then
		local k = (_seqIdx[part] or 0) % n + 1; _seqIdx[part] = k
		return list[k]
	end
	return list[math.random(n)]
end
-- sample a ColorSequence at t in [0,1]
local function evalColorSeq(seq, t)
	if typeof(seq) ~= "ColorSequence" then return Color3.new(1,1,1) end
	local kps = seq.Keypoints
	t = math.clamp(t, 0, 1)
	if t <= kps[1].Time then return kps[1].Value end
	for i = 1, #kps-1 do
		local a, b = kps[i], kps[i+1]
		if t >= a.Time and t <= b.Time then
			local span = b.Time - a.Time
			return a.Value:Lerp(b.Value, (span > 0) and (t-a.Time)/span or 0)
		end
	end
	return kps[#kps].Value
end
-- sample a NumberSequence at t in [0,1]. `seed` (in [-1,1], fixed per particle) applies the
-- keypoint's Envelope as a per-particle random offset -- one random draw per particle, held
-- constant while both Value and Envelope interpolate over its lifetime (matches how a real
-- ParticleEmitter's NumberSequenceKeypoint envelope behaves). seed defaults to 0 (no offset).
local function evalNumberSeq(seq, t, default, seed)
	if typeof(seq) ~= "NumberSequence" then return default or 0 end
	local kps = seq.Keypoints
	t = math.clamp(t, 0, 1)
	seed = seed or 0
	local function sampleAt(tt)
		if tt <= kps[1].Time then return kps[1].Value, kps[1].Envelope end
		for i = 1, #kps-1 do
			local a, b = kps[i], kps[i+1]
			if tt >= a.Time and tt <= b.Time then
				local span = b.Time - a.Time
				local f = (span > 0) and (tt-a.Time)/span or 0
				return a.Value + (b.Value - a.Value) * f, a.Envelope + (b.Envelope - a.Envelope) * f
			end
		end
		return kps[#kps].Value, kps[#kps].Envelope
	end
	local val, env = sampleAt(t)
	return val + seed*env
end
-- Face direction (Enum.NormalId) that particles emit along, expressed in the emitter's own frame.
local function dirVector(cf, nid)
	if nid == Enum.NormalId.Bottom then return -cf.UpVector
	elseif nid == Enum.NormalId.Right then return cf.RightVector
	elseif nid == Enum.NormalId.Left then return -cf.RightVector
	elseif nid == Enum.NormalId.Front then return cf.LookVector
	elseif nid == Enum.NormalId.Back then return -cf.LookVector
	end
	return cf.UpVector  -- Top (default)
end
-- spawn a single particle at the part's centre, moving along its up axis at Speed. The clone is
-- a plain copy of RenderPart -- whatever's inside it comes along exactly as authored (mirror).
local function spawnOne(part, template)
	if #particles >= MAX then return end
	local myBatch = _curBatch
	local app, emis = cfgOf(part,"Appearance"), cfgOf(part,"Emission")
	local lr = gget(emis,"Lifetime", 1)
	local life = (typeof(lr)=="NumberRange") and (lr.Min + math.random()*(lr.Max-lr.Min)) or (typeof(lr)=="number" and lr or 1)
	life = math.max(0.05, life)
	local _nid = gget(emis,"EmissionDirection",Enum.NormalId.Top)
	local _sa = gget(cfgOf(part,"Placement"),"SpreadAngle", nil)
	-- perturb a direction within the SpreadAngle cone (fresh roll each call so Scatter can re-try)
	local function applySpread(d)
		if typeof(_sa)=="Vector2" and (_sa.X~=0 or _sa.Y~=0) then
			local u = (math.abs(d.Y) > 0.99) and Vector3.xAxis or Vector3.yAxis
			local base = CFrame.lookAt(Vector3.zero, d, u)
			d = (base * CFrame.Angles(math.rad((math.random()*2-1)*_sa.X), math.rad((math.random()*2-1)*_sa.Y), 0)).LookVector
		end
		return d
	end
	local function rollDir() return applySpread(dirVector(part.CFrame, _nid)) end
	-- SHAPE (Shape group): sample a spawn offset LOCAL to the emitter within the chosen region, plus
	-- its outward unit direction. Point = centre (unchanged). Box/Sphere/Cylinder/Disc scatter it.
	local _shp = cfgOf(part,"Shape")
	local _shapeOn      = gget(_shp,"ShapeOn",false) == true
	local _shapeName    = gget(_shp,"Shape","Sphere")
	local _shapeSurface = gget(_shp,"ShapeStyle","Volume") == "Surface"
	local _shapeInOut   = gget(_shp,"ShapeInOut","Outward")
	local _emisS = cfgOf(part,"Emission")
	local _lifeV = gget(_emisS,"Lifetime",1)
	local _shpPeriod = (typeof(_lifeV)=="NumberRange" and _lifeV.Max) or (type(_lifeV)=="number" and _lifeV) or 1
	if _shpPeriod < 0.1 then _shpPeriod = 0.1 end
	local _shapeU = ((shapeClk[part] or 0) % _shpPeriod) / _shpPeriod   -- emitter cycle time 0..1 (loops over Lifetime)
	local _sxr = math.max(0, evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeSizeX",nil), _shapeU, 4))
	local _syr = math.max(0, evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeSizeY",nil), _shapeU, 4))
	local _szr = math.max(0, evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeSizeZ",nil), _shapeU, 4))
	local _rotX = evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeRotationX",nil), _shapeU, 0)
	local _rotY = evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeRotationY",nil), _shapeU, 0)
	local _rotZ = evalNumberSeq(gget(_shp,"ShapeRotationZ",nil), _shapeU, 0)
	local _shapeRotOn = (_rotX~=0 or _rotY~=0 or _rotZ~=0)
	local _shapeRotCF = _shapeRotOn and CFrame.Angles(math.rad(_rotX), math.rad(_rotY), math.rad(_rotZ)) or nil
	local function sampleShape()
		if not _shapeOn then return Vector3.zero, nil end
		local ang = math.random()*2*math.pi
		if _shapeName=="Sphere" then
			local uu = math.random()*2-1; local ss = math.sqrt(math.max(0,1-uu*uu))
			local d = Vector3.new(ss*math.cos(ang), uu, ss*math.sin(ang))
			local frac = _shapeSurface and 1 or (math.random()^(1/3))
			local p = Vector3.new(d.X*_sxr, d.Y*_syr, d.Z*_szr) * frac
			return p, (p.Magnitude>1e-4 and p.Unit or d)
		elseif _shapeName=="Disc" then
			local frac = _shapeSurface and 1 or math.sqrt(math.random())
			local p = Vector3.new(math.cos(ang)*_sxr*frac, 0, math.sin(ang)*_szr*frac)
			return p, (p.Magnitude>1e-4 and p.Unit or Vector3.new(1,0,0))
		elseif _shapeName=="Cylinder" then
			local frac = _shapeSurface and 1 or math.sqrt(math.random())
			local h = (math.random()*2-1)*_syr
			local p = Vector3.new(math.cos(ang)*_sxr*frac, h, math.sin(ang)*_szr*frac)
			return p, Vector3.new(math.cos(ang),0,math.sin(ang))
		else -- Box
			local p = Vector3.new((math.random()*2-1)*_sxr, (math.random()*2-1)*_syr, (math.random()*2-1)*_szr)
			if _shapeSurface then
				local rr={_sxr,_syr,_szr}; local c={p.X,p.Y,p.Z}; local fi=math.random(3)
				c[fi]=((math.random()<0.5) and -1 or 1)*rr[fi]
				p = Vector3.new(c[1],c[2],c[3])
			end
			return p, (p.Magnitude>1e-4 and p.Unit or Vector3.new(0,1,0))
		end
	end
	-- One random draw per axis/property, held for this particle's whole life (see evalNumberSeq).
	-- Axes locked together (SineVFX_Lock_<axis>) share ONE envelope-random draw WITHIN their group,
	-- so their random wobble stays in sync; unlocked axes each get their own draw.
	local function seedGroup(cfg, axes)
		local shared = math.random()*2-1; local out = {}
		for _,a in ipairs(axes) do
			out[a] = (cfg and cfg:GetAttribute("SineVFX_Lock_"..a)==true) and shared or (math.random()*2-1)
		end
		return out
	end
	local parts = cfgOf(part,"Particles")
	local sE  = seedGroup(app,    {"SizeX","SizeY","SizeZ"})
	local rE  = seedGroup(emis, {"RotationX","RotationY","RotationZ"})
	local rsE = seedGroup(emis, {"RotSpeedX","RotSpeedY","RotSpeedZ"})
	local aE  = seedGroup(emis,  {"AccelX","AccelY","AccelZ"})
	local _plc = cfgOf(part,"Placement")
	local pE  = seedGroup(_plc, {"PositionX","PositionY","PositionZ"})
	local arE = seedGroup(_plc, {"AnchorRotationX","AnchorRotationY","AnchorRotationZ"})
	local arsE= seedGroup(_plc, {"AnchorRotSpeedX","AnchorRotSpeedY","AnchorRotSpeedZ"})
	local env = { Transparency=math.random()*2-1, Speed=math.random()*2-1,
		SizeX=sE.SizeX, SizeY=sE.SizeY, SizeZ=sE.SizeZ,
		RotationX=rE.RotationX, RotationY=rE.RotationY, RotationZ=rE.RotationZ,
		RotSpeedX=rsE.RotSpeedX, RotSpeedY=rsE.RotSpeedY, RotSpeedZ=rsE.RotSpeedZ,
		AccelX=aE.AccelX, AccelY=aE.AccelY, AccelZ=aE.AccelZ,
		PositionX=pE.PositionX, PositionY=pE.PositionY, PositionZ=pE.PositionZ,
		AnchorRotationX=arE.AnchorRotationX, AnchorRotationY=arE.AnchorRotationY, AnchorRotationZ=arE.AnchorRotationZ,
		AnchorRotSpeedX=arsE.AnchorRotSpeedX, AnchorRotSpeedY=arsE.AnchorRotSpeedY, AnchorRotSpeedZ=arsE.AnchorRotSpeedZ }
	local clone = acquire(template)
	clone.Anchored, clone.CanCollide, clone.CastShadow, clone.Locked = true, false, false, true
	clone.Archivable = false
	local sx = math.max(0.001, evalNumberSeq(gget(app,"SizeX",nil), 0, 1, env.SizeX))
	local sy = math.max(0.001, evalNumberSeq(gget(app,"SizeY",nil), 0, 1, env.SizeY))
	local sz = math.max(0.001, evalNumberSeq(gget(app,"SizeZ",nil), 0, 1, env.SizeZ))
	clone.Size         = Vector3.new(sx, sy, sz)  -- absolute studs, not a scale multiplier
	clone.Material     = gget(app,"Material",Enum.Material.Plastic)
	clone.Transparency = evalNumberSeq(gget(app,"Transparency",nil), 0, 0, env.Transparency)
	clone.Color        = evalColorSeq(gget(app,"Color",nil), 0)
	-- LockedToPart: when on, position/direction are tracked in the part's LOCAL frame (particles ride
	-- the moving/rotating emitter) and Offset/Acceleration are local; when off, everything is world.
	local locked  = gget(parts,"LockedToPart",false) == true
	local baseRot = locked and CFrame.identity or part.CFrame.Rotation
	local start   = locked and Vector3.zero or part.CFrame.Position
