					evalNumberSeq(prm.accYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AccelY),
					evalNumberSeq(prm.accZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AccelZ)) * dt
				-- after a collision the particle moves on pure stored velocity (bounces + gravity carry it)
				local vel = p.collided and p.accVel or (p.dir * sp + p.accVel)
				local newBase = p.basePos + vel * dt
				-- COLLISION: size/shape-aware shapecast prev->new (unlocked only; toggle + budget gated)
				if prm.collide and _rayBudget > 0 then
					-- Work in WORLD space so it collides even when LockedToPart is on (the particle rides the
					-- part but still hits the world). cf=nil for unlocked -> everything is already world.
					local cf = (p.locked and p.part.Parent) and p.part.CFrame or nil
					local wPrev = cf and (cf * p.basePos) or p.basePos
					local wNew  = cf and (cf * newBase)   or newBase
					local wVel  = cf and cf:VectorToWorldSpace(vel) or vel
					local delta = wNew - wPrev
					if delta.Magnitude > 1e-4 then
						_rayBudget = _rayBudget - 1
						local res
						if p.ball then
							res = workspace:Spherecast(wPrev, math.min(sx,sy,sz)*0.5, delta, collParams())
						else
							local worldRot = cf and (cf.Rotation * p.baseRot) or p.baseRot
							res = workspace:Blockcast(CFrame.new(wPrev) * worldRot, Vector3.new(sx,sy,sz), delta, collParams())
						end
						if res then
							local n   = res.Normal                 -- world normal
							local vnv = wVel:Dot(n) * n            -- component into the surface
							local vt  = wVel - vnv                 -- component along the surface
							local rv  = vt * (1 - p.friction) - vnv * p.bounce
							local rn = rv:Dot(n); if rn < 2 then rv = rv - rn * n end  -- kill tiny rebound
							-- store velocity/normal back in the particle's tracking space
							p.accVel = cf and cf:VectorToObjectSpace(rv) or rv
							p.collided = true
							p.groundNormal = cf and cf:VectorToObjectSpace(n) or n
							-- rest a hair (skin) above the surface so the next cast has clearance (no phase-through)
							local wRest = wPrev + delta.Unit * res.Distance + n * 0.05
							newBase = cf and cf:PointToObjectSpace(wRest) or wRest
							if rv.Magnitude < 1.5 then p.settled = true end
						end
					end
				end
				if p.dest then
					-- Head toward the destination at the particle's own SPEED (it may not reach it if Speed
					-- is low -- that's fine). If it DOES arrive, clamp onto the point WITHOUT freezing: the
					-- particle stays un-settled so the bezier weave keeps playing and shrinks to nothing by
					-- end-of-life, so it eases in smoothly instead of being yanked to a dead stop.
					-- Park on the destination once reached. For an InOrder route the carrot drives the
					-- leg-to-leg handoff, so only park after it has finished the whole route -- clamping
					-- mid-route would snap the particle onto a waypoint and put the hard corner back.
					if (not p.destSeq) or p.routeDone then
						local _b = p.dest - p.basePos; local _a = p.dest - newBase
						if _b:Dot(_a) <= 0 or _a.Magnitude < 1e-3 then newBase = p.dest end
					end
				end
				p.basePos = newBase
				-- ROLL: once on a surface, spin the shape about (groundNormal x velocity) at v/radius so it
				-- visibly rolls as it slides. Accumulated in world space, applied in the None orientation.
				if p.collided then
					local vt = p.accVel - p.accVel:Dot(p.groundNormal) * p.groundNormal
					local vs = vt.Magnitude
					if vs > 0.05 then
						local axis = p.groundNormal:Cross(vt)
						if axis.Magnitude > 1e-4 then
							local radius = math.max(0.1, (sx + sy + sz) / 6)
							p.rollCF = CFrame.fromAxisAngle(axis.Unit, (vs / radius) * dt) * p.rollCF
						end
					end
				end
			end
			-- Rotation angles (deg): Rotation curve + accumulated RotSpeed spin. These drive BOTH the
			-- part's own facing AND (when it has an Offset) its orbit around the Pivot point.
			p.spinX = p.spinX + evalNumberSeq(prm.rsXSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotSpeedX)*dt
			p.spinY = p.spinY + evalNumberSeq(prm.rsYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotSpeedY)*dt
			p.spinZ = p.spinZ + evalNumberSeq(prm.rsZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotSpeedZ)*dt
			local rx = evalNumberSeq(prm.rotXSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotationX) + p.spinX
			local ry = evalNumberSeq(prm.rotYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotationY) + p.spinY
			local rz = evalNumberSeq(prm.rotZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.RotationZ) + p.spinZ
			local Rrot = CFrame.Angles(math.rad(rx), math.rad(ry), math.rad(rz))
			-- POSITION: per-frame emitter-local offset (baseRot maps it to tracking space; for locked
			-- baseRot=identity so it's part-local, then the cf below rides the part)
			local posVec = Vector3.new(
				evalNumberSeq(prm.posXSeq, tt, 0, p.env.PositionX),
				evalNumberSeq(prm.posYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.PositionY),
				evalNumberSeq(prm.posZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.PositionZ))
			local trackedPos = p.basePos + p.baseRot:VectorToWorldSpace(posVec)
			if p.curlCtrl and not p.settled then
				-- AMPLITUDE follows distance-to-destination (Route) or lifetime -> closer to it = smaller
				-- radius, shaped non-linearly by the Radius curve.
				local _param = tt
				if p.dest and p.initDist and p.initDist>1e-4 then
					_param = math.clamp(1 - (p.dest - p.basePos).Magnitude / p.initDist, 0, 1)
				end
				local _amp = evalNumberSeq(prm.radiusSeq, _param, 0)
				-- Taper the weave to ZERO as it reaches the centre so the VISIBLE particle actually lands on
				-- the centre (not held out by a leftover radius). Shape still comes from the Radius curve.
				if p.dest and p.initDist and p.initDist>1e-4 then
					local _ts = tonumber(prm.taperStart) or 0
					local _tf = (_param <= _ts) and 1 or ((1 - _param) / math.max(1e-4, 1 - _ts))
					_amp = _amp * math.clamp(_tf, 0, 1)
				end
				-- TRAVERSAL advances by the emitter's Bezier Speed (a curve over life), INDEPENDENT of travel
				-- speed -- so the weave keeps moving even after the base path reaches the centre and clamps.
				p.bezPhase = (p.bezPhase or 0) + evalNumberSeq(prm.bezSpeedSeq, tt, 1)*dt
				local _ct = p.curlCtrl; local _N = #_ct
				-- Catmull-Rom spline through the control points: a smooth CURVE (not straight segments)
				-- so the weave actually looks like a bezier/spline as it passes from point to point.
				local _x = p.bezPhase; local _i = math.floor(_x); local _f = _x - _i
				local _p0,_p1,_p2,_p3 = curlP(_ct,_N,_i), curlP(_ct,_N,_i+1), curlP(_ct,_N,_i+2), curlP(_ct,_N,_i+3)
				local _f2 = _f*_f; local _f3 = _f2*_f
				-- Catmull-Rom (standard tension 0.5) through the weave points.
				-- There was a Smoothness knob here that blended this against a straight polyline through
				-- the SAME points. Removed: measured, it moved the path by ~8% of one point spacing across
				-- its whole 0..1 range (~17% worst case) -- invisible at any realistic Radius. Raising the
				-- tension to widen its range doesn't work either: past ~0.5 the tangents overshoot and the
				-- path spikes, which reads as LESS smooth, inverting the knob. Don't re-add it as-is; a
				-- knob over weave-point DENSITY (see pts, above) is what actually reads.
				local _h00 = 2*_f3-3*_f2+1; local _h10 = _f3-2*_f2+_f; local _h01 = -2*_f3+3*_f2; local _h11 = _f3-_f2
				local _m1 = (_p2-_p0)*0.5; local _m2 = (_p3-_p1)*0.5
				trackedPos = trackedPos + (_p1*_h00 + _m1*_h10 + _p2*_h01 + _m2*_h11) * _amp			end
			-- ANCHOR ROTATION: orbit the position around the emitter centre (spawn anchor). Curve
			-- (AnchorRotation) + accumulated continuous spin (AnchorRotSpeed).
			p.anchorSpinX = p.anchorSpinX + evalNumberSeq(prm.aRsXSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotSpeedX)*dt
			p.anchorSpinY = p.anchorSpinY + evalNumberSeq(prm.aRsYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotSpeedY)*dt
			p.anchorSpinZ = p.anchorSpinZ + evalNumberSeq(prm.aRsZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotSpeedZ)*dt
			local aRx = evalNumberSeq(prm.aRotXSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotationX) + p.anchorSpinX
			local aRy = evalNumberSeq(prm.aRotYSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotationY) + p.anchorSpinY
			local aRz = evalNumberSeq(prm.aRotZSeq, tt, 0, p.env.AnchorRotationZ) + p.anchorSpinZ
			if aRx~=0 or aRy~=0 or aRz~=0 then
				local Ra = CFrame.Angles(math.rad(aRx), math.rad(aRy), math.rad(aRz))
				trackedPos = p.anchor + Ra:VectorToWorldSpace(trackedPos - p.anchor)
			end
			-- resolve tracking space -> world (locked rides the live part CFrame each frame)
			local worldPos, worldDir, worldBaseRot
			if p.locked and p.part.Parent then
				local cf = p.part.CFrame
				worldPos = cf * trackedPos
				worldDir = cf:VectorToWorldSpace(p.dir)
				worldBaseRot = cf.Rotation * p.baseRot
			else
				worldPos = trackedPos; worldDir = p.dir; worldBaseRot = p.baseRot
			end
			-- ZOffset: shift the RENDERED position toward(+)/away(-) from the camera.
			local cam = workspace.CurrentCamera
			-- OFF-SCREEN CULL (Optimization > Cull Offscreen): the particle already integrated its next
			-- position above; when it is not in view we skip ALL render work (orientation, CFrame, colour,
			-- size, nested) until it re-enters the frustum. Cheap check via WorldToViewportPoint.
			if prm.cullOffscreen and cam then
				local _, _onScreen = cam:WorldToViewportPoint(worldPos)
				if not _onScreen then i += 1; continue end
			end
			local drawPos = worldPos
			local zSizeScale = 1
			if prm.zoffset ~= 0 and cam then
				local toCam = cam.CFrame.Position - worldPos; local dist = toCam.Magnitude
				if dist > 1e-4 then
					local shift = math.min(prm.zoffset, dist - 0.5)
					drawPos = worldPos + (toCam/dist) * shift
					-- ZOffset only changes depth, NOT apparent size: moving the particle `shift`
					-- closer to the camera makes it look bigger by dist/(dist-shift), so scale the
					-- real size by the inverse. Fixes particles ballooning up close with ZOffset.
					local newDist = dist - shift
					if newDist > 1e-3 then zSizeScale = newDist / dist end
				end
			end
			-- base orientation frame from the Orientation mode; Rrot spins the part's facing on top
			local om = prm.orientation
			local vmag = worldDir.Magnitude
			local frame
			if om=="FacingCamera" and cam then
				frame = CFrame.lookAt(drawPos, cam.CFrame.Position, cam.CFrame.UpVector)
			elseif om=="FacingCameraWorldUp" and cam then
				frame = CFrame.lookAt(drawPos, cam.CFrame.Position, Vector3.yAxis)  -- billboard, never rolls
			elseif om=="VelocityParallel" and vmag>1e-4 then
				frame = CFrame.lookAt(drawPos, drawPos + worldDir)  -- front (LookVector) follows travel
			elseif om=="VelocityPerpendicular" and vmag>1e-4 then
				-- part's UP (flat-face normal) points along travel -> the face lies across the path
				local up = worldDir.Unit
				local ref = (math.abs(up.Y)>0.99) and Vector3.xAxis or Vector3.yAxis
				local right = up:Cross(ref).Unit
				local back  = right:Cross(up).Unit
				frame = CFrame.fromMatrix(drawPos, right, up, back)
			else
				frame = CFrame.new(drawPos) * p.rollCF * worldBaseRot  -- None: include accumulated roll
			end
			inst.CFrame = frame * Rrot  -- Rrot also spins the part's own facing (orbit is tidally locked)
			-- Cache this particle's transform RELATIVE TO its part so throttled/held frames can re-glue a
			-- LOCKED particle to the LIVE part every frame (a fast-moving part won't drag its particles).
			if p.locked and p.part.Parent then p._locCF = p.part.CFrame:Inverse() * inst.CFrame end
			if prm.colorSeq then inst.Color = evalColorSeq(prm.colorSeq, tt) end
			if prm.transSeq then inst.Transparency = evalNumberSeq(prm.transSeq, tt, 0, p.env.Transparency) end
			if p.atts then
				-- Scale nested attachment offsets by the particle's current size RELATIVE TO THE RENDERPART's
				-- authored size (attachments are authored against RenderPart), so beams/trails between them
				-- track the particle's real dimensions as its Size animates.
				local ss = p.spawnSize
				local rx = (ss and ss.X>1e-6) and sx/ss.X or 1
				local ry = (ss and ss.Y>1e-6) and sy/ss.Y or 1
				local rz = (ss and ss.Z>1e-6) and sz/ss.Z or 1
				for _,e in ipairs(p.atts) do
					if e.a and e.a.Parent then e.a.Position = Vector3.new(e.base.X*rx, e.base.Y*ry, e.base.Z*rz) end
				end
			end
			if p.nestedPending then
				-- Enable / emit nested effects NOW: only after the attachments above have been scaled to this
				-- particle's real size at least once, so nested beams/trails don't flash at the unscaled
				-- RenderPart positions on the very first frame.
				p.nestedPending = nil
				for _,ch in ipairs(inst:GetDescendants()) do
					_nestedFire(ch, p.enOnSpawn, p.emOnSpawn)
				end
			end
			inst.Size = Vector3.new(sx, sy, sz) * zSizeScale  -- absolute studs; zSizeScale keeps ZOffset from changing visual size
			i += 1
		end
	end
end
-- ===== SIM CORE (END) =======================================================================
-- Below here is PLUGIN-ONLY (backfill/migration + the edit-mode selection gizmo) and is never
-- sliced into the runtime module.
function M.init()
	active = false
	-- backfill spec attributes onto emitters transformed before a new attr (e.g. Material,
	-- Transparency) existed, so their Property rows show a value and the sim reads a real setting.
	for _, part in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTagged(TAG)) do
		if not part:IsA("BasePart") then continue end
		-- EmitCount has no useful nil-default (0 particles = Emit button silently no-ops) --
		-- backfill emitters transformed before TransformWindow started seeding it.
		if part:GetAttribute("EmitCount") == nil then part:SetAttribute("EmitCount", 1) end
		local pf = part:FindFirstChild(PROPS)
		if not pf then continue end
		-- backfill the Optimization group (added later) so old emitters expose the StepRate toggle+slider
		local optCfg = pf:FindFirstChild("Optimization")
		if not optCfg then optCfg = Instance.new("Configuration"); optCfg.Name = "Optimization"; optCfg.Parent = pf end
		if optCfg:GetAttribute("StepRateOn") == nil then optCfg:SetAttribute("StepRateOn", false) end
		if optCfg:GetAttribute("StepRate") == nil then optCfg:SetAttribute("StepRate", 60) end  -- only fill if missing; leave an existing 0 as 0
		if optCfg:GetAttribute("CullOffscreen") == nil then optCfg:SetAttribute("CullOffscreen", false) end
		-- Size is now an ABSOLUTE dimension in studs, not a scale multiplier. Backfill any
		-- part missing SizeX/Y/Z from the RenderPart's own size (unchanged look), scaled by
		-- the old single-axis "Size" attribute if present (that WAS a multiplier).
		local appCfg = pf:FindFirstChild("Appearance")
		if appCfg and appCfg:GetAttribute("SizeX") == nil then
			local template = part:FindFirstChild(RENDER)
			local rsize = (template and template:IsA("BasePart")) and template.Size or Vector3.new(1,1,1)
			local legacy = appCfg:GetAttribute("Size")
			local mult = (typeof(legacy) == "number") and legacy or 1
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeX", NumberSequence.new(rsize.X*mult))
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeY", NumberSequence.new(rsize.Y*mult))
			appCfg:SetAttribute("SizeZ", NumberSequence.new(rsize.Z*mult))
		end
		-- Shape: migrate old scalar ShapeRadius -> per-axis ShapeSize seqs, and old vec3 ShapeRotation
		-- -> per-axis ShapeRotation seqs, so emitters made before this change keep their look.
		local shpCfg = pf:FindFirstChild("Shape")
		if shpCfg then
			if shpCfg:GetAttribute("ShapeSizeX") == nil then
				local oldR = shpCfg:GetAttribute("ShapeRadius"); if type(oldR) ~= "number" then oldR = 4 end
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeSizeX", NumberSequence.new(oldR))
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeSizeY", NumberSequence.new(oldR))
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeSizeZ", NumberSequence.new(oldR))
			end
			if shpCfg:GetAttribute("ShapeRotationX") == nil then
				local oldRot = shpCfg:GetAttribute("ShapeRotation")
				local rv = (typeof(oldRot) == "Vector3") and oldRot or Vector3.new()
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeRotationX", NumberSequence.new(rv.X))
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeRotationY", NumberSequence.new(rv.Y))
				shpCfg:SetAttribute("ShapeRotationZ", NumberSequence.new(rv.Z))
			end
		end
		for _, g in ipairs(EmitterSpec.groups) do
			local cfg = pf:FindFirstChild(g.name)
			if not cfg then
				-- group added after this emitter was transformed -- create it so its
				-- Property rows have a holder and the sim reads real settings.
				cfg = Instance.new("Configuration"); cfg.Name = g.name; cfg.Parent = pf
			end
			for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
				-- t="object" rows are child instances (Destinations), never attributes
				if d.t ~= "object" and cfg:GetAttribute(d.a) == nil then cfg:SetAttribute(d.a, d.d) end
			end
		end
		-- MIGRATION -> Particles.Route. Two old spellings feed in: the original Bezier "Center" bool,
		-- and the short-lived FinalDestination/DestinationPick naming. Runs AFTER the backfill above and
		-- relies on it: backfill has just seeded Route="Default", so a still-"Default" value means the
		-- user never set one and the old value should win. Each step clears its source attribute, which
		-- makes the whole thing idempotent -- a second init() finds nothing to migrate.
		local _bez, _pcl = pf:FindFirstChild("Bezier"), pf:FindFirstChild("Particles")
		if _pcl then
			local _fdOld = _pcl:GetAttribute("FinalDestination")
			if _fdOld ~= nil then
				if _pcl:GetAttribute("Route") == "Default" and _fdOld ~= "Default" then _pcl:SetAttribute("Route", _fdOld) end
				_pcl:SetAttribute("FinalDestination", nil)
			end
			-- PickCount/TargetCount are both retired: the filled slots ARE the count now. Clear them
			-- so nothing reads a stale value (one emitter had TargetCount=9, past its own max of 8).
			_pcl:SetAttribute("PickCount", nil)
			_pcl:SetAttribute("TargetCount", nil)
			-- the slot ObjectValues were PickN before they were TargetN
			local _df0 = _pcl:FindFirstChild(EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER)
			if _df0 then
				for _i = 1, 8 do
					local _ov = _df0:FindFirstChild("Pick" .. _i)
					if _ov and not _df0:FindFirstChild("Target" .. _i) then _ov.Name = "Target" .. _i end
				end
			end
			-- DestinationPick and the later "Pick" both fed today's RoutePick
			for _, _oldName in ipairs({"DestinationPick", "Pick"}) do
				local _dpOld = _pcl:GetAttribute(_oldName)
				if _dpOld ~= nil then
					if _pcl:GetAttribute("RoutePick") == "Random" and _dpOld ~= "Random" then _pcl:SetAttribute("RoutePick", _dpOld) end
					_pcl:SetAttribute(_oldName, nil)
				end
			end
			-- the round-robin mode was called "Sequential" before it was "Even"
			if _pcl:GetAttribute("RoutePick") == "Sequential" then _pcl:SetAttribute("RoutePick", "Even") end
		end
		if _bez and _pcl then
			local _old = _bez:GetAttribute("ToCenter")
			if _old ~= nil then
				if _old == true and _pcl:GetAttribute("Route") == "Default" then
					_pcl:SetAttribute("Route", "Center")
				end
				_bez:SetAttribute("ToCenter", nil)
			end
		end
		-- the Custom target list lives as ObjectValue children under Particles/Destinations
		if _pcl and not _pcl:FindFirstChild(EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER) then
			local _df = Instance.new("Folder"); _df.Name = EmitterSpec.DEST_FOLDER; _df.Parent = _pcl
		end
	end
end
-- SHAPE spawn-region OUTLINE (edit-mode gizmo): a translucent HandleAdornment of the Shape
-- group's spawn region, shown ONLY for a SELECTED SineVFX3D emitter whose Shape toggle is on.
-- Updates live (~10fps) from the Shape attributes. Never runs in the shipped runtime module.
local Selection = game:GetService("Selection")
local adornFolder
local adorns = {}   -- [part] = HandleAdornment
local adornConn
local _adornAcc = 0
local ADORN_COL = Color3.fromRGB(80, 190, 255)
-- Shape gizmo tint: the live theme accent (falls back to ADORN_COL if unavailable)
local function adornColor() local ok,c=pcall(function() return Theme.C.accent end); return (ok and typeof(c)=="Color3" and c) or ADORN_COL end
local ADORN_CLS = { Sphere="SphereHandleAdornment", Box="BoxHandleAdornment",
	Cylinder="CylinderHandleAdornment", Disc="CylinderHandleAdornment" }
local function adornFolderGet()
	if adornFolder and adornFolder.Parent then return adornFolder end
	local cg = game:GetService("CoreGui")
	adornFolder = cg:FindFirstChild("SineVFX_ShapeAdorn")
	if not adornFolder then
		adornFolder = Instance.new("Folder"); adornFolder.Name = "SineVFX_ShapeAdorn"; adornFolder.Parent = cg
	end
	return adornFolder
end
local function clearAdorns()
	for _, a in pairs(adorns) do pcall(function() a:Destroy() end) end
	table.clear(adorns)
	if adornFolder then pcall(function() adornFolder:Destroy() end); adornFolder = nil end
end
-- WIREFRAME outline (just the geometry, not a filled blob). Rebuilt only when the shape or
-- radius changes (adornSig cache); the Adornee makes it follow/rotate with the emitter for free.
local adornSig = {}
local function buildWire(a, name, sv)
	local r = 1  -- build in unit space; `line` bakes the per-axis half-extents (sv Vector3)
	local function line(p1, p2)
		a:AddLine(Vector3.new(p1.X*sv.X, p1.Y*sv.Y, p1.Z*sv.Z), Vector3.new(p2.X*sv.X, p2.Y*sv.Y, p2.Z*sv.Z))
	end
	local function ring(axis, off)
		local N = 28; local prev
		for i = 0, N do
			local ang = i/N*2*math.pi
			local c, s = math.cos(ang)*r, math.sin(ang)*r
			local p
			if axis == "y" then p = Vector3.new(c, off or 0, s)
			elseif axis == "x" then p = Vector3.new(off or 0, c, s)
			else p = Vector3.new(c, s, off or 0) end
			if prev then line(prev, p) end
			prev = p
		end
	end
	if name == "Sphere" then
		local N = 24
		local function parallel(y, rr)
			local prev
			for i = 0, N do
				local ang = i/N*2*math.pi
				local p = Vector3.new(math.cos(ang)*rr, y, math.sin(ang)*rr)
				if prev then line(prev, p) end; prev = p
			end
		end
		local function meridian(lon)
			local dx, dz = math.cos(lon), math.sin(lon); local prev
			for i = 0, N do
				local t = i/N*2*math.pi
				local p = Vector3.new(dx*math.sin(t)*r, math.cos(t)*r, dz*math.sin(t)*r)
				if prev then line(prev, p) end; prev = p
			end
		end
		for k = 1, 5 do local phi = k/6*math.pi; parallel(math.cos(phi)*r, math.sin(phi)*r) end
		for k = 0, 7 do meridian(k/8*math.pi) end
	elseif name == "Disc" then
		local M = 24
		for _, rr in ipairs({r, r*0.66, r*0.33}) do
			local prev
			for i = 0, M do local ang = i/M*2*math.pi
				local p = Vector3.new(math.cos(ang)*rr, 0, math.sin(ang)*rr)
				if prev then line(prev, p) end; prev = p end
		end
		for i = 0, 7 do local ang = i/8*2*math.pi
			line(Vector3.zero, Vector3.new(math.cos(ang)*r, 0, math.sin(ang)*r)) end
	elseif name == "Cylinder" then
		for _, yy in ipairs({r, r*0.5, 0, -r*0.5, -r}) do ring("y", yy) end
		for i = 0, 7 do
			local ang = i/8*2*math.pi; local c, s = math.cos(ang)*r, math.sin(ang)*r
			line(Vector3.new(c, r, s), Vector3.new(c, -r, s))
		end
	else -- Box: 12 cube edges over [-sv, sv]
		local sset = {-r, r}; local corners = {}
		for _, x in ipairs(sset) do for _, y in ipairs(sset) do for _, z in ipairs(sset) do
			corners[#corners+1] = Vector3.new(x, y, z)
		end end end
		for i = 1, #corners do for j = i+1, #corners do
			local d = corners[i] - corners[j]; local nz = 0
			if math.abs(d.X) > 1e-4 then nz += 1 end
			if math.abs(d.Y) > 1e-4 then nz += 1 end
			if math.abs(d.Z) > 1e-4 then nz += 1 end
			if nz == 1 then line(corners[i], corners[j]) end
		end end
	end
end
local function updateAdorns()
	local want = {}
	for _, obj in ipairs(Selection:Get()) do
		if obj:IsA("BasePart") and CollectionService:HasTag(obj, TAG) then
			local shp = cfgOf(obj, "Shape")
			-- SineVFX_ShapeGizmo (Properties > Misc) hides the wireframe outline without touching
			-- the Shape itself: absent/true = drawn, false = off. Per-instance and persisted.
			if gget(shp, "ShapeOn", false) == true and obj:GetAttribute("SineVFX_ShapeGizmo") ~= false then want[obj] = shp end
		end
	end
	for part, a in pairs(adorns) do
		if not want[part] or not part.Parent then pcall(function() a:Destroy() end); adorns[part] = nil; adornSig[part] = nil end
	end
	for part, shp in pairs(want) do
		local name = gget(shp, "Shape", "Sphere")
		local sxr = math.max(0.01, evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeSizeX",nil), 0, 4))
		local syr = math.max(0.01, evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeSizeY",nil), 0, 4))
		local szr = math.max(0.01, evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeSizeZ",nil), 0, 4))
		local sig = name .. "|" .. string.format("%.3f,%.3f,%.3f", sxr, syr, szr)
		if adornSig[part] ~= sig then
			if adorns[part] then pcall(function() adorns[part]:Destroy() end) end
			local a = Instance.new("WireframeHandleAdornment")
			a.Adornee = part; a.Color3 = adornColor(); a.CFrame = CFrame.new()
			a.AlwaysOnTop = false; a.ZIndex = 0
			buildWire(a, name, Vector3.new(sxr, syr, szr))
			a.Parent = adornFolderGet()
			adorns[part] = a; adornSig[part] = sig
		end
		local grx = evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeRotationX",nil), 0, 0)
		local gry = evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeRotationY",nil), 0, 0)
		local grz = evalNumberSeq(gget(shp,"ShapeRotationZ",nil), 0, 0)
		if adorns[part] then adorns[part].Color3 = adornColor() end
		if adorns[part] then adorns[part].CFrame = CFrame.Angles(math.rad(grx),math.rad(gry),math.rad(grz)) end
	end
end
function M.setActive(v)
	if v == active then return end
	active = v
	if v then
		myGeneration = (genHolder():GetAttribute(GEN_ATTR) or 0) + 1
		genHolder():SetAttribute(GEN_ATTR, myGeneration)
		-- clean up the legacy token older versions wrote onto Terrain (it
		-- replicated in Team Create and dirtied the place file)
		if workspace.Terrain:GetAttribute(GEN_ATTR) ~= nil then
			pcall(function() workspace.Terrain:SetAttribute(GEN_ATTR, nil) end)
		end
		if not conn then conn = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(step) end
		if not adornConn then adornConn = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
			_adornAcc += dt
			if _adornAcc < 0.1 then return end
			_adornAcc = 0; updateAdorns()
		end) end
	else
		if conn then conn:Disconnect(); conn = nil end
		if adornConn then adornConn:Disconnect(); adornConn = nil end
		clearAdorns()
		flush()
	end
end
-- One-off burst of `count` particles (Emit window / shared.sv.emit delegation). Instant,
-- like ParticleEmitter:Emit(count) -- Duration is NOT a burst-spread parameter here, it's handled
-- by callers toggling Appearance.Enabled around the window (mirrors runEmit's em.Enabled=true
-- bracket for real ParticleEmitters, which drives this module's own continuous Rate-based
-- emission in step() for the duration, independent of this instant manual burst).
function M.burst(part, count)
	if not active then return end
	if not part or not part:IsA("BasePart") then return end
	count = tonumber(count) or 0
	if count <= 0 then return end
	local template = part:FindFirstChild(RENDER)
	if not template or not template:IsA("BasePart") then return end
	_burstSeq += 1; _curBatch = _burstSeq  -- this burst spaces only against ITS OWN particles, not older bursts
	for _ = 1, count do spawnOne(part, template) end
	_curBatch = 0
end
-- Live 3D-particle count attributable to a set of emitter parts (for the Particle Counter).
-- set = { [part]=true }; returns how many live spawned clones belong to those parts.
function M.countForParts(set)
	if not set then return 0 end
	local n = 0
	for _, p in ipairs(particles) do
		if p.part and set[p.part] then n += 1 end
	end
	return n
end
function M.stop() M.setActive(false) end
-- ================= TrailRuntime (merged in as M.Trail) =================
-- Relocated verbatim from Modules.TrailRuntime; wrapped in an IIFE so its locals stay
-- isolated. `script` still resolves to EmitterRuntime, so its require paths are unchanged.
M.Trail = (function()
-- TrailRuntime: edit-mode PREVIEW for SineVFX transformed trails (tagged Trails, blueprint =
-- Utilities.TrailSpec). Two jobs, and they are separate:
--
--   1. DRIVE THE SOURCE. The tagged trail keeps rendering and is written from its attributes every
--      frame, so editing Colour/Gradient/Size/Transparency changes what you see immediately. The
--      lifetime graphs have no age to sample on a source that never dies, so they are read at t=0.
--
--   2. EMIT COPIES. A copy is a clone of the trail + its two Attachments on a part in the Terrain
--      container. Copies DO NOT FLY. The part is pinned to the emitting part's CFrame every frame,
--      so a copy only moves because the emitter moves; emitting while it is still adds nothing on
--      screen, by design. Each copy ages t=0..1 over Duration and is then destroyed.
--
-- TWO LIFETIMES: Emission.Lifetime is the trail's OWN (the ribbon's linger, written straight onto
-- Trail.Lifetime), Emission.Duration is how long until an emitted trail is deleted.
--
-- WIDTH: the runtime NEVER writes attachment positions -- their spacing is the user's authored
-- geometry. Size scales the WidthScale sequence instead (see apply/scaleSeq).
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")
local TrailSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).TrailSpec
local RibbonShared = require(script.Parent.RibbonShared)
local valEq        = RibbonShared.valEq
local evalNum       = RibbonShared.evalNum
local evalCol       = RibbonShared.evalCol
local e0           = RibbonShared.e0
local tintGradient  = RibbonShared.tintGradient
local fadeGradient  = RibbonShared.fadeGradient
local TAG       = TrailSpec.TAG
local PROPS     = TrailSpec.PROPS_FOLDER
local CONTAINER = TrailSpec.CONTAINER
local MAX = 400   -- hard cap on live copies; a runaway Rate must not lock Studio up
local M = {}
local active = false
local conn = nil
local container = nil
local live = {}   -- array of { trail, src, age, life }
local accs = {}   -- [sourceTrail] = emission accumulator
local function ensureContainer()
	if container and container.Parent then return container end
	local terrain = workspace.Terrain
	container = terrain:FindFirstChild(CONTAINER)
	if not container then
		container = Instance.new("Folder")
		container.Name = CONTAINER
		container.Archivable = false   -- preview output must not land in the saved place file
		container.Parent = terrain
	end
	return container
end
local function cfgOf(trail, name)
	local pf = trail:FindFirstChild(PROPS)
	return pf and pf:FindFirstChild(name) or nil
end
local function gget(trail, grp, attr, default)
	local c = cfgOf(trail, grp)
	if not c then return default end
	local v = c:GetAttribute(attr)
	if v == nil then return default end
	return v
end
-- Sample a NumberSequence at t in [0,1]. Envelopes are ignored: a ribbon is one continuous object,
-- so a per-frame random inside the envelope would read as jitter rather than spread.
-- The two colour axes composed: tint the along-LENGTH Gradient by the over-LIFETIME Color. Done
-- per keypoint so the gradient's shape survives; multiplying by one flat colour would flatten it.
-- Transparency's two axes composed: fade the along-length TransparencyGradient by the over-life
-- Transparency scalar, in OPACITY space (opacity = 1-transparency, multiplied), matching Colour x Gradient.
-- Size composed onto WidthScale: scale every keypoint, preserving the curve's shape. Roblox clamps
-- WidthScale to 0..1, so Size can always thin the ribbon but can only widen it where WidthScale is
-- below 1 (0.5 x Size 2 = 1.0). Size does NOT touch the attachments -- their spacing is the user's
-- authored geometry and the runtime must never write it.
local function scaleSeq(seq, k)
	if typeof(seq) ~= "NumberSequence" then return NumberSequence.new(math.clamp(k, 0, 1)) end
	local out = {}
	for i, p in ipairs(seq.Keypoints) do
		out[i] = NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(p.Time, math.clamp(p.Value * k, 0, 1), p.Envelope)
	end
	return NumberSequence.new(out)
end
-- Everything an attribute drives on a Trail instance, applied at age `t`. Shared by the SOURCE
-- (t=0, it has no age) and every emitted COPY (t = age/life) so the two can never drift apart.
--
-- `isCopy` exists for ONE reason: Enabled. Appearance.Enabled is the SOURCE's draw toggle, and a
-- copy must ignore it. A copy was spawned specifically to be seen, and gating it on the source's
-- toggle means turning the source off (the whole point of emitting: hide the original, show the
-- emitted ones) silently disabled every copy too.
local function apply(dst, src, t, isCopy)
	-- Colour: Gradient is the ribbon's actual colour (along its length). Color is the
	-- OVER-LIFETIME tint and only means anything on something that ages -- i.e. an emitted copy.
	-- The SOURCE trail has no lifetime (it just sits at t=0), so tinting it by Color would just be
	-- a confusing constant recolour; it shows its Gradient straight. Copies compose Gradient x Color.
	local grad = gget(src, "Appearance", "Gradient", nil)
	if isCopy then
		local c = evalCol(gget(src, "Appearance", "Color", nil), t, Color3.new(1,1,1))
		dst.Color = tintGradient(grad, c)
	else
		dst.Color = (typeof(grad) == "ColorSequence") and grad or ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1))
	end
	dst.Transparency = fadeGradient(gget(src, "Appearance", "TransparencyGradient", nil), evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "Transparency", nil), t, 0))
	dst.Texture        = gget(src, "Appearance", "Texture", "")
	dst.TextureLength  = evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "TextureLength", nil), t, 1)
	dst.LightEmission  = gget(src, "Appearance", "LightEmission", 0)
	dst.LightInfluence = gget(src, "Appearance", "LightInfluence", 0)
	dst.Brightness     = gget(src, "Appearance", "Brightness", 1)
	dst.FaceCamera     = gget(src, "Appearance", "FaceCamera", false)
	-- copies always draw; only the source obeys Appearance.Enabled
	dst.Enabled        = isCopy or (gget(src, "Appearance", "Enabled", true) and true or false)
	pcall(function() dst.TextureMode = Enum.TextureMode[tostring(gget(src, "Appearance", "TextureMode", "Stretch"))] end)
	dst.MinLength  = evalNum(gget(src, "Emission", "MinLength", nil), t, 0.1)
	dst.MaxLength  = evalNum(gget(src, "Emission", "MaxLength", nil), t, 0)
	dst.Lifetime   = math.max(gget(src, "Emission", "Lifetime", 1), 0)   -- the ribbon's linger, NOT Duration
	-- width: Size (over lifetime) scales WidthScale (along length) into the final ribbon width
	local sz = evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "Size", nil), t, 1)
	dst.WidthScale = scaleSeq(gget(src, "Emission", "WidthScale", NumberSequence.new(1)), sz)
end
-- ===== external-edit sync ================================================================
-- The source trail is driven from its attributes every frame, which alone would clobber any edit
-- made OUTSIDE the Properties window (native Properties panel, SineVFX tools, scripts). driveSource()
-- writes each real prop from its attribute AND records what it wrote; a per-prop watcher folds any
-- OTHER change back into the matching attribute so the next drive reproduces it. Deferred signals
-- mean we compare against the last-driven value instead of using a "writing now" flag.
local watchers = setmetatable({}, {__mode = "k"})  -- [trail] = { conns={...}, applied={[prop]=v} }
-- real Trail property <-> (group, attr). dec(inst, attrVal) -> real (source, t=0); enc(inst, real) -> attr.
local MAP = {
	{p="Enabled",        g="Appearance", a="Enabled",     dec=function(_,v) return v and true or false end, enc=function(_,v) return v and true or false end},
	{p="Color",          g="Appearance", a="Gradient",    dec=function(_,v) return (typeof(v)=="ColorSequence") and v or ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)) end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="Transparency",   g="Appearance", a="TransparencyGradient",dec=function(_,v) return (typeof(v)=="NumberSequence") and v or NumberSequence.new(0) end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="Texture",        g="Appearance", a="Texture",     dec=function(_,v) return v or "" end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="TextureLength",  g="Appearance", a="TextureLength",dec=function(_,v) return e0(v,1) end, enc=function(_,v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="TextureMode",    g="Appearance", a="TextureMode", dec=function(_,v) return Enum.TextureMode[tostring(v or "Stretch"):match("[^.]+$")] or Enum.TextureMode.Stretch end, enc=function(_,v) return v.Name end},
	{p="LightEmission",  g="Appearance", a="LightEmission",dec=function(_,v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 0 end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="LightInfluence", g="Appearance", a="LightInfluence",dec=function(_,v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 0 end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="Brightness",     g="Appearance", a="Brightness",  dec=function(_,v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 1 end, enc=function(_,v) return v end},
	{p="FaceCamera",     g="Appearance", a="FaceCamera",  dec=function(_,v) return v and true or false end, enc=function(_,v) return v and true or false end},
	{p="MinLength",      g="Emission",   a="MinLength",   dec=function(_,v) return e0(v,0.1) end, enc=function(_,v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="MaxLength",      g="Emission",   a="MaxLength",   dec=function(_,v) return e0(v,0) end, enc=function(_,v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="Lifetime",       g="Emission",   a="Lifetime",    dec=function(_,v) return math.max((typeof(v)=="number") and v or 1, 0) end, enc=function(_,v) return math.max(v,0) end},
	-- WidthScale = the authored WidthScale sequence scaled by Size at t=0 (source keeps Size scaling).
	{p="WidthScale",     g="Emission",   a="WidthScale",  dec=function(inst,v) return scaleSeq(v, e0(gget(inst,"Appearance","Size",nil),1)) end, enc=function(inst,v) local sz=e0(gget(inst,"Appearance","Size",nil),1); if sz<=0 then sz=1 end return scaleSeq(v, 1/sz) end},
}
local function dropWatcher(trail)
	local w = watchers[trail]; if not w then return end
	for _, c in ipairs(w.conns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
	watchers[trail] = nil
end
local function ensureWatcher(trail)
	local w = watchers[trail]
	if w then return w end
	w = { conns = {}, applied = {} }
	watchers[trail] = w
	for _, mm in ipairs(MAP) do
		local ok, sig = pcall(function() return trail:GetPropertyChangedSignal(mm.p) end)
		if ok and sig then
			w.conns[#w.conns+1] = sig:Connect(function()
				local ww = watchers[trail]; if not ww then return end
				local cur = trail[mm.p]
				if valEq(cur, ww.applied[mm.p]) then return end   -- the value WE drove; not external
				local cfg = cfgOf(trail, mm.g)
				if not cfg then return end
				local okE, enc = pcall(mm.enc, trail, cur)
				if okE then cfg:SetAttribute(mm.a, enc); ww.applied[mm.p] = cur end
			end)
		end
	end
	return w
end
local function driveSource(trail)
	local w = ensureWatcher(trail)
	for _, mm in ipairs(MAP) do
		local cfg = cfgOf(trail, mm.g)
		local av = cfg and cfg:GetAttribute(mm.a)
		local okD, real = pcall(mm.dec, trail, av)
		if okD then
			pcall(function() trail[mm.p] = real end)
			w.applied[mm.p] = trail[mm.p]
		end
	end
end
-- The part the source trail's attachments hang off: what a copy pins itself to.
local function sourceRoot(trail)
	local a0 = trail.Attachment0
	local p = a0 and a0.Parent
	return (p and p:IsA("BasePart")) and p or nil
end
-- A copy is JUST a Trail. It is cloned into the container folder and pointed at the SAME
-- attachments the source uses -- nothing else is copied and the attachments are left exactly as the
-- user authored them. So a copy needs no carrier part and no pinning: it follows whatever the
-- attachments are on, and it cannot fly off on its own.
local function spawnOne(trail)
	if #live >= MAX then return end
	local a0, a1 = trail.Attachment0, trail.Attachment1
	if not (a0 and a1) then return end   -- a trail with no attachments draws nothing
	local ct = trail:Clone()
	-- The clone carries the source's tag and Properties folder with it. Left alone it would be
	-- picked up as an emitter in its own right and emit its own copies every frame.
	CollectionService:RemoveTag(ct, TAG)
	local cpf = ct:FindFirstChild(PROPS); if cpf then cpf:Destroy() end
	ct:SetAttribute("EmitCount", nil)
	ct.Attachment0 = a0   -- the SOURCE's own attachments, not clones
	ct.Attachment1 = a1
	ct.Enabled = true     -- apply() re-reads Appearance.Enabled each frame; this is the initial state
	ct.Archivable = false -- preview output must not land in the saved place file
	ct.Parent = ensureContainer()
	-- Duration, not Lifetime: Lifetime is the ribbon's linger and goes onto the Trail itself.
	local life = gget(trail, "Emission", "Duration", 1)
	if typeof(life) ~= "number" then life = 2 end
	live[#live+1] = { trail = ct, src = trail, age = 0, life = math.max(life, 0.01) }
end
local function destroyCopy(p)
	if p.trail and p.trail.Parent then p.trail:Destroy() end
end
local function updateOne(p, dt)
	local src = p.src
	if not (src and src.Parent and CollectionService:HasTag(src, TAG)) then return false end
	p.age += dt
	if p.age >= p.life then return false end
	-- Follow the source's attachments live: if the user repoints them, the copy must track that
	-- rather than hold a stale reference.
	local a0, a1 = src.Attachment0, src.Attachment1
	if not (a0 and a1) then return false end
	if p.trail.Attachment0 ~= a0 then p.trail.Attachment0 = a0 end
	if p.trail.Attachment1 ~= a1 then p.trail.Attachment1 = a1 end
	apply(p.trail, src, p.age / p.life, true)
	return true
end
local function flush()
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do destroyCopy(p) end
	live = {}
	accs = {}
	for trail in pairs(watchers) do dropWatcher(trail) end
	-- Proxies are Archivable=false, so a Trail left pointing at one would dangle if the place were
	-- saved after the preview stops. Always restore the authored attachments on the way out.
	for _, trail in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTagged(TAG)) do
		if trail:IsA("Trail") then pcall(releaseProxies, trail) end
	end
	if container and container.Parent then container:Destroy() end
	container = nil
end
-- True if this tagged trail is TEMPLATE content (nested inside a 3D particle emitter, or a stray
-- tagged clone in the preview container) rather than a standalone transformed trail.
local function step(dt)
	local tagged = CollectionService:GetTagged(TAG)
	if #tagged == 0 and #live == 0 and next(watchers) == nil then return end   -- idle fast-path
	local taggedSet = {}
	for _, trail in ipairs(tagged) do
		if not (trail:IsA("Trail") and trail:IsDescendantOf(game)) then continue end
		-- Template content (nested in a 3D particle's RenderPart, or a spawned particle's own
		-- copy of it) still gets DRIVEN -- otherwise editing its rows in Properties looked like it
		-- did nothing -- it just never emits standalone copies of itself.
		taggedSet[trail] = true
		-- 1) drive the source (external-edit aware) so property edits are visible immediately
		pcall(driveSource, trail)
		-- 2) continuous emission. A ribbon nested in a RenderPart emits exactly like a standalone
		-- one: everything SineVFX can emit behaves the same wherever it lives. Only copies WE
		-- spawned into the preview container are excluded (above), or emission would compound.
		if not gget(trail, "Emission", "Emitting", false) then accs[trail] = 0; continue end
		local rate = gget(trail, "Emission", "Rate", 1)
		if typeof(rate) ~= "number" or rate <= 0 then continue end
		local a = (accs[trail] or 0) + rate * dt
		while a >= 1 do a -= 1; spawnOne(trail) end
		accs[trail] = a
	end
	for trail in pairs(watchers) do
		if not taggedSet[trail] then dropWatcher(trail) end
	end
	local keep = {}
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do
		local ok, alive = pcall(updateOne, p, dt)
		if ok and alive then keep[#keep+1] = p else destroyCopy(p) end
	end
	live = keep
end
function M.init()
	active = false
	-- A previous session's copies are pure preview output and must never accumulate in the place.
	local terrain = workspace:FindFirstChildOfClass("Terrain")
	local old = terrain and terrain:FindFirstChild(CONTAINER)
	if old then old:Destroy() end
	-- A session that ended without a clean stop (crash, force-close) can leave proxies installed
	-- and the Trail pointing at one. Hand every tagged trail back its authored attachments.
	for _, trail in ipairs(CollectionService:GetTagged(TAG)) do
		if trail:IsA("Trail") then pcall(releaseProxies, trail) end
	end
end
-- One-off burst of `count` copies (the Emit button's path).
function M.burst(trail, count)
	if not (typeof(trail) == "Instance" and trail:IsA("Trail") and CollectionService:HasTag(trail, TAG)) then return end
	for _ = 1, math.max(count or 1, 0) do spawnOne(trail) end
end
-- True if `inst` is a transformed trail (so the Emit window can offer it without knowing the tag).
function M.isTrailEmitter(inst)
	return typeof(inst) == "Instance" and inst:IsA("Trail") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, TAG)
end
function M.setActive(v)
	if v == active then return end
	active = v
	if v then
		if not conn then conn = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(step) end
	else
		if conn then conn:Disconnect(); conn = nil end
		flush()
	end
end
-- Live emitted copies belonging to the given source trails ([trail]=true). Mirrors
-- EmitterRuntime.countForParts: the copies live in the Terrain container, NOT under the selection,
-- so the ParticleCounter cannot find them by walking the scene and has to ask the runtime.
function M.countForTrails(set)
	if not set then return 0 end
	local n = 0
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do
		if p.src and set[p.src] then n += 1 end
	end
	return n
end
function M.stop() M.setActive(false) end
return M
end)()
-- ================= BeamRuntime (merged in as M.Beam) =================
M.Beam = (function()
-- BeamRuntime: edit-mode PREVIEW for SineVFX transformed beams (tagged Beams, blueprint =
-- Utilities.BeamSpec). The Beam counterpart of TrailRuntime. Two jobs, and they are separate:
--
--   1. DRIVE THE SOURCE. The tagged beam keeps rendering and is written from its attributes every
--      frame so Properties-window edits show immediately. BUT direct edits to the beam's REAL
--      properties (native panel, tools, scripts) must also stick -- see "external-edit sync" below.
--
--   2. EMIT COPIES. A copy is a clone of the beam pointed at the SAME two Attachments, parented
--      into the Terrain container. Copies DO NOT FLY. Each copy ages t=0..1 over Lifetime and is
--      then destroyed.
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local RunService = game:GetService("RunService")
local BeamSpec = require(script.Parent.Parent.Utilities.Transformed).BeamSpec
local RibbonShared = require(script.Parent.RibbonShared)
local valEq        = RibbonShared.valEq
local evalNum       = RibbonShared.evalNum
local evalCol       = RibbonShared.evalCol
local e0           = RibbonShared.e0
local tintGradient  = RibbonShared.tintGradient
local fadeGradient  = RibbonShared.fadeGradient
local TAG       = BeamSpec.TAG
local PROPS     = BeamSpec.PROPS_FOLDER
local CONTAINER = BeamSpec.CONTAINER
local MAX = 400
local M = {}
local active = false
local conn = nil
local container = nil
local live = {}   -- array of { beam, src, age, life }
local accs = {}   -- [sourceBeam] = emission accumulator
local function ensureContainer()
	if container and container.Parent then return container end
	local terrain = workspace.Terrain
	container = terrain:FindFirstChild(CONTAINER)
	if not container then
		container = Instance.new("Folder")
		container.Name = CONTAINER
		container.Archivable = false
		container.Parent = terrain
	end
	return container
end
local function cfgOf(beam, name)
	local pf = beam:FindFirstChild(PROPS)
	return pf and pf:FindFirstChild(name) or nil
end
local function gget(beam, grp, attr, default)
	local c = cfgOf(beam, grp)
	if not c then return default end
	local v = c:GetAttribute(attr)
	if v == nil then return default end
	return v
end
-- Transparency's two axes composed: fade the along-length TransparencyGradient by the over-life
-- Transparency scalar. Done in OPACITY space (opacity = 1-transparency, multiplied) so the two
-- stack the same intuitive way Colour x Gradient does. `over` is a scalar (the over-life sample).
-- apply(): drives an emitted COPY at age t. (The SOURCE is driven by driveSource() so external edits
-- to its real props can be reflected back into the attributes -- see below.)
local function apply(dst, src, t)
	local grad = gget(src, "Appearance", "Gradient", nil)
	local c = evalCol(gget(src, "Appearance", "Color", nil), t, Color3.new(1,1,1))
	dst.Color = tintGradient(grad, c)
	dst.Transparency = fadeGradient(gget(src, "Appearance", "TransparencyGradient", nil), evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "Transparency", nil), t, 0))
	dst.Texture        = gget(src, "Appearance", "Texture", "")
	dst.TextureLength  = evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "TextureLength", nil), t, 1)
	dst.TextureSpeed   = evalNum(gget(src, "Appearance", "TextureSpeed", nil), t, 1)
	dst.LightEmission  = gget(src, "Appearance", "LightEmission", 0)
	dst.LightInfluence = gget(src, "Appearance", "LightInfluence", 0)
	dst.Brightness     = gget(src, "Appearance", "Brightness", 1)
	dst.Enabled        = true   -- copies always draw
	pcall(function() dst.TextureMode = Enum.TextureMode[tostring(gget(src, "Appearance", "TextureMode", "Stretch")):match("[^.]+$")] end)
	dst.ZOffset    = gget(src, "Appearance", "ZOffset", 0)
	dst.FaceCamera = gget(src, "Shape", "FaceCamera", false)
	dst.CurveSize0 = evalNum(gget(src, "Shape", "CurveSize0", nil), t, 0)
	dst.CurveSize1 = evalNum(gget(src, "Shape", "CurveSize1", nil), t, 0)
	local seg = gget(src, "Shape", "Segments", 10)
	if typeof(seg) == "number" then dst.Segments = math.max(math.floor(seg + 0.5), 1) end
	dst.Width0 = math.max(evalNum(gget(src, "Shape", "Width0", nil), t, 1), 0)
	dst.Width1 = math.max(evalNum(gget(src, "Shape", "Width1", nil), t, 1), 0)
end
-- ===== external-edit sync ================================================================
-- The source beam is driven from its attributes every frame, which alone would clobber any edit
-- made OUTSIDE the Properties window (native Properties panel, SineVFX tools, scripts). So:
--   * driveSource() writes each real prop from its attribute AND records the value it wrote.
--   * a per-prop change watcher folds any OTHER change (i.e. one this runtime did not make) back
--     into the matching attribute, so the next driveSource reproduces it instead of fighting.
-- Deferred signals mean we cannot rely on a "we're writing now" flag; instead the watcher compares
-- the current value against the last value we drove and ignores a match.
local watchers = setmetatable({}, {__mode = "k"})  -- [beam] = { conns = {...}, applied = {[prop]=v} }
-- real Beam property <-> (group, attr) with a codec. dec(v) turns the ATTRIBUTE into the real prop
-- (source at t=0); enc(v) turns an externally-edited real prop back into the attribute.
local MAP = {
	{p="Enabled",        g="Appearance", a="Enabled",        dec=function(v) return v and true or false end, enc=function(v) return v and true or false end},
	{p="Color",          g="Appearance", a="Gradient",       dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="ColorSequence") and v or ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)) end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="Transparency",   g="Appearance", a="TransparencyGradient", dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="NumberSequence") and v or NumberSequence.new(0) end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="Texture",        g="Appearance", a="Texture",        dec=function(v) return v or "" end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="TextureLength",  g="Appearance", a="TextureLength",  dec=function(v) return e0(v,1) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="TextureSpeed",   g="Appearance", a="TextureSpeed",   dec=function(v) return e0(v,1) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="LightEmission",  g="Appearance", a="LightEmission",  dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 0 end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="LightInfluence", g="Appearance", a="LightInfluence", dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 0 end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="Brightness",     g="Appearance", a="Brightness",     dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 1 end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="ZOffset",        g="Appearance", a="ZOffset",        dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and v or 0 end, enc=function(v) return v end},
	{p="TextureMode",    g="Appearance", a="TextureMode",    dec=function(v) return Enum.TextureMode[tostring(v or "Stretch"):match("[^.]+$")] or Enum.TextureMode.Stretch end, enc=function(v) return v.Name end},
	{p="FaceCamera",     g="Shape",      a="FaceCamera",     dec=function(v) return v and true or false end, enc=function(v) return v and true or false end},
	{p="Segments",       g="Shape",      a="Segments",       dec=function(v) return (typeof(v)=="number") and math.max(math.floor(v+0.5),1) or 10 end, enc=function(v) return math.max(math.floor(v+0.5),1) end},
	{p="Width0",         g="Shape",      a="Width0",         dec=function(v) return math.max(e0(v,1),0) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(math.max(v,0)) end},
	{p="Width1",         g="Shape",      a="Width1",         dec=function(v) return math.max(e0(v,1),0) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(math.max(v,0)) end},
	{p="CurveSize0",     g="Shape",      a="CurveSize0",     dec=function(v) return e0(v,0) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
	{p="CurveSize1",     g="Shape",      a="CurveSize1",     dec=function(v) return e0(v,0) end, enc=function(v) return NumberSequence.new(v) end},
}
local function dropWatcher(beam)
	local w = watchers[beam]; if not w then return end
	for _, c in ipairs(w.conns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
	watchers[beam] = nil
end
local function ensureWatcher(beam)
	local w = watchers[beam]
	if w then return w end
	w = { conns = {}, applied = {} }
	watchers[beam] = w
	for _, mm in ipairs(MAP) do
		local ok, sig = pcall(function() return beam:GetPropertyChangedSignal(mm.p) end)
		if ok and sig then
			w.conns[#w.conns+1] = sig:Connect(function()
				local ww = watchers[beam]; if not ww then return end
				local cur = beam[mm.p]
				if valEq(cur, ww.applied[mm.p]) then return end   -- the value WE drove; not external
				local cfg = cfgOf(beam, mm.g)
				if not cfg then return end
				local okE, enc = pcall(mm.enc, cur)
				if okE then cfg:SetAttribute(mm.a, enc); ww.applied[mm.p] = cur end
			end)
		end
	end
	return w
end
-- Drive the source's real props from its attributes, recording each written value so the watcher
-- can tell our own writes apart from external edits.
local function driveSource(beam)
	local w = ensureWatcher(beam)
	for _, mm in ipairs(MAP) do
		local cfg = cfgOf(beam, mm.g)
		local av = cfg and cfg:GetAttribute(mm.a)
		local okD, real = pcall(mm.dec, av)
		if okD then
			pcall(function() beam[mm.p] = real end)
			w.applied[mm.p] = beam[mm.p]   -- read back post-clamp so the watcher compares the real stored value
		end
	end
end
local function spawnOne(beam)
	if #live >= MAX then return end
	local a0, a1 = beam.Attachment0, beam.Attachment1
	if not (a0 and a1) then return end
	local cb = beam:Clone()
	CollectionService:RemoveTag(cb, TAG)
	local cpf = cb:FindFirstChild(PROPS); if cpf then cpf:Destroy() end
	cb:SetAttribute("EmitCount", nil)
	cb.Attachment0 = a0
	cb.Attachment1 = a1
	cb.Enabled = true
	cb.Archivable = false
	cb.Parent = ensureContainer()
	local life = gget(beam, "Emission", "Lifetime", 1)
	if typeof(life) ~= "number" then life = 2 end
	live[#live+1] = { beam = cb, src = beam, age = 0, life = math.max(life, 0.01) }
end
local function destroyCopy(p)
	if p.beam and p.beam.Parent then p.beam:Destroy() end
end
local function updateOne(p, dt)
	local src = p.src
	if not (src and src.Parent and CollectionService:HasTag(src, TAG)) then return false end
	p.age += dt
	if p.age >= p.life then return false end
	local a0, a1 = src.Attachment0, src.Attachment1
	if not (a0 and a1) then return false end
	if p.beam.Attachment0 ~= a0 then p.beam.Attachment0 = a0 end
	if p.beam.Attachment1 ~= a1 then p.beam.Attachment1 = a1 end
	apply(p.beam, src, p.age / p.life)
	return true
end
local function flush()
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do destroyCopy(p) end
	live = {}
	accs = {}
	for beam in pairs(watchers) do dropWatcher(beam) end
	if container and container.Parent then container:Destroy() end
	container = nil
end
-- True if this tagged beam is TEMPLATE content (nested inside a 3D particle emitter, or a stray
-- tagged clone that landed in the preview container) rather than a standalone transformed beam.
-- Such a beam must not be driven or emit copies of its own.
local function step(dt)
	local tagged = CollectionService:GetTagged(TAG)
	if #tagged == 0 and #live == 0 and next(watchers) == nil then return end
	local taggedSet = {}
	for _, beam in ipairs(tagged) do
		if beam:IsA("Beam") and beam:IsDescendantOf(game) then
			taggedSet[beam] = true
			pcall(driveSource, beam)   -- 1) drive source (with external-edit sync)
			-- 2) continuous emission (same rule as the Trail step: nesting changes nothing)
			if gget(beam, "Emission", "Emitting", false) then
				local rate = gget(beam, "Emission", "Rate", 1)
				if typeof(rate) == "number" and rate > 0 then
					local a = (accs[beam] or 0) + rate * dt
					while a >= 1 do a -= 1; spawnOne(beam) end
					accs[beam] = a
				end
			else
				accs[beam] = 0
			end
		end
	end
	-- drop watchers for beams that are no longer tagged / gone
	for beam in pairs(watchers) do
		if not taggedSet[beam] then dropWatcher(beam) end
	end
	local keep = {}
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do
		local ok, alive = pcall(updateOne, p, dt)
		if ok and alive then keep[#keep+1] = p else destroyCopy(p) end
	end
	live = keep
end
function M.init()
	active = false
	local terrain = workspace:FindFirstChildOfClass("Terrain")
	local old = terrain and terrain:FindFirstChild(CONTAINER)
	if old then old:Destroy() end
end
function M.burst(beam, count)
	if not (typeof(beam) == "Instance" and beam:IsA("Beam") and CollectionService:HasTag(beam, TAG)) then return end
	for _ = 1, math.max(count or 1, 0) do spawnOne(beam) end
end
function M.isBeamEmitter(inst)
	return typeof(inst) == "Instance" and inst:IsA("Beam") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, TAG)
end
function M.setActive(v)
	if v == active then return end
	active = v
	if v then
		if not conn then conn = RunService.Heartbeat:Connect(step) end
	else
		if conn then conn:Disconnect(); conn = nil end
		flush()
	end
end
function M.countForBeams(set)
	if not set then return 0 end
	local n = 0
	for _, p in ipairs(live) do
		if p.src and set[p.src] then n += 1 end
	end
	return n
end
function M.stop() M.setActive(false) end
return M
end)()
return M

-- EmitterAPI: shared.sv emit/enable/disable functions
-- Usage:
--   local API = require(this)
--   API.init(soundRef, Helpers)   -- soundRef = { widget = nil }
--   shared.sv = API.sv
local API = {}
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local EmitterRuntime = require(script.Parent.EmitterRuntime)
local EMITTER_3D_TAG = "SineVFX3D"
local _soundRef
local _Helpers
local _emitHook  -- optional fn(em,count) called on every :Emit (for the Particle Counter)
function API.setEmitHook(fn) _emitHook = fn end
local activeClones = {}  -- [sourceSound] = clone, tracks all looping/playing clones

function API.init(soundRef, Helpers)
	_soundRef = soundRef
	_Helpers  = Helpers
end

-- Returns the sound widget parent (always valid now since widget is persistent)
local function getSoundParent()
	if _soundRef and _soundRef.widget then
		return _soundRef.widget
	end
	-- fallback just in case (should never happen)
	return game:GetService("SoundService")
end

-- Waits until the clone is playable, giving up after 5s (a broken or private
-- SoundId never fires Loaded and used to hang the thread + leak the clone).
local function waitLoaded(clone)
	local t0 = os.clock()
	while not clone.IsLoaded do
		if os.clock() - t0 > 5 then return false end
		task.wait(0.1)
	end
	return true
end

local function stopClone(snd)
	local clone = activeClones[snd]
	if clone then
		pcall(function() clone:Stop(); clone:Destroy() end)
		activeClones[snd] = nil
	end
end

API.sv = {
	emit = function(...)
		for _, instance in ipairs({...}) do
			if not instance then continue end
			if instance:IsA("BasePart") and CollectionService:HasTag(instance, EMITTER_3D_TAG) then
				-- 3D emitter: burst its particle clones (the 3D system) AND still fall through to
				-- the normal path below, so RenderPart's descendants are treated like any part's.
				-- Mirrors the real-ParticleEmitter branch below: Enabled brackets the Duration
				-- window (drives EmitterRuntime's own continuous Rate-based emission), the manual
				-- burst is instant and independent of Duration -- just like em:Emit(count).
				local count = instance:GetAttribute("EmitCount") or 0
				local delay = instance:GetAttribute("EmitDelay") or 0
				local dur   = instance:GetAttribute("EmitDuration") or 0
				task.spawn(function()
					if delay > 0 then task.wait(delay) end
					if not instance.Parent then return end
					local pf = instance:FindFirstChild("Properties"); local app = pf and pf:FindFirstChild("Appearance")
					if dur > 0 and app then app:SetAttribute("Enabled", true) end
					if count > 0 then EmitterRuntime.burst(instance, count) end
					if dur > 0 then
						task.wait(dur)
						if instance.Parent and app then app:SetAttribute("Enabled", false) end
					end
				end)
			end
			local emitters    = _Helpers.findEmitters(instance)
			local sounds      = _Helpers.findSounds(instance)
			local toggleables = _Helpers.findToggleables(instance)
			for _, em in pairs(emitters) do
				task.spawn(function()
					local delay = em:GetAttribute("EmitDelay")    or 0
					local count = em:GetAttribute("EmitCount")    or 1
					local dur   = em:GetAttribute("EmitDuration") or 0
					if delay > 0 then task.wait(delay) end
					if dur > 0 then em.Enabled = true end
					em:Emit(count)
					if _emitHook then pcall(_emitHook, em, count) end
					if dur > 0 then task.wait(dur); em.Enabled = false end
				end)
			end
			for _, t in pairs(toggleables) do
				task.spawn(function()
					local delay = t:GetAttribute("EmitDelay")    or 0
					local dur   = t:GetAttribute("EmitDuration") or 0
					if dur <= 0 then return end
					if delay > 0 then task.wait(delay) end
					t.Enabled = true
					task.wait(dur); t.Enabled = false
				end)
			end
			for _, snd in pairs(sounds) do
				task.spawn(function()
					local delay = snd:GetAttribute("EmitDelay")    or 0
					local dur   = snd:GetAttribute("EmitDuration") or 0
					if delay > 0 then task.wait(delay) end
					local clone = snd:Clone()
					clone.Looped = dur > 0
					clone.Parent = getSoundParent()
					if not waitLoaded(clone) then clone:Destroy() return end
					clone:Play()
					if dur > 0 then
						-- stop any clone already looping for this sound (from enable()
						-- or an earlier emit) so it can't be orphaned mid-loop
						stopClone(snd)
						activeClones[snd] = clone
						task.wait(dur)
						if activeClones[snd] == clone then activeClones[snd] = nil end
						pcall(function() clone:Stop(); clone:Destroy() end)
					else
						clone.Ended:Connect(function() clone:Destroy() end)
					end
				end)
			end
		end
	end,

	enable = function(...)
		for _, instance in ipairs({...}) do
			if not instance then continue end
			local emitters    = _Helpers.findEmitters(instance)
			local sounds      = _Helpers.findSounds(instance)
			local toggleables = _Helpers.findToggleables(instance)
			for _, em in pairs(emitters)    do em.Enabled = true end
			for _, t  in pairs(toggleables) do t.Enabled  = true end
			for _, snd in pairs(sounds) do
				stopClone(snd)
				task.spawn(function()
					local clone = snd:Clone()
					clone.Looped = true
					clone.Parent = getSoundParent()
					if not waitLoaded(clone) then clone:Destroy() return end
					clone:Play()
					stopClone(snd)  -- an emit-with-duration may have raced in meanwhile
					activeClones[snd] = clone
				end)
			end
		end
	end,

	disable = function(...)
		for _, instance in ipairs({...}) do
			if not instance then continue end
			local emitters    = _Helpers.findEmitters(instance)
			local sounds      = _Helpers.findSounds(instance)
			local toggleables = _Helpers.findToggleables(instance)
			for _, em in pairs(emitters)    do em.Enabled = false end
			for _, t  in pairs(toggleables) do t.Enabled  = false end
			for _, snd in pairs(sounds) do stopClone(snd) end
		end
	end,
}

return API

-- RibbonShared: pure sequence/gradient helpers shared by TrailRuntime & BeamRuntime.
-- No module state (only pure functions) -- safe to require anywhere. Extracted from the
-- byte-identical copies that used to live in both runtimes (modularization 2026-07-18).

local function valEq(a, b)
	if typeof(a) ~= typeof(b) then return false end
	local t = typeof(a)
	if t == "ColorSequence" or t == "NumberSequence" then
		local ka, kb = a.Keypoints, b.Keypoints
		if #ka ~= #kb then return false end
		for i = 1, #ka do
			local x, y = ka[i], kb[i]
			if x.Time ~= y.Time or x.Value ~= y.Value then return false end
			if t == "NumberSequence" and x.Envelope ~= y.Envelope then return false end
		end
		return true
	end
	return a == b
end

local function evalNum(seq, t, default)
	if typeof(seq) ~= "NumberSequence" then return default end
	local kps = seq.Keypoints
	if #kps == 0 then return default end
	if t <= kps[1].Time then return kps[1].Value end
	if t >= kps[#kps].Time then return kps[#kps].Value end
	for i = 1, #kps - 1 do
		local a, b = kps[i], kps[i+1]
		if t >= a.Time and t <= b.Time then
			local span = b.Time - a.Time
			if span <= 0 then return b.Value end
			return a.Value + (b.Value - a.Value) * ((t - a.Time) / span)
		end
	end
	return kps[#kps].Value
end

local function evalCol(seq, t, default)
	if typeof(seq) ~= "ColorSequence" then return default end
	local kps = seq.Keypoints
	if #kps == 0 then return default end
	if t <= kps[1].Time then return kps[1].Value end
	if t >= kps[#kps].Time then return kps[#kps].Value end
	for i = 1, #kps - 1 do
		local a, b = kps[i], kps[i+1]
		if t >= a.Time and t <= b.Time then
			local span = b.Time - a.Time
			if span <= 0 then return b.Value end
			return a.Value:Lerp(b.Value, (t - a.Time) / span)
		end
	end
	return kps[#kps].Value
end

local function e0(seq, d) return evalNum(seq, 0, d) end

local function tintGradient(grad, c)
	if typeof(grad) ~= "ColorSequence" then return ColorSequence.new(c) end
	local out = {}
	for i, k in ipairs(grad.Keypoints) do
		out[i] = ColorSequenceKeypoint.new(k.Time, Color3.new(k.Value.R * c.R, k.Value.G * c.G, k.Value.B * c.B))
	end
	return ColorSequence.new(out)
end

local function fadeGradient(grad, over)
	over = math.clamp(over, 0, 1)
	if typeof(grad) ~= "NumberSequence" then return NumberSequence.new(over) end
	local out = {}
	for i, k in ipairs(grad.Keypoints) do
		local v = 1 - (1 - math.clamp(k.Value, 0, 1)) * (1 - over)
		out[i] = NumberSequenceKeypoint.new(k.Time, math.clamp(v, 0, 1), 0)
	end
	return NumberSequence.new(out)
end

return { valEq=valEq, evalNum=evalNum, evalCol=evalCol, e0=e0, tintGradient=tintGradient, fadeGradient=fadeGradient }

-- Updater: version + update check against the public GitHub repo.
--
-- The repo is the single source of truth for "what is the latest version". The plugin never parses
-- HTML or the GitHub API (rate-limited, needs auth for private repos) -- it reads one small JSON
-- file from raw.githubusercontent.com, which is CDN-cached and unauthenticated.
--
--   version.json   { "version": "1.4.0", "published": "...", "notes": "...", "download": "..." }
--   CHANGELOG.md   human-readable, fetched only when the user asks to see it
--
-- A cache-busting query is appended because raw.githubusercontent caches aggressively (~5 min).
local HttpService = game:GetService("HttpService")

local M = {}

-- Bump this on every release. It is what the update check compares against, and what Settings shows.
M.VERSION = "0.7.9"

M.REPO       = "RoPotat0/Sine-VFX"
M.RAW        = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/" .. M.REPO .. "/main/"
M.RELEASES   = "https://github.com/" .. M.REPO .. "/releases/latest"

-- "1.4.10" > "1.4.9": compare numerically per segment, not as strings.
function M.parse(v)
	local out = {}
	for n in tostring(v or ""):gmatch("%d+") do out[#out+1] = tonumber(n) end
	return out
end
function M.newer(a, b)   -- true if a is a strictly newer version than b
	local pa, pb = M.parse(a), M.parse(b)
	for i = 1, math.max(#pa, #pb) do
		local x, y = pa[i] or 0, pb[i] or 0
		if x ~= y then return x > y end
	end
	return false
end

local function fetch(url)
	local ok, res = pcall(function()
		return HttpService:GetAsync(url .. (url:find("?") and "&" or "?") .. "t=" .. tostring(os.time()), true)
	end)
	if not ok then return nil, tostring(res) end
	return res
end

-- Returns a state table: { checking=false, latest="1.4.0", behind=true/false, notes=..., err=... }
-- Never throws: a failed check must not stop the plugin from loading.
function M.check()
	local body, err = fetch(M.RAW .. "version.json")
	if not body then return { checking = false, err = err or "request failed" } end
	local ok, data = pcall(function() return HttpService:JSONDecode(body) end)
	if not ok or type(data) ~= "table" or not data.version then
		return { checking = false, err = "bad version.json" }
	end
	return {
		checking = false,
		latest   = tostring(data.version),
		behind   = M.newer(data.version, M.VERSION),
		notes    = data.notes,
		download = data.download or M.RELEASES,
	}
end

function M.changelog()
	return fetch(M.RAW .. "CHANGELOG.md")
end

return M

		small   = { font = Enum.Font.GothamMedium,  size = 11 },

	},

	-- Icon asset IDs (all windows)
	ICONS = {
		pin          = "120413064155653",
		close        = "101421401454601",
		reset        = "438217405",
		repeatOff    = "131602901815003",
		repeatOn     = "97414960795019",
		replace      = "92441021070637",
		multiply     = "77849852923386",
		dropdown     = "116140507142780",
		hub          = "14895352873",
		emit         = "14915555120",
		tools        = "95700282244988",
		settings     = "9405931596",
		module       = "117954004806550",
	},

	-- Hover lerp strength toward white
	HOVER_STRENGTH = 0.4,
	FADE_STRENGTH  = 0.2,  -- filled-button background lighten on hover

	TI      = TweenInfo.new(0.1,  Enum.EasingStyle.Quad,   Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- general UI tween
	FLASH_TI= TweenInfo.new(0.2, Enum.EasingStyle.Quad,   Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- pin flash
	SCALE_TI= TweenInfo.new(0.15, Enum.EasingStyle.Cubic,  Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- handle scale
	SCALE_TI_OUT= TweenInfo.new(0.5, Enum.EasingStyle.Cubic,  Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- handle scale
	DD_TI   = TweenInfo.new(0,  Enum.EasingStyle.Quad,   Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- dropdown open
	DD_CLOSE= TweenInfo.new(0, Enum.EasingStyle.Quad,   Enum.EasingDirection.Out),  -- dropdown close
