-- UIKit: UI component factory
-- Hot reload: the loader can swap this plugin's code in place, so every ALWAYS-ON connection
-- (the ones made at module load rather than per window mount) is registered here and disconnected
-- by the teardown. Without it a reload would leave the old copy's loops running forever.
local function SVFXlive(c)
	local reg = _G.__SineVFX_LIVE
	if not reg then reg = {}; _G.__SineVFX_LIVE = reg end
	reg[#reg+1] = c
	return c
end
-- Usage: local UIKit = require(this)(tweenService, Theme)
return function(tweenService, Theme)
	local C       = Theme.C
	local TI      = Theme.TI
	local DRAG_TI = Theme.DRAG_TI
	local kit     = {}

	local INSTANT_TI = TweenInfo.new(0)
	local _instant   = false  -- when true, all tw() calls are instant

	function kit.setInstant(v) _instant = v end

	function kit.tw(obj, props, info)
		if _instant then
			-- Apply properties instantly without a tween
			for k, v in pairs(props) do
				pcall(function() obj[k] = v end)
			end
		else
			tweenService:Create(obj, info or TI, props):Play()
		end
	end

	-- twNow: always animates, ignoring the global instant flag. Use for
	-- hover/interaction feedback so a concurrent window-mount can't make it snap.
	function kit.twNow(obj, props, info)
		tweenService:Create(obj, info or TI, props):Play()
	end

	local _cornerScale = 1
	local _corners = {}  -- STRONG (weak tables drop parented-instance proxies on GC); pruned in setCornerScale
	local _cornerPart = {}
	local _partScale = {}
	function kit.corner(p, r, part)
		local c = Instance.new("UICorner")
		local base = r or 10
		if not part then part = (base>=10 and "window") or (base>=7 and "card") or (base>=4 and "button") or "small" end
		_corners[c] = base
		_cornerPart[c] = part
		c.CornerRadius = UDim.new(0, base * _cornerScale * (_partScale[part] or 1))
		c.Parent = p
		return c
	end

	function kit.setCornerScale(m)
		m = tonumber(m) or 1
		_cornerScale = m
		for c, base in pairs(_corners) do
			if c.Parent then c.CornerRadius = UDim.new(0, base * m * (_partScale[_cornerPart[c]] or 1)) else _corners[c] = nil; _cornerPart[c] = nil end
		end
	end

	function kit.setPartScale(part, m)
		m = tonumber(m) or 1
		_partScale[part] = m
		for c, base in pairs(_corners) do
			if not c.Parent then _corners[c]=nil; _cornerPart[c]=nil elseif _cornerPart[c]==part then c.CornerRadius = UDim.new(0, base * _cornerScale * m) end
		end
	end
	function kit.getCornerScale() return _cornerScale end

	function kit.safeFont(el)
		local f=el.Font
		if f~=Enum.Font.Unknown then return f end
		local bn=el:GetAttribute("SVFXbaseFont")
		if bn then local ok,e2=pcall(function() return Enum.Font[bn] end); if ok and e2 then return e2 end end
		return Enum.Font.Gotham
	end
	function kit.makeBorder(p, col, t)
		local s = Instance.new("UIStroke")
		s.Color           = col or C.border
		s.Thickness       = t or 1
		s.Transparency    = 0.3
		s.ApplyStrokeMode = Enum.ApplyStrokeMode.Border
		s:SetAttribute("SVFXborder", col == nil)
		-- Border CATEGORY for per-element outline colors (only "themed" borders: default or C.border;
		-- an explicit accent/danger color is left alone). button > field > card by the parent's class.
		if col == nil or col == C.border then
			local cat = "card"
			if p and (p:IsA("TextButton") or p:IsA("ImageButton")) then cat = "button"
			elseif p and p:IsA("TextBox") then cat = "field" end
			s:SetAttribute("SVFXbordercat", cat)
		end
		s.Parent          = p
		return s
	end

	function kit.fadeButton(btn, strength)
		strength = strength or Theme.FADE_STRENGTH
		local base = btn:GetAttribute("BaseColor") or btn.BackgroundColor3
		kit.tw(btn, { BackgroundColor3 = base:Lerp(Color3.new(1,1,1), strength) },
			Theme.FADE_TI)
	end

	-- Hover-lift: on hover, lighten the element's OWN colour toward white by
	-- `strength` (default Theme.HOVER_STRENGTH); restore it on leave. Use for ANY
	-- text/icon button so every one highlights the same way — a lighter shade of
	-- its own colour, never a different theme colour. `el` = the coloured object
	-- (Text/Image Label or Button); `trigger` = what senses the hover (defaults el).
	-- Captures the resting colour at call time, so call AFTER setting it.
	-- Resting colour is read from the "HoverBase" attribute if present (falling back
	-- to the current colour at call time), so callers whose resting colour changes
	-- later (e.g. a toggle button) just update that attribute -- no need to re-call
	-- hoverLift, which would otherwise stack duplicate Enter/Leave connections.
	-- `propOverride` lifts a different property than the auto-picked one -- pass "BackgroundColor3"
	-- for a filled button, whose highlight is its FILL rather than its label.
	function kit.hoverLift(el, trigger, strength, towardColor, propOverride)
		trigger = trigger or el
		strength = strength or Theme.HOVER_STRENGTH
		towardColor = towardColor or Color3.new(1,1,1)
		local prop = propOverride or ((el:IsA("ImageLabel") or el:IsA("ImageButton")) and "ImageColor3" or (el:IsA("UIStroke") and "Color") or "TextColor3")
		if el:GetAttribute("HoverBase")==nil then el:SetAttribute("HoverBase", el[prop]) end
		local function base() return el:GetAttribute("HoverBase") end
		trigger.MouseEnter:Connect(function() kit.tw(el, { [prop] = base():Lerp(towardColor, strength) }) end)
		trigger.MouseLeave:Connect(function() kit.tw(el, { [prop] = base() }) end)
		return el
	end

	-- Restrict a TextBox to numeric input only (digits, minus, decimal point)
	-- Skips filtering for placeholder values like "..."
	function kit.numericOnly(box)
		box:GetPropertyChangedSignal("Text"):Connect(function()
			local t = box.Text
			if t == "" or t == "-" or t == "." or t == "..." then return end
			local filtered = t:match("^-?%d*%.?%d*") or ""
			if filtered ~= t then
				-- preserve the caret: removing chars (e.g. a 2nd dot) used to fling it to the
				-- front of the dot. Shift it left by however many chars were stripped.
				local cp = box.CursorPosition
				box.Text = filtered
				if cp and cp > 0 then box.CursorPosition = math.clamp(cp - (#t - #filtered), 1, #filtered + 1) end
			end
		end)
	end

	function kit.setIconImage(iconLabel, iconAssetId)
		local sid = tostring(iconAssetId)
		iconLabel.Image = "rbxthumb://type=Asset&id=" .. sid .. "&w=150&h=150"
	end

	-- Standard top-bar icon button (right-aligned by xOff)
	function kit.makeTopBtn(parent, iconId, xOff, iconSize)
		local btn = Instance.new("TextButton")
		btn.Size = UDim2.new(0, 24, 0, 24)
		btn.Position = UDim2.new(1, xOff, 0.5, -12)
		btn.BackgroundColor3 = C.surface2
		btn.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		btn.AutoButtonColor = false
		btn.Text = ""
		btn.ZIndex = 5
		btn.Parent = parent
		kit.corner(btn, 6)
		local icon = Instance.new("ImageLabel")
		icon.Size = UDim2.new(0, iconSize, 0, iconSize)
		icon.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, 0.5)
		icon.Position = UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0.5, 0)
		icon.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		icon.ImageColor3 = C.textDim
		icon.ZIndex = 6
		icon.Parent = btn
		kit.setIconImage(icon, iconId)
		btn.MouseEnter:Connect(function() kit.tw(icon, { ImageColor3 = icon.ImageColor3:Lerp(Color3.new(1,1,1), Theme.HOVER_STRENGTH) }) end)
		btn.MouseLeave:Connect(function() kit.tw(icon, { ImageColor3 = btn:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") and C.Pinned or C.textDim }) end)
		if kit.attachTooltip then local tip = kit._topBtnTip and kit._topBtnTip(iconId); if tip then kit.attachTooltip(btn, tip) end end
		return btn, icon
	end

	-- Top-bar button with explicit UDim2 position (for asymmetric layouts)
	function kit.makeTopBtnPos(parent, iconId, posUdim, iconSize)
		local btn = Instance.new("TextButton")
		btn.Size = UDim2.new(0, 24, 0, 24)
		btn.Position = posUdim
		btn.BackgroundColor3 = C.surface2
		btn.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		btn.AutoButtonColor = false
		btn.Text = ""
		btn.ZIndex = 5
		btn.Parent = parent
		kit.corner(btn, 6)
		local icon = Instance.new("ImageLabel")
		icon.Size = UDim2.new(0, iconSize, 0, iconSize)
		icon.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, 0.5)
		icon.Position = UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0.5, 0)
		icon.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		icon.ImageColor3 = C.textDim
		icon.ZIndex = 6
		icon.Parent = btn
		kit.setIconImage(icon, iconId)
		btn.MouseEnter:Connect(function() kit.tw(icon, { ImageColor3 = icon.ImageColor3:Lerp(Color3.new(1,1,1), Theme.HOVER_STRENGTH) }) end)
		btn.MouseLeave:Connect(function() kit.tw(icon, { ImageColor3 = btn:GetAttribute("PinnedActive") and C.Pinned or C.textDim }) end)
		return btn, icon
	end

	--[[ Toggle switch
	     Returns { track, knob, button, setOn(bool, onColor?) }
	     onColor defaults to C.loop
	--]]
	function kit.makeToggle(parent, trackW, trackH, knobD, zBase)
		zBase = zBase or 1
		local track = Instance.new("Frame")
		track.Size = UDim2.new(0, trackW, 0, trackH)
		track.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0, 0.5)
		track.Position = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0.5, 0)
		track.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleOffColor or C.base
		track.BorderSizePixel = 0
		track.Parent = parent; track:SetAttribute("SVFXtoggleoff", true); track:SetAttribute("ToggleIsOff", true)
		kit.corner(track, trackH // 2, "toggle")

		local knob = Instance.new("Frame")
		knob.Size = UDim2.new(0, knobD, 0, knobD)
		knob.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, 0.5)
		knob.Position = UDim2.new(0, trackH/2, 0.5, 0)
		knob.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleKnobOffColor or Theme.toggleKnobColor or C.textDim
		knob.BorderSizePixel = 0
		knob.ZIndex = zBase + 1
		knob.Parent = track
		knob:SetAttribute("SVFXtoggleknob", true)
		kit.corner(knob, knobD // 2, "toggle")

		local btn = Instance.new("TextButton")
		btn.Size = UDim2.new(1, 12, 1, 12)
		btn.Position = UDim2.new(0, -6, 0, -6)
		btn.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		btn.Text = ""
		btn.ZIndex = zBase + 2
		btn.Parent = track

		local function setOn(on, onColor)
			track:SetAttribute("ToggleIsOff", not on)
			if on then
				kit.tw(track, { BackgroundColor3 = onColor or C.loop })
				kit.tw(knob,  { Position = UDim2.new(0, trackW - trackH/2, 0.5, 0), BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleKnobOnColor or Theme.toggleKnobColor or C.white })
			else
				kit.tw(track, { BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleOffColor or C.base })
				kit.tw(knob,  { Position = UDim2.new(0, trackH/2, 0.5, 0), BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleKnobOffColor or Theme.toggleKnobColor or C.textDim })
			end
		end

		-- Mixed / indeterminate state: knob centered, orange track — shown when a multi-selection
		-- has some instances on and some off, so the toggle isn't misleadingly all-on or all-off.
		local function setMixed(onColor)
			kit.tw(track, { BackgroundColor3 = Color3.fromRGB(235, 145, 40) })
			kit.tw(knob,  { Position = UDim2.new(0, trackW/2, 0.5, 0), BackgroundColor3 = Theme.toggleKnobColor or C.white })
		end

		return { track = track, knob = knob, button = btn, setOn = setOn, setMixed = setMixed }
	end

	--[[ Horizontal slider (for Emit emitter window rows)
	     Returns { track, fill, knob, kStroke, minLbl, maxLbl, hitbox }
	--]]
	function kit.makeHSlider(parentRow, KSIZE, ROW_H)
		local track = Instance.new("Frame")
		track.Position = UDim2.new(0, (KSIZE + 5) / 2, 0, ROW_H + 8)
		track.Size = UDim2.new(1, -(KSIZE + 5), 0, 6)
		track.BackgroundColor3 = C.base
		track:SetAttribute("SVFXslTrack", true)   -- repainted explicitly on theme change (see Main.recolorGui)
		track.BorderSizePixel = 0
		track.ZIndex = 10
		track.ClipsDescendants = false
		track.Parent = parentRow
		kit.corner(track, 3)

		local fill = Instance.new("Frame")
		fill.Size = UDim2.new(0, 0, 1, 0)
		fill.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
		fill:SetAttribute("SVFXslFill", true)
		fill.BorderSizePixel = 0
		fill.ZIndex = 4
		fill.Parent = track
		kit.corner(fill, 3)

		local knob = Instance.new("Frame")
		knob.Size = UDim2.new(0, KSIZE, 0, KSIZE + 5)
		knob.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, 0.5)
		knob.Position = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0.5, 0)
		knob.BackgroundColor3 = C.accent
		knob:SetAttribute("SVFXslKnob", true)
		knob.BorderSizePixel = 0
		knob.ZIndex = 6
		knob.Parent = track
		kit.corner(knob, KSIZE // 2)
		local kStroke = kit.makeBorder(knob, C.accent, 1.5)
		kStroke:SetAttribute("SVFXslStroke", true)

		local minLbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
		minLbl.Size = UDim2.new(0, 35, 0, 10)
		minLbl.Position = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0, ROW_H + 17)
		minLbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		minLbl.Text = "0"
		minLbl.TextColor3 = C.textDim
		minLbl.Font = Enum.Font.Gotham
		minLbl.TextSize = 10
		minLbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		minLbl.ZIndex = 3
		minLbl.Parent = parentRow

		local maxLbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
		maxLbl.Size = UDim2.new(0, 35, 0, 10)
		maxLbl.Position = UDim2.new(1, -35, 0, ROW_H + 17)
		maxLbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		maxLbl.Text = "100"
		maxLbl.TextColor3 = C.textDim
		maxLbl.Font = Enum.Font.Gotham
		maxLbl.TextSize = 10
		maxLbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Right
		maxLbl.ZIndex = 3
		maxLbl.Parent = parentRow

		local hitbox = Instance.new("TextButton")
		hitbox.Size = UDim2.new(1, 20, 1, 20)
		hitbox.Position = UDim2.new(0, -10, 0, -10)
		hitbox.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		hitbox.Text = ""
		hitbox.Active = true
		hitbox.ZIndex = 7
		hitbox.Parent = track

		return { track=track, fill=fill, knob=knob, kStroke=kStroke, minLbl=minLbl, maxLbl=maxLbl, hitbox=hitbox }
	end

	--[[ Vertical slider (for color window HSV sliders)
	     Returns: track, grad, knob, hit
	--]]
	function kit.makeVSlider(parent, order)
		local track = Instance.new("Frame")
		track.Size = UDim2.new(0, 9, 1, 0)
		track.BackgroundColor3 = Color3.new(1, 1, 1)
		track.BorderSizePixel = 0
		track.LayoutOrder = order
		track.Parent = parent
		kit.corner(track, 6)
		kit.makeBorder(track, C.border)

		local grad = Instance.new("UIGradient")
		grad.Rotation = 90
		grad.Parent = track

		local knob = Instance.new("Frame")
		knob.Size = UDim2.new(1, 8, 0, 6)
		knob.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, 0.5)
		knob.Position = UDim2.new(0.5, 0, 0, 0)
		knob.BackgroundColor3 = C.white
		knob.BorderSizePixel = 0
		knob.ZIndex = 5
		knob.Parent = track
		kit.corner(knob, 4)
		kit.makeBorder(knob, C.border, 1.5)

		local hit = Instance.new("TextButton")
		hit.Size = UDim2.new(1, 16, 1, 0)
		hit.Position = UDim2.new(0, -8, 0, 0)
		hit.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		hit.Text = ""
		hit.ZIndex = 6
		hit.Parent = track

		return track, grad, knob, hit
	end

	--[[ Scrollable list with custom scrollbar (extracted from Shifter pattern)
	     opts: { plugin, uis, sbW?, sbGap? }
	     Returns: { list (ScrollingFrame), layout, sbTrack, update }
	     Add items to .list. Set .layout.Padding after if needed.
	--]]
	-- Effective UIScale applied to an element: walk ancestors for the UIScale the window
	-- factory parents on each window's `main`. Returns 1 when unscaled, so callers that
	-- divide by it are a no-op at the default scale.
	function kit.uiScaleOf(o)
		local n = o
		while n do
			local u = n:FindFirstChildOfClass("UIScale")
			if u then return u.Scale end
			n = n.Parent
		end
		return 1
	end

	function kit.makeScrollableList(listContainer, opts)
		local plugin = opts.plugin
		local uis    = opts.uis
		local SB_W   = opts.sbW   or 7
		local SB_GAP = opts.sbGap or 3
		local OtherGap = opts.OGap

		local scrollFrame = Instance.new("ScrollingFrame")
		scrollFrame.Size                 = UDim2.new(1, 0, 1, 0)
		scrollFrame.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		scrollFrame.BorderSizePixel      = 0
		scrollFrame.ScrollBarThickness   = 0
		scrollFrame.ScrollBarImageTransparency = 1
		pcall(function() scrollFrame.TopImage=""; scrollFrame.MidImage=""; scrollFrame.BottomImage="" end)
		scrollFrame.ElasticBehavior      = Enum.ElasticBehavior.Never
		scrollFrame.CanvasSize           = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0, 0)
		scrollFrame.ClipsDescendants     = true
		scrollFrame.Parent               = listContainer
		
		local layout = Instance.new("UIListLayout")
		layout.SortOrder = Enum.SortOrder.LayoutOrder
		layout.Parent    = scrollFrame

		local sbTrack = Instance.new("Frame")
		sbTrack.AnchorPoint      = Vector2.new(1, 0)
		sbTrack.Position         = UDim2.new(1, - OtherGap, 0, 0)
		sbTrack.Size             = UDim2.new(0, SB_W, 1, 0)
		sbTrack.BackgroundColor3 = C.base
		sbTrack.BorderSizePixel  = 0
		sbTrack.Visible          = false
		sbTrack.Parent           = listContainer
		kit.corner(sbTrack, 3)

		local sbThumb = Instance.new("Frame")
		sbThumb.Size                  = UDim2.new(1, 0, 0.3, 0)
		sbThumb.BackgroundColor3      = C.slider
		sbThumb.BackgroundTransparency = 0.4
		sbThumb.BorderSizePixel       = 0
		sbThumb.Parent                = sbTrack
		kit.corner(sbThumb, 3)

		local function update()
			scrollFrame.ScrollBarThickness = 0; scrollFrame.ScrollBarImageTransparency = 1
			local contentH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y
			local visH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteSize.Y
			local _pad = scrollFrame:FindFirstChildOfClass("UIPadding"); local _extra = _pad and (_pad.PaddingTop.Offset + _pad.PaddingBottom.Offset) or 0
			if contentH <= visH + 1 or visH <= 0 then
				sbTrack.Visible   = false
				scrollFrame.ScrollingEnabled = false
				scrollFrame.ScrollBarThickness = 0
				scrollFrame.Size  = UDim2.new(1, 0, 1, 0)
				return
			end
			scrollFrame.ScrollingEnabled = false  -- native wheel off; custom smooth wheel is the sole scroller
			scrollFrame.ScrollBarThickness = 0
			sbTrack.Visible  = true
			scrollFrame.Size = UDim2.new(1, -(SB_W + SB_GAP + OtherGap), 1, 0)
			local ratio = math.clamp(visH / contentH, 0.1, 1)
			local posR  = scrollFrame.CanvasPosition.Y / math.max(1, contentH - visH)
			sbThumb.Size     = UDim2.new(1, 0, ratio, 0)
			sbThumb.Position = UDim2.new(0, 0, posR * (1 - ratio), 0)
		end

		scrollFrame:GetPropertyChangedSignal("CanvasPosition"):Connect(update)
		scrollFrame:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteSize"):Connect(update)
		layout:GetPropertyChangedSignal("AbsoluteContentSize"):Connect(function()
			local _pad = scrollFrame:FindFirstChildOfClass("UIPadding")
			local _extra = _pad and (_pad.PaddingTop.Offset + _pad.PaddingBottom.Offset) or 0
			scrollFrame.CanvasSize = UDim2.new(0, 0, 0, layout.AbsoluteContentSize.Y/kit.uiScaleOf(scrollFrame) + _extra)
			update()
		end)

		local _wheelTarget, _wheelConn  -- custom smooth-wheel state
		local function _stopWheel() if _wheelConn then _wheelConn:Disconnect(); _wheelConn=nil end; _wheelTarget=nil end
		-- Scrollbar thumb drag
		sbThumb.InputBegan:Connect(function(inp)
			if inp.UserInputType ~= Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then return end
			_stopWheel()
			plugin:Activate(false)
			local startY   = uis:GetMouseLocation().Y
			local startPos = scrollFrame.CanvasPosition.Y
			local mc = uis.InputChanged:Connect(function(i)
				if i.UserInputType ~= Enum.UserInputType.MouseMovement then return end
				local dy       = uis:GetMouseLocation().Y - startY
				local contentH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y
				local visH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteSize.Y
				local ratio    = contentH / math.max(1, sbTrack.AbsoluteSize.Y)
				scrollFrame.CanvasPosition = Vector2.new(0, math.clamp(startPos + dy * ratio, 0, math.max(0, contentH - visH)))
			end)
			local ec; ec = uis.InputEnded:Connect(function(i)
				if i.UserInputType ~= Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then return end
				mc:Disconnect(); ec:Disconnect(); plugin:Deactivate()
			end)
		end)

		-- Mouse wheel: custom SMOOTH scroll (native off). Each notch nudges a target the CanvasPosition
		-- eases toward -- shorter distance per notch, but still fluid. opts.wheelStep tunes the distance.
		local WHEEL_STEP = tonumber(opts.wheelStep) or 50
		scrollFrame.InputChanged:Connect(function(inp)
			if inp.UserInputType ~= Enum.UserInputType.MouseWheel then return end
			local contentH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteCanvasSize.Y
			local visH = scrollFrame.AbsoluteSize.Y
			local maxY = math.max(0, contentH - visH)
			_wheelTarget = math.clamp((_wheelTarget or scrollFrame.CanvasPosition.Y) - inp.Position.Z * WHEEL_STEP, 0, maxY)
			if not _wheelConn then
				_wheelConn = SVFXlive(game:GetService("RunService").RenderStepped:Connect(function()
					if _wheelTarget == nil or not scrollFrame.Parent then _stopWheel(); return end
					local cur = scrollFrame.CanvasPosition.Y
					local d = _wheelTarget - cur
					if math.abs(d) < 0.5 then scrollFrame.CanvasPosition = Vector2.new(0, _wheelTarget); _stopWheel(); return end
					scrollFrame.CanvasPosition = Vector2.new(0, cur + d * 0.28)
				end))
			end
		end)

		return { list = scrollFrame, layout = layout, sbTrack = sbTrack, update = update }
	end

	-- Transform a serialized CurveWindow bezier string ("t,v,otdt,otdv,env,itdt,itdv,sync;...").
	-- valMult scales the VALUE components (v, ot.dv, env, it.dv); timeMult scales the TIME
	-- components (t, ot.dt, it.dt). Used by Resizer/Retimer/Copier/Shifter so curves survive.
	function kit.bezierTransform(str, valMult, timeMult)
		if type(str)~="string" or str=="" then return str end
		valMult = valMult or 1; timeMult = timeMult or 1
		local out={}
		for part in string.gmatch(str,"[^;]+") do
			local f={}
			for num in string.gmatch(part,"[^,]+") do f[#f+1]=tonumber(num) end
			if f[1] and f[2] then
				local t   = (f[1] or 0)*timeMult
				local v   = (f[2] or 0)*valMult
				local otdt= (f[3] or 0)*timeMult
				local otdv= (f[4] or 0)*valMult
				local env = (f[5] or 0)*valMult
				local itdt= (f[6]~=nil) and f[6]*timeMult or otdt
				local itdv= (f[7]~=nil) and f[7]*valMult or otdv
				local sync= f[8] or 1
				out[#out+1]=string.format("%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%.6f,%d",t,v,otdt,otdv,env,itdt,itdv,sync)
			end
		end
		return table.concat(out,";")
	end

-- ══ Tooltips ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
-- Hover any element wired with kit.attachTooltip(inst, text) and, after a delay,
-- a description popup appears at the cursor. Global on/off + delay live in settings
-- (SineVFX_tooltipsOn / SineVFX_tooltipDelay), read live on each hover.
local _ttGui, _ttFrame, _ttLabel, _ttPlugin, _ttUis, _ttStroke, _ttFollow, _ttCurrent
local _ttToken = 0
-- Every hovered element, not just the last one. Roblox fires MouseEnter on a GuiObject even when
-- something is drawn over it, so a dropdown floating above a property row leaves BOTH hovered, and
-- last-writer-wins would let whichever fired last own the tooltip. Highest `prio` wins instead
-- (ties break to the most recent), so an overlay's tooltip beats the row underneath it.
local _ttHover = {}   -- [inst] = {text=, prio=, seq=}
local _ttSeq = 0
local _ttRun = game:GetService("RunService")

function kit.initTooltips(coreGui, uis, plugin)
	_ttUis = uis; _ttPlugin = plugin
	if _ttGui and _ttGui.Parent then return end
	local stale = coreGui:FindFirstChild("SineVFX_Tooltip"); if stale then stale:Destroy() end  -- clear a prior reload's overlay
	local g = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
	g.Name = "SineVFX_Tooltip"; g.ResetOnSpawn = false; g.IgnoreGuiInset = false
	g.DisplayOrder = 2000000000; g.ZIndexBehavior = Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling; g.Enabled = true
	g.Parent = coreGui
	local f = Instance.new("Frame")
	f.Name = "TT"; f.AutomaticSize = Enum.AutomaticSize.XY; f.Size = UDim2.fromOffset(0,0)
	f.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.C.base; f.BackgroundTransparency = 0.03; f.BorderSizePixel = 0
	f.Visible = false; f.ZIndex = 10; f.Parent = g
	kit.corner(f, 6); _ttStroke = kit.makeBorder(f, Theme.C.accent); _ttStroke.Thickness = 1.5
	local pad = Instance.new("UIPadding")
	pad.PaddingLeft = UDim.new(0,8); pad.PaddingRight = UDim.new(0,8)
	pad.PaddingTop = UDim.new(0,5); pad.PaddingBottom = UDim.new(0,5); pad.Parent = f
	local lbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
	lbl.BackgroundTransparency = 1; lbl.AutomaticSize = Enum.AutomaticSize.XY; lbl.Size = UDim2.fromOffset(0,0)
	lbl.Font = Enum.Font.Gotham; lbl.TextSize = 13; lbl.TextColor3 = Theme.C.text
	lbl.TextXAlignment = Enum.TextXAlignment.Left; lbl.TextYAlignment = Enum.TextYAlignment.Top
	lbl.TextWrapped = true; lbl.Parent = f
	local mx = Instance.new("UISizeConstraint"); mx.MaxSize = Vector2.new(280, math.huge); mx.Parent = lbl
	_ttGui, _ttFrame, _ttLabel = g, f, lbl
end

local function ttEnabled()
	if not _ttPlugin then return true end
	local v = _ttPlugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_tooltipsOn")
	if v == nil then return true end
	return v == true
end
local function ttDelay()
	local v = _ttPlugin and tonumber(_ttPlugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_tooltipDelay"))
	if not v or v < 0 then v = 0.3 end
	return v
end
-- Still on screen? Roblox fires no MouseLeave when an element is hidden or destroyed out from
-- under the cursor, so without this its entry sticks in _ttHover and the tooltip hangs around
-- forever. Rows hide/show constantly and dropdowns close by going invisible, so this happens a lot.
local function ttAlive(inst)
	if not inst or not inst.Parent then return false end
	local cur = inst
	while cur and cur ~= game do
		if cur:IsA("GuiObject") then
			if not cur.Visible then return false end
		elseif cur:IsA("LayerCollector") then
			return cur.Enabled
		end
		cur = cur.Parent
	end
	return true
end

local _ttVisible = false   -- is a tooltip currently on screen (drives instant-swap vs delay)
local ttReposition  -- fwd decl

local _ttGraceTok = 0
local function ttHide()
	_ttToken += 1
	_ttVisible = false
	if _ttFollow then _ttFollow:Disconnect(); _ttFollow = nil end
	if _ttFrame then _ttFrame.Visible = false end
end
kit.hideTooltip = ttHide

local function ttTarget()
	local sz = _ttFrame.AbsoluteSize
	local m  = _ttUis:GetMouseLocation()
	local vp = _ttGui.AbsoluteSize
	local x, y = m.X + 14, m.Y + 18
	if x + sz.X > vp.X - 4 then x = m.X - sz.X - 8 end
	if y + sz.Y > vp.Y - 4 then y = m.Y - sz.Y - 8 end
	return math.max(4, x), math.max(4, y)
end
ttReposition = function(snap)
	if not _ttFrame or not _ttFrame.Visible then return end
	-- the owner can vanish mid-show (row hidden, dropdown closed) without a MouseLeave
	if _ttCurrent and not ttAlive(_ttCurrent) then
		_ttHover[_ttCurrent] = nil; _ttCurrent = nil
		ttHide()
		return
	end
	local tx, ty = ttTarget()
	if snap then
		_ttFrame.Position = UDim2.fromOffset(tx, ty)
	else
		-- smooth trailing follow (lerp toward the cursor target each frame)
		local p  = _ttFrame.Position
		local nx = p.X.Offset + (tx - p.X.Offset) * 0.25
		local ny = p.Y.Offset + (ty - p.Y.Offset) * 0.25
		_ttFrame.Position = UDim2.fromOffset(math.floor(nx + 0.5), math.floor(ny + 0.5))
	end
end
local function ttShow(text)
	if not _ttFrame or type(text) ~= "string" or text == "" then return end
	_ttLabel.Text = text
	_ttFrame.BackgroundColor3 = Theme.C.base
	_ttLabel.TextColor3 = Theme.C.text
	if _ttStroke then _ttStroke.Color = Theme.C.accent end
	_ttFrame.Visible = true
	_ttVisible = true
	ttReposition(true)  -- snap to the cursor on first appearance
	if _ttFollow then _ttFollow:Disconnect() end
	_ttFollow = SVFXlive(_ttRun.RenderStepped:Connect(function() ttReposition(false) end))  -- then trail it
end

-- pick the hovered element that should own the tooltip: highest prio, then most recently entered
local function ttBest()
	local best, bp, bs = nil, -math.huge, -1
	for inst, e in pairs(_ttHover) do
		if not ttAlive(inst) then
			_ttHover[inst] = nil            -- gone or hidden while hovered; no MouseLeave ever came
		elseif e.prio > bp or (e.prio == bp and e.seq > bs) then
			best, bp, bs = inst, e.prio, e.seq
		end
	end
	return best
end

local function ttEval()
	local inst = ttBest()
	if not inst then
		-- Nothing hovered right now. If a tooltip is up, keep it for a short GRACE period rather
		-- than hiding immediately: moving between two adjacent elements fires LEAVE(old) then
		-- ENTER(new), and hiding in that gap is what made the next tooltip re-wait the full delay.
		_ttCurrent = nil
		_ttGraceTok += 1; local gt = _ttGraceTok
		if _ttVisible then
			task.delay(0.12, function() if gt == _ttGraceTok and ttBest() == nil then ttHide() end end)
		else
			ttHide()
		end
		return
	end
	_ttGraceTok += 1                      -- we have a target: cancel any pending grace-hide
	if _ttCurrent == inst then return end   -- already pending/showing for this one
	_ttCurrent = inst
	local e = _ttHover[inst]
	_ttToken += 1; local tok = _ttToken
	local function reveal()
		if _ttCurrent ~= inst or tok ~= _ttToken or not ttEnabled() then return end
		-- verify the cursor is ACTUALLY over the element before popping: MouseEnter can leave
		-- stale hover state (a higher element swallows the leave, or the layout shifts out from
		-- under the cursor), which made tooltips appear when nothing was really hovered.
		if _ttUis and inst.Parent then
			local mp = _ttUis:GetMouseLocation()
			local ins = game:GetService("GuiService"):GetGuiInset()
			local ap, sz = inst.AbsolutePosition, inst.AbsoluteSize
			local function within(mx, my) return mx >= ap.X and mx <= ap.X + sz.X and my >= ap.Y and my <= ap.Y + sz.Y end
			if not within(mp.X, mp.Y) and not within(mp.X - ins.X, mp.Y - ins.Y) then
				_ttHover[inst] = nil; _ttCurrent = nil; ttEval(); return
			end
		end
		ttShow(type(e.text) == "function" and e.text() or e.text)
	end
	if _ttVisible then
		reveal()                       -- a tooltip is already up: swap to the new one INSTANTLY
	else
		task.delay(ttDelay(), reveal)  -- nothing showing yet: wait the hover delay
	end
end

-- Wire an element so hovering it long enough pops a description tooltip. text may be a
-- string or a function returning one (evaluated at show time). prio (default 0) decides who wins
-- when overlapping elements are hovered at once -- give floating overlays a higher number than the
-- rows they cover.
function kit.attachTooltip(inst, text, prio)
	if not inst or (type(text) ~= "string" and type(text) ~= "function") then return end
	if type(text) == "string" and text == "" then return end
	prio = tonumber(prio) or 0
	inst.MouseEnter:Connect(function()
		if not ttEnabled() then return end
		_ttSeq += 1
		_ttHover[inst] = {text = text, prio = prio, seq = _ttSeq}
		ttEval()
	end)
	inst.MouseLeave:Connect(function()
		if _ttHover[inst] == nil then return end
		_ttHover[inst] = nil
		-- Don't force-hide here: re-pick instead. If another element is still hovered (or the
		-- cursor slid straight onto a new one) ttEval swaps to it -- instantly, since a tooltip
		-- was already up -- so moving between elements never gets stuck off. Hides only when
		-- nothing is left hovered.
		if _ttCurrent == inst then _ttCurrent = nil end
		ttEval()
	end)
	inst.InputBegan:Connect(function(io)
		if io.UserInputType == Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then
			_ttHover[inst] = nil; _ttCurrent = nil; ttHide()
		end
	end)
end

-- Known top-bar icon ids -> tooltip (pin/close appear on every window via makeTopBtn).
-- Close buttons intentionally have NO tooltip.
kit._topBtnTip = function(iconId)
	local id = tostring(iconId)
	-- pin + close top-bar buttons have NO tooltip (per user)
	return nil
end
	return kit
end

-- Window: draggable + resizable window factory with built-in handles
-- Hot reload: the loader can swap this plugin's code in place, so every ALWAYS-ON connection
-- (the ones made at module load rather than per window mount) is registered here and disconnected
-- by the teardown. Without it a reload would leave the old copy's loops running forever.
local function SVFXlive(c)
	local reg = _G.__SineVFX_LIVE
	if not reg then reg = {}; _G.__SineVFX_LIVE = reg end
	reg[#reg+1] = c
	return c
end
return function(ctx, Theme, UIKit)
	local C       = Theme.C
	local DRAG_TI = Theme.DRAG_TI
	local tw      = UIKit.tw
	local corner  = UIKit.corner
	local mkBord  = UIKit.makeBorder
	local plugin       = ctx.plugin
	local uis          = ctx.userInputService
	local tweenService = ctx.tweenService
	local runService   = ctx.runService

	local FLASH_TI = Theme.FLASH_TI
	local SCALE_TI = Theme.SCALE_TI
	local SCALE_TI_OUT = Theme.SCALE_TI_OUT

	-- Shared Z counter so the last-touched window always renders on top (#5)
	local DORDER_BASE = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_displayOrder")); if not DORDER_BASE then DORDER_BASE = 10000 end
	local globalZTop = DORDER_BASE
	local managedGuis = {}  -- STRONG; pruned in setBaseDisplayOrder
	local UI_SCALE = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_uiScale")) or 1
	-- Edge margin = the on-screen keep-inside gap. A toggle can disable it (effective 0)
	-- while keeping the numeric value so re-enabling restores it. Both persist per-layout.
	local _edgeMarginVal = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_edgeMargin")) or 5
	local _edgeMarginOn  = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_edgeMarginOn"); if _edgeMarginOn == nil then _edgeMarginOn = true end
	local EDGE_MARGIN = _edgeMarginOn and _edgeMarginVal or 0
	local KEEP_VIS = 24  -- when edge margin is disabled, always keep this many px on-screen so a window can't be lost
	local scaleObjs = {}  -- STRONG; pruned in setUiScale
	local topGuis = {}  -- ScreenGuis kept above all windows (Hub FAB/strip)
	local themeBgs = {}  -- [ImageLabel]=bgFrame ; window background-image layers (STRONG)
	local _bgImage, _bgFit, _bgImgOp, _bgOp, _bgTile, _bgColor, _bgStatic = "", "Fit", 1, 1, "128,128", Color3.new(1,1,1), false
	local FIT_MAP = { Fit=Enum.ScaleType.Fit, Stretch=Enum.ScaleType.Stretch, Tile=Enum.ScaleType.Tile, Crop=Enum.ScaleType.Crop }
	local function positionBg(img, bg)
		if _bgStatic then
			local cam=workspace.CurrentCamera; local vp=(cam and cam.ViewportSize) or Vector2.new(2048,2048)
			img.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0); img.Position=UDim2.new(0, -bg.AbsolutePosition.X, 0, -bg.AbsolutePosition.Y); img.Size=UDim2.fromOffset(math.ceil(vp.X), math.ceil(vp.Y))
		else
			img.AnchorPoint=Vector2.new(0,0); img.Position=UDim2.new(0,0,0,0); img.Size=UDim2.new(1,0,1,0)
		end
	end
	local function applyThemeBgTo(img, bg)
		img.Image = (_bgImage~="") and ("rbxassetid://".._bgImage) or ""
		img.ImageTransparency = 1 - _bgImgOp
		img.ImageColor3 = _bgColor
		img.ScaleType = FIT_MAP[_bgFit] or Enum.ScaleType.Fit
		local _tw,_th = tostring(_bgTile):match("(%d+)%s*,%s*(%d+)"); _tw=tonumber(_tw) or 128; _th=tonumber(_th) or _tw
		img.TileSize = (_bgFit=="Tile") and UDim2.new(0,_tw,0,_th) or UDim2.new(1,0,1,0)
		bg.BackgroundTransparency = 1 - _bgOp
		positionBg(img, bg)
	end

	-- static bg is screen-pinned, repositioned on each window's AbsolutePosition/AbsoluteSize change (see below)


	-- Shared: track last hovered handle hitbox so we can hide it if MouseLeave doesn't fire. The
	-- auto-hide sweep runs inside the combined throttled loop below (not its own 60fps loop).
	local hoveredHandle = {}  -- [hb] = doLeave

	-- Shared focus tracker: last hovered window stays focused until another is entered
	local focusedWin = nil
	local focusLocked = false  -- true while dragging/resizing
	local preserveZ = false  -- true during a theme remount: restore saved z instead of jumping to front
	local winFocusData = {}  -- [main] = { stroke, onFocus }
	local lastCreated = nil  -- {main,stroke} of most recently created window (for focus-on-open)

	local function setFocus(mainFrame, stroke)
		if focusedWin == mainFrame then return end
		if focusedWin then
			local prev = winFocusData[focusedWin]
			if prev then
				if prev.stroke then tw(prev.stroke, {Transparency = 1, Thickness = 0}) end
				if prev.onFocus then prev.onFocus(false) end
			end
		end
		focusedWin = mainFrame
		if not winFocusData[mainFrame] then winFocusData[mainFrame] = {} end
		winFocusData[mainFrame].stroke = stroke
		if stroke then tw(stroke, {Transparency = 0.3, Thickness = 1.5}) end
		-- Focus/hover does NOT change z-order (only drag, resize, and opening do).
		local data = winFocusData[mainFrame]
		if data and data.onFocus then data.onFocus(true) end
	end

	-- Combined, ~30fps mouse housekeeping (was TWO separate 60fps loops): hover-handle auto-hide +
	-- window focus tracking. Both are purely mouse-position driven and imperceptible at 30fps; one
	-- throttled loop halves the per-frame work + GetMouseLocation allocations (fewer GC hitches).
	local _hkAcc = 0
	local _hkConn, _hkReg
	_hkConn = game:GetService("RunService").Heartbeat:Connect(function(dt)
		-- register once, from inside: the connection object does not exist until the call returns
		if not _hkReg then _hkReg = true; SVFXlive(_hkConn) end
		_hkAcc += dt
		if _hkAcc < 0.05 then return end
		_hkAcc = 0
		-- Idle fast-path: with no hovered handle and no tracked windows (all closed / plugin
		-- toggled off) there is nothing to sweep, so skip the GetMouseLocation allocation and
		-- both rect loops entirely until a window or handle exists again.
		if next(hoveredHandle) == nil and next(winFocusData) == nil then return end
		local m = uis:GetMouseLocation()
		-- hover-handle auto-hide (MouseLeave sometimes doesn't fire)
		if next(hoveredHandle) ~= nil then
			for hb, doLeave in pairs(hoveredHandle) do
				if not hb or not hb.Parent then hoveredHandle[hb]=nil; continue end
				local p = hb.AbsolutePosition; local sz = hb.AbsoluteSize
				if (m.X < p.X or m.X > p.X+sz.X or m.Y < p.Y or m.Y > p.Y+sz.Y) then
					doLeave(); hoveredHandle[hb]=nil
				end
			end
		end
		-- window focus tracking (topmost window under the mouse by DisplayOrder)
		if focusLocked then return end
		local bestFrame, bestZ = nil, -1
		for mainFrame in pairs(winFocusData) do
			if not mainFrame or not mainFrame.Parent then winFocusData[mainFrame]=nil; continue end
			if mainFrame:IsA("GuiObject") and not mainFrame.Visible then continue end
			local p = mainFrame.AbsolutePosition; local s = mainFrame.AbsoluteSize
			if m.X >= p.X and m.X <= p.X+s.X and m.Y >= p.Y and m.Y <= p.Y+s.Y then
				local _sg = mainFrame:FindFirstAncestorWhichIsA("ScreenGui")
				local z = (_sg and _sg.DisplayOrder) or (mainFrame:IsA("GuiObject") and mainFrame.ZIndex or 0)
				if z > bestZ then bestZ=z; bestFrame=mainFrame end
			end
		end
		if bestFrame then setFocus(bestFrame, winFocusData[bestFrame] and winFocusData[bestFrame].stroke) end
	end)

	local liveWins = {}  -- [main]=reapplyState for every live window, for fast layout reposition
	local WinFactory = {}

	function WinFactory.new(cfg)
		local gui       = cfg.gui
		local MIN_W     = cfg.minW or 20
		local MAX_W     = cfg.maxW or 1000
		local MIN_H     = cfg.minH or 20
		local MAX_H     = cfg.maxH or 1000
		local NO_Y_RESIZE = cfg.noYResize or false
		local TOPBAR_H  = cfg.topbarH or 24
		local getPinned = cfg.getPinned or function() return false end
		local SKEY      = cfg.stateKey
		local NO_SAVE_H = cfg.noSaveH or false  -- don't save/restore height for programmatic-height windows
		-- Per-window scale. Windows that opt out (cfg.noScale) render at native size so their
		-- internal pixel-precise drawing (graph/gradient) isn't double-scaled; their size math
		-- must then treat scale as 1. Scaled windows track the LIVE global UI_SCALE.
		local function winSc() return cfg.noScale and 1 or UI_SCALE end

		-- Load persisted state
		local savedState = SKEY and plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_win_" .. SKEY) or nil

		local conns         = {}
		local activeHandles = {}
		local dragging      = false
		local startMouse, startPosAbs
		local rHover, lHover, tHover, bHover = false, false, false, false
		local resizeConn    = nil

		-- Restore size: only restore W if noSaveH, restore both if not
		-- Clamp initH to MAX_H so a stale saved height never exceeds the current limit
		local initW = (savedState and savedState.w) or cfg.w
		local initH = (not NO_SAVE_H and savedState and savedState.h) or cfg.h
		if MAX_H and initH > MAX_H then initH = MAX_H end
		local currentSize = { w = initW, h = initH }
		local naturalPos = nil  -- user-intended pos; viewport-shrink clamp restores toward this

		-- ── Root frame ────────────────────────────────────────
		local main = Instance.new("Frame")
		main.Name = cfg.name or "WindowWrapper"
		main.Size = UDim2.new(0, initW, 0, initH)
		main.BackgroundTransparency = 1
		main.BorderSizePixel = 0

			if screen.X == 0 or screen.Y == 0 then return end

			if absSize.X == 0 or absSize.Y == 0 then return end

			local ax = center.X < screen.X / 2 and 0 or 1
			local sx = ax
			local ay = center.Y < screen.Y / 2 and 0 or 1

			if size.X <= 0 or size.Y <= 0 then return end

				if dragging or next(activeHandles)~=nil then return end

				if screen.X<=0 or screen.Y<=0 then return end
				local EDGE=EDGE_MARGIN
				-- Display size derives from the NATURAL (user-chosen) size in currentSize, so
				-- when the viewport grows back the window restores its real size. Deliberately
				-- does NOT write currentSize, does NOT saveState, and does NOT re-anchor -- it
				-- only shrinks to fit and nudges the offset to keep the window fully inside.
				local maxW2=math.max(MIN_W, math.min(MAX_W, (screen.X-2*EDGE)/winSc()))
				local maxH2=math.max(MIN_H, (screen.Y-2*EDGE)/winSc())
				if MAX_H then maxH2=math.min(maxH2, MAX_H) end

				local dH=math.clamp(currentSize.h, MIN_H, maxH2)

				local tlY=math.clamp(anchorY-ap.Y*rH, EDGE, math.max(EDGE, screen.Y-rH-EDGE))

			local HOVERED = UDim2.new(1,2,0,25)  -- end: full width, full height

			local hb = Instance.new("TextButton")
			hb.AnchorPoint = Vector2.new(0.5, anchorY)
			hb.Position    = UDim2.new(0.5,0, yScale, yOffset>0 and 5 or -5)

			hb.BackgroundTransparency=1; hb.Text=""; hb.ZIndex=3; hb.Parent=main

			local function doLeave()
				if hType=="T" then tHover=false end
				if hType=="B" then bHover=false end
				if not activeHandles[kind] then
					local inwardPos = UDim2.new(0.5,0,yScale,yOffset + (anchorY==0 and 20 or -20))
					tw(h,{Size=NORM, Position=inwardPos},SCALE_TI_OUT)

				if hType=="T" then tHover=true end
				if hType=="B" then bHover=true end

				if next(activeHandles)==nil and not dragging then
					h.BackgroundColor3 = col
					h.BackgroundTransparency = 0
					h.Size = NORM

		local function ensureResizeLoop()
			if resizeConn then return end
			resizeConn = runService.Heartbeat:Connect(function()
				if next(activeHandles) == nil then
					resizeConn:Disconnect(); resizeConn = nil; return
				end
				local _, ref = next(activeHandles)
				-- A pure click on a handle (no movement) must not resize/reposition. Same
				-- reasoning as drag: the size/pos reconstruction isn't an exact identity
				-- under the on-screen clamps + scale rounding, so a bare click nudges it.
				if not resizeMoved then
					local rd = uis:GetMouseLocation() - ref.startMouse
					if math.abs(rd.X) < 1 and math.abs(rd.Y) < 1 then return end
					resizeMoved = true
				end
				local nW = ref.startSizeVis.X
				local nH = ref.startSizeVis.Y
				local tX = ref.startPosAbs.X
				local tY = ref.startPosAbs.Y
				local sX, sY = main.Position.X.Scale, main.Position.Y.Scale
				local aX, aY = main.AnchorPoint.X, main.AnchorPoint.Y
				local EDGE = EDGE_MARGIN
				local sW, sH = gui.AbsoluteSize.X, gui.AbsoluteSize.Y
				for kind, data in pairs(activeHandles) do
					local d = uis:GetMouseLocation() - data.startMouse
					-- when edge margin is disabled, don't cap growth at the screen edge (cap only at MAX_W/MAX_H)
					if kind == "Right" then
						local cap = MAX_W*winSc(); if _edgeMarginOn then cap = math.min(cap, sW - EDGE - data.startPosAbs.X) end
						nW = math.clamp(data.startSizeVis.X + d.X, MIN_W*winSc(), cap)
					elseif kind == "Left" then
						local cap = MAX_W*winSc(); if _edgeMarginOn then cap = math.min(cap, data.startPosAbs.X + data.startSizeVis.X - EDGE) end
						nW = math.clamp(data.startSizeVis.X - d.X, MIN_W*winSc(), cap)
						tX = data.startPosAbs.X + (data.startSizeVis.X - nW)
					elseif kind == "Bottom" and not NO_Y_RESIZE then
						local cap = (MAX_H or 9999)*winSc(); if _edgeMarginOn then cap = math.min(cap, sH - EDGE - data.startPosAbs.Y) end
						nH = math.clamp(data.startSizeVis.Y + d.Y, MIN_H*winSc(), cap)
					elseif kind == "Top" and not NO_Y_RESIZE then
						local cap = (MAX_H or 9999)*winSc(); if _edgeMarginOn then cap = math.min(cap, data.startPosAbs.Y + data.startSizeVis.Y - EDGE) end
						nH = math.clamp(data.startSizeVis.Y - d.Y, MIN_H*winSc(), cap)
						tY = data.startPosAbs.Y + (data.startSizeVis.Y - nH)
					end
				end
				currentSize.w = nW/winSc(); currentSize.h = nH/winSc()
				if NO_Y_RESIZE then currentSize.h = main.Size.Y.Offset end
				lastResizeFinal = { tx=tX, ty=tY, nW=nW, nH=currentSize.h*winSc() }
				main.Size = UDim2.new(0, currentSize.w, 0, currentSize.h)
					main.Position = UDim2.new(sX, tX-sX*gui.AbsoluteSize.X+aX*nW, sY, tY-sY*gui.AbsoluteSize.Y+aY*nH)
				if _bgStatic and themeBg.Parent then themeBg.Position = UDim2.new(0, -tX, 0, -tY) end			end)
		end

		-- ── Edge/side resize hook with co-hover corner detection ──────
		-- handleInfo maps kind → { vis, norm } so the resize-end handler can hide ALL registered visuals
		local handleInfo = {}

		local function hookResize(hitbox, kind, visH, normSize)
			handleInfo[kind] = { vis = visH, norm = normSize }
			hitbox.InputBegan:Connect(function(inp)
				if inp.UserInputType ~= Enum.UserInputType.MouseButton1 then return end
				if getPinned() then flashPinned(visH); return end
				bringToFront()
				-- Sync currentSize.w from AbsoluteSize so startSizeAbs and currentSize.w agree
				-- Don't touch currentSize.h: NO_Y_RESIZE windows manage it externally;
				-- Y-resize windows use ref.startSizeAbs.Y in the heartbeat anyway
				currentSize.w = main.Size.X.Offset
				currentSize.h = main.Size.Y.Offset
				resizeMoved = false
				local data = { startMouse=uis:GetMouseLocation(), startSizeVis=visualSize(), startPosAbs=main.AbsolutePosition }
				-- Register this handle + any co-hovered handle for free corner resize
				local registered = { kind }
				activeHandles[kind] = data
				local coHover = { Top=tHover, Bottom=bHover, Right=rHover, Left=lHover }
				coHover[kind] = nil
				for coKind, isHov in pairs(coHover) do
					if not isHov then continue end
					if NO_Y_RESIZE and (coKind=="Top" or coKind=="Bottom") then continue end
					if not MAX_H  and (coKind=="Top" or coKind=="Bottom") then continue end
					activeHandles[coKind] = data
					table.insert(registered, coKind)
				end
				tw(dragStroke, { Transparency=0.5, Color=C.handle })
				plugin:Activate(false); ensureResizeLoop()
				local ec; ec = inp.Changed:Connect(function()
					if inp.UserInputState ~= Enum.UserInputState.End then return end
					for _, k in ipairs(registered) do activeHandles[k] = nil end
					ec:Disconnect()
					if activeResizeTween then activeResizeTween:Cancel(); activeResizeTween = nil end
					-- Hide visuals for ALL registered handles, not just the primary one
					local hm = {Right=rHover, Left=lHover, Top=tHover, Bottom=bHover}
					for _, k in ipairs(registered) do
						if not hm[k] and handleInfo[k] then
							tw(handleInfo[k].vis, {BackgroundTransparency=1, Size=handleInfo[k].norm}, SCALE_TI)
						end
					end
					if next(activeHandles)==nil then
						if not anyHovered() and not dragging then tw(dragStroke,{Transparency=1}) end
						plugin:Deactivate()
						-- Bare click (no movement): leave size/position exactly as they were.
						if resizeMoved and lastResizeFinal then
							applyResponsivePosition(
								Vector2.new(lastResizeFinal.tx, lastResizeFinal.ty),
								Vector2.new(lastResizeFinal.nW, lastResizeFinal.nH)
							)
						end
						lastResizeFinal = nil
					end
				end)
			end)
		end

		-- Corner handles removed — co-hover logic above handles corner resize naturally
		-- Wire edge handles
		hookResize(_tHb, "Top",    _tH, UDim2.new(0.5,0,0,5))
		hookResize(_bHb, "Bottom", _bH, UDim2.new(0.5,0,0,5))
		hookResize(rHb,  "Right",  rH,  UDim2.new(0,5,0.5,0))
		hookResize(lHb,  "Left",   lH,  UDim2.new(0,5,0.5,0))

		local function destroy()
			liveWins[main] = nil
			winFocusData[main] = nil
			if focusedWin == main then focusedWin = nil end
			saveState()  -- save on close
			for _, c in pairs(conns) do pcall(function() c:Disconnect() end) end
			conns = {}
			if resizeConn then resizeConn:Disconnect(); resizeConn = nil end
			if main and main.Parent then main:Destroy() end
		end

		return {
			main        = main,
			bgFrame     = bgFrame,
			topbar      = topbar,
			dragHandle  = dragHandle,
			dragStroke  = dragStroke,
			currentSize = currentSize,
			destroy     = destroy,
			applyResponsivePosition = applyResponsivePosition,
			setMaxH    = function(v) MAX_H = math.max(v, MIN_H) end,
			isResizing = function() return next(activeHandles) ~= nil end,
			setFocusCallback = function(fn)
				if winFocusData[main] then
					winFocusData[main].onFocus = fn
					-- Start dimmed if not the focused window
					if focusedWin ~= main then fn(false) end
				end
			end,
		}
	end

	-- Set the themed background image + opacities on every window (live)
	WinFactory.refreshFocusColors = function()
		for mf, data in pairs(winFocusData) do
			if mf and mf.Parent and data and data.onFocus then data.onFocus(mf == focusedWin) end
		end
	end
	WinFactory.setThemeBg = function(image, fit, imgOp, bgOp, tile, color, static)
		_bgImage = tostring(image or ""):match("%d+") or ""
		_bgFit = fit or "Fit"; _bgImgOp = tonumber(imgOp) or 1; _bgOp = tonumber(bgOp) or 1; _bgTile = (tile~=nil and tostring(tile)) or "128,128"
		_bgStatic = static and true or false
		if typeof(color)=="Color3" then _bgColor=color elseif type(color)=="string" and color~="" then local r,g,b=color:match("(%d+)%s*,%s*(%d+)%s*,%s*(%d+)"); if r then _bgColor=Color3.fromRGB(tonumber(r),tonumber(g),tonumber(b)) end end
		for img, bg in pairs(themeBgs) do
			if img and img.Parent and bg and bg.Parent then applyThemeBgTo(img, bg) else themeBgs[img]=nil end
		end
	end

	-- Keep a ScreenGui permanently above every window (can't be covered; wins focus when hovered)
	WinFactory.keepGuiOnTop = function(g)
		if not g then return end
		for i=#topGuis,1,-1 do if not topGuis[i] or not topGuis[i].Parent then table.remove(topGuis,i) end end
		topGuis[#topGuis+1] = g
		globalZTop += 1; g.DisplayOrder = globalZTop
	end

	-- Focus the most recently created window (interactive open -> comes to front + focus outline)
	WinFactory.setPreserveZ = function(v) preserveZ = v and true or false end
	WinFactory.focusLast = function()
		if lastCreated and lastCreated.main and lastCreated.main.Parent then
			local newMain = lastCreated.main
			local d = winFocusData[newMain]
			if d and d.bringToFront then d.bringToFront() end  -- newly opened window = highest z
			-- Don't STEAL focus if the user is actively hovering another window/Hub, or focus
			-- already sits on another window/Hub. In those cases the newly opened window still
			-- comes to front (visible) but keeps focus where the user left it.
			local m = uis:GetMouseLocation()
			local hoveringOther = false
			for mf in pairs(winFocusData) do
				if mf ~= newMain and mf and mf.Parent and (not mf:IsA("GuiObject") or mf.Visible) then
					local p = mf.AbsolutePosition; local sz = mf.AbsoluteSize
					if m.X >= p.X and m.X <= p.X+sz.X and m.Y >= p.Y and m.Y <= p.Y+sz.Y then hoveringOther = true; break end
				end
			end
			local focusedOther = (focusedWin ~= nil and focusedWin ~= newMain and focusedWin.Parent ~= nil)
			if hoveringOther or focusedOther then return end
			setFocus(newMain, lastCreated.stroke)
		end
	end

	-- Expose setFocus so non-WinFactory UIs can participate
	WinFactory.registerFocusFrame = function(frame, onFocusFn)
		winFocusData[frame] = { stroke = nil, onFocus = onFocusFn }
		frame.MouseEnter:Connect(function()
			if not focusLocked and frame.Visible then setFocus(frame, nil) end
		end)
	end

	WinFactory.setBaseDisplayOrder = function(n)
		n = math.floor((tonumber(n) or 10000) + 0.5)
		DORDER_BASE = n
		-- AUTHORITATIVE rebase: pack EVERY SineVFX gui into a tight band starting at `n`,
		-- preserving current relative stacking order. A plain delta-shift left windows
		-- scattered by focus-climb drift (globalZTop grows on every bringToFront), so the
		-- setting felt like it only moved some windows. Rebasing makes the number truly
		-- drive ALL windows. topGuis (Hub FAB, ParticleCounter) are forced to the top.
		local managed = {}
		for g in pairs(managedGuis) do
			if g and g.Parent then managed[#managed+1] = g else managedGuis[g] = nil end
		end
		table.sort(managed, function(a,b) return a.DisplayOrder < b.DisplayOrder end)
		local top = {}
		for _, g in ipairs(topGuis) do if g and g.Parent then top[#top+1] = g end end
		table.sort(top, function(a,b) return a.DisplayOrder < b.DisplayOrder end)
		local z = n
		for _, g in ipairs(managed) do g.DisplayOrder = z; z = z + 1 end
		for _, g in ipairs(top)     do g.DisplayOrder = z; z = z + 1 end
		globalZTop = z
	end

	-- Register a gui NOT built by WinFactory.new (e.g. the Hub backing window) so it
	-- tracks the Display Order base. Its DisplayOrder is authored against the 10000
	-- default base; apply the current offset now and let setBaseDisplayOrder shift it
	-- with every other managed gui. Without this it strands at a fixed value and ends
	-- up above/below the windows depending on the base (covered the windows at low base).
	WinFactory.registerManagedGui = function(g)
		if not g then return end
		g.DisplayOrder = g.DisplayOrder + (DORDER_BASE - 10000)
		managedGuis[g] = true
	end

	WinFactory.setUiScale = function(sv)
		sv = tonumber(sv) or 1
		UI_SCALE = sv
		for o in pairs(scaleObjs) do
			if o and o.Parent then o.Scale = sv else scaleObjs[o] = nil end
		end

		if WinFactory._sliderSetters and WinFactory._sliderSetters["SineVFX_uiScale"] then
			pcall(WinFactory._sliderSetters["SineVFX_uiScale"], sv)
		end

		-- Re-run each window's content-height fit after the new scale settles, so an
		-- auto-sized window grows/shrinks to fit and its custom scrollbar doesn't linger.
		if WinFactory._scaleHooks then
			task.defer(function()
				for fn in pairs(WinFactory._scaleHooks) do pcall(fn, sv) end
			end)
		end
	end
	WinFactory.onScaleChanged = function(fn)
		if not fn then return end
		WinFactory._scaleHooks = WinFactory._scaleHooks or {}
		WinFactory._scaleHooks[fn] = true
		return function() if WinFactory._scaleHooks then WinFactory._scaleHooks[fn] = nil end end
	end
	WinFactory.getUiScale = function() return UI_SCALE end
	WinFactory.setEdgeMargin = function(n)
		_edgeMarginVal = math.max(0, math.floor((tonumber(n) or 5)+0.5))
		EDGE_MARGIN = _edgeMarginOn and _edgeMarginVal or 0
		if WinFactory._sliderSetters and WinFactory._sliderSetters["SineVFX_edgeMargin"] then pcall(WinFactory._sliderSetters["SineVFX_edgeMargin"], _edgeMarginVal) end
	end
	WinFactory.setEdgeMarginEnabled = function(on)
		_edgeMarginOn = (on and true or false)
		EDGE_MARGIN = _edgeMarginOn and _edgeMarginVal or 0
		if WinFactory._toggleSetters and WinFactory._toggleSetters["SineVFX_edgeMarginOn"] then pcall(WinFactory._toggleSetters["SineVFX_edgeMarginOn"], _edgeMarginOn) end
	end
	WinFactory.getEdgeMargin = function() return _edgeMarginVal end
	WinFactory.getEdgeMarginEnabled = function() return _edgeMarginOn end
	WinFactory.registerScaled = function(obj)
		if not obj then return end
		local u = Instance.new("UIScale"); u.Scale = UI_SCALE; u.Parent = obj; scaleObjs[u] = true; return u
	end

	-- Reposition every live window from its saved state (no rebuild). Used by layout apply.
	WinFactory.reapplyAll = function(skip, stateMap)
		for m, e in pairs(liveWins) do
			if m and m.Parent and not (skip and e.key and skip[e.key]) then
				pcall(e.fn, stateMap and e.key and stateMap[e.key] or nil)
			end
		end
	end

	-- Standard window scaffold shared by tool/panel windows: builds the ScreenGui + the
	-- draggable/resizable frame (WinFactory.new) and wires the common chrome -- title (+optional
	-- tooltip), pin toggle with persistence, close, and unfocused dimming. Returns the pieces each
	-- window still needs so per-window code stays tiny. o: { stateKey(req), title, tooltip, guiName,
	-- displayOrder, w,h,pos,minW,maxW,minH,maxH,noYResize,noSaveH,topbarH, onClose, onFocus,
	-- managed, keepOnTop }.
	WinFactory.standardWindow = function(o)
		local sk = o.stateKey
		local isPinned = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_"..sk) == true
		local gui = Instance.new("ScreenGui")
		gui.Name = o.guiName or ("SineVFX_"..sk)
		gui.ResetOnSpawn = false; gui.ZIndexBehavior = Enum.ZIndexBehavior.Sibling
		gui.DisplayOrder = o.displayOrder or 10005; gui.Parent = ctx.coreGui
		if o.keepOnTop then WinFactory.keepGuiOnTop(gui) end
		if o.managed  then WinFactory.registerManagedGui(gui) end
		local win = WinFactory.new({
			stateKey = sk, gui = gui, name = o.wrapperName or (sk.."Wrapper"),
			w=o.w, h=o.h, pos=o.pos, minW=o.minW, maxW=o.maxW, minH=o.minH, maxH=o.maxH,
			noYResize=o.noYResize, noSaveH=o.noSaveH, topbarH=o.topbarH,
			getPinned=function() return isPinned end,
		})
		local topbar = win.topbar
		local titleLbl = Instance.new("TextLabel")
		titleLbl.Size=UDim2.new(1,-60,1,0); titleLbl.Position=UDim2.new(0,12,0,0)
		titleLbl.BackgroundTransparency=1; titleLbl.Text=o.title or ""
		titleLbl.TextColor3=C.text; titleLbl.Font=Enum.Font.GothamBold
		titleLbl.TextSize=13; titleLbl.TextXAlignment=Enum.TextXAlignment.Left
		titleLbl.ZIndex=6; titleLbl.Parent=topbar
		if o.tooltip and UIKit.attachTooltip then UIKit.attachTooltip(titleLbl, o.tooltip) end
		local pinBtn, pinIcon = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, "120413064155653", -48, 15)
		local closeBtn = UIKit.makeTopBtn(topbar, "101421401454601", -26, 10)
		pinBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(function()
			isPinned = not isPinned
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_pinned_"..sk, isPinned)
			pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", isPinned)
			tw(pinIcon, {ImageColor3 = isPinned and C.Pinned or C.textDim})
		end)
		if isPinned then pinBtn:SetAttribute("PinnedActive", true); pinIcon.ImageColor3 = C.Pinned end
		if o.onClose then closeBtn.MouseButton1Click:Connect(o.onClose) end
		win.setFocusCallback(function(focused)
			local col     = focused and C.text    or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			local iconCol = focused and C.textDim or C.textDim:Lerp(Color3.new(0,0,0), 0.15)
			task.defer(function()
				for _, ch in ipairs(topbar:GetDescendants()) do
					if ch:GetAttribute("NoFocusTint") then continue end
					if ch:IsA("TextLabel") then tw(ch, {TextColor3 = col})
					elseif ch:IsA("ImageLabel") then
						tw(ch, {ImageColor3 = (ch.Parent and ch.Parent:GetAttribute("PinnedActive")) and C.Pinned or iconCol})
					end
				end
			end)
			if o.onFocus then o.onFocus(focused) end
		end)
		return {
			gui=gui, win=win, main=win.main, bgFrame=win.bgFrame, topbar=topbar,
			titleLbl=titleLbl, pinBtn=pinBtn, pinIcon=pinIcon, closeBtn=closeBtn,
			getPinned=function() return isPinned end,
		}
	end

	return WinFactory
end

-- Preloader: preloads all icon images used by Sine VFX plugin
-- Call Preloader.preload() once at startup before opening any windows
local Preloader = {}

-- Every asset ID used across all windows
local ASSET_IDS = {
	-- Topbar buttons (all windows)
	"120413064155653",  -- pin
	"101421401454601",  -- close
	-- Hub strip
	"14895352873",       -- hub fab
	"73997112371450",   -- emit
	"438217405",        -- settings
	-- EmitWindow
	"131602901815003",  -- repeat off
	"97414960795019",   -- repeat on
	-- ColorWindow
	"92441021070637",   -- replace mode
	"77849852923386",   -- multiply mode
	"438217405",        -- reset (shared by ColorWindow, ShifterWindow, ResizerWindow, RetimerWindow)
	-- ShifterWindow reset (alternate)
	"6034687995",
	-- ShifterWindow dropdown arrow
	"116140507142780",
	-- ShifterWindow CLASS_ICONS (fill in your actual IDs here)
	-- "your_particle_icon_id",
	-- "your_sound_icon_id",
	-- "your_beam_icon_id",
	-- "your_trail_icon_id",
	-- "your_basepart_icon_id",
	-- "your_light_icon_id",
}

function Preloader.preload()
	local contentProvider = game:GetService("ContentProvider")
	local assets = {}
	for _, id in ipairs(ASSET_IDS) do
		table.insert(assets, "rbxthumb://type=Asset&id=" .. id .. "&w=150&h=150")
	end
	-- PreloadAsync accepts string URLs directly, no ImageLabels needed
	task.spawn(function()
		pcall(function() contentProvider:PreloadAsync(assets) end)
	end)
end

return Preloader

-- ThemeStore: custom-theme persistence + live apply.
-- Live apply works by MUTATING Theme.C in place (every module holds the same C
-- table ref and reads C.<key> at build time), then remounting open windows.
-- MUST be required before any saved theme is applied, so DEFAULT captures originals.
return function(Theme, plugin, onApply)
	local store = {}
	local C = Theme.C
	local HttpService = game:GetService("HttpService")
	local KEYS = {"base","windowBg","content","header","hub","surface","surface2","CurveBG","border","accent","slider","handle","loop","rep","repet","repet2","danger","text","textDim","white","Pinned","axisX","axisY","axisZ"}
	-- Friendly labels for the editor UI
	local LABELS = {
		base="Base Color", windowBg="Window Background", content="Properties List Background", header="Header Color", hub="Hub", surface="Surface Color", surface2="Button", CurveBG="Graph Panel",
		border="Card / Window Outline", accent="Accent Color", slider="Scrollbar Color", handle="Resize Handle", loop="Toggle On Color", rep="Repeat Button",
		repet="Repeat Slider Track", repet2="Repeat Slider Knob", danger="Danger Color", text="Text Color",
		textDim="Faded Text", white="Bright Text", Pinned="Pinned Outline", axisX="Axis X (3D)", axisY="Axis Y (3D)", axisZ="Axis Z (3D)",
	}
	store.KEYS = KEYS
	store.LABELS = LABELS
	-- Font families (NOT individual weights). 'reg'/'bold' let apply preserve bold where the UI was bold.
	local F = Enum.Font
	local function FP(enum) local ok,ff=pcall(function() return Font.fromEnum(enum) end); if ok and ff then return ff.Family end return nil end
	local FONT_FAMILIES = {
		{ label="Default" },
		{ label="Gotham",          reg=F.Gotham,          family=FP(F.Gotham) },
		{ label="Source Sans",     reg=F.SourceSans,      family=FP(F.SourceSans) },
		{ label="Arial",           reg=F.Arial,           family=FP(F.Arial) },
		{ label="Arimo",           reg=F.Arimo,           family=FP(F.Arimo) },
		{ label="Roboto",          reg=F.Roboto,          family=FP(F.Roboto) },
		{ label="Roboto Mono",     reg=F.RobotoMono,      family=FP(F.RobotoMono) },
		{ label="Roboto Condensed",reg=F.RobotoCondensed, family=FP(F.RobotoCondensed) },
		{ label="Nunito",          reg=F.Nunito,          family=FP(F.Nunito) },
		{ label="Oswald",          reg=F.Oswald,          family=FP(F.Oswald) },
		{ label="Ubuntu",          reg=F.Ubuntu,          family=FP(F.Ubuntu) },
		{ label="Titillium Web",   reg=F.TitilliumWeb,    family=FP(F.TitilliumWeb) },
		{ label="Josefin Sans",    reg=F.JosefinSans,     family=FP(F.JosefinSans) },
		{ label="Jura",            reg=F.Jura,            family=FP(F.Jura) },
		{ label="Merriweather",    reg=F.Merriweather,    family=FP(F.Merriweather) },
		{ label="Garamond",        reg=F.Garamond,        family=FP(F.Garamond) },
		{ label="Bodoni",          reg=F.Bodoni,          family=FP(F.Bodoni) },
		{ label="Code",            reg=F.Code,            family=FP(F.Code) },
		{ label="Fantasy",         reg=F.Fantasy,         family=FP(F.Fantasy) },
		{ label="Michroma",        reg=F.Michroma,        family=FP(F.Michroma) },
		{ label="Sci-Fi",          reg=F.SciFi,           family=FP(F.SciFi) },
		{ label="Denk One",        reg=F.DenkOne,         family=FP(F.DenkOne) },
		{ label="Fredoka One",     reg=F.FredokaOne,      family=FP(F.FredokaOne) },
		{ label="Luckiest Guy",    reg=F.LuckiestGuy,     family=FP(F.LuckiestGuy) },
		{ label="Bangers",         reg=F.Bangers,         family=FP(F.Bangers) },
		{ label="Permanent Marker",reg=F.PermanentMarker, family=FP(F.PermanentMarker) },
		{ label="Patrick Hand",    reg=F.PatrickHand,     family=FP(F.PatrickHand) },
		{ label="Indie Flower",    reg=F.IndieFlower,     family=FP(F.IndieFlower) },
		{ label="Kalam",           reg=F.Kalam,           family=FP(F.Kalam) },
		{ label="Amatic SC",       reg=F.AmaticSC,        family=FP(F.AmaticSC) },
		{ label="Special Elite",   reg=F.SpecialElite,    family=FP(F.SpecialElite) },
		{ label="Grenze Gotisch",  reg=F.GrenzeGotisch,   family=FP(F.GrenzeGotisch) },
		{ label="Creepster",       reg=F.Creepster,       family=FP(F.Creepster) },
	}
	store.FONT_FAMILIES = FONT_FAMILIES
	function store.fontFamily(label) for _,f in ipairs(FONT_FAMILIES) do if f.label==label then return f end end return nil end
	-- Non-color theme properties (image / opacity / radius / font / text). kind drives the editor control.
	local PROPS = {
		{ key="bgImage",        kind="image", default="",            label="Background Image" },
		{ key="imageColor",     kind="color", default="255,255,255", label="Image Color" },
		{ key="imageFit",       kind="enum",  default="Fit",         label="Image Fit", options={"Fit","Stretch","Tile","Crop"} },
		{ key="tileSize",       kind="size",  default="128,128",     label="Pattern Size" },
		{ key="imageStatic",    kind="bool",  default=false,         label="Static" },
		{ key="borderRadius",   kind="num",   default=1,             label="All Corners",           min=0,   max=2 },
		{ key="windowCorners",  kind="num",   default=0.8,             label="Window Corners",        min=0,   max=2 },
		{ key="cardCorners",    kind="num",   default=1,             label="Card Corners",          min=0,   max=2 },
		{ key="buttonCorners",  kind="num",   default=1,             label="Button Corners",        min=0,   max=2 },
		{ key="toggleCorners",  kind="num",   default=1,             label="Toggle Corners",        min=0,   max=2 },
		{ key="font",           kind="font",  default="Default",     label="Font" },
		{ key="textScale",      kind="num",   default=1,             label="Text Size",             min=0.7, max=2 },
		{ key="imageTransparency",   kind="num", default=0, label="Image Transparency",      min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="bgTransparency",      kind="num", default=0, label="Background Transparency", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="baseTransparency",    kind="num", default=0, label="Base Transparency",       min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="surfaceTransparency", kind="num", default=0, label="Surface Transparency",    min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="accentTransparency",  kind="num", default=0, label="Accent Transparency",     min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="borderTransparency",  kind="num", default=0, label="Border Transparency",     min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="textTransparency",    kind="num", default=0, label="Text Transparency",       min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="dangerTransparency",  kind="num", default=0, label="Danger Transparency",     min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="surface2Transparency",kind="num", default=0, label="Button Transparency",     min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="curveBgTransparency", kind="num", default=0, label="Panel Transparency",      min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="topbarTransparency",  kind="num", default=0, label="Top Bar Transparency",    min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="contentTransparency", kind="num", default=1, label="Content Transparency",    min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="contentBorderTransparency", kind="num", default=0, label="Content Border Transparency", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="hubTransparency",     kind="num", default=0, label="Hub Transparency",        min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="treeLineColor",       kind="color", default="138, 187, 255", label="Tree Line Color" },
		{ key="treeLineTransparency",kind="num",   default=0.7,           label="Tree Line Transparency", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="treeLineGroupTransparency",kind="num", default=0.92, label="Tree Line Group Fade", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="propFieldColor",      kind="color", default="21,30,48",    label="Property Field Color" },
		{ key="propFieldTransparency",       kind="num", default=0, label="Property Field Transparency", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="propFieldBorderTransparency", kind="num", default=0, label="Property Field Border Transparency", min=0, max=1 },
		{ key="toggleOffColor",      kind="color", default="38,53,80",    label="Toggle Off Color" },
		{ key="toggleKnobColor",     kind="color", default="255,255,255", label="Toggle Ball Color" },
		{ key="toggleKnobOnColor",   kind="color", default="255,255,255", label="Toggle Ball (On)" },
		{ key="toggleKnobOffColor",  kind="color", default="149,168,203", label="Toggle Ball (Off)" },
		{ key="dropdownBorderColor",  kind="color", default="97,126,165",   label="Header Outline Color" },
		{ key="buttonBorderColor",    kind="color", default="32,34,47",   label="Button Outline Color" },
		{ key="fieldBorderColor",     kind="color", default="32,34,47",   label="Field Outline Color" },
		{ key="toggleBorderColor",    kind="color", default="34,35,43",   label="Toggle Outline Color" },
		{ key="windowBorderColor",    kind="color", default="99,129,183",     label="Window Outline Color" },
		{ key="propNameBorderColor",  kind="color", default="22,26,38",   label="Property Name Outline" },
		{ key="propValueBorderColor", kind="color", default="40,47,69",   label="Property Value Outline" },
		{ key="contentBorderColor",   kind="color", default="36,41,62",   label="Content Border Color" },
	}
	store.PROPS = PROPS
	local PDEF = {}; for _,p in ipairs(PROPS) do PDEF[p.key]=p.default end
	store.DEFAULT_PROPS = PDEF
	local function coerceProp(p, v)
		if v==nil then return p.default end
		if p.kind=="num" then v=tonumber(v); if not v then return p.default end; if p.min then v=math.max(p.min,v) end; if p.max then v=math.min(p.max,v) end; return v end
		if p.kind=="bool" then if type(v)=="boolean" then return v end; return v=="true" or v==1 end
		if p.kind=="size" then local a,b=tostring(v):match("(%d+)%s*,%s*(%d+)"); if not a then a=tostring(v):match("%d+"); b=a end; if not a then return p.default end; return a..","..b end
		return tostring(v)
	end
	function store.resolveProps(theme)
		local raw = theme and theme.props
		local m={}
		for _,p in ipairs(PROPS) do
			local v = raw and raw[p.key]
			if v==nil and raw and p.key:match("Transparency$") then
				local ov=tonumber(raw[(p.key:gsub("Transparency$","Opacity"))]); if ov then v=1-ov end
			end
			m[p.key]=coerceProp(p, v)
		end
		return m
	end
	store.activeProps = PDEF  -- props of the currently-applied theme (read by Main's apply path)

	-- Capture default palette ONCE
	if C.windowBg==nil then C.windowBg=Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
	if C.content==nil then C.content=Color3.fromRGB(35,45,70) end
	if C.header==nil then C.header=Color3.fromRGB(42,52,78) end
	if C.hub==nil then C.hub=Color3.fromRGB(45,55,83) end
	local DEFAULT = {}
	for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do DEFAULT[k] = C[k] end
	store.DEFAULT = DEFAULT

	local function c2s(c)
		return string.format("%d,%d,%d", math.floor(c.R*255+0.5), math.floor(c.G*255+0.5), math.floor(c.B*255+0.5))
	end
	local function s2c(s, fb)
		if typeof(s)=="Color3" then return s end
		if type(s)~="string" then return fb end
		local r,g,b = s:match("(%-?%d+)%s*,%s*(%-?%d+)%s*,%s*(%-?%d+)")
		if not r then return fb end
		local function cl(x) x=tonumber(x) or 0; if x<0 then x=0 elseif x>255 then x=255 end return math.floor(x) end
		return Color3.fromRGB(cl(r),cl(g),cl(b))
	end
	store.c2s, store.s2c = c2s, s2c

	-- Detect old-value collisions between keys (e.g. a theme where Border happens to match
	-- Base/Surface2). recolorGui's live-preview recolor guesses an element's theme key by
	-- matching its CURRENT color against each key's OLD value -- when two keys share an old
	-- value that guess is ambiguous, so recolorGui skips those to avoid recoloring an
	-- unrelated element that just happened to share the same color.
	local function computeDup(prev)
		-- Only count keys that recolorGui actually MATCHES by value. Tag-handled (windowBg/content/hub)
		-- and remount-only (axisX/Y/Z) keys must NOT inflate the dup count, or a unique matched color
		-- (danger/loop/accent/surface) that merely shares a value with one of them gets wrongly skipped.
		local SKIP = { windowBg=true, content=true, hub=true, axisX=true, axisY=true, axisZ=true, surface2=true }  -- surface2 ("Button Highlight") is vestigial + defaults to base's value; skipping it keeps base unique so editing Base Color updates live
		local counts = {}
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do
			if not SKIP[k] then
				local v = prev[k]
				if v then local s=c2s(v); counts[s]=(counts[s] or 0)+1 end
			end
		end
		return counts
	end

	local THEMES_KEY = "SineVFX_themes"
	local ACTIVE_KEY = "SineVFX_activeTheme"

	-- list -> array of { name=string, colors={ key="r,g,b" } }
	function store.list()
		local raw = plugin:GetSetting(THEMES_KEY)
		local out = {}
		if type(raw)=="table" then
			for _,t in ipairs(raw) do
				if type(t)=="table" and type(t.name)=="string" then
					local cols = {}
					if type(t.colors)=="table" then for k,v in pairs(t.colors) do if type(v)=="string" then cols[k]=v end end end
					local props = {}
					if type(t.props)=="table" then for k,v in pairs(t.props) do props[k]=v end end
					out[#out+1] = { name=t.name, colors=cols, props=props }
				end
			end
		end
		return out
	end
	function store.saveList(themes)
		local raw = {}
		for _,t in ipairs(themes) do
			local cols = {}
			for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do
				local v = t.colors and t.colors[k]
				cols[k] = (typeof(v)=="Color3") and c2s(v) or (type(v)=="string" and v) or c2s(DEFAULT[k])
			end
			raw[#raw+1] = { name=t.name, colors=cols, props=store.resolveProps(t) }
		end
		plugin:SetSetting(THEMES_KEY, raw)
	end

	-- resolve a theme's stored colors to a full { key=Color3 } map
	function store.resolve(theme)
		local m = {}
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do
			local v = theme and theme.colors and theme.colors[k]
			m[k] = v and s2c(v, DEFAULT[k]) or DEFAULT[k]
		end
		return m
	end

	function store.getActiveName()
		local a = plugin:GetSetting(ACTIVE_KEY)
		return (type(a)=="string") and a or nil
	end

	-- apply by name (nil => default). Mutates C in place, persists active, fires onApply (remount).
	function store.apply(name)
		local cols = DEFAULT; local props = PDEF
		if name then
			for _,t in ipairs(store.list()) do if t.name==name then cols = store.resolve(t); props = store.resolveProps(t); break end end
		end
		local prev={}; for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do prev[k]=C[k] end; store.prevColors=prev; store.prevDup=computeDup(prev)
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do C[k] = cols[k] or DEFAULT[k] end
		store.activeProps = props
		plugin:SetSetting(ACTIVE_KEY, name or nil)
		store.applyCounter = (store.applyCounter or 0) + 1
		if onApply then task.defer(onApply) end
	end
	-- Rebuild every window from the CURRENT Theme.C without bumping applyCounter (so an open
	-- Theme Editor doesn't close) and without changing the active theme. Used by the editor as a
	-- correctness backstop after in-place live edits: the in-place recolor GUESSES an element's
	-- theme key by colour value, so it can recolour the wrong thing; a fresh remount fixes it.
	function store.liveRemount()
		if onApply then task.defer(onApply) end
	end
	-- like apply() but does NOT remount; Main's onLive updates the live UI in place (fast, no lag)
	function store.applyLive(name)
		local cols = DEFAULT; local props = PDEF
		if name then for _,t in ipairs(store.list()) do if t.name==name then cols = store.resolve(t); props = store.resolveProps(t); break end end end
		local prev={}; for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do prev[k]=C[k] end; store.prevColors=prev; store.prevDup=computeDup(prev)
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do C[k] = cols[k] or DEFAULT[k] end
		store.activeProps = props
		plugin:SetSetting(ACTIVE_KEY, name or nil)
		if store.onLive then store.onLive() end
	end
	-- re-mutate C from the persisted active theme (used at plugin load, no remount needed)
	function store.applySilent()
		local name = store.getActiveName()
		local cols = DEFAULT; local props = PDEF
		if name then
			for _,t in ipairs(store.list()) do if t.name==name then cols = store.resolve(t); props = store.resolveProps(t); break end end
		end
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do C[k] = cols[k] or DEFAULT[k] end
		store.activeProps = props
	end

	-- unique-name helper
	function store.uniqueName(base)
		base = base or "New Theme"
		local taken = { ["Default"] = true }  -- the built-in name is reserved
		for _,t in ipairs(store.list()) do taken[t.name]=true end
		if not taken[base] then return base end
		local i=2
		while taken[base.." "..i] do i+=1 end
		return base.." "..i
	end

	-- export -> JSON code string; import(code) -> theme table or nil,err
	function store.export(theme)
		local cols = {}
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do
			local v = theme.colors and theme.colors[k]
			cols[k] = (typeof(v)=="Color3") and c2s(v) or (type(v)=="string" and v) or c2s(DEFAULT[k])
		end
		return HttpService:JSONEncode({ sinevfx_theme=1, name=theme.name, colors=cols, props=store.resolveProps(theme) })
	end
	function store.import(code)
		if type(code)~="string" or #code==0 then return nil, "Paste a theme code first" end
		local ok, data = pcall(function() return HttpService:JSONDecode(code) end)
		if not ok or type(data)~="table" or type(data.colors)~="table" then return nil, "Invalid theme code" end
		local cols = {}
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do cols[k] = (type(data.colors[k])=="string") and data.colors[k] or c2s(DEFAULT[k]) end
		local props = store.resolveProps({ props = (type(data.props)=="table" and data.props or {}) })
		return { name=(type(data.name)=="string" and data.name) or "Imported", colors=cols, props=props }
	end

	-- transient hand-off between windows
	store.pendingExport = nil  -- theme table the Export window should display
	store.pendingEdit   = nil  -- theme name the editor should open into edit-view (or false=new)

	-- Built-in STARTER themes (seeded once into the user's theme list; then normal editable/deletable
	-- themes). Each only overrides palette colors; missing colors/props inherit Default via resolve.
	local SEMANTIC_KEEP = { loop=true, danger=true, Pinned=true, axisX=true, axisY=true, axisZ=true, white=true }
	local function _hueRot(c, dh)
		local h,sat,val = Color3.toHSV(c)
		if sat < 0.04 then return c end   -- greys / whites: leave untouched
		return Color3.fromHSV((h + dh) % 1, sat, val)
	end
	-- Presets = the DEFAULT palette with only its HUE rotated to the preset's accent, so every
	-- preset is exactly the default look in a different hue. Semantic colours (green toggle, red
	-- danger, gold pin, R/G/B axes, white) stay put; everything else (accent, the blue family, the
	-- dark-navy neutrals, and the coloured props) rotates together.
	local function genPalette(accS)
		local A  = s2c(accS, DEFAULT.accent)
		local defH = select(1, Color3.toHSV(DEFAULT.accent))
		local aH   = select(1, Color3.toHSV(A))
		local dh = (aH - defH) % 1
		local colors = {}
		for _,k in ipairs(KEYS) do
			colors[k] = SEMANTIC_KEEP[k] and c2s(DEFAULT[k]) or c2s(_hueRot(DEFAULT[k], dh))
		end
		colors.accent = accS  -- exact requested accent hue
		local props = {}
		for _,p in ipairs(PROPS) do
			if p.kind == "color" then
				local base = s2c(p.default, nil)
				if base then props[p.key] = c2s(_hueRot(base, dh)) end
			end
		end
		return colors, props
	end
	-- Built-in themes = the DEFAULT recipe (genPalette) in a different accent hue.
	local PRESETS = {
		{ name="Crimson",  accent="228,90,95"  },
		{ name="Emerald",  accent="70,200,150" },
		{ name="Amethyst", accent="180,130,240"},
		{ name="Amber",    accent="240,175,80" },
		{ name="Slate",    accent="150,165,200"},
		{ name="Grey", colors={
			base="48,50,56", windowBg="40,42,48", content="50,52,58", hub="40,42,48", surface="64,66,74",
			surface2="55,57,64", CurveBG="30,31,36", border="68,70,78", header="58,60,68", accent="140,158,192",
			slider="105,110,122", handle="182,187,198", loop="140,213,101", rep="176,183,197", repet="80,84,94",
			repet2="202,207,217", danger="237,135,150", text="228,230,236", textDim="160,165,176", white="255,255,255",
			Pinned="238,212,159", axisX="237,135,150", axisY="140,213,101", axisZ="107,169,244" },
		  props={ toggleOffColor="55,57,64", propFieldColor="40,42,48", windowBorderColor="98,103,115",
			dropdownBorderColor="125,131,145", toggleKnobOffColor="160,165,176" } },
		{ name="Black", colors={
			base="16,16,19", windowBg="10,10,12", content="18,18,21", hub="10,10,12", surface="30,30,35",
			surface2="22,22,26", CurveBG="6,6,8", border="34,34,40", header="26,26,31", accent="170,176,190",
			slider="80,82,92", handle="185,189,200", loop="140,213,101", rep="178,183,196", repet="55,57,66",
			repet2="205,208,218", danger="237,135,150", text="228,230,236", textDim="142,146,158", white="255,255,255",
