			Pinned="238,212,159", axisX="237,135,150", axisY="140,213,101", axisZ="107,169,244" },
		  props={ toggleOffColor="30,30,35", propFieldColor="10,10,12", windowBorderColor="62,64,74",
			dropdownBorderColor="95,99,112", toggleKnobOffColor="142,146,158" } },
		{ name="White", colors={
			base="235,237,242", windowBg="225,228,236", content="240,242,247", hub="225,228,236", surface="249,250,253",
			surface2="230,233,240", CurveBG="246,247,251", border="205,210,220", header="214,219,229", accent="22,24,30",
			slider="125,132,148", handle="45,49,60", loop="86,168,70", rep="95,104,124", repet="172,180,197",
			repet2="60,66,82", danger="200,72,92", text="26,30,42", textDim="96,104,122", white="30,34,46",
			Pinned="176,142,60", axisX="200,80,96", axisY="86,168,70", axisZ="60,110,196" },
		  props={ toggleOffColor="214,218,228", propFieldColor="228,231,238", windowBorderColor="175,184,202",
			dropdownBorderColor="150,160,180", toggleKnobColor="255,255,255", toggleKnobOnColor="255,255,255",
			toggleKnobOffColor="120,128,145", treeLineColor="45,49,60", propNameBorderColor="195,200,212",
			propValueBorderColor="185,192,206", contentBorderColor="205,210,220", fieldBorderColor="195,200,212",
			buttonBorderColor="200,205,216", toggleBorderColor="200,205,216" } },
	}
	store.PRESETS = PRESETS
	-- Built-in themes (Default + the 5 presets) are read-only: not editable, not deletable.
	store.PRESET_NAMES = {}
	for _,p in ipairs(PRESETS) do store.PRESET_NAMES[p.name] = true end
	function store.isBuiltin(name) return name == nil or name == "Default" or store.PRESET_NAMES[name] == true end
	function store.seedPresets()
		local VER = 12  -- v12: White theme reworked (black accent, dark bright-text)  -- v8: presets redesigned to match the dark-navy Default  -- v7: presets fully regenerated from the Default recipe (genPalette)
		local cur = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_presetsVer")) or (plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_presetsSeededV1") and 1 or 0)
		if cur >= VER then return end
		local themes = store.list()
		local idx = {}; for i,t in ipairs(themes) do idx[t.name]=i end
		for _,p in ipairs(PRESETS) do
			local colors, props
			if p.colors then colors, props = p.colors, p.props or {}
			else colors, props = genPalette(p.accent) end
			local i = idx[p.name]
			if i then themes[i].colors = colors; themes[i].props = props
			else themes[#themes+1] = { name=p.name, colors=colors, props=props } end
		end
		store.saveList(themes)
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_presetsVer", VER)
	end
	-- Fix invisible OFF toggles on existing user themes (toggleOffColor == base).
	-- Fix built-in preset tree-line colors that all shipped identical (185,199,245); match each palette.
	function store.fixTreeLine()
		if plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_treeLineFixV1") then return end
		local MAP = { Crimson="240,150,155", Emerald="140,230,190", Amethyst="205,175,248", Amber="248,205,140", Slate="185,195,220" }
		local themes = store.list()
		local changed = false
		for _,t in ipairs(themes) do
			local want = MAP[t.name]
			if want then
				t.props = t.props or {}
				local cur = t.props.treeLineColor
				if cur == nil or cur == "" or cur == "185,199,245" or cur == "200,205,220" then
					t.props.treeLineColor = want; changed = true
				end
			end
		end
		if changed then store.saveList(themes) end
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_treeLineFixV1", true)
	end
	function store.fixToggleOff()
		if plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_toggleOffFixV1") then return end
		local function s2rgb(s) local r,g,b=tostring(s):match("(%-?%d+)%s*,%s*(%-?%d+)%s*,%s*(%-?%d+)"); if r then return tonumber(r),tonumber(g),tonumber(b) end end
		local function colStr(t,key) local v=t.colors and t.colors[key]; if type(v)=="string" then return v end return c2s(DEFAULT[key]) end
		local themes = store.list()
		local changed = false
		for _,t in ipairs(themes) do
			t.props = t.props or {}
			local baseStr = colStr(t,"base")
			local off = t.props.toggleOffColor
			if (not off) or off=="" or off==baseStr then
				local br,bg,bb = s2rgb(baseStr)
				local sr,sg,sb = s2rgb(colStr(t,"surface"))
				if br and sr then
					t.props.toggleOffColor = string.format("%d,%d,%d", math.floor((br+sr)/2+0.5), math.floor((bg+sg)/2+0.5), math.floor((bb+sb)/2+0.5))
					changed = true
				end
			end
		end
		if changed then store.saveList(themes) end
		plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_toggleOffFixV1", true)
	end

	return store
end

-- LayoutStore: save / apply / import / export WINDOW LAYOUTS.
-- "Default" is a built-in (always present + first + not deletable); active layout persisted.
return function(plugin)
	local HttpService = game:GetService("HttpService")
	local store = {}
	local STATE_KEYS = {"ColSeqEditor","ColorControl","ColorWheel","Copier","CurveEditor","EmitterController","PropsWindow","Resizer","Retimer","Shifter","ToolsWindow","TrailPreview","VfxModule","Settings","VFXCode","ParticleCounter"}
	local WIN_IDS    = {"EmitterController","VFXCode","ColorControl","Shifter","Resizer","Retimer","Copier","TrailPreview","CurveEditor","ColSeqEditor","ColorWheel","Tools","Props","VfxModule","Settings","ParticleCounter"}
	store.STATE_KEYS, store.WIN_IDS = STATE_KEYS, WIN_IDS
	local LKEY = "SineVFX_layouts"
	local AKEY = "SineVFX_activeLayout"
	local CORNER_KEYS = { fab="SineVFX_fabCorner", transform="SineVFX_transformCorner", counter="SineVFX_pcCorner" }
	local K_SCALE = "SineVFX_uiScale"
	local K_EDGE  = "SineVFX_edgeMargin"
	local K_EDGEON = "SineVFX_edgeMarginOn"  -- boolean; nil/absent == enabled
	local DEFAULT_JSON = [===[{"sinevfx_layout":1,"edgeMarginOn":true,"name":"Default","edgeMargin":5,"scale":1,"wins":{"ColorControl":{"ay":1,"ax":0,"oy":-76,"ox":5,"h":455,"sy":1,"z":32493,"w":70,"sx":0},"Resizer":{"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":5,"ox":-716,"h":111,"sy":0,"z":30592,"w":118.81817904385653,"sx":1},"ColSeqEditor":{"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":318,"ox":-425,"h":146,"sy":0,"z":28357,"sx":1,"w":599},"Copier":{"sx":1,"w":119.00000321237664,"sy":0,"z":30460,"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":5,"ox":-466},"TrailPreview":{"sx":0,"w":197,"sy":1,"z":30658,"ay":1,"ax":0,"oy":-325,"ox":538},"EmitterController":{"sx":0,"w":143,"sy":0,"z":31897,"ay":0,"ax":0,"oy":5,"ox":5},"PropsWindow":{"sx":1,"w":322,"sy":0,"z":32262,"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":5,"ox":-5},"Settings":{"ay":1,"ax":1,"oy":-146,"ox":-314,"h":649,"sy":1,"z":32469,"w":442,"sx":1},"CurveEditor":{"ay":1,"ax":1,"oy":-151,"ox":-194,"h":515,"sy":1,"z":28351,"sx":1,"w":900},"VFXCode":{"ay":1,"ax":0,"oy":-5,"ox":5,"h":65,"sy":1,"z":30004,"w":507,"sx":0},"VfxModule":{"sx":0,"w":443,"sy":0,"z":30470,"ay":0,"ax":0,"oy":393,"ox":551},"Shifter":{"sx":1,"w":127,"sy":0,"z":32312,"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":5,"ox":-333},"Retimer":{"ay":0,"ax":1,"oy":5,"ox":-592,"h":111,"sy":0,"z":30203,"w":117,"sx":1}},"open":{"ColorControl":true,"Retimer":true,"Resizer":true,"Copier":true,"Shifter":true,"VfxModule":false,"TrailPreview":false,"Props":true,"EmitterController":true,"VFXCode":true,"Settings":false,"CurveEditor":true,"Tools":false,"ColSeqEditor":false,"ParticleCounter":true},"corners":{"transform":"TL","fab":"BR","counter":"BC"}}]===]

	function store.builtinDefault()
		local ok,data=pcall(function() return HttpService:JSONDecode(DEFAULT_JSON) end)
		if ok and type(data)=="table" and type(data.wins)=="table" then return {name="Default", wins=data.wins, open=(type(data.open)=="table" and data.open or {}), corners=(type(data.corners)=="table" and data.corners or {}), scale=tonumber(data.scale) or 1, edgeMargin=tonumber(data.edgeMargin) or 5, edgeMarginOn=(data.edgeMarginOn~=false)} end
		return {name="Default", wins={}, open={}}
	end

	local function savedLayouts()
		local raw = plugin:GetSetting(LKEY); local out={}
		if type(raw)=="table" then
			for _,l in ipairs(raw) do
				if type(l)=="table" and type(l.name)=="string" then
					out[#out+1] = { name=l.name, wins=(type(l.wins)=="table" and l.wins or {}), open=(type(l.open)=="table" and l.open or {}), corners=(type(l.corners)=="table" and l.corners or {}), scale=tonumber(l.scale), edgeMargin=tonumber(l.edgeMargin), edgeMarginOn=l.edgeMarginOn }
				end
			end
		end
		return out
	end
	store.savedLayouts = savedLayouts

	-- DISPLAY list: Default ALWAYS first (saved override if any, else built-in), then the rest
	function store.list()
		local saved = savedLayouts(); local out = {}
		-- Default is ALWAYS the built-in preset (immutable) -- ignore any saved "Default" override.
		for _,l in ipairs(saved) do if l.name~="Default" then out[#out+1]=l end end
		table.insert(out, 1, store.builtinDefault())
		return out
	end
	-- Never persist a "Default" entry -- Default is immutable and comes from builtinDefault().
	function store.saveList(layouts)
		local out = {}
		for _,l in ipairs(layouts) do if l.name~="Default" then out[#out+1]=l end end
		plugin:SetSetting(LKEY, out)
	end

	function store.getActive() local a=plugin:GetSetting(AKEY); return (type(a)=="string" and a) or "Default" end
	function store.setActive(name) plugin:SetSetting(AKEY, name or "Default") end

	function store.snapshot()
		local wins, open, corners = {}, {}, {}
		for _,k in ipairs(STATE_KEYS) do local v=plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_win_"..k); if v~=nil then wins[k]=v end end
		for _,id in ipairs(WIN_IDS) do open[id] = plugin:GetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id)==true end
		for key,setting in pairs(CORNER_KEYS) do local v=plugin:GetSetting(setting); if v~=nil then corners[key]=v end end
		local scale = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting(K_SCALE)) or 1
		local edgeMargin = tonumber(plugin:GetSetting(K_EDGE)) or 5
		local edgeMarginOn = (plugin:GetSetting(K_EDGEON) ~= false)  -- nil/absent == enabled
		return wins, open, corners, scale, edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn
	end
	function store.uniqueName(base)
		base = base or "Layout"
		local taken={["Default"]=true}; for _,l in ipairs(savedLayouts()) do taken[l.name]=true end
		if not taken[base] then return base end
		local i=2; while taken[base.." "..i] do i+=1 end; return base.." "..i
	end
	function store.addLayout(layout)
		local name = store.uniqueName(layout.name or "Layout")
		local saved = savedLayouts(); saved[#saved+1] = { name=name, wins=layout.wins or {}, open=layout.open or {}, corners=layout.corners or {}, scale=layout.scale, edgeMargin=layout.edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn=layout.edgeMarginOn }
		store.saveList(saved)
		return name
	end
	function store.saveCurrent(name)
		local wins, open, corners, scale, edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn = store.snapshot()
		return store.addLayout({ name=name or "Layout", wins=wins, open=open, corners=corners, scale=scale, edgeMargin=edgeMargin, edgeMarginOn=edgeMarginOn })
	end
	function store.writeToSettings(layout)
		for _,k in ipairs(STATE_KEYS) do
			local v = layout.wins and layout.wins[k]
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_win_"..k, v ~= nil and v or nil)
		end
		for _,id in ipairs(WIN_IDS) do
			plugin:SetSetting("SineVFX_winopen_"..id, (layout.open and layout.open[id]==true) or nil)
		end
		if type(layout.corners)=="table" then
			for key,setting in pairs(CORNER_KEYS) do
				local v=layout.corners[key]
				if v~=nil then plugin:SetSetting(setting, v) end
			end
		end
		if layout.scale ~= nil then plugin:SetSetting(K_SCALE, tonumber(layout.scale) or 1) end
		if layout.edgeMargin ~= nil then plugin:SetSetting(K_EDGE, tonumber(layout.edgeMargin) or 5) end
		if layout.edgeMarginOn ~= nil then plugin:SetSetting(K_EDGEON, layout.edgeMarginOn and true or false) end
	end
	-- Same writes as writeToSettings, but spread across frames and cancellable. plugin:SetSetting
	-- is ~20ms each; doing ~40 of them synchronously froze Load for ~1s. The window positions are
	-- applied from memory immediately by the caller, so this only needs to persist for next reload.
	function store.writeToSettingsAsync(layout)
		store._writeTok = (store._writeTok or 0) + 1
		local tok = store._writeTok
		task.spawn(function()
			local jobs = {}
			for _,k in ipairs(STATE_KEYS) do
				local v = layout.wins and layout.wins[k]
				jobs[#jobs+1] = { "SineVFX_win_"..k, (v ~= nil) and v or nil }
			end
			for _,id in ipairs(WIN_IDS) do
				jobs[#jobs+1] = { "SineVFX_winopen_"..id, (layout.open and layout.open[id]==true) or nil }
			end
			if type(layout.corners)=="table" then
				for key,setting in pairs(CORNER_KEYS) do local v=layout.corners[key]; if v~=nil then jobs[#jobs+1]={setting,v} end end
			end
			if layout.scale ~= nil then jobs[#jobs+1]={K_SCALE, tonumber(layout.scale) or 1} end
			if layout.edgeMargin ~= nil then jobs[#jobs+1]={K_EDGE, tonumber(layout.edgeMargin) or 5} end
			if layout.edgeMarginOn ~= nil then jobs[#jobs+1]={K_EDGEON, layout.edgeMarginOn and true or false} end
			for n,j in ipairs(jobs) do
				if store._writeTok ~= tok then return end  -- another layout load superseded this one
				plugin:SetSetting(j[1], j[2])
				if n % 3 == 0 then task.wait() end  -- yield so no single frame stalls
			end
		end)
	end
	function store.delete(name)
		if name=="Default" then return end
		local out={}; for _,l in ipairs(savedLayouts()) do if l.name~=name then out[#out+1]=l end end
		store.saveList(out)
	end
	-- Rename a persisted layout. Trims whitespace; rejects empty, "Default" (immutable/reserved),
	-- and duplicate names (reject + let caller revert). Keeps the active pointer consistent.
	-- Returns ok(bool), result: on success result=the (trimmed) new name; on failure result=error string.
	function store.rename(oldName, newName)
		if type(newName)~="string" then return false,"Invalid name" end
		newName = newName:match("^%s*(.-)%s*$") or newName
		if newName=="" then return false,"Name can't be empty" end
		if oldName=="Default" or newName=="Default" then return false,"\"Default\" is reserved" end
		if newName==oldName then return true,newName end
		local saved = savedLayouts()
		for _,l in ipairs(saved) do if l.name==newName then return false,"A layout named \""..newName.."\" already exists" end end
		local found=false
		for _,l in ipairs(saved) do if l.name==oldName then l.name=newName; found=true; break end end
		if not found then return false,"Layout not found" end
		store.saveList(saved)
		if store.getActive()==oldName then store.setActive(newName) end
		return true,newName
	end
	function store.export(layout)
		return HttpService:JSONEncode({ sinevfx_layout=1, name=layout.name, wins=layout.wins or {}, open=layout.open or {}, corners=layout.corners or {}, scale=tonumber(layout.scale) or 1, edgeMargin=tonumber(layout.edgeMargin) or 5, edgeMarginOn=(layout.edgeMarginOn~=false) })
	end
	function store.import(code)
		if type(code)~="string" or #code==0 then return nil,"Paste a layout code first" end
		local ok,data = pcall(function() return HttpService:JSONDecode(code) end)
		if not ok or type(data)~="table" or type(data.wins)~="table" then return nil,"Invalid layout code" end
		-- sanitize every wins entry: only known-shaped numeric fields survive, so a
		-- malformed code can't poison the plugin settings and break window restore
		local wins={}
		for k,w in pairs(data.wins) do
			if type(k)=="string" and type(w)=="table" then
				local cw={}
				for _,f in ipairs({"ax","ay","sx","ox","sy","oy","w","h","z"}) do
					if w[f]~=nil then cw[f]=tonumber(w[f]) end
				end
				for _,f in ipairs({"open","pinned"}) do
					if type(w[f])=="boolean" then cw[f]=w[f] end
				end
				wins[k]=cw
			end
		end
		return { name=(type(data.name)=="string" and data.name) or "Imported", wins=wins, open=(type(data.open)=="table" and data.open or {}), corners=(type(data.corners)=="table" and data.corners or {}), scale=tonumber(data.scale), edgeMargin=tonumber(data.edgeMargin), edgeMarginOn=data.edgeMarginOn }
	end
	store.refresh = nil
	return store
end

-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
--  TesterGate  –  restricts plugin access to whitelisted testers
--  Whitelist lives in a public GitHub repo as a JSON array of
--  Roblox UserIds. Edit that file to add/remove testers — no
--  replugin needed (CDN cache is bypassed via ?t= cache-bust).
--
--  NOTE: this is client-side gating. It deters casual sharing,
--  not a determined person who rips the source. Good enough for
--  a tester list; not a substitute for a server.
-- ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

local HttpService   = game:GetService("HttpService")
local StudioService = game:GetService("StudioService")

local TesterGate = {}

TesterGate.WHITELIST_URL =
	"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/RoPotat0/SineVFX-Testers/main/whitelist.json"

-- Returns: authorized (bool), reason (string), userId (number)
--   reason ∈ "ok" | "not-signed-in" | "network" | "parse" | "denied"
local CACHE_KEY = "SineVFX_testerOK"  -- remembers the last userId the list authorized

function TesterGate.check()
	local userId = StudioService:GetUserId()
	if userId == 0 then
		return false, "not-signed-in", userId
	end

	local plugin = TesterGate.plugin

	-- ?t=<time> busts GitHub's ~5min CDN cache so new testers work instantly
	local ok, body = pcall(function()
		return HttpService:GetAsync(TesterGate.WHITELIST_URL .. "?t=" .. os.time())
	end)
	if not ok then
		-- Network failed (offline, GitHub blip, or HTTP-permission denied). Don't
		-- lock out someone the list has authorized BEFORE on this machine; only a
		-- machine that has never fetched successfully stays closed.
		if plugin and plugin:GetSetting(CACHE_KEY) == userId then
			return true, "ok-cached", userId
		end
		return false, "network", userId
	end

	local ok2, list = pcall(function() return HttpService:JSONDecode(body) end)
	if not ok2 or type(list) ~= "table" then
		if plugin and plugin:GetSetting(CACHE_KEY) == userId then
			return true, "ok-cached", userId
		end
		return false, "parse", userId
	end

	for _, id in ipairs(list) do
		if tonumber(id) == userId then
			if plugin then plugin:SetSetting(CACHE_KEY, userId) end
			return true, "ok", userId
		end
	end
	-- fetch succeeded and the id isn't listed: genuinely revoked -> clear cache
	if plugin and plugin:GetSetting(CACHE_KEY) == userId then plugin:SetSetting(CACHE_KEY, nil) end
	return false, "denied", userId
end

return TesterGate

-- Helpers: pure utility functions (no dependencies)
local Helpers = {}
local function descTo(rootInst, maxDepth)
	maxDepth = maxDepth or 4
	local out = {}
	local function rec(inst, d) if d<=0 then return end for _,ch in ipairs(inst:GetChildren()) do out[#out+1]=ch; rec(ch, d-1) end end
	rec(rootInst, maxDepth)
	return out
end

-- Emit-path finders walk the FULL descendant tree (no depth cap): when you
-- emit a specific effect you want every part of it to fire, however deeply it
-- is nested. This runs once per emit, so GetDescendants() is cheap.
function Helpers.findEmitters(instance)
	local list = {}
	if instance:IsA("ParticleEmitter") then table.insert(list, instance) end
	for _, d in ipairs(instance:GetDescendants()) do
		if d:IsA("ParticleEmitter") then table.insert(list, d) end
	end
	return list
end

function Helpers.findSounds(instance)
	local list = {}
	if instance:IsA("Sound") then table.insert(list, instance) end
	for _, d in ipairs(instance:GetDescendants()) do
		if d:IsA("Sound") then table.insert(list, d) end
	end
	return list
end

function Helpers.findToggleables(instance)
	local list = {}
	local function chk(inst)
		if inst:IsA("Beam") or inst:IsA("Trail") or inst:IsA("Light") then
			table.insert(list, inst)
		end
	end
	chk(instance)
	for _, d in ipairs(instance:GetDescendants()) do chk(d) end
	return list
end

local function getSegments(instance)
	if not instance then return nil end
	local segs = {}
	local current = instance
	while current and current ~= game do
		if current == workspace then
			table.insert(segs, 1, "workspace")
			break
		end
		table.insert(segs, 1, current.Name)
		current = current.Parent
	end
	return segs
end
Helpers.getSegments = getSegments

-- valid bare Lua identifier? (anything else must be bracket-quoted, or the
-- generated code line is a syntax error: workspace.My-Part is a SUBTRACTION)
local function isIdent(name)
	return string.match(name, "^[%a_][%w_]*$") ~= nil
end
Helpers.isIdent = isIdent

-- ["name"] with backslashes and quotes escaped so ANY name stays valid Lua
local function quoteName(name)
	return '["' .. name:gsub("\\", "\\\\"):gsub('"', '\\"') .. '"]'
end
Helpers.quoteName = quoteName

function Helpers.getFullPath(instance)
	if not instance then return "nil" end
	local segs = getSegments(instance)
	if not segs or #segs == 0 then
		local name = instance.Name
		if isIdent(name) then return name end
		return quoteName(name)
	end
	local first = segs[1]
	local path
	if first == "workspace" then
		path = "workspace"
	elseif isIdent(first) then
		path = "game." .. first
	else
		path = "game" .. quoteName(first)
	end
	for i = 2, #segs do
		local name = segs[i]
		if isIdent(name) then
			path = path .. "." .. name
		else
			path = path .. quoteName(name)
		end
	end
	return path
end

return Helpers

-- Transformed: single module bundling the SineVFX transformed-VFX blueprints + 3D helpers.

-- Merged from EmitterSpec/TrailSpec/BeamSpec + Emitter3D/Trail3D/Beam3D (each relocated verbatim

-- inside an IIFE so locals stay isolated; the *3D `require(script.Parent.*Spec)` calls were

-- rewired to the in-module spec locals). Exposed as .EmitterSpec/.TrailSpec/.BeamSpec/.Emitter3D/.Trail3D/.Beam3D.

local __M = {}

local EmitterSpec = (function()
-- EmitterSpec: the single blueprint for a SineVFX 3D particle emitter.
-- transform() (TransformWindow) BUILDS the instance tree from this, PropertiesWindow RENDERS
-- editable rows from it, and EmitterRuntime SIMULATES from it -> one source of truth.
--
-- Layout (PART-based): the original part stays as the emitter root (tag=TAG, hidden/transparent);
-- a "RenderPart" clone (unlocked, visible) is the particle template; ALL settings live as
-- attributes on grouped Configurations under the Properties folder.
--
-- Attr descriptor: { a=<attributeName>, t=<uiType>, d=<default>, min=?, max=?, opts=? }
--   opts (enum only): list of allowed EnumItem names shown in the dropdown.
--   showIf: hide this row unless another attr matches. { a=<attr>, eq=<v> } | { a=<attr>,
--     oneOf={...} } | { a=<attr>, isnot=<v> }. The referenced attr may live in ANOTHER group
--     (attr names are unique across groups, so it resolves), and PropertiesWindow watches it and
--     refreshes -- so a toggle hides/shows its dependants live. Purely cosmetic: the runtime still
--     reads the attribute, so a hidden row keeps its last value.

-- Every material EXCEPT Air (Air is the invisible "no material" value, useless for a visible particle).
local MATERIAL_OPTS = {}
do
	for _, m in ipairs(Enum.Material:GetEnumItems()) do
		if m ~= Enum.Material.Air then MATERIAL_OPTS[#MATERIAL_OPTS+1] = m.Name end
	end
end

local NORMALID_OPTS = {"Top","Bottom","Left","Right","Front","Back"}  -- EmissionDirection (Enum.NormalId face)
local ORIENT_OPTS = {"None","FacingCamera","FacingCameraWorldUp","VelocityParallel","VelocityPerpendicular"}  -- Orientation (string; None = keep emitter orientation)

local SPEC = {
	TAG          = "SineVFX3D",
	RENDER_NAME  = "RenderPart",
	PROPS_FOLDER = "Properties",
	DEST_FOLDER  = "Destinations",   -- Folder of ObjectValues under Properties/Particles (see FinalDestination)

	groups = {
		{ name="Appearance", attrs={
			{ a="Enabled",      t="bool",   d=false },                                        -- starts OFF
			{ a="Color",        t="colseq", d=ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)) },         -- gradient over lifetime (like ParticleEmitter.Color)
			-- Per-axis SIZE IN STUDS over the particle's lifetime (like ParticleEmitter.Size --
			-- an absolute value, not a scale multiplier), each its own NumberSequence + envelope.
			-- Edited via one combined row with X/Y/Z toggle buttons in PropertiesWindow
			-- (t="axisseq" merges same-`axis` attrs into one UI). Seeded from the RenderPart's
			-- own authored dimensions at transform time (TransformWindow), not from `d` below.
			{ a="SizeX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0, axis="Size" },
			{ a="SizeY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0, axis="Size" },
			{ a="SizeZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0, axis="Size" },
			{ a="Transparency", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, max=1 },          -- transparency over lifetime (like ParticleEmitter.Transparency)
			{ a="Material",     t="enum",   d=Enum.Material.Glass, opts=MATERIAL_OPTS },    -- constant material of every particle
			{ a="Orientation",  t="enum",   d="None", opts=ORIENT_OPTS },  -- how each particle faces; None = keep the emitter's orientation
			{ a="ZOffset",      t="num",    d=0 },                                            -- render depth (studs toward/away from camera, like ParticleEmitter.ZOffset)
		}},
		{ name="Emission", attrs={
			{ a="EmissionDirection", t="enum", d=Enum.NormalId.Top, opts=NORMALID_OPTS },  -- face particles fly out of
			{ a="Rate",     t="num",    d=1,  min=0 },              -- particles / second (while Enabled)
			{ a="Speed",    t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0) },  -- studs/sec along emission direction, over lifetime (graph)
			{ a="Lifetime", t="range",  d=NumberRange.new(1,1) },   -- random lifetime per particle (min,max seconds)
			-- Per-axis ROTATION in degrees over the particle's lifetime (base orientation offset
			-- around the particle's local X/Y/Z), each its own NumberSequence + envelope. X/Y/Z row.
			{ a="RotationX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Rotation" },
			{ a="RotationY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Rotation" },
			{ a="RotationZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Rotation" },
			-- Per-axis ROTATION SPEED in degrees/sec over lifetime (continuous spin accumulated
			-- around X/Y/Z on top of Rotation), each its own NumberSequence + envelope. X/Y/Z row.
			{ a="RotSpeedX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="RotSpeed" },
			{ a="RotSpeedY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="RotSpeed" },
			{ a="RotSpeedZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="RotSpeed" },
			-- Per-axis ACCELERATION (studs/s^2) over lifetime, integrated into velocity. World axes,
			-- or local to the emitter when LockedToPart is on. X/Y/Z row (e.g. Y=-50 = gravity).
			{ a="AccelX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Acceleration" },
			{ a="AccelY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Acceleration" },
			{ a="AccelZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Acceleration" },
		}},
		{ name="Placement", attrs={
			-- SPREAD ANGLE (degrees, Vector2 x,y): randomises each particle's launch direction within
			-- +/- X (pitch) & +/- Y (yaw). Wide spread + Offset = a ring/shell; also gives Scatter room.
			{ a="SpreadAngle", t="range", d=Vector2.new(0,0) },
			-- OFFSET (studs, random range) along each particle's own (spread-randomised) direction;
			-- random per particle in [Min,Max] (Min==Max = exact). Wide SpreadAngle => a ring/shell.
			{ a="Offset", t="range", d=NumberRange.new(0,0), min=0 },
			-- SPACING (studs): MINIMUM gap between particles. On spawn, if the spread-driven position
			-- lands within this distance of an existing particle, it re-rolls the spread up to 3x for a
			-- gap; if none is found the particle is SKIPPED. Needs SpreadAngle/Offset for room.
			{ a="Spacing", t="num", d=0, min=0 },
			-- POSITION (studs, per-axis graph over lifetime): a fixed emitter-local x/y/z offset added
			-- to every particle, animatable over life. X/Y/Z row.
			{ a="PositionX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Position" },
			{ a="PositionY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Position" },
			{ a="PositionZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="Position" },
			-- ANCHOR ROTATION (deg over lifetime, per axis): ORBIT each particle's position AROUND the
			-- emitter centre (the spawn anchor) -> swirl / vortex. AnchorRotSpeed = continuous spin (deg/s).
			{ a="AnchorRotationX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotation" },
			{ a="AnchorRotationY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotation" },
			{ a="AnchorRotationZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotation" },
			{ a="AnchorRotSpeedX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotSpeed" },
			{ a="AnchorRotSpeedY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotSpeed" },
			{ a="AnchorRotSpeedZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="AnchorRotSpeed" },
		}},
		{ name="Bezier", attrs={
			-- Random bezier-ish spline paths. SplineOn activates it; each particle bends a random
			-- direction/amount (Curve, studs) with Waves bends along the way (1 arc, 2 S-curve...). The
			-- bend is zero at both ends, so a particle ALWAYS finishes exactly where it would WITHOUT the
			-- spline (its natural Speed/Acceleration endpoint); the spline only changes the route there.
			{ a="SplineOn", t="bool", d=false, label="Bezier" },
			{ a="Radius",   t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, label="Radius", showIf={a="SplineOn", eq=true} },
			{ a="BezierSpeed", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0, label="WeaveSpeed", showIf={a="SplineOn", eq=true} },  -- weave traversal speed over life, independent of travel Speed
			{ a="Aim", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0.5), min=0, max=1, label="Aim", showIf={a="SplineOn", eq=true} },  -- 0..1: biases each weave point toward the target it's heading for (centre); 1 = aims straight at it
			{ a="TaperStart", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1, label="TaperStart", showIf={a="SplineOn", eq=true} },  -- 0..1: fraction of the trip travelled before the weave radius starts shrinking to land exactly on target (0 = shrink the whole way, current behaviour)
		}},
		{ name="Shape", attrs={
			-- SPAWN SHAPE: region particles spawn from, in the emitter's local axes. Toggle OFF =
			-- spawn at the emitter centre (default, unchanged). ON = scatter the spawn position across
			-- the chosen Box/Sphere/Cylinder/Disc region (radius = ShapeRadius). A translucent outline
			-- of the region shows in the viewport while the emitter is selected (edit-mode preview).
			{ a="ShapeOn", t="bool", d=false, label="Shape" },
			{ a="Shape", t="enum", d="Sphere", opts={"Box","Sphere","Cylinder","Disc"}, label="Type", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			-- VOLUME = spawn anywhere inside the shape; SURFACE = only on its shell/edge.
			{ a="ShapeStyle", t="enum", d="Volume", opts={"Volume","Surface"}, showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			-- Initial direction relative to the shape centre (non-Point shapes): Outward (explosion),
			-- Inward (implosion), or In & Out. SpreadAngle still perturbs on top.
			{ a="ShapeInOut", t="enum", d="Outward", opts={"Outward","Inward","InAndOut"}, showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			-- SIZE per local axis (studs, half-extent), graphed over the emitter's cycle time (loops over
			-- the particle Lifetime). Non-uniform axes = ellipsoid / flat disc / stretched box.
			{ a="ShapeSizeX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(4), min=0, axis="ShapeSize", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			{ a="ShapeSizeY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(4), min=0, axis="ShapeSize", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			{ a="ShapeSizeZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(4), min=0, axis="ShapeSize", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			-- ROTATE the spawn region (and its In/Out dirs) per local axis (degrees), graphed over the
			-- same emitter cycle time -- e.g. tilt a Disc or spin the region.
			{ a="ShapeRotationX", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="ShapeRotation", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			{ a="ShapeRotationY", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="ShapeRotation", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
			{ a="ShapeRotationZ", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), axis="ShapeRotation", showIf={a="ShapeOn", eq=true} },
		}},
		{ name="Particles", attrs={
			{ a="LockedToPart", t="bool", d=false },  -- particles ride the emitter part; makes Placement Offset/Position + Acceleration local
			-- ROUTE: where each particle heads, using its own Speed. Default = its normal path.
			-- Center = the emitter centre (this was the Bezier "Center" toggle). Custom = the Target1..N
			-- target Instances. On arrival the particle CLAMPS onto the point but keeps living, so any
			-- bezier weave tapers to nothing by end-of-life instead of stopping dead.
			{ a="Route", t="enum", d="Default", opts={"Default","Center","Custom"} },
			-- Scatter (studs): each particle rolls a random offset inside a ball of this radius and
			-- heads for THAT instead of the exact destination, so a crowd does not converge on one
			-- point. Rolled once at spawn and kept, so a particle tracking a moving target keeps its
			-- own offset rather than jittering. 0 = every particle aims at the exact point.
			{ a="RouteRandom", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=50 },
			-- How targets are handed out. Random/Nearest/Even each send a particle to exactly ONE
			-- target; InOrder is the odd one out and routes each particle THROUGH every target in turn
			-- (Target1 -> Target2 -> ...), advancing as it arrives and stopping on the last.
			-- Even = round-robin in Target1..N order, so the targets get an even share.
			{ a="RoutePick", t="enum", d="Random", opts={"Random","Nearest","Even","InOrder"} },
			-- InOrder only: rounds the corners of the tour. 0 = pivot on the spot at each target
			-- (hard corner). Higher = start bending toward the next target sooner, so the particle
			-- arcs through the turn and cuts the corner. Scaled by the next leg's length, so it
			-- behaves the same whether targets are 5 or 500 studs apart.
			{ a="RouteSmooth", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1 },
			-- The target slots. NOT attributes: Roblox attributes cannot hold Instance references, so
			-- each is a real ObjectValue named TargetN inside the DEST_FOLDER folder. t="object" --
			-- writers that seed/backfill attributes MUST skip these descriptors.
			-- Progressive: TargetN's row only appears once Target(N-1) actually holds something, so
			-- the list grows as you fill it and there is no count to keep in sync (a TargetCount
			-- attribute could be typed past its own max, which showed every slot at once).
			{ a="Target1", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target2", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target3", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target4", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target5", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target6", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target7", t="object", d=nil },
			{ a="Target8", t="object", d=nil },
		}},
		{ name="Nested", attrs={
			-- Effects/attachments the user drops INSIDE the RenderPart. Each spawned 3D particle is a
			-- fresh clone of RenderPart, so nested state starts as authored; these act on the CLONE only.
			{ a="EnableOnSpawn",    t="bool", d=false, label="Enable" },           -- nested ParticleEmitter/Beam/Trail/Light .Enabled=true + Sound:Play() when a particle spawns
			{ a="EmitOnSpawn",      t="bool", d=false, label="Emit" },             -- fire ANYTHING nested on spawn like the Emit button: ParticleEmitter:Emit(), Sound:Play(), Beam/Trail/Light Enabled=true
			{ a="ScaleAttachments", t="bool", d=true,  label="ScaleAttachments" }, -- nested Attachment offsets scale with the particle's animated Size (beams/trails between them grow/shrink)
		}},
		{ name="Collision", attrs={
			-- COLLISIONS: when on, each particle raycasts against the world each frame and bounces off
			-- surfaces (unlocked emitters only; a per-frame raycast budget caps the cost). Best paired
			-- with a downward Acceleration (gravity).
			{ a="Collisions", t="bool", d=false },
			{ a="Bounce",   t="range", d=NumberRange.new(0,0), min=0, max=1, showIf={a="Collisions", eq=true} },  -- 0 = stick, 1 = full bounce (random per particle in [Min,Max])
			{ a="Friction", t="range", d=NumberRange.new(0,0), min=0, max=1, showIf={a="Collisions", eq=true} },  -- 0 = keep slide speed, 1 = kill it (random per particle)
		}},
		{ name="Optimization", attrs={
			-- STEP RATE: throttle this emitter's particle simulation to save CPU. OFF (default) = update
			-- every frame (full quality). When ON, the sim runs `StepRate` steps/second, coalescing motion
			-- between steps -- trades smoothness for performance when there are lots of particles.
			{ a="StepRateOn", t="bool", d=false, label="StepRate" },
			{ a="StepRate",   t="num",  d=60, min=0, max=120, label="StepFps", showIf={a="StepRateOn", eq=true} },
			-- CULL OFFSCREEN: while a particle isn't in the camera view, keep advancing its position but
			-- skip all render work (facing/CFrame/colour/size/nested). It snaps to the right spot when it
			-- comes back on screen. Cheap way to cut cost when most particles are off-screen.
			{ a="CullOffscreen", t="bool", d=false, label="Cull Offscreen" },
		}},
	},
}
-- Attribute names must be UNIQUE ACROSS GROUPS, not just within one: PropertiesWindow's EMITTER_DEF
-- and Emitter3D's ATTR_GROUP are both keyed by attribute name alone, so a name used twice makes the
-- later group silently steal the earlier group's rows -- they then read/write the wrong
-- Configuration. That has shipped before: the duplicate reads/writes fine but the WRONG type lands
-- in the wrong place, and it surfaced only as a type error deep in the runtime, nowhere near the
-- spec. Use `label=` to show a short name in the UI while keeping the attribute name unique.
do
	local seen = {}
	for _, g in ipairs(SPEC.groups) do
		for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
			if seen[d.a] then
				error(("[SineVFX] EmitterSpec: duplicate attribute %q in groups %q and %q -- attribute names must be unique across ALL groups")
					:format(d.a, seen[d.a], g.name), 0)
			end
			seen[d.a] = g.name
		end
	end
end

return SPEC
end)()

local TrailSpec = (function()
-- TrailSpec: the blueprint for a SineVFX TRANSFORMED TRAIL -- the Trail counterpart of EmitterSpec.
-- transform() (TransformWindow) BUILDS the Properties folder from this, PropertiesWindow RENDERS
-- the rows, and TrailRuntime DRIVES it -> one source of truth, same as EmitterSpec.
--
-- WHAT IT IS: the trail you authored, with SineVFX properties on top, that can also EMIT copies of
-- itself. Two halves:
--
--   THE SOURCE TRAIL keeps rendering and is driven live from these attributes every frame. Editing
--   Color/Gradient/Size/Transparency changes what you see immediately. It is NOT a hidden template.
--
--   EMITTED COPIES are clones of the trail + its two Attachments, dropped on a part in a Terrain
--   container folder. They DO NOT FLY: the copy is glued to the emitting part and only ever moves
--   because that part moves. Emitting while the part is still adds nothing on screen, by design.
--   Each copy lives for Lifetime seconds, ageing through the Color/Size/Transparency graphs.
--
-- TWO COLOUR AXES, and they are not the same thing:
--   Color    -- over a copy's LIFETIME. The whole ribbon shifts colour as that copy ages.
--   Gradient -- along the ribbon's LENGTH (head to tail). This is what Roblox's Trail.Color IS.
-- TrailRuntime multiplies them together, so they compose instead of fighting. Width works the
-- same way: WidthScale shapes the ribbon along its LENGTH, and Size scales that whole sequence
-- over the copy's LIFETIME. Neither ever touches the attachments -- their spacing is the user's
-- authored geometry and the runtime must not write it.
-- On the SOURCE trail the lifetime graphs are sampled at t=0, their start.
--
-- Descriptor fields match EmitterSpec: { a, t, d, min, max, opts, label, showIf, axis, desc }.
--
-- NOTE ON NAME COLLISIONS: these attribute names (Enabled/Color/Transparency/Lifetime/...)
-- DELIBERATELY overlap EmitterSpec's. That is safe ONLY because every consumer keys its lookup
-- tables by KIND first (PropertiesWindow DEF_BY_KIND, Emitter3D/Trail3D ATTR_GROUP per-spec).
-- Never merge the two specs into one flat table.

local TEXTUREMODE_OPTS = {"Stretch","Wrap","Static"}

local SPEC = {
	TAG          = "SineVFXTransformedTrail",
	PROPS_FOLDER = "Properties",
	CLASS        = "Trail",
	-- The SAME Terrain folder the 3D particle emitters use: one SineVFX folder for everything, not
	-- a second one just for trails. Trade-off worth knowing: EmitterRuntime's flush() empties the
	-- whole folder, so stopping the particle preview also clears emitted trails.
	CONTAINER    = "SineVFXParticles",

	groups = {
		{ name="Appearance", attrs={
			-- The NORMAL trail toggle: drives the real Trail.Enabled, i.e. does the source ribbon
			-- draw at all. Distinct from Emission > Emitting, which is about spawning copies. The
			-- two names must differ (the uniqueness guard below is per spec, not per group).
			{ a="Enabled", t="bool", d=true,
			  desc="Draw the trail. This is the trail's own Enabled. To emit copies instead, use Emission > Emitting." },
			{ a="Color", t="colseq", d=ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)),
			  desc="Colour over a copy's lifetime. The whole ribbon shifts as it ages. Multiplied with Gradient." },
			{ a="Gradient", t="colseq", d=ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)),
			  desc="Colour along the ribbon's length, from the head to the fading tail. Multiplied with Color." },
			-- SIZE is a MULTIPLIER ON WIDTHSCALE, not an absolute width and NOT the attachment
			-- spacing: the runtime never writes the user's attachments. Roblox clamps WidthScale to
			-- 0..1, so Size always thins the ribbon but only widens it where WidthScale is below 1.
			{ a="Size", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0,
			  desc="Multiplies Width Scale over a copy's lifetime, so the ribbon can thin or thicken as it ages. 1 leaves Width Scale as drawn." },
			{ a="Transparency", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, max=1,
			  desc="Extra transparency over a copy's lifetime, on top of Transparency Gradient." },
			{ a="TransparencyGradient", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, max=1, label="Transparency Gradient",
			  desc="Transparency along the ribbon's length, head to tail. This is the trail's own transparency." },
			{ a="Texture", t="asset", d="",
			  desc="Image drawn along the ribbon. Leave empty for a plain trail." },
			{ a="TextureMode", t="enum", d="Stretch", opts=TEXTUREMODE_OPTS },
			{ a="TextureLength", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0,
			  desc="Texture length over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="LightEmission", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1 },
			{ a="LightInfluence", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1 },
			-- Matches the native rule in PropertiesWindow: glow strength genuinely does nothing at
			-- LightInfluence 1 (the scene's lighting fully takes over), but still counts below it.
			{ a="Brightness", t="num", d=1, min=0, showIf={a="LightInfluence", isnot=1},
			  desc="Glow strength. Has no effect once Light Influence reaches 1." },
			{ a="FaceCamera", t="bool", d=false },
		}},
		{ name="Emission", attrs={
			{ a="Emitting", t="bool", d=false,
			  desc="Emit copies of the trail continuously at Rate. Starts off. This is separate from Appearance > Enabled, which just draws the trail itself." },
			{ a="Rate", t="num", d=1, min=0,
			  desc="Copies emitted per second while Enabled is on." },
			-- TWO lifetimes, and they are different things:
			--   Lifetime     -- the trail's OWN Lifetime (drives the real Trail.Lifetime): how long the
			--                   ribbon lingers behind the attachments. Means exactly what it means on a
			--                   normal Trail, and seeds from the live trail because the name matches.
			--   Duration     -- how long an emitted trail lives before it is deleted. Also the axis the
			--                   Colour/Size/Transparency graphs age along.
			{ a="Lifetime", t="num", d=1, min=0,
			  desc="Seconds the ribbon lingers behind the attachments. This is the trail's own Lifetime." },
			{ a="Duration", t="num", d=1, min=0,
			  desc="Seconds an emitted trail lives before it is deleted. The Colour, Size and Transparency graphs run over this time." },
			{ a="MinLength", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0.1), min=0,
			  desc="Studs the attachments must travel before the ribbon starts drawing, over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="MaxLength", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0,
			  desc="Longest the ribbon can get, in studs, over a copy's lifetime. 0 means no limit." },
			{ a="WidthScale", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0, max=1,
			  desc="Width along the ribbon's length (0 is the tail end, 1 is the head). Size multiplies this over time." },
		}},
	},
}

-- Same uniqueness guard as EmitterSpec, and for the same reason: consumers key by attribute name
-- WITHIN a kind, so a duplicate inside this spec makes the later group silently steal the earlier
-- one's rows. (Collisions with EmitterSpec are fine -- see the note at the top.)
do
	local seen = {}
	for _, g in ipairs(SPEC.groups) do
		for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
			if seen[d.a] then
				error(("[SineVFX] TrailSpec: duplicate attribute %q in groups %q and %q -- attribute names must be unique across ALL groups")
					:format(d.a, seen[d.a], g.name), 0)
			end
			seen[d.a] = g.name
		end
	end
end

return SPEC

end)()

local BeamSpec = (function()
-- BeamSpec: the blueprint for a SineVFX TRANSFORMED BEAM. See TrailSpec for the shared model.
--
-- TWO COLOUR AXES and TWO TRANSPARENCY AXES, same pattern:
--   Color / Transparency          -- over a copy's LIFETIME (the copy shifts as it ages).
--   Gradient / TransparencyGradient -- along the beam's LENGTH. These ARE Roblox's Beam.Color and
--                                      Beam.Transparency, and are what the source beam syncs to.
-- BeamRuntime composes them (Gradient tinted by Color; TransparencyGradient faded by Transparency).
--
-- Width0/Width1/CurveSize0/CurveSize1/TextureLength/TextureSpeed are NumberSequences sampled over a
-- copy's LIFETIME. On the SOURCE beam every lifetime graph is sampled at t=0, its start.

local TEXTUREMODE_OPTS = {"Stretch","Wrap","Static"}

local SPEC = {
	TAG          = "SineVFXTransformedBeam",
	PROPS_FOLDER = "Properties",
	CLASS        = "Beam",
	CONTAINER    = "SineVFXParticles",

	groups = {
		{ name="Appearance", attrs={
			{ a="Enabled", t="bool", d=true,
			  desc="Draw the beam. This is the beam's own Enabled. To emit copies instead, use Emission > Emitting." },
			{ a="Color", t="colseq", d=ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)),
			  desc="Colour over a copy's lifetime. The whole beam shifts as it ages. Multiplied with Gradient." },
			{ a="Gradient", t="colseq", d=ColorSequence.new(Color3.new(1,1,1)),
			  desc="Colour along the beam's length, from attachment 0 to attachment 1. This is the beam's own colour." },
			{ a="Transparency", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, max=1,
			  desc="Extra transparency over a copy's lifetime, on top of Transparency Gradient." },
			{ a="TransparencyGradient", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0), min=0, max=1, label="Transparency Gradient",
			  desc="Transparency along the beam's length. This is the beam's own transparency." },
			{ a="Texture", t="asset", d="",
			  desc="Image drawn along the beam. Leave empty for a plain beam." },
			{ a="TextureMode", t="enum", d="Stretch", opts=TEXTUREMODE_OPTS },
			{ a="TextureLength", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0,
			  desc="Texture length over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="TextureSpeed", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1),
			  desc="How fast the texture scrolls, over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="LightEmission", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1 },
			{ a="LightInfluence", t="num", d=0, min=0, max=1 },
			{ a="Brightness", t="num", d=1, min=0, showIf={a="LightInfluence", isnot=1},
			  desc="Glow strength. Has no effect once Light Influence reaches 1." },
			{ a="ZOffset", t="num", d=0,
			  desc="Draws the beam nearer or farther from the camera, in studs." },
		}},
		{ name="Shape", attrs={
			{ a="Width0", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0,
			  desc="Beam width at attachment 0 over a copy's lifetime, in studs." },
			{ a="Width1", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(1), min=0,
			  desc="Beam width at attachment 1 over a copy's lifetime, in studs." },
			{ a="CurveSize0", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0),
			  desc="Curvature control handle at attachment 0, over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="CurveSize1", t="numseq", d=NumberSequence.new(0),
			  desc="Curvature control handle at attachment 1, over a copy's lifetime." },
			{ a="Segments", t="num", d=10, min=1, intgr=true,
			  desc="How many straight segments the beam is drawn with. More = smoother curves." },
			{ a="FaceCamera", t="bool", d=false },
		}},
		{ name="Emission", attrs={
			{ a="Emitting", t="bool", d=false,
			  desc="Emit copies of the beam continuously at Rate. Starts off. This is separate from Appearance > Enabled, which just draws the beam itself." },
			{ a="Rate", t="num", d=1, min=0,
			  desc="Copies emitted per second while Enabled is on." },
			{ a="Lifetime", t="num", d=1, min=0,
			  desc="Seconds an emitted beam lives before it is deleted. Every lifetime graph runs over this time." },
		}},
	},
}

do
	local seen = {}
	for _, g in ipairs(SPEC.groups) do
		for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do
			if seen[d.a] then
				error(("[SineVFX] BeamSpec: duplicate attribute %q in groups %q and %q")
					:format(d.a, seen[d.a], g.name), 0)
			end
			seen[d.a] = g.name
		end
	end
end

return SPEC

end)()

local Emitter3D = (function()
-- Emitter3D: shared helper for tools (Shifter/Resizer/Retimer/etc.) to read & write the
-- attribute-backed properties of a SineVFX 3D particle emitter (a tagged BasePart whose real
-- "properties" live as attributes on Configuration folders under its Properties folder).
local CollectionService = game:GetService("CollectionService")
local EmitterSpec = EmitterSpec

local M = {}
M.TAG = EmitterSpec.TAG

-- attribute name -> the group/config it lives on (built from the spec, single source of truth)
local ATTR_GROUP = {}
for _, g in ipairs(EmitterSpec.groups) do
	for _, d in ipairs(g.attrs) do ATTR_GROUP[d.a] = g.name end
end

function M.isEmitter(inst)
	return typeof(inst) == "Instance" and inst:IsA("BasePart") and CollectionService:HasTag(inst, M.TAG)
end

-- DIRECT SELECTION WINS. Nested content is hidden from the tools so that selecting an EMITTER
-- doesn't drag its internals in as if they were standalone -- but if the user reached past the
-- emitter and clicked the nested Trail/emitter/sound itself, they mean that instance, and every
-- tool should treat it exactly as it would outside a RenderPart. Without this, dropping something
-- into a RenderPart silently stripped it of every tool that used to recognise it.
-- Cached briefly: the tools call this once per candidate while walking a selection, and Selection
-- :Get() allocates a fresh table each call.
local _selSvc, _selSet, _selAt = nil, nil, -1
local function _directlySelected(inst)
	if typeof(inst) ~= "Instance" then return false end
	if _selSvc == nil then
		local ok, sv = pcall(function() return game:GetService("Selection") end)
		_selSvc = (ok and sv) or false
	end
	if not _selSvc then return false end
	local now = os.clock()
	if (not _selSet) or (now - _selAt) > 0.05 then
		local ok, list = pcall(function() return _selSvc:Get() end)
		if not ok then return false end
		_selSet = {}
		for _, o in ipairs(list) do _selSet[o] = true end
		_selAt = now
	end
	return _selSet[inst] == true
end

-- True if `inst` IS a 3D emitter or lives INSIDE one (its RenderPart, Properties folder, or any
-- nested effect) AND was not picked directly. Tools use this to leave the 3D-particle subtree
-- alone -- only the emitter itself is a target (as a "SineVFX3D"), never its internals treated as
-- plain BaseParts/etc.
function M.isInsideEmitter(inst)
	if _directlySelected(inst) then return false end
	local cur = inst
	while cur and cur ~= game do
		if typeof(cur) == "Instance" and cur:IsA("BasePart") and CollectionService:HasTag(cur, M.TAG) then
			return true
		end
		cur = cur.Parent
	end
